items.comment_full:
Nice place, great location.
However, if you are uncomfortable with homeless people literally sleeping up against the house, laying on the entrance way, asking you for drugs as you walk in/out of garage, and defecating on the driveway, this is not the place for you. Be aware, it is Venice, after all.
Other than that, the place was fine.
Property manager, Andrew (not the owner), who interacted with us on site was kind and attentive. Would have felt more like a proper Airbnb experience had we been hosted by the actual host / owner, but not a huge deal. Either way, Andrew promptly sorted out any issues we had.
Location is unbeatable. Right by the pier / ocean.
Nice chatty neighbor who was helpful and reassured us when we didn’t feel comfortable with the homeless folks situation. He even gave pro tips about how to easily clean up the human feces.....let it dry for a couple days, apparently, and comes up in one piece. Lucky, Andrew, the property manager, swiftly came over and sorted it....we weren’t ok with waiting a couple days or cleaning it up ourselves.
Interior is generally as photographed. Spacious and comfortable. Great kitchen. Huge master bedroom.
Floors were really dirty, need to wear slippers or just keep your shoes on. We found being barefoot or in socks to be unpleasant. There was an ‘old house’ smell to the place when we arrived. We either got used to it or it went away after a day or so.
Prior to our arrival, as we were coming from China with our infant son, I had asked the host to help buy a $25 IKEA height chair that we would reimburse him for (and that he could keep at house for future guests). We were told to order / pay for it ourselves and have it delivered. Not a big deal, but as rental fee was north of USD 5,000 for our stay at his place, we expected he could handle this tiny request for us.
I wouldn’t recommend this place to families with kids for the above reasons. We genuinely felt unsafe with the homeless people situation. But, with no kids and a group of friends, this place is fine.
items.one_line: Homeless defecation on driveway, neighbor's feces tips

So *believe me* when I tell you this level of noise is un-freaking-
items.comment_full: Not as advertised in my opinion. We had to leave after the first night, mostly because of the noise. I could go on and on about a slew of vitriolic texts from Neil and Katie, including "You have an ugly personality," an "ugly nature," and "Grow up and get lost. Thanks for your $$!" Bottom line: this place is BEYOND LOUD at night. I’m not talking cool, ambient city noise. We live in a huge city with nightly helicopters, etc. We also love the West Village, having stayed there countless times, including on Horatio, 8th, and 10th. So *believe me* when I tell you this level of noise is un-freaking-bearable. There's a club nearby that plays loud live music from 10:00pm until 3:00am Every. Single. Night. For many people this isn't a problem. If you’re in NYC to party until after 3:00am, if you sleep like the dead, or if you’re hearing impaired, you won’t be bothered. But this place is listed as family/kid friendly and it isn't. It also says it has working smoke detectors and it doesn’t. I only noticed this because the bedroom A/C was plugged in half way and kept shorting out every time I touched it. Now about the words “LUXURY” and “LOVELY.” Um, no. It’s 1/3 Nice, 1/3 Meh and 1/3 Holy-crap-that’s-gross. The living room is nice. Very large by NY standards, with high ceilings and tasteful (if maritime steampunk) decor. Totally different furniture than the pictures, but we didn't care. The bedrooms are small, but the bed we lay awake in all night saying FML was comfortable (although when we pulled the sheets there was a mysterious stain on the mattress). But what’s really gross is the bathroom. The shower is yucky, and the floor is sticky with that unmistakable old-urine-seeped-into-the-floor-forever smell. There were a bunch of cockroaches at night. I caught a picture of one cute little “evening-time” roach, but was too freaked to take a picture of the 5-6 roaches that scattered during the night. None of the doorknobs work, but for some reason the bathroom door is scotch-taped open. There is an enormous pile of garbage —er, sorry, “Recycling” outside the front door. Honestly, the garbage and the roaches can be very common for NYC, especially on the 1st floor, but you shouldn’t throw around the words LUXURY and LOVELY. We asked for a refund for the second night and Neil said "Nope!" Later, he offered us a one night refund "take it or leave it" but only after the 15 day AirBnB review period had expired. Neil will say I started with the "name calling." It's totally true. I called him a liar, the only "name" I ever used. Not my finest moment, but he was gaslighting us, saying we were outliers b/c we had a problem with the noise (“Nobody else had a problem” then “You should have read the reviews BEFORE” then back to “100s of people have stayed. Only you have a problem.") Which is it?
items.one_line: Live music club blasts til 3am, host calls guests ugly, insults

items.comment_full:
**WARNING DANGEROUS AND UNSAFE CONDITIONS - DETAILS BELOW**
[TLDR - 24 hour "security guard" was sleeping while I was beaten and robbed out front of the building]
Long Version:
I stayed for 3 of 5 days of my reservation at this listing. It was clean and the listing was accurate. However, on the third night I returned home and there were 3 men waiting in front of the building smoking.
**When I approached I was attacked and robbed.**
I resisted and much as I could, and the attack dragged on for about 1 & 1/2 minutes. There are cameras and a 24 hour guard, so I assumed if I resisted long enough, then the guard would see the altercation, call the police, and come outside to run off the attackers. This never happened. After the attackers left, I burst into the building and yelled to call the police. The police arrived, and when we asked for footage the guard claimed there was none...very strange since I can see all of the cameras everywhere.
I knew something was not right so I immediately returned to my room and packed my bags to leave.
On the way out, not even an hour after I was attacked and the police had been on the scene, THE SECURITY GUARD WAS SLEEPING AND SNORING LOUDLY IN THE LOBBY. I took video of this, it's posted here for anyone who wishes to view it:
https://photos.app.goo.gl/y4uDNfMSTacqvxgs9
It is my belief that there is in fact video footage, but the guard was trying to cover up the fact that he was sleeping while I was being attacked and robbed. Do not stay at this building, guest safety is not a consideration for them.
items.one_line: Attacked and robbed outside, security guard was sleeping

items.comment_full: When me and my friends were looking for a place to stay. We listed few amenities that we needed. Also used filter. When we found Alex's place, we decided to stay as it had definitely nice view and met all the listed amenities including parking lot. However, after the first night of stay, our car has disappeared from the parking lot, without any notes. This was terrifying because we thought the car has been stolen. Alex and us called the building manager, however he CCTV records were all deleted. Then, we called cops and etc. so we tracked out vehicle with the help of police and found that is has been towed!! According to the building policies, the car can not be parked. Unless its listed on the visitor list. As the building also doesn't allow airbnb, Alex did not make a notice to the manager. We had really frustrating days going to towing company where, Alex used us to collect evidence regarding her problem. Overall, it was horrible and I hope anyone else goes through what we experienced. Sorry Alex, u said u gave us 5 star but we are not.
items.one_line: Car towed from parking lot, CCTV deleted, guest used as evidence collector

items.comment_full:
We stayed in the Guesthouse from January-March while completing one of my clinical rounds. I was not really around much given that, but my husband used the guesthouse as a workplace the majority of the time.

It is important to note a few things: There are multiple rentals at this property with duplicate photos. There are some photos of the outside of the guesthouse, but no other accurate representations of the inside other than the kitchen sink. The photos imply that there are multiple rooms, a bedroom with bed and separate living area. However, the guesthouse is a studio-loft with no doors inside. The bed is a mattress on the floor of a lofted area that overlooks the living/kitchen and is open to the bathroom. The photos provided are primarily of the main house. Being a future Occupational Therapist, I would be remiss if I didn't mention that it's somewhat difficult to get in and out of the loft given the staircase/set-up, and know that you cannot stand up fully after you're in there (I'm 5'2"). I certainly don't mean to sound completely negative, but I feel that they're important details to know when comparing places.

The garden is beautiful but it also requires a lot of work which means gardeners several days a week working throughout the day. My husband was working from the guesthouse so it was less oasis and a bit distracting.

Michal's very sincere in his desire that you have a good visit and enjoy his space. He proved to be a very kind, polite and available host, providing towels, sheets and the basics. For our needs however, we wouldn't have chosen it knowing the layout/openness compared to the pictures. It is a fine studio apartment in a beautiful area, but for an extended stay during which we had planned on having visitors it didn't really work. It can get a little weird when you have your in-laws spending the night on the floor, then stepping over them in their sleep only to wake them when you pee because there is no doors and the sound travels pretty well. Maybe that's just me but there isn't much privacy. We did ask about the situation and he told us Airbnb had taken the photos and set up the listing, but as of now (1-month after our stay finished) they haven't been changed, so hopefully it's in the works so there isn't additional unnecessary frustration. It just ended up being more costly than we would have paid for a place that didn't really accommodate our guests. Note that Michal allows guests for free which is nice; he has a twin size blow-up mattress available to you.

Know that the area is safe and quiet nights/weekends. Se ran/walked/biked around the area regularly without problems. The downtown is close enough to walk/bike (4miles) but be prepared that it will be all uphill on the way back!

This place is beautiful and Michal's great-it just wasn't at all what we expected.
items.one_line: Photos show main house, guest receives studio loft on floor

items.comment_full:
Do not stay in this place. Hieu's property may be lovely in the pictures, but up close it is poorly maintained, disgustingly dirty, and not well cared for. What's more, the host has been shown to be petty and vindictive, engaging in retaliatory behavior and harassment.
When I went there for my wife's 40th birthday party, as part of a surprise I spent a year organizing, it was one thing to have to clean the place, but quite another to have to deal with a 10ft - 15ft fire that started because the grease trap on the grill had not been emptied in months or perhaps all summer. Two of my guests were almost burned as a result and it's by the grace of God no one was harmed.
And that was before the host started to retaliate against and harass me.
But let's start at the beginning.
There were multiple issues with this property:
1. The house wasn't clean. The countertops smelled of mildew and and had film on them when we arrived, and had to be wiped up across the house and in the bathrooms. They had clearly used either no solution or an old and dirty rag and it was very unpleasant.
2. The washing machine was the same. The smell of mildew was strong I could smell it down the hallway. I ran the cleaning cycle on it just get rid of the smell.
3. When we stripped the beds, we found crumbs in the master bedroom on the sheets meaning they had not been cleaned prior to our arrival. Another of our friends found hair that wasn't hers on the pillow the first night.
4. The walls and baseboards had marks that needed scrubbing that made the house feel dirty and ill used.
5. There was hair in the scrubbing brush in the sink. It looked like it had been used to clean a floor or shower drain and it was disgusting. It was certainly not something I wanted to use to clean dishes. We were able to clean it (which was also disgusting) and ran it through the dishwasher, not because we wanted to, but for want of other cleaning supplies.
6. We had to sweep the upstairs as well because the floors were gritty.
7. The grill had not been cleaned in a very long time. There were also no appropriate cleaning tools for the grill either. The one brush outside was a plastic scrub brush that was not for grills (which was clear because it had been partially melted) and still used for that purpose. This is a cooking instrument and who wants to eat burgers mixed with melted plastic? More on this later.
8. There was a pair of high heels left in the downstairs bedroom. They were not hard to see and it's clear whomever was supposed to "clean" the room never did so, otherwise they would have been picked up instead of just being left there. It was very weird and made my guests uncomfortable.
The place was also in a general state of minor to significant disrepair. Examples include:
1. the back door knob was clearly coming about and fell apart the morning we left. I was able to fix it but not permanently.
2. The master bathroom toilet paper holder was coming out of the wall due to loose drywall anchors.
3. The Gas range on the stove was not working properly to broken burners/ loose connections. One didn't even work and had been taped so it couldn't be turned. The griddle range was so loose gas couldn't flow properly leaving an issue where gas was coming out but not lighting, which is serious.
4. There were multiple recreation items provided but could not be used because there were so many leaks in them they would not hold air.
5. There were pieces of the bannister that were loose and falling down.
6. The lock on the master bathroom was not installed properly and coming apart, and most importantly, didn't actually lock the door.
7. There were large very obvious scratches in some of the fixtures that made it feel run down and certainly not the luxurious retreat advertised.
8. In one corner was a pile of loose unconnected wires and other electrical components. This was a clear safety issue and made me very glad I didn't bring my kids as it was clearly a safety risk.
9. There was a sizable and very poorly patched hole in the wall by the garage door.
10. There was a disgustingly dirty cooler outside which apparently was supposed to have been thrown away but wasn't.
11. There were no fire starters because the two provided were both empty. When I complained to the host, she told me to drive to the nearest town and get some more.
But the biggest issue was by far the grill. Once it was clear the gas range wasn't working (which is just as well because the only frying pan provided was a 10in pan and I'm not entirely sure how to cook for seven people in such a small pan), we decided to do the grill. After using a paper towel (only one roll was provided so it was used sparingly) to attempt to clean it, it was clear that wouldn't cut it. The grill hadn't been cleaned in some while given the amount of carbonized food (and melted plastic) on the grill plates. So we used the top grill rack to cook our food.
However, on the second day, the grease trap erupted in a 10-15 foot flame with thick black smoke that left discolorations on the second floor window. It happened so quickly it almost burnt two of my guests. Thankfully, we knew enough to turn off the gas and leave the grill closed and it eventually died out.
But that left no cooking implements. Thankfully, they had a slow cooker and a really fun bar just walking distance away. The
items.one_line: Dirty property, grease trap fire threatens guests, host retaliates with harassment

Ginette was a nice lady who quickly explained the "recycling" and "house keys" rules of the apartment and left us to our stay. - That same night at around midnight, my wife and I were looking to go to bed for a well-deserved rest after our 24 hour flight, and were loo
items.comment_full: Our experience with Nath and the entire booking was the WORST we had on Airbnb. The story goes as follows: - We arrived to the apartment for check-in at around 8 pm in the evening for a long 42 nights' stay, Nath was not there to welcome us or give us a tour of the apartment, but she sent her friend "Ginette" to give us the keys. Ginette was a nice lady who quickly explained the "recycling" and "house keys" rules of the apartment and left us to our stay. - That same night at around midnight, my wife and I were looking to go to bed for a well-deserved rest after our 24 hour flight, and were looking at switching off all the lights in the apartments. We could not however find the switch for the small closet's light in the bedroom and were not able to sleep without turning it off. That is when my wife saw a small button and pressed it hoping it would turn off the light. When the button didn't do anything and made a "click" sound, we immediately saw that this was the "panic button" that Nath had told us about, but never mentioned its exact location. - We then immediately took action and called the police to explain that this was a false alarm, they told us it was OK, and no one came from their side. We eventually went to bed and no one was disturbed. To our shock and surprise the 2nd day, we wake up to a very disturbing message from Nath telling us that we are not "worhty" of renting her apartment and that we need to pay 500$ for the panic button issue (even though the police didn't eventually come and no one was disturbed), and threatening us that if we do not follow her "rules" of the apartment that we will be kicked out. We were very unsettled and unhappy with this message as we are regular Airbnb clients with very good reviews everywhere we go in the world. The button pressed was a mishap and was fixed from our side very quickly. She even went a step further and wrote to us saying "don't open the windows, don't touch the heating, nor the central heating system, nor anything else!" - this meant that if we felt too hot or too cold in the apartment, we could not even change the heating to suit our needs. This immediately made us feel like living in a prison, and felt that the best way was for us to leave the apartment (even if that meant we had to look for another place at the last minute and in the peak season of Montreal). We asked the host to cancel our reservation and then went ahead and sent a complaint to the Airbnb team. Other than this issue, the house itself is in a good neighborhood, is spacious and very well equipped, but in an old condition. The wooden...
items.one_line: Guest accidentally triggers panic button, host checks up constantly

When it became clear during the first night that the arrangement would not be adequate for us to continue there (see below) and we cancelled our remaining nights, she graciously returned her second night's charges even though we cancelled a few hours too late to be reimbursed automatically by airBnB according to the refund policy on Lia's page.
The reason for our cancellation during the first night was the level of noise. The description does include the phrase "with airplanes reminding us that we live
items.comment_full:
Lia is a gracious host. She was very prompt with communication and very accommodating. When it became clear during the first night that the arrangement would not be adequate for us to continue there (see below) and we cancelled our remaining nights, she graciously returned her second night's charges even though we cancelled a few hours too late to be reimbursed automatically by airBnB according to the refund policy on Lia's page.
The reason for our cancellation during the first night was the level of noise. The description does include the phrase "with airplanes reminding us that we live in a European capital," but that does not quite do justice to the fact that the building is directly in the path of the final approach to the airport for one of the two runways at the Lisbon Airport. After midnight, the air traffic slows, but we heard at least one descent as late as 2:00 AM, and the traffic resumed at 6:00 AM. Since the airport is only 3.0 km away as the crow flies, a typical airplane approach angle of 3.0 degrees means that approaching airplanes pass about 300 meters directly above the building. I have lived in urban centers in Spain and am used to noise such as garbage trucks coming by at 1:00 or 2:00 in the morning. However, the noise here was a different level, especially with the windows open. I am generally a rock-solid sleeper, but I was only able to sleep for about four hours, while my mother did not sleep at all. This problem could be mitigated somewhat by closing the windows, but in the summer, the room then gets too warm for sleeping (afterwards, on looking at the photos, I saw that the photos show an electric fan in the bedroom, but we did not see a fan when we looked around for one).
My strong recommendation for those who plan to stay here is to bring earplugs.
items.one_line: Lisbon apartment under airport runway descent path literally

items.comment_full:
A spectacular view of Paris from a large terrace, shame about the cockroach infested apartment and unwarranted flooding accusations by the owner.
On the third evening of our six night stay the first cockroaches appeared and their numbers increased each evening. We dreaded returning to the flat as there was no relaxation or sleep until we had spent an hour or more killing off the cockroaches.
Clearly not a pleasant way to end a day spent in Paris. We informed the owner immediately and she admitted that it was an on going problem within the building. However, after following up with photos and stating we had found one wandering across a closed suitcase she insinuated that we had brought the cockroaches with us, who would do that ?
The owner offered to treat the problem on the 4th day after initially refusing to admit there was an infestation. However, we hoped this could wait until our departure as cockroach treatment makes them more active. This was evident on Saturday evening when the apartment had been treated and the owner finally offered to repay us for our last night if we could find somewhere else to stay. This was an offer that came too late!
If we had known about the buildings issues we would not have booked this Airbnb. In all, we killed 25 cockroaches in 4 days but that was only part of the problem.
On the day we arrived the caretaker advised us of a minor water leak under the sink that had as yet not been resolved. A bucket had been placed under the sink pipe work which had also been sealed with white silicone. Apart from not being able to close the cupboard below the sink we thought no more of it, until we arrived home and received a demand from the owner for €2000 as compensation for not informing her of the leak which led to flooding and possible lifting of the wood flooring. When we did finally leave Paris early Sunday morning the only thing we left behind on the wood floors were dead cockroaches.
After many wonderful stays in Airbnb locations around the world and receiving outstanding reviews, I feel it is only right to forewarn others that are considering staying here. The cockroaches and insane accusations are something I hope never to encounter again.
items.one_line: Host accuses guests of bringing cockroaches to Paris apartment

items.comment:

items.comment_full:
What Nightmares Are Made Of ...
This was the worst experience of my entire life, from start to finish. I booked a stay in this apartment from Aug 28 - Oct 2 to facilitate my move to Paris for study. The host Carline bullied me and forced me to leave because I had fallen asleep with a lamp on.
The first red flag was when she sent me a derrogatory message when I requested to stay, questioning whether or not I was a woman and demanding that I confirm this and making remarks about my name. I was insulted however, as a 23 year old student, my income is very limited so I decided to proceed based on the price.
On the day of my arrival in Paris, I had been sending Carline updates throughout the day in order to organise checking in at her place. I became very concerned when I realised I wasnt receiving any responses. In the taxi from the airport I became very anxious and asked her if she could send her phone number. This was her first response to me all day, no hello, no sentence, just the number.
So, I called. No answer. Then I texted. No answer.
In terms of location, it is not exactly the safest so needless to say when I realised I was 2 minutes away and that I would be there standing on the side of my street with my suitcases at 12am I became extremely alarmed. Just as the car pulled up, she said she was coming thorugh text. No greeting from her just started walking expecting me to follow her. I totally understand that with all travelling there will always be some degree of a communication issue but you know what a smile is internationally recognised.
The lift in the building starts after the first full flight of stairs and is in the middle of the stairs so I had to carry my extemely heavy lugagge up them to get to the lift and then down half a flight of stairs to reach the apartment. It wasn't easy for me to do but what made it worse was her agressively whispering at me again and again to be quiet and stop making noise. That was just a complete impossibiltiy and made me so uncomfortable.
Inside the apartment, she gestures towards the room to the right and says its mine, gives me a key and then goes to her room. No information, no tour of the apartment, no guidelines, nothing. Once inside, I realsied the room looked different but after countless hours of travelling this wasnt going to stop me from falling on to the bed.
The next day i didnt see her. It felt really strange not having had any tour of the place because then I felt unsure of what exactly I was allowed to use or where I should keep some of my things in the bathroom and kitchen.
The room I was given was not the bedroom from the picture. It was clearly the living space of the apartment but it had a proper bed and a wardrobe so I didn't mind. What did bother me was the smell in the room. It smelled strange, like a fishy drainy type of smell. I kept the windows fully open at all times to help with this. There was also some rubbish left in the room and all the blankets and bedding looked like they were about 20 years old.
I didn't complain about any of this to Carline, not wanting to cause any problems.
My third night there, I was in the middle of a conversation on the phone when I heard a knock on my door. Carline was there to tell me how to queitly open and close the door. I was so insulted as she literally demonstating open the door, going outisde, closing the door and opening it again. "I dont like any noise". I can asure you that I am a normal and respectful person and i certainly wasnt going around slamming doors by any means. I thought this was so strange and so condescening. She also demonstated for me how I should walk correctly in the appartment.
On the tips of my toes.
I think that moment said a lot and I should have made a run for it then.
A couple nights after that, when passing in the hallway late at night she stopped me to tell me I need to eat fruit. That she has seen the fridge and gave me a disguted look. More fruit okay. I was so embarrassed and confused. I just tried to laugh it off even though she was completely serious.
After that, I hadnt seen Carline in days and I thought everything was normal.
On my 10th day there, I woke up to a text message from Carline. She tells me that she has decided that I need to leave because I didnt turn off the light and this is abuse. She continues to say that I must not watch the news because there is war and electricity is expensive. She says that this behaviour is unacceptable and she is calling Airbnb.
What a text to wake up to. I started shaking in bed. I have no friends or family in Paris, what was I supposed to do, all accomodation was booked or astronmically expensive. I just couldnt believe it. All because I had left a small table lamp on after falling asleep from exhaustion. Curiously, I remember that I didnt turn off the light, I didnt even change my clothes that night, however when I woke up the light was off. I could have apoligsed, we could have spoken about it but no. Just get out.
Thus ensued 8 days of spending all my spare time looking for another place to live and contacting airbnb support. I was only allowed to stay there until I found a place under the condition that
items.one_line: Host bullies guest out for sleeping with lamp on

items.comment:
It does what it says on the tin, but feels a bit misleading anyway. Will do in a pinch but not ideal for a nice holiday.
Minor inconveniences added up:
1) Noisy neighbours
2) Sewage and marijuana smell
3) "Balcony" door doesn't close properly and main door lets hallway light in
4) Have desklamps but missing lightbulbs
5) Have shower but showerhead is broken
6) Have internet but stutters even for one user watching Netflix
7) Have TV but no channels only subscription services
8) Have kitchen but no cutlery or dishrag
9) Have balcony but in the basement

items.comment: This home is not somewhere you want to take your family.  Upon pulling up to the home, you see an overgrown front yard with long and weed infested yard.  Upon entering:  It was filthy with cobwebs everywhere, both inside and out.  You looked out the windows and saw filth and cobwebs on all windows.  You then tried to open blinds, only to find cobwebs and dirt on them.  The kitchen had a cracked tile right in the middle, with a rug thrown over it.  When Chelsea was questioned about it, she told us "it adds character to the house".  It was unsafe and felt like we had to walk around this all the
items.comment_full: This home is not somewhere you want to take your family.  Upon pulling up to the home, you see an overgrown front yard with long and weed infested yard.  Upon entering:  It was filthy with cobwebs everywhere, both inside and out.  You looked out the windows and saw filth and cobwebs on all windows.  You then tried to open blinds, only to find cobwebs and dirt on them.  The kitchen had a cracked tile right in the middle, with a rug thrown over it.  When Chelsea was questioned about it, she told us "it adds character to the house".  It was unsafe and felt like we had to walk around this all the time in the middle of the kitchen.  It says it sleeps 12 but there is NO way; plus the beds do not have box springs and real mattresses.  They are like a bed "topper" so the sleeping is quite uncomfortable.  There are two hooks to hang towels, so had to hang towels on hangars around the house to dry.  There is really NO closet to hang anything.  One bedroom had NO closet; one bedroom had a locked closet (which turned out to be where our much need toilet paper, etc. was... but NO hanging of anything in this closet); the third bedroom had a closet STUFFED with tables, vacuum, xmas decorations and looked TOO filthy to hang any dresses, clothes etc.  The one bedroom that had a ceiling fan had it running the wrong way for summer (was actually pulling heat down on you... instead of cooling you off) and it was absolutely filthy, so I had to turn it off so the dirt was not flying around the room.  The couch in the home was ripped and this room looked nothing like the picture for this house.  If you planned on enjoying the long backyard.... forget it.  The deck is filthy, patio furniture full of dirt and tables were filthy with dirty ashtrays on top.  The weber grill was full of old charcoal; but thankfully we did not plan on using bbq.  We literally saw the back and looked at it once, then due to the filth, etc. we never went out in the back again.  The front porch had minimal chairs and had dirty table, dirty ashtrays, and cobwebs everywhere.  This house had potential, but perhaps they allowed too many college stays, or I don't know what but this house needs a bad paint job (marks and messy looking walls) and serious CLEANING.  The chairs around the kitchen table are very uncomfortable and there was only seating for six.  I luckily found folding chairs.  If it lists as "12" can be there, shouldn't there be seating to feed at least that???  Really was hoping for a nice home for our rare family vacations... but this one was NOT that at all.  We did make the best of our trip... but really MAD I paid SO much $$$$ for three nights to stay in this home.  Chelsea did respond, but usually late for what I needed.  For the price you are going to pay for this home, there HAS to be other homes to go and enjoy Nashville!!  This was overpriced,  lacked serious basic items, and was filthy inside & out.  Quite disappointing.
items.one_line: Cracked tile "adds character"; cobwebs, no closets, bed toppers

items.comment: It wasn’t horrible considering we booked it so late and had nothing else to book but there was a makeup hand print on our wall, the door didn’t close unless we locked it, there was a mouse hole stuffed with like tissue and tape and it was quite dirty and outdated but it did the trick. Also, it was absurdly loud due to the location and we watched a huge rat eat a mouse right outside the bnb when we were walking to the nearby store, my boyfriend was not phased but it most certainly freaked me out after seeing the patched mouse hole lol. However, he was a very nice host and very easy to contact.

HOWEVER, we then had an extremely upsetting experience on the third night which ruined our trip. After a busy day/night touring about the city, we arrived back to the Airbnb at midnight, absolutely exhausted, only to find ANOTHER PERSON OCCUPYING OUR ROOM, blasting music, smoking, and clearly on drugs. In short, this person had re
items.comment_full: This is a wonderful apartment located on the beautiful Parque México in Condesa. The location is simply unbeatable. The complimentary breakfast is convenient; a great time to people/dog watch whilst fueling up for the day. Our first two nights of our stay were great. HOWEVER, we then had an extremely upsetting experience on the third night which ruined our trip. After a busy day/night touring about the city, we arrived back to the Airbnb at midnight, absolutely exhausted, only to find ANOTHER PERSON OCCUPYING OUR ROOM, blasting music, smoking, and clearly on drugs. In short, this person had reserved for another room in the same building, but was mistakenly let into our apartment by the staff of the cafe below. He then decided to post-up in our space, despite the presence of all our belongings (probably a testament to how high he was...). I won't get into the nitty-gritty details, but essentially we were forced to pack up our stuff (as the room reeked of smoke, etc.) and re-locate to another room in the building to get a few hours of sleep until morning, and then re-re-locate back to our original room the following day for our final night. Had we had more time, we probably would have attempted to find another hotel for the remainder of the trip, but we were simply too tired and traumatized to go through that process. That being said, Alejandra and her son Alex were very responsive as hosts. Alex was able to come onsite immediately and help negotiate this unfortunate situation. We were fully reimbursed for the duration of our stay, and we have discussed with him how to tighten up the security protocol to ensure that this never happens again (as we would NEVER want another guest to endure this experience).
items.one_line: Stranger on drugs steals bedroom, blasts music, smokes

This isn't really Jerry's fault but still impacted our stay in a major way an
items.comment_full:
Good location, parking easy. Inside very dark with dim lighting, place has a musty smell. Beds a bit uncomfortable. All of this as expected I suppose. However, the toilet had major issues for the first half of our stay. We plunged it multiple times, Jerry did arrange for a plumber but that didn't completely resolve the issue. The issue appeared to be old pipes in the building. Ultimately we went next door to the drug store to poop and never used any toilet paper in the toilet and were mostly able to flush the toilet. This isn't really Jerry's fault but still impacted our stay in a major way and when we reached out for a discount for having to plunge the toilet and wait around for the plumber we had no response.
Kitchen was nice, adequately equipped but no dish drainer, saw two cockroaches. AC loud.
Pool and hot tub reasonable, closest pool doesn't have a hot tub. Nice beach/views across the street.

The worst part ma
items.comment_full:
Listing mensonger!! Misleading listing, if not a scam!
This is definitely not a villa, more like a shack or maybe a studio with a mezzanine and two tiny rooms built out of an old garage !
The upstairs room has a very little area where adults could not stand up without hitting the ceiling, also there is no door and you can see the kitchen from the bathtub.
Coffee machine was listed however there was a sign asking for $1 per cup, as well as snack for sell in the fridge. This is not the Airbnb experience we have experience ever, especially for 300+ per night !!!
The worst part maybe was that the AC was not working, as we checked in on the hottest day of the year (104degrees) we would have appreciated the host show some honesty and to let us know when we booked. Instead she let us know when we checked in and it was too late to cancel. But wait there’s more!! The so called master room was very small with very low slanted ceiling and no air running. The doors wouldn’t even open completely so we had to push the bed on the side to be able to open half of it. Without ventilation, the room stinks of humidity and taking a shower means steaming the entire room unless of course you enjoy a shower with all doors open. The electric blinds were missing the remote so we had to use the curtain instead which wouldn’t cover the entire window area. Also a bit weird that the master bedroom is not even silenced to the house and one has to go through the outside to reach other rooms in the so called « villa »…
On the first night, a Saturday we were not able to sleep until 4am because of loud bass electronic music coming from the neighborhood.
During the entire weekend ash were falling on us from what seemed to be close by camping barbecue unless it was from the wooden fire pizza place next door. Anything left outside for too long would gets dirty from ash.
The host laundry was still in the drier.
If the listing had been more accurate, I would have not booked it, especially not for that crazy price !!!
The pool while pretty small was however pretty nice and therefore I won’t be asking for a full refund and would feel ok with a half refund.
This listing needs to be taken down until it is updated ! I actually was planning to ask the host to address the listing lies and I would not leave too bad of a review, but her reaction of bad faith made me change my mind and wanting to share this bad experience tu hopefully help other guys not get screwed over as we feel we did !
Please advise on the refund process asap!
items.one_line: Villa actually a garage with $1 coffee machine

items.comment: I would love to write a stellar review of this apartment, managed by Del Parque Flats, but unfortunately, each of my hopeless-believer-in-second-third-and-even-fourth-chances efforts to provide an occasion to write such a review was met with a slammed door. Or, rather, a door that was impossible to slam (keep reading this review if you’d like to understand this joke). From a basic usability standpoint, this apartment was not unlike an abandoned waterpark, a frightening mess top to bottom. Similar to my joke above, the punchline was delivered as a punch that leaves one feeling depressed and tir
items.comment_full:
I would love to write a stellar review of this apartment, managed by Del Parque Flats, but unfortunately, each of my hopeless-believer-in-second-third-and-even-fourth-chances efforts to provide an occasion to write such a review was met with a slammed door. Or, rather, a door that was impossible to slam (keep reading this review if you’d like to understand this joke). From a basic usability standpoint, this apartment was not unlike an abandoned waterpark, a frightening mess top to bottom. Similar to my joke above, the punchline was delivered as a punch that leaves one feeling depressed and tired, waiting for an explanatory setup that may never arrive. The apartment itself is well-located, has comfortable living spaces, and seems reasonably priced—if it weren’t for the hassle of actually staying there.
My wife and I booked a two night stay here through Airbnb. I tend to be very gracious with Airbnb hosts, as I’ve hosted folks through the platform before, have operated several rental properties myself, and understand that there are many variables and factors with temporary rentals. However, I can’t speak lowly enough about the interactions I had with Del Parque Flats representatives via Airbnb once I reached out letting them know about a couple of issues upon arrival.
When we arrived, there were about four gallons of water on the bathroom floor, leaking out into the hallway and the bedroom next door. There were also obvious signs of long term water damage (water stains on the bedroom floor, a bathroom door that had water damage running up at least eight inches of the door and trim, causing the door to never be able to shut—hey, there’s your setup!). There was one soaked towel already sitting in the water, which did seem a strange omen, but I figured maybe there was some kind of leak, unknown to property management team, and I’d better get to work informing them and cleaning up.
I took pictures immediately and forwarded them to Del Parque Flats, letting them know about the situation and that it was there when we arrived late in the day, the day of check in. I then began to mop up water with two of the extra towels sitting on one of the extremely rickety beds, which I think may have been imported from a U.S. penitentiary. This was quite disgusting, as the towels were white and the water had clearly been sitting and soaking into the wood, the pipes, the door, and anything touching the ground in the surrounding area. However, this dirty job had to be done in order to enter the restroom at all. After about a half hour of cleaning, we could finally use the restroom.
After the first response for Del Parque Flats the following morning, which stated simply that they’d noted the situation and would have a look after we checked out, I gently suggested that they reduce our cleaning fee ($105). I explained that of course no one intentionally did this, and I’m sure it was some kind of overflow from previous guests, etc. All the niceties; I’m from Minnesota, after all. Since I had to spend a half hour (and more time later) wiping up dirty water and shifting towels around, and couldn’t even close the bathroom door for any privacy during the entirety of the stay, this seemed like a reasonable request. This was when things got funny (if you like badly told jokes, which you must, because you’re the type of person who can’t stop reading this review).
I reached back out after radio silence and our check-out. This was when Del Parque Flats began to shift their story, claiming that it was actually me who had caused the water—yes, even though it was all there and I sent photos upon arrival, which are timestamped, by the way. They claim that it came from a bucket that sits in the living room, which is about twenty-five feet away from where the icky bathroom liquid actually was, and that I had failed to empty the bucket, so it overflowed. Now, other than defying the laws of physics, contradicting all photographic evidence provided, and being an outright lie, one may now be asking oneself: why would there be a bucket of water that guests must empty every several hours in the living room? Let me tell you.
One of the many endearing quirks (if I were writing a kinder review) of this property is that the dehumidifier aspect of the AC system drains into a bucket in the living room rather than outside, where the water must ultimately go. When you arrive, without any previously delivered directions, you’re expected to locate a sheet of crusty paper that’s taped to the wall, sometimes behind a curtain, above this bucket, and follow the page long directions on the ins-and-outs of emptying it. It was this very sheet of paper that Del Parque Flats claimed I had never read. Wrong again!
(As an aside, I actually have an MFA in creative writing, and while this alone does not make anyone a great writer, I do really enjoy reading. I also grew up as a religious fundamentalist, and while I’ve since escaped that particular form of hell on earth, I am still quite the rule-follower. So rest assured, I had read the sheet of paper with directions, and I was taking great pleasure in the fact that I was following the rules—a personality defect common in obsessive neurotics like myself.)
I am an anxious person by nature and anxiously read the little sheet of paper that first night after anxiously looking around for sources of the water, in between anxiously wringing out sopping towels of bathroom water into the tub. You see, I’ve had many anxiety dreams where I awake to my home flooding, and this is simply too on-the-nose for me, as my analyst has pointed out many times that I have some sort of fear of problems outside of my control “drowning” or “suffocating” me. The good
items.one_line: Apartment like abandoned waterpark, door won't slam

It was Easy to find, well located, and clean. After getting in our pajamas, we decided to go out on the courtyard balcony. The door locked behind us with all money, passports and phones inside. An American couple was outside and let us use their phone to call Airbnb. Flavio said he was far away and for us to go t
items.comment_full: The place and the location was fine.  However this was the worst airbnb experience in my life. Flavio threatened me not to write this review with the consequences of trashing me - but I finally decided to review . - My husband and I arrived late at 10pm to his place in Roma from Ohio. It was Easy to find, well located, and clean. After getting in our pajamas, we decided to go out on the courtyard balcony. The door locked behind us with all money, passports and phones inside. An American couple was outside and let us use their phone to call Airbnb. Flavio said he was far away and for us to go to a hotel. We had no I.d, money, or phone. We walked to campo and tried to get the police/firemen, but were told they don’t get involved. We tried locksmith but no one answered. 4 hours passed. We started to sleep outside but at 3am, the American couple let us sit at their table in their unit. At 8am, a locksmith finally answered  and $250 later, we were inside. - All convos  recorded by Airbnb.
items.one_line: Locked out at 10pm, spent night outside freezing

We knew the location was in an nightlife area, but the noise pollution was extreme. We tried to sleep on the couch in the living room, but the nuisance was also very bad there. The host has provided earplugs, thank you, but the walls are so thin that you are also bothered by the neighbors' conversations and footsteps. On Mondays you will be woken up earlier than stated in the advertisement by loud construction noises, around 6:30 am. No view of the city skyline, wifi not working well. The basic facilities are also not enough for 5 days, we had to buy extra toiletpaper. Nevertheless, the apartment was clean and the host responded quickly.

items.comment:
This cabin has many issues, but there are two which make me think it should not be used.
There is a light switch on the bathroom wall, which I believe is illegal. Under the draining board, which is made of wooden slats, with wide gaps, is an electric plug outlet, with an extension lead with kettle and toaster connected. Any liquid spill could cause injury or death. I am not a professional electrician but I consider these issues vitally important.
Other things included rather amateur plumbing, some of it on the floor, no heating (but a Honeywell controller on table), CO2 detector

items.comment:
Do not stay here if you want to sleep. the location is on top of a bar which has VERY loud music all night (until 7am on Fri and Sat) with a bass beat which vibrates the floors of the whole house. Earplugs are provided but especially on a warm night you cannot open windows to get a breeze. The apartment is very central and spacious in general but one of the bedrooms is only accessible through the other one and there is one bathroom with a small sink, shower and toilet. Our group wouldn't have minded that but as we didn't sleep, we were quite exhausted.
Casper provided some extra glasses

items.comment_full: We spent 4 nights in mid-June and were absolutely pleased with Nuno's flats (there are 2, back to back). Although small, ours was intelligently laid out with almost everything placed where you needed it. Bright and airy. It felt right the moment we stepped in the door. The kitchen has a view of the city to the west, while the bedroom looks out east. And the terrace...!! I've been around most of the world's continents and can safely say that I do not remember ever having had a city view from a lodging with such a sweep. A panorama of more than 320 degrees. You can have breakfast and watch the sun rise over the Sao Jorge castle and then later have drinks at sunset looking over the bridge, the christo and the Tages. Breathless, and as a bonus there's an itinerant peacock that flits around the rooftops at night barking like a monkey in heat. True you've got a walk-up of 4 flights of narrow stairs (plus the stairway to the roof) but small price to pay for an experience so unique. However, like any corner of paradise, there is a small catch that I must pass on to you. It wasn't a big deal for us, but could be for you depending on who you're sharing the flat with. Due to the configuration of the apt, the bathroom is situated between the kitchen/living area and the bedroom with a short passage linking them. Problem: the toilet sits in full view just along that corridor (behind which is a normally enclosed shower). It's not as bad as it might sound as the WC is nominally shut off from both ends of the apt. But you will have to 'organise' yourselves to avoid any surprise visits. The other apt apparently is a fully closed bath and doesn't have this little quirk. Situation-wise it's only a few minutes walk downhill to Rossio station (faster if you skate the slipstones). The tram 28 is 5 minutes away at the bottom of Rua da Rosa. The street is somewhat quieter than the others in Barrio Alto which will quickly be appreciated. There's a small grocer's a few meters down the street with fruits, veggies, beer and wine at very decent prices...All in all I'd have given it 5 stars, but because of the WC the rating drops to 4 1/2.  Final word, we had a panic moment on our last night when we couldn't get into the flat. Nuno came himself at midnight on the biggest festival night of the year, just as we realised that the fault was with us and not the lock. Our embarrassment was as deep as the grace with which he shrugged it all off. Highly recommended place.
items.one_line: Peacock barks like monkey in heat despite amazing terrace view

items.comment: Disclaimer: the space itself was perfectly fine. It was very clean and cozy, the host communicated very well, the view of the streets is beautiful, and we were even welcomed with a bottle of wine to congratulate us on our honeymoon with a sweet note from the host. We would have liked to give a higher review but unfortunately, our stay was ruined by loud prevailing noise from the upstairs neighbors that occurred every night but one during our stay. Some noise is understandable especially when staying in an apartment building, but this wasn’t occasional, and it wasn’t music or party noise, but s
items.comment_full: Disclaimer: the space itself was perfectly fine. It was very clean and cozy, the host communicated very well, the view of the streets is beautiful, and we were even welcomed with a bottle of wine to congratulate us on our honeymoon with a sweet note from the host. We would have liked to give a higher review but unfortunately, our stay was ruined by loud prevailing noise from the upstairs neighbors that occurred every night but one during our stay. Some noise is understandable especially when staying in an apartment building, but this wasn’t occasional, and it wasn’t music or party noise, but sex sounds (moaning and bed thumping), and lasted the ENTIRE night multiple nights. The first night the moaning and thumping noises were almost every hour on the hour from 11pm-7:30am, and the following night again until 4:30am. We reached out to the host each time it happened and he assured us that he has spoken with the neighbors, even showed us a screenshot of the neighbor’s response saying they were not making any noise and he encouraged us to call the police if it continued as noise is restricted after 10pm, but the noise continued. It was honestly just disturbing and so loud and we were unable to enjoy the space at all. We ended up checking out one night early just to be able to get a good night’s sleep. Unfortunately we can’t recommend staying here until the upstairs noise is dealt with.
items.one_line: Honeymoon ruined by upstairs sex sounds hourly all night

items.comment:

items.comment_full:
SPIDERS IN BEDS, SPIDER NETS, LOUD/BROKEN BEDROOM DOORS, AND DIRTY AIR VENTS ARE NOT ACCEPTABLE and you as a host unwilling to do anything this situation is even worse! After traveling all day and being completely tired, we arrived to Frank's place thinking we could finally rest and go to sleep but really what we discovered inside our beds what HORROR and nothing desirable by any means.
We walked into the place and a gave a bowl of water to our dog and went straight to the bedrooms, upon opening one of the bedroom doors or should I say dragging the bedroom door against the cement floors (that would definitely wake up any neighbor if you needed any privacy), we turned on the lights and noticed right away something black running around beds as well as walls. Upon closer examination, we noticed SPIDERS on the beds as well as inside the bedding sheets.
While all I really wanted to do is go to bed, my husband didn't want to stay another minute here. We decided that both of us would not be able to go to sleep with the notion that SPIDERS will be crawling at night inside of our beds. We called Airbnb and started to look into other sleeping options for us as we really needed to get up early for work the following day. Note that all of this was already happening pass midnight and this is the last thing anyone would want to do after a full day of traveling. I was not able to reach Airbnb via phone this evening (being on hold for more than 30 minuted) but I did leave them a message explaining our situation. We ended up going straight to the nearby hotel as we really needed to get some rest.
The following day we had a full day at work and I am still yet to hear from anyone pertaining to this issue. I called Airbnb again to hear that someone will be getting back to us. Once I heard back from Airbnb, they instructed to also write to the host separately which honestly I assumed we did when we wrote to Airbnb but this was all very unclear to me and honestly this is the first time I even had to deal with a situation like this. Once I heard back from Airbnb, I also directly messaged Frank explaining that we could not go to sleep with spiders inside the beds. He then wrote to us saying that he had SPIDER issues in the past and that he'll refund us. A little after that he messaged us blaming that we stayed at his place because there was a used water bowl (that we gave to our dog to drink upon our arrival) and the bed sheets were open (because we inspected them for SPIDERS) but clearly were never used as they were still tucked in.
Now anyone with a healthy mind would know there will be no need for us to go spend more money on top of what we already paid to AIRBNB and stay at the hotel if only the place was clean and in good sleeping conditions. To say the least, I AM EXTREMELY disappointed with the lack of your customer service, and your RUDE behavior and you not willing to do anything about this situation but yet admitting that you had SPIDER issues in the past. I have been a host as well as a guest with Airbnb for many years and NEVER have I spoken with any of my guests nor hosts, the way you treated us. This is NOT acceptable and I do not recommend staying here because God forbid if you have any issues you'll end up being accused and mistreated just like us. The host did not refund us and accused us of staying there although clearly, we have hotel bills with the time of our arrival with the proof that we never stayed at his place. I will never recommend you as a host especially with the way you mistreated us!
items.one_line: Spiders in beds and sheets greet exhausted travelers

items.comment_full:
PLEASE READ BEFORE YOU BOOK!!
My stay here was a nightmare. I was traveling with my partner and our ESA, just looking for a place to lay our heads for a few days, so our standards were already low. But this unit was absolutely DISGUSTING. The first thing i noticed was the difference in quality of the room compared to the pictures. There are things shown on the pictures that did not translate in person, for example the back of the entrance door is really dirty and the paint is scratched all over, the toilet paper holder is broken off, the hair dryer is so old that it does not work, and the window curtains were covered in bleached stains. No big deal, although those are cheap repairs that should’ve been taken care of prior to checking in a guest. But where I drew the line was with the linens. We checked in exhausted from a very long day, and the bed sheets and pillowcases were stained, covered in sand & dust, and FULL OF HAIR. All kinds-long, short, even animal hairs (our dog is hypoallergenic, so she doesn’t shed). This told me right off the bat that the sheets had probably not been changed or replaced for a while. There were no bed sheet replacements/spares either. I contacted the host and asked for a cancellation, the hosts response was “we can only refund the cleaning fee but we cannot even refund you for your first night”. It was already past midnight, so i knew booking anything else was not a choice. We had to lay “clean” towels all over the bed and sleep on the opposite side. To top it all off, the smoke alarm went off at 3:30am and we couldnt sleep for the rest of the night between the noise and the itching on our skin from the lack of sanitation. Needless to say I had to ask airbnb support to step in and we were able to get a full refund. The host Eden is worse than the place itself, she was impossible to reason with and instead of treating us as humans she gave us her fake customer service pitch, not caring at all that the unit shes renting was in such terrible condition. All about the money.
P.S. if you still want to book this place after reading this review, make sure you read the full description and contact her with any questions. I made the mistake of booking last minute because the place accepted pets, and she then proceeded to charge me a $70 fee for a certified Emotional Support Animal and $100 for overnight parking fee (which, the parking is already designated for each unit, but she is so money hungry that she will still charge you extra for it)
items.one_line: Sheets covered in sand, dust, hair including animal hairs

items.comment_full:
I and my wife were not satisfied with the house. Firstly, the next house was turned on the music too loudly all night even while we were sleeping, also we woke up because of the too loud music.
Secondly, the mattress of the bed in the room is the worst one that I have in my life. We couldn't sleep well because of the mattress, and we had back pain the day after. It felt like water or air mattress.
The worst thing that I had was the host asked us money after we left. My wife started her period abruptly, and she so embarrassed. So she tried to use everything carefully. But we needed to share the bathroom together with the man host. She tried to use the toilet carefully but she left blood on the toilet. But she didn't recognize it until she went to bed. While we are sleeping.. it was around 11pm.. The host called us several times (more than 3 times) outside the door even though we turned the light off. And he asked me to clean the toilet. I did.. because I know.. this was our fault, and actually, I and my wife felt sorry about that. We tried to make everything clean before we left. We made sure that everything was clean in the room and also the bed sheet. However, that night, He attached a picture of two red watery (not bloody) spots on the bed sheet and asked us 80 USD for his ruined bed sheet. We still not sure those spots were hers, because we checked carefully on the bed sheet. Anyway, he said that is his brand new bed sheet but it didn't seem like. We saw some dust before we went to bed, so we used our own pillow cover and our towels on the bed. The first time, my wife felt so sorry to him so she sent him a message and apologized.. but he didn't a response to us. If he didn't know about my wife's period, he would never think those two red watery spots came from her. He might think of other stains.
We are sorry for his bed sheet but THE WOMAN'S PERIOD IS NOT BE ASHAMED, DO NOT NEED TO FEEL GUILTY, AND NOT A FAULT!!
Actually, his message ruined our 1st anniversary trip to New York. He is still a kind, good man, and great host but this experience which he gave was bad for us.
And, this experience remained us as a trauma, so we won't use the Airbnb again because of this bad experience.

items.comment:
I’ve been traveling through Mexico for 9 months and this was by far the best airbnb I’ve stayed in so far! Do not hesitate to rent this place!!
Let’s start with the negative things to get them out of the way: any noise from the street comes into the bedroom as there are single pane windows. It’s typically a very quiet street, but if you’re a light sleeper, I would recommend ear plugs for sure! There’s a gentleman who starts sweeping the street at 5:30 am almost every day and for some reason, it’s very loud sweeping and repeatedly woke me up until I started using the earplugs. Also, random
items.comment_full:
I’ve been traveling through Mexico for 9 months and this was by far the best airbnb I’ve stayed in so far! Do not hesitate to rent this place!!
Let’s start with the negative things to get them out of the way: any noise from the street comes into the bedroom as there are single pane windows. It’s typically a very quiet street, but if you’re a light sleeper, I would recommend ear plugs for sure! There’s a gentleman who starts sweeping the street at 5:30 am almost every day and for some reason, it’s very loud sweeping and repeatedly woke me up until I started using the earplugs. Also, random motos and the occasional street vender make it harder to sleep in without them.
The building itself is also somewhat noisy, I noticed a lot of noise one day from the apartment directly above me but it wasn’t a consistent thing. The floors are just pretty creaky.
Next, there is not a lot of natural light in the apartment. The bedroom is awesome but the kitchen/ living/ office space barely has any as there aren’t any outward facing windows. This was a little tough for me as I love having natural light in a house to give me energy, but if you open the patio door it does help a bit.
Lastly is the shower. It took me many times to get it right! There is always plenty of hot water- but it gets too hot, and then when you turn it down too fast, it turns cold and you end up standing there freezing while waiting to get the hot water back. It’s a game of patience and slow adjustments.
NOW- let’s talk about the great things!! The bedroom is SO spacious!! Walk in closet, huge comfortable king size bed, lots of storage and shelving and two nightstands. Not to mention the amazing lighting which features two different modes depending on the type of light you prefer. This is also a feature in the living room. So smart!
The kitchen, albeit tiny, is totally functional with an adorable use of space. Ernesto was kind enough to loan me a toaster oven for my stay, so I had all my cooking needs met.
Who can forget to mention —location location location!!!! Oh my goodness, what a dreamy neighborhood!! A 10 minute walk to Chapultepec Park and 20 minute walk to the Museo de Anthropology, numerous amazing restaurants nearby (including two top of the line spots in the 5 star hotel just a few blocks away), Oxxo just down the block, two incredible wine shops a few blocks away, the gorgeous Polanco district just up the street, super safe and clean streets.... the list goes on and on. I loved this neighborhood.
The building offers 5- gallon water bottles for 50¢ and the water jug station is right outside the front door making it super easy to refill when needed! And there is a washer/dryer in the roof available for use. Once a week a sweet woman comes to clean and she even does your dishes!! And speaking of clean— there are NO bugs. Like seriously NONE. Not one cockroach, not one spider, not one ant. After all this time in Mexico, I was very pleasantly shocked to have that experience!!
The best part of all might just be Ernesto himself— the kindest most thoughtful and accommodating host I’ve ever had the pleasure to rent from! He was always quick with a response should I need anything at all. I feel so grateful to have found this gem of an apartment to spend my time in CDMX. Could have stayed for months and months. Stop looking and rent this place!
items.one_line: Street sweeper's 5:30am aggressive sweeping defeats all sleep attempts

It is impossible to sleep through, difficult to h
items.comment_full:
This was one of my worst AirBnB stays but absolutely not due to the host because he’s says he was unaware of construction.
The building next door was majorly damaged in the 2017 earthquake and is being demolished and rebuilt. Starting between 7-7:30 am every single morning but Sunday, the workers start jack hammering, sawing and blasting music. This all goes on right behind the wall where the bed is los Ted and floats through the large back windows overlooking a shared parking lot/courtyard. The noise goes until between 5:30-7 pm at night. It is impossible to sleep through, difficult to hear the TV, or hold a call while this is going on. I was there in December 2021 and the construction is going to go on for at least another year, possibly two years. Having a washing machine, a heater and the reviews of how quiet the place are why I booked. In the evenings after the construction workers leave, it is usually very quiet. Sometimes a neighbor throws a party but it stops around midnight (I was there during Christmas festivities). Another neighbor in a different building will use an electric saw at times in the shared area outside the back windows and will do this until 11 at the latest or so.
The photos of the apartment pretty much show it as it is. You walk in the front door and there is glass to the right that allows light in from the light shaft. One of the windows is cracked and sometimes cigarette smoke from a neighbor would float into the apartment through that area but mostly just linger by the front door. I am an asthmatic so sharing for those that cannot be around smoke.
The TV, washing machine, and hot water are all awesome. This place has a heater/AC combo, which is rare. I had to run the heater each night/all night because besides the sheets, there is one very thin blanket on the bed (think airplane blanket thickness and texture). The heater makes a clicking noise if it is turned on and the vent is shooting the hot air straight down so reach up and sim it towards the couch/tv and it stops.
The bathroom surprisingly had a small heater too, which was nice in the morning. The host provides two very nice towels. The sheets are of great quality too but had a small repaired hole in the top sheet. Bring slippers with you though because walking barefoot on the floor resulted in picking up dirt and things due to past tenants wearing shoes in the apartment and the floor not being mopped so well.
Another reviewer from September talked about how the internet sucked and it should be upgraded. As of December 2021, it still sucks. It would constantly stop working and the restart. Restarting the modem did nothing to resolve the ongoing problem. At first, I thought it was just a daytime issue (the slowness and stopping) but realized that it’s an anytime of the day thing. It made uploading photos at night from my trip a lengthy process. One night it took an hour to see 30 minutes of a show I was trying to stream. However, I did not book this place because it had any specific kind of internet speed, but those looking to work remotely from here should be aware the internet (and construction noise) are not going to be conducive to working from this place.
The studio apartment is located one floor up from ground floor. The two security guards that switch off are both very nice and helpful. The check-in instructions for this place are lacking. Basically go to the front desk and the security guy will give you the key/show you where to go. Watch out when going up and down the black stairs between PB and floor the studio is located on. They are dangerously slippery, which I learned my first morning staying there. Gave me the largest bruise this rugby player has ever had and I’m still trying to figure out how to get rid of the hematoma that remains a month later.
Absent the construction, this studio would have been perfect for a solo traveler. A bit more expensive than the much larger two bedroom apartment I stayed in on Calle Milan in September, but that place did not have the washer unit, heater or front security.
items.one_line: Earthquake rebuilding jackhammers destroy sanity for two years straight

items.comment_full:
Very good location, but it is NOT quiet. I have years of experience with long term stays in CDMX, this was the worst place I've stayed, and that includes a place that had to be fumigated for cockroaches, twice!
The apartment faces into an open space with other apartments, you will be part of their conversations, fights, meetings, and the incessantly barking dog that the owner does not stop. The building is new, but of very poor quality construction: you will hear the snoring above, the vibrating alarm clock, and when they have sex. You will hear the neighbours conversing next door, and the conversations in the halls. You may experience the pleasure of saws, hammers, and drills over your early morning coffee. There seems to be little or no common courtesy for ones' neighbours in this building.
Location: very good, within 10 minutes walk of 4 metro stations/3 metro lines, 15 minutes to Metrobus, and 10 minutes to Troleybus. I am a fast walker, Bellas Artes is 20 minutes walk. Lots of places to eat in Colonia Centro, not so much in this part of Doctores, but lots of street food vendors. Outside of rush hour traffic, only about 20 minutes via Uber/taxi to the airport.
I contacted the host twice, both times she did not respond, I'm not sure why, so I had to contact Airbnb customer service; this was a first time to ask Airbnb for help. If you plan an extended stay, be sure to find out about extra towels and sheets; you may be required to launder your own because the host won't change them; I had to ask a few times.
items.one_line: Worse than cockroach fumigation due to relentless neighbor noise

items.comment: Meeting Jonathan went great, seems like a nice young man. Met in front of his place the first night. A prior message from me stated that I worked every day of my stay, 7 days a week, 12 hours a day. I’m a very quiet individual and keep to myself. I’m clean and respect everyone’s property and keep my noise to a respectful level, if not, more quiet than the average person to avoid any intrusion on anyones sleep or daily schedule. I informed Jonathan upon seeing him my first evening stay that I worked in the executive protection field/Private Investigation field as a owner/operator for high profi
items.comment_full: Meeting Jonathan went great, seems like a nice young man. Met in front of his place the first night. A prior message from me stated that I worked every day of my stay, 7 days a week, 12 hours a day. I’m a very quiet individual and keep to myself. I’m clean and respect everyone’s property and keep my noise to a respectful level, if not, more quiet than the average person to avoid any intrusion on anyones sleep or daily schedule. I informed Jonathan upon seeing him my first evening stay that I worked in the executive protection field/Private Investigation field as a owner/operator for high profile clients. Jonathan not only has written rules about quiet time but insured me that coming in at 12:00 AM every night would be no issue for me to get sleep. In his rules, he explicated cited that quiet time was to be followed after 10:00 PM. Every weekend that I stayed there Jonathan had people over partying loudly with loud talking, laughing, cussing, yelling and movement directly above where I attempted to sleep. Sleep was not obtains during these days. I wrote Jonathan the first weekend and explained to him that I could not get sleep and asked if he was part of the party. He stated he was sorry and that it would not occur again. The following weekend, the same partying occurred Fri and Saturday evening when I got home. I complained via phone text and Airbnb messaging. Jonathan never replied back at all. This was extremely disappointing since my room wasn’t directly underneath the party room. Also, during this time, house maintenance and repair occurred which wasn’t so much of a problem but Jonathan insured that it would only last one week. It lasted three weeks. The third weekend I arrived home at approximately 12:00 AM to find out there was a large room raging upstairs. Loud yelling, cursing, movement of furniture possibly and stomping was heard directly above me. After recording the noise for about thirty minutes, I went upstairs and knocked on the door. At this time, I found out Jonathan lived at the house full time at it was him that was having the parties. Jonathan stated he was having a bachelor party and he didn’t realize I was home although he admitted he knew I was home every evening at approximately 12:00 AM. Being extremely upset I asked Jonathan how he was going to compensate me for the three weeks of interrupted sleep. Jonathan felt that the breaking of rules, public disturbance and violation of Airb&b rules didn’t apply but profusely apologized to me. He stated he wouldn’t do it again. I told him I would involved the police next time and wouldn’t tolerate anymore disruption of my sleep and demanded he make it right. I explained to him that I recorded audio of the disruption and expected him to do the right thing and compensate me for those days he kept me up. Today, the 10th of May, I left the room cleaner than I found it. Took photos and documented in case he disagreed. Upon my initial arrival he warned me that the construction guys had used my bathroom. I found a used razor and hair in the sink and on the counter. There was hair and dirt all along the edges of the bathroom. This was a private room with one bathroom. I cleaned the restroom upon my departure and took off the pillow cases and out them in the floor for him to clean. I left the bed sheets on and washed three towels professionally for him and left those on the bathroom counter. I swept the floor with the only swifter/cleaning tool that I could find. I wanted to leaved the room cleaner than I found it. With the cleaning deposit already paid, I felt I was going above and beyond for Jonathan. Also, parking is not a 7/8 as stated. Parking is hard to find especially on his party nights when his friends took up any parking that would normally be open in front of his garage door. His garage was not used to house vehicles but was a kitchen and washroom along with keeping of his tools and personal belongings including ongoing construction/maintenance supplies. I sent him a copy of his rules on the second weekend and he still would not reply back to apologize. He had my phone number and could have communicated through Airb&b. I will be contact Airb&b for action to be taken against Jonathan. Unfortunately, I would not recommend him for any future hosting, especially “Super Host”. It is my opinion he’s not mature enough to attain this title.
items.one_line: Host parties loudly above sleeping executive protection professional
