items.comment_full:
Full disclosure: the conditions of the apartment related to our comfort were so terrible, that me and my girlfriend of two years fought a lot and eventually broke up. We were fighting loudy for at least 5 days out of our 20 days of stay (never during the night to respect the other guests) and the receptionist were patient and kind enough. I apologize for the inconvenience caused. Hopefully you saw how respectful we were towards the employees.
Apart from this, the apartment was a mess at the beginning. The room was clearly not done. Beds were ok, no towels, room was messy and dirty all thorough (cleaning lady was not at fault, see point below). You will hopefully see the pictures of the "stoves" we used (which were bordeline-1820 and filthy dirty), the beds were separated despite our multiple requests to have joint beds (during the night they opened-up on the middle, so to speak) and we even found an insect on the bed. But the shower.... oh the shower.
Look I work as a receptionist in 5stars hotels. I don't expect 5 4 or 3 stars. But our shower head was fully moldy and leaking constantly. We asked not less than 6 times to fix it. The engineer went up to our room at least 4 times, before removing the head (as per my suggestion). And after that, it was still leaking. 4 days before the check-out, eventually it was fixed. Drain was clogged. Bathroom was flooded after each shower.
Now I will not discredit the single employees of the hotel; I will mention positively the cleaning lady and the foreign lady at the reception. The cleaning lady was GREAT. Huge smile all the time, always ready to clean. Able to do a proper cleaning (in fact I am just perplexed about the bug). But other employees were rude. Some others employee entered our room without knocking (we were half naked, we learnt to lock the door later). The first time they checked the shower, they left a huge mess on the bathroom.
In summary, I apologize for the inconvenience for the loudness, but I cannot pass over the rest of the issues this airbnb has. I am expecting a partial refund (just because we were a nuisance for a few days. I would have asked for a full refund).
items.one_line: Terrible apartment conditions cause couple to break up mid-stay

items.comment:

items.comment_full:
I WOULD NOT advise anyone to stay here.
We had a very bad experience to the point we actually had to source and pay for other accommodation during our stay in vienna.
We arrived to the property around 11.30 pm at night, got the keys fine from the instructions (which are very thorough) but felt very uncomfortable as soon as we entered. Firstly we were approached by several people hanging around the gate, offering strange comments and as two girls on their own new to vienna, it wasn’t a nice experience. The place from the exterior is metal gates and my freind described it as a prison like building.
We got to the floor and flat next door (508) door was wide open. There was screaming coming from in the room and we quickly tried to locate the lock to your apartment in the pitch black! No lights working, so had to use flashlights.
When we got into the flat the inside lock was half broken! The latch hanging off, the flat was fine (very basic, not as nice as the pictures but it was fine) but again we could hear screaming and hysteric (scary) laughing coming from flat 508, which felt like it was coming closer. I’m not completely sure but I’m pretty confident this is a student accommodation to make people aware.
I don’t know if they were playing a joke or were genuinely screaming for an actual reason, We stayed in the flat for 20 minutes, unable to lock correctly due to the broken latch and feeling extremely uncomfortable. I have stayed in many air bnbs but I genuinely worried for our safety within minutes of entering. I have read reviews today and also seem strange behaviour from that flat has been noted before! I completely understand this is not the hosts fault, but we felt unable to stay.
I hope this reflects how freaked out we were by the behaviour coming from next door. We found other accommodation which wasn’t ideal at midnight in a forge in city as 2 girls! The whole atmosphere in this place is strange, I understand if you arrive in the middle of the day it might feel different, but this was our experience, and I hope this helps others not have the same, or at least to make people aware.
I did contact Olga, but got told she’d never had a bad experience with the neighbours before, from the previous reviews too, it seems this just isn’t true.
items.one_line: Screaming neighbors, broken lock, rented other accommodation

items.comment_full: We had to carry our luggage up to the third floor apartment and were thoroughly exhausted from the exercise. We found out later that there was an easier but unadvertised way. We left the place for a hotel after four days even though we had paid for eight days mainly because we did not have a telephone that could be used in Vienna. We were astonished that the apartment did not have a land-line telephone. Since I had to reach Lufthansa immediately because of a suitcase that did not arrive with us, I had to go down to the street and ask a lady walking her dog to let me use her phone. The brother of the owner told me to go get a chip for 20 Euros that would let us use the phone we brought from the U.S. but I did not wish to get lost in Vienna streets trying to find a store. We did not know the streets nor could I call a taxi without a functioning phone. Ms. Daniela Ratkovic, the owner, wrote me later that "Nobody ever told you there was a phone. You should inform yourself about those things in advance. Gesendet mit meinem HTC." Yes, she was right. It is strange, however, that the owner of the apartment next door (Apartment 30) provided TWO telephones for the guests to use. The couple that stayed there learned about our predicament and showed us their phones and the “apartment manual.” That owner obviously cares about guests; no wonder she owns SIX apartments for rent. By the way, I asked our landlord to refund the portion of money we had paid, but so far I received no response on the matter. There is a washing machine in the kitchen, but no detergent. Ms. Ratkovic wrote me "I am offering washing machine as a service for you, but you should bring your own detergent with you. Across the street, there is a supermarket." "Across the street"? We found no such store on Pillergasse. The shower is 50 by 50 centimeters or two by two feet wide and it was quite difficult to bend down and pick up the soap and shampoo. There is no soap dish or shelf to place them. The main bed room has a large bed fit for two large people. But the bed with 10 centimeter thick foam fills the room except a meter of space on the side of the door. My wife woke up every time I had to go to bathroom and she was not happy about it. Lena's brother is a kind man. He took us to a restaurant several blocks away the first day we arrived; we enjoyed the dinner there. He works the night shift, I found out, when I called him for the detergent. Poor fellow was understandably upset. I apologize again. Vienna and Salzburg are magnificent cities. We enjoyed our trip. We also learned much from Pillergasse. We will remember it a...
items.one_line: No phone, host blamed guest for not knowing unlisted requirement

items.comment_full:
Constance herself is super nice and very sweet. However, her husband made me feel extremely uncomfortable.
When I first got there, he checked me in and showed me my room. He proceeded to closing the door with the two of us in there. I was frightened and quickly asked where the bathroom was so he could leave the bedroom.
On the second day, I left my room to use the bathroom and he opened the door on me. He didn't knock or apologize and the bathroom door doesn't lock well so every time I was in the bathroom, I was concerned the door would open again.
In addition, the halls are dark so I turn on the light before going into the bathroom. Every time I was in the bathroom, I would hear him walk out of his area and turn the lights off. I know conserving energy is important but I literally wanted the light on for the 2 minutes I was in the bathroom so I wouldn't have to walk into a dark hallway. As a result, I carried my phone just so I could use the flashlight on it.
When I first met Constance the second night, I saw her husband's silhouette at the door down the hall and he was staring. Again... this made me feel extremely uncomfortable but I still think Constance is a very nice host.
The bathroom is also not the cleanest since it's shared with 2 other guest rooms. That's fine but I was wiping down the floors with some toilet paper since it was a little wet after I showered and the toilet paper was black and covered in dust!!
Also, I'm not sure who was watching tv late at night but while traveling, I prefer to sleep earlier and wake up earlier but the tv was blasting way past midnight and this made it hard to sleep.
The location is great and the cost is low, but for a solo female, I would definitely not recommend staying here. Again, Constance is great but her husband made me feel very uncomfortable.
items.one_line: Husband closed door menacingly, turned off bathroom light

items.comment_full:
I'm going to be as objective and fair as possible, which is somewhat difficult given that we were robbed at gunpoint literally on the steps leading into this apartment.

To back up, my girlfriend and I (both in our early 30s) stayed in this apartment between Dec. 30, 2013 and Jan. 3, 2014. The apartment is exactly as advertised and Connie was very responsive with us leading up to our stay at the apartment. I echo everyone else's reviews in commending her for the extra touches like having snacks, water, etc. The place also was very clean.

The only negative I had about the actual place is that it shares a wall with Connie's residence and you can hear a fair amount of ambient noise through the wall. This was not a big deal but was not ideal.

For our first three nights (including New Years Eve), we experienced no safety issues and found the neighborhood to be quiet and dark but a fairly quick walk to the Quarter and Frenchman street.

On Jan. 2, however, we were robbed at gunpoint at 10:15 pm on the doorstep of our apartment. After we crossed the street to the apartment, a robber, acting alone, took a few hundred dollars in cash before fleeing in a car that had been idling on the corner. Luckily, he did not physically hurt us and did not steal anything other than cash.

We called the cops immediately and they were surprisingly quick and attentive. Being from Chicago and no stranger to the high rate of urban street crime, I have been impressed with their response. We also immediately called and texted Connie, who did not respond until the next morning, but I was able to get a hold of her husband, who happened to be in Baton Rouge that night. Disappointingly, we were informed that the surveillance cameras on the porch had been disabled one to two weeks previously because they had been malfunctioning.

We stayed at the apartment that night and then moved to a hotel in the Central Business District for our last night of the vacation. Connie credited back our payment for that night, which ended up being roughly the same amount as we spent on the hotel.

Connie and her husband obviously share no blame for the independent actions of an armed criminal. It is equally obvious that this type of thing can happen anywhere, especially in tourist-heavy New Orleans during tourist-heavy New Years. However, I feel compelled to share this story because I would want to be similarly informed.
items.one_line: Robbed at gunpoint on apartment steps, host very responsive

items.comment_full: We read all the positive reviews for this place and while the host tries her best many thing felt short. We always inspect the sheets when we get somewhere and one of the sheet was full of blood specs and someone had written on it in greek with a blue pen. The bathroom is scary, there is a wooden plank on the roof of where the bowl is. You can see through the cracks to the upstairs neighbours. You can definitively hear them so they can probably hear you on the bowl. The water from the shower does have a lot of hot water but it is not constant, one moment it will be burning hot then icy cold then back to scatering hot. It is also the only room with poor light. The tub is full of spots and feels very old. The rest of the place is cute and the host filled it up with a ton of things such as food, water and replenishes your towel. Our main issue however was that it was very very noisy. We heard fighting and yelling from morning to night. There were construction noises early in the morning and someone generally making noises on the stairs as early as 6:00 am in the morning every morning. These were not made by other guest in the airbnb around but from the locals who live there as the shouting were in greek. I find that for the price during the peak (250 a night) you should expect a lot more.
items.one_line: Bloody sheets, see-through bathroom walls, scalding shower temperature

items.comment_full:
Well the view is awesome and the location is unbeatable. But our experience beyond that was soured very quickly and we ended up having to leave shortly after arriving.
We put our stuff down and marveled at the view out of the window. Every time we washed our hands in the bathroom we noticed a small leak on the ground but decided to ignore that… we went to dinner and came back and I decided to take a shower. The host let us know that the shower is dangerously hot so to be careful. I turned on the shower and waited truly a few seconds to get in so I could get a sense of the temperature. The shower head then fell off and water spurted all around the bathroom from the spout without a shower head. At the same exact time, the power went out. No light and no power in the outlets to charge our phones which we’re running low.
I texted the host a few times and he did not respond. I then called him and told him that the bathroom was half flooded and all of the power was out. He seemed unsurprised that there was a flood in the bathroom (I suspect from the leaky sink situation from before). We were sitting in the dark and starting to feel desperate. At this point it was 11 pm and the host said he wouldn’t come then but would come in the morning. He instructed us on how to restart the power. My husband went into a dark cellar and tried to restart the power but that didn’t work. We tried in the apartment with the power box also and it didn’t work.
At this point we knew we needed to leave and the first part of our Florence honeymoon had turned stressful and desperate-feeling. We ended up getting a hotel around midnight.
items.one_line: Shower head falls off, power outage, host doesn't respond to messages

items.comment:
My family and I booked a 28days stay at this listing. My parents, slept in the main room with bathroom. They arrived first from Cabo. My husband and I, we arrived a week after from the US and slept in the 2nd room.
Unfortunately, I am giving this place overall of 2/5 stars, with these breakdowns:
1. Cleanliness- 1/5. the first day of arrival, both bathrooms smelled like sewer. The lobby also smelled like sewer when we arrived, so we thought maybe they are cleaning the septic tank? the smell was so potent so we ended up mopping and washing both bathrooms and turned on the fans. The couc
items.comment_full:
My family and I booked a 28days stay at this listing. My parents, slept in the main room with bathroom. They arrived first from Cabo. My husband and I, we arrived a week after from the US and slept in the 2nd room.
Unfortunately, I am giving this place overall of 2/5 stars, with these breakdowns:
1. Cleanliness- 1/5. the first day of arrival, both bathrooms smelled like sewer. The lobby also smelled like sewer when we arrived, so we thought maybe they are cleaning the septic tank? the smell was so potent so we ended up mopping and washing both bathrooms and turned on the fans. The couch was dusty and we can't find a vacuum cleaner, so we just had to wipe it down. A week after our stay, we woke up with many rashes and bites on our bodies. It is until then, I stripped down the bed sheet and saw a bed bug crawling out of the bed in the 2nd room. I asked my parents to strip down their bedding and saw another bed bug crawling out to their pillow. My mom told me, that when they first arrived, the next day she woke up with rashes, but thought it was her dust allergy, so she washed their bedding and blanket.
This is important to note: according to the United States Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, "One of the easiest ways to identify a bed bug infestation is by the tell-tale bite marks on the face, neck, arms, hands, or any other body parts while sleeping. However, these bite marks may take as long as 14 days to develop in some people so it is important to look for other clues when determining if bed bugs have infested an area. " - in which this is the case for us that it is showing a week after initial bite.
Reference: https://www.cdc.gov/parasites/bedbugs/faqs.html
My parents' rashes weren't as bad as mine and my husband's that slept in the 2nd room. Bed bugs breed, metabolize, and go through their life cycle more quickly when it's warmer, this makes sense because, in the 2nd room, it is small and very hot throughout the day- we immediately contacted host&co-host, and they sent an exterminator, claiming it was the best in town and we can absolutely stay again after the treatment is done. Unfortunately, that was not the case. The exterminator did tell us he found eggs and some bugs while exterminating the bedrooms. After, we went out to dinner and came back late at night. My husband and I went to our room, and we saw another bed bug on top of the headboard. We moved the bed and looked behind the headboard, and we saw many white small eggs behind it with a hole in the headboard itself. We found the NEST. Both of us ended up sleeping on the floor in the kitchen- and we all left the next day.
2. Accuracy- 3/5. The listing also mentioned shampoo and soap would be included, but there were no shampoo and soap in both bathrooms. It is also stated in the listing, that they will provide cooking basics such as oil, salt, and pepper, but there were no oil, salt, and pepper. Also, they said in the listing there will be silverware. There were 4 of us with only 1 spoon and 1 fork. We ended up buying them ourselves at the City Market which is very close by.
Gym, Jacuzzi, Steam Room, Pool Room:
We were told that these amenities are available for us to use. When we asked the front-desk receptionist where these rooms are, they said we have to make a reservation. This is not mentioned in the listing, but we thought that's fine, so we tried making the reservation. But we were told if we are late or do not attend the reservation, we will get charged MXN 3000, and these rooms are closed at 10 pm.
This is also not mentioned in the listing. But again, we thought that is okay, so we tried making a reservation, but they said to come back the next day because these rooms are closed for the day.
When we came back the next day, another receptionist told us that we were not able to use it, because we are "airbnb guests". That's when I told the host, and the co-host tried making a reservation for us, but we were told the last jacuzzi hour was 4pm. Then, I mentioned what the receptionist said to us that it was closed at 10pm, then we were told by the co-host to find the administrator lady to make an appointment to match what time we'd like to go, because he was not able to do it over the phone. We went downstairs to make an appointment for the next day at 4pm.
The next day came, and we went to the gym and jacuzzi only to be told that we had to sign a waiver, and have a reservation. WE DID make a reservation, and after arguing back and forth with the worker, we were finally able to use it.
BUT, we only used it 1 time, because the next day we wanted to make a reservation, we were told we cannot use it anymore. So, we didn't even bother telling this to co-host and just move on with our day.
3. Check-in- 1/5. As I said above, my parents arrived first. We were told the key had been dropped off at the front desk. Upon arrival, the check-in process was a hassle. After a long flight, and arriving at this place, they waited for at least 20 minutes for the receptionist to find their key, only to tell them the Host never left them a key. We contacted the host, and they had to wait another 15-20 minutes for them to find the key, although he had already called them. They are also not very friendly.
4. Communication- 5/5.
items.one_line: Sewer smell, bed bugs, rashes: perfect family vacation

items.comment_full: ROACHES AHEAD. The location of this place is prime. Literally steps from the boardwalk and beach, close to restaurants and shops. There is a full size kitchen with everything you need to prepare meals. Full size stove and refrigerator. There is a pool on premises which is a nice extra. Besides this, nothing else good to mention. There is Roku on all TVs, but it’s locked and YouTube is not one the channels in the list. So if you would like to watch YouTube, you would have to do it on your phone. I did contact the owner about this and their response, more than 24 hr  later was it’s locked, no solution offered. The fire alarm is connected to electricity but did not have a battery. We had to unplug it as it would ring every 30 seconds or so. No one could sleep with that. I also did contact the owner about this at the same time I contacted about YouTube and like previously mentioned their response took over 24 hr. Also, the only reason they responded was because I had to send pictures of roaches inside the apartment. 3 roaches on just one night. We had to go out to Walgreens to get insecticide and kill the roaches ourselves as the excuse was it must be raining or they recently fumigated. I believe when you have a rental property you should be extra careful with these types of things, and there are no good excuses for this! One day we came back to over 5 roaches! In addition, the refrigerator smelled like someone put fish inside and it went bad. You could smell this strong scent every time the refrigerator was opened. One day we came back and another renter was holding a party in the shared patio and they had taken over the pool like it was their own. We had a great time because we wouldn’t let all of this ruin our vacation but will definitely NOT be coming back.
items.one_line: Fire alarm rings every thirty seconds, owner slow to respond overall

items.comment: the shower was dangerous and unusable for the first two nights. it only operated at seemingly the hottest temperature possible, and we could not figure out how to cool it down. on the first night, while we tried to figure it out, it got so hot that the smoke detector went off several times, making for an intensely hectic first night. we thought we eventually figured it out that first night, but i think the hot water just started to run out, because the next day we experienced the same boiling hot water, with *no* ability to cool it down. on that second night, Tiffany sent a repairman to fix th
items.comment_full:
the shower was dangerous and unusable for the first two nights. it only operated at seemingly the hottest temperature possible, and we could not figure out how to cool it down. on the first night, while we tried to figure it out, it got so hot that the smoke detector went off several times, making for an intensely hectic first night. we thought we eventually figured it out that first night, but i think the hot water just started to run out, because the next day we experienced the same boiling hot water, with *no* ability to cool it down. on that second night, Tiffany sent a repairman to fix the shower. he eventually did fix it, but it took up our entire evening. the next day, some superintendent showed up unannounced to double check. good move, i guess, but would have greatly appreciated a heads up. furthermore, the included instructions for operating the shower were next to useless--riddled with typos and overall lack of clarity.
i communicated all of our obvious dissatisfaction to Tiffany and requested a partial refund for the first two nights. i thought this was reasonable, having paid approximately $1000 CAD for our weeklong stay, with the expectation of having a usable shower on those first two nights. Tiffany repeatedly misconstrued my feedback, seemingly intentionally, in order to skirt around the obvious issues with her listing, claiming the shower was simply "finicky" and that the smoke detector had to be there by law. um, yeah, i'm aware of such regulations--that wasn't the point of what i was telling her. ultimately, she firmly refused any sort of refund.
some other little things were off and unsatisfying: scratched pans, flaky half-melted spatulas, no pepper in the kitchen (which Tiffany said was due to covid regulations and that I could go to dollarama and get some; when I pointed out to her that pepper was indeed included in the listing, she offered to get us some, but at that point, it wasn't even about the pepper! it was about her negligence and inaccuracy of the listing). there was also a fairly persistent cigarette smell in the hallways and stairwell.
overall, Tiffany was extremely condescending with us, and seemed to not even care about the issues we experienced with her listing. sure, she sent a repairman to fix it, but we didn't get our first two nights back. i'm also not even sure what the repairman did to the shower fully qualifies as "fixed" -- but that's Tiffany's responsibility to figure out.
I'm giving two stars because the location was good, and the place was superficially nice. beyond that, utterly horrible experience and I couldn't be happier to be out.
items.one_line: Boiling shower triggers smoke detector, second visit same next night

items.comment_full:
This penthouse is stunning, to look at. However, in terms of function and location, I don't recommend it. First off, the Palms hotel is really sketchy. The other guests are sketchy and shady and I would never stay here again simply because it's in the Palms. But the problem is also with the penthouse. They try to sell it as LUXURY, however, everything is broken, so definitely not a luxurious experience.
First of all, there was urine on the pillows on the couch, literally was wet and smelled of it. I mentioned this to the host and the host didn't care, COOL, super luxurious huh.
The jacuzzi light was also broken so don't expect a lit up jacuzzi at night, what a shame.
There is no ice machine in the penthouse, nor in the hotel. Everything is broken here, from chairs (my friend was sitting in a chair that just toppled over because it was already broken) to some of the curtains are jammed and won't move.
The carpet has exposed nails that hurt, overall the room is not well maintained at all. What a shame because it's so beautiful. The stereo also doesn't work so bring your own speakers for music.
They advertise a queen bed and king bed in 1 room and a king bed in the second room. However, the queen bed is a rollaway bed, not an actual bed, it's tiny and looks to be a full size, not an actual queen bed.
What really pissed me off is the countless messages I sent the host but was completely ignored. They DID email us the day we checked out and asked for a 5 star rating LOL. Horrible service Cassandra and Bryan, I wouldn't stay in any of their listings ever.
items.one_line: Urine pillows and exposed carpet nails in luxury penthouse

Free Horror Movie Experience
If you’ve ever wanted to star in a real-life horror movie, this BnB is the perfect set! Picture this: two women, just having a normal conversation at a reasonable volume—no party, no loud music—yet somehow, the mysterious neighbor decided we were disturbing the peace and complains about us. But the real nightmare begins when an unknown man showed up at 12pm, ringing our doorbell repeatedly. When we asked who he is, he refused to answer clearly. Instead, he kept pressing the doorbell non-stop, turning our intercom into an unwanted live-stream of our private conversation.
Desperate to survive Night of the Haunted Doorbell, we kept the receiver open all night to stop him from ringing again. But in the morning, as soon as we put it back—ding dong!
Naturally, we turn to the host for help—it was midnight no response at that time, but no solution afterward either.
Good location and apartment in general, but not recommend If you can't stand insane neighborhood
items.one_line: Mystery man's relentless doorbell haunts night like horror film

items.comment:

I will give you a detailed visual and mental tour:
The front patio entrance of the space is decorated in an ensemble of trash such as a random pile up of cardboard boxes, a rusty old bike, and trashbags scattered about. As you step into the unit, you are welcomed by hardwood floors tattered in months of built up dirt and crust remains. It had a stench of old water and wood which I imagi
items.comment_full:
The stay at this airbnb came with many pros and cons but unfortunately for us, the cons outweigh the pros. The location was great, although that was why we chose it. The Airbnb itself was indescribable.
I will give you a detailed visual and mental tour:
The front patio entrance of the space is decorated in an ensemble of trash such as a random pile up of cardboard boxes, a rusty old bike, and trashbags scattered about. As you step into the unit, you are welcomed by hardwood floors tattered in months of built up dirt and crust remains. It had a stench of old water and wood which I imagine can’t be good. The stairway leading up to the second floor is in the same shape and aesthetic as the wood floors, except slightly worse. When you find your way to the top of the staircase there is, for lack of a better word and I mean quite literally, an abstruse garbage dump of dozens of pizza boxes, a couch flipped upside down and to the side, and other unknown items at the top. There is a couch fragilely leaning up against the overhead banister, as if one of us could bump it slightly and it could topple down onto the floor below. That’s enough to look at to go back down stairs and take a shower. You enter the unit through a questionable door that is in desperate need of either being replaced or sprayed down with a pressure washer. Just to be clear, almost everything in this house was filthy except the bathroom and bed. Entering the actual unit, it is a nice set up with kitchen, bathroom and bedroom. However, you can’t help but notice that almost every wall in this unit has been tattered in years with mysterious scuff marks, residuals of dirt, random grime. The dirt build-up on the walls and doors would make you think that whoever stayed in this unit walked on walls with muddy shoes. Not to mention the overwhelming amount of large house flies buzzing throughout the space and little gnats lingering around. The bathroom is somewhat tidy and clean, minus the dead cockroach waiting for us on the floor upon arrival. It also did lose some points as there was only two clean towels waiting for us and a third used one hung on the door. We had to wash the third towel in the host's washer and dryer downstairs in the basement, which was currently filled with a mix of his own personal laundry. Yum! The laundry was in the basement of the unit, which had the appearance that it was undergoing a renovation but had a lot of random clutter scattered throughout. The kitchen also lost some points. When we opened the fridge, I began to wonder if someone occupied this unit full-time because there was an array of different condiments and other food products that were outside of the standard Airbnb expectations. The silverware was nowhere to be found, there was no microwave, and the swarming gnats were unpleasant. Overall, the space felt a little too lived in especially when you considered all of the dog hair that laid on all the furniture and floors. Sadly, we couldn’t walk around the unit barefoot without picking up dirt and hair. Lastly, the host was pretty responsive until we asked for the wifi password. We ate up most of our cellular data that weekend and couldn't utilize our laptops because of it. On the plus side, the AC units worked well, as did the faucets and shower head. But that is about it. Considering how unpleasant and uncomfortable the stay was, I am politely asking for a refund. Airbnb should promote better quality stays and experiences. With this being a representation of Airbnb, it is not a good look.
items.one_line: Trash patio leads to filthy floors, garbage stairway, pizza box dump

And this wasn't the worst of it - both nigh
items.comment_full:
Don't pay any attention to the details of this apartment - based on our stay last weekend, it is a complete fabrication. They say to take your shoes off and hold down the noise to be respectful of their neighbors - WHAT A JOKE.
When we arrived the entire apartment reeked of weed - it permeated every crevice - and we later found out that it wasn't the previous tenant - it's the people living or renting the adjoining apartment. The smoked all day and all night to the point that all of our clothes smelled like pot - even when we got them home.
And this wasn't the worst of it - both nights of our stay there were raging parties in the adjoining apartment - with music so loud the apartment was vibrating and people screaming and yelling. It got so bad the second night that the police had to be called twice, with the second time 15 police officers broke up a party of about 100 people.
Sure the hosts were in contact with us and the other tenants the entire time, but the fact remains that these parties kept us up until 2am both nights.
items.one_line: Weed stench and raging parties featuring 100-person police bust.

items.comment_full: We were not lucky with a super host and family friendly listing. The first step of check-in was a bad start. We were in Disney land and I had to leave my family in the park and come to Paris just for keys collection. I could have spent an extra 5 hours in Disneyland with family, had check-in process been little flexible. Lucky homes (who were to give us key) told me to book a hotel if I am late by few minutes so I had to come in between to pick up key. We had a stroller and stairs are unusually difficult to navigate especially at night with some floors of missing light (may be sensor gone bad). No option to keep stroller at ground floor. Bathroom is nice but it is a direct view to neighbor balcony. It was very uncomfortable to us. There is only one wardrobe and that too was full of host clothes so our clothes were on floor for 3 days. How can you expect this from a Super host? In Kitchen, Utensils were stinking, only one mug for milk but we have 2 kids. Normally it is expected to have some basic grocery at home but surprised to see not a single crystal of sugar at home. Unfortunately, washing machine was not working. Toilet seat is defective. TV channels and books are in French and nothing in English. Loud party sounds from outside on Sunday night. Only thing which worked spotless was internet. Location is nice with shops and metro is not too far. In my opinion, it is a good place for a couple looking for a sleeping place. A big No to families especially with young kids. May be we were especially unlucky to get an expensive deal. In past, we used properties with super hosts so our expectations were accordingly but it was a disappointment. Recommended for young couples only if you are getting a great deal but not recommended for families.
items.one_line: Forced to leave Disneyland for key, host bathroom exposed

items.comment:

The kitchen was functional with various pots, pans, utensils, electric kettle, etc. We contributed a bread knife and sharpener. :) We sh
items.comment_full:
Biagio is a friendly, fantastic host and detail-oriented. He explained everything very thoroughly at check-in and checked in through messaging a few times to make sure we were okay or ask if we needed anything.
The property is nice. By the end of our week-long stay, we called it "The house of 1000 LED's." You really have to get used to all of the different light switches and remotes and which lights they are for and how to find the switches you need.
The kitchen was functional with various pots, pans, utensils, electric kettle, etc. We contributed a bread knife and sharpener. :) We shopped at Lidl in town and cooked (simple things) often. There is a fridge with small freezer to use, oven, & gas range, No microwave.
We slept in the bedroom downstairs. The bed was comfortable. We both used the toilet and shower upstairs where *the walls of the bathroom are **not** made of glass* (!!!) There are blinds to lower in the bathroom downstairs so no one has to watch you poop, but we couldn't get them to move.
WiFi works fantastically, also downstairs with the extender. You just have to use whichever WiFi for the level you are on and it works perfectly.
Plenty of towels.
There is a wonderful terrace with a nice view, and is undoubtedly great in warmer months.
There is a Ring camera (or similar) installed and pointed at the door of the property.
The heater downstairs works well. Upstairs you have the fireplace with pellets. You have to dump pellets into the tray and dump them into the the chute. Biagio had pellets there, but as we were going through about 15 kgs of pellets a day just to stay warm when we were upstairs, we bought our own so the next guests would have some and Biagio wouldn't have to bring more to us or get them started. A 15kg bag can be bought at Bricoman for about 5 Euros. The closer shop "ARD" has them in 10kg bags for the same price.
A few notes: we were there in January, off-season for tourism, obviously, and in 2022 so we are in the middle of Omicron-Corona. There was nothing going on and extremely little to do. Restaurants and Cafes around the property were understandably closed. Some might call this very, very boring. But we were happy to just be somewhere else for a week. To take walks, go on a drive here or there, or just relax (and bang our heads on the stairs every day even though Biagio warned us about that--one of us even had a bloody head). One day we drove, for example, to Marina Di Ragusa. Even though there was also NOTHING going on there, we saw the sea, walked a little, and ate at a seaside restaurant called "Rude," which was okay. In Regusa we also ate at Marilyn Monroe Cafe, which also had nice pizzas and friendly staff.
The one thing I will say is that I am VERY VERY mad at myself for booking the spa part of the property!!!! This was a VERY EXPENSIVE MISTAKE despite a small discount in the first day. I paid for two people for the duration of our stay, but only *I* "used" it twice for five minutes (!!) both times. The problem is that there is no cover on the jacuzzi, so the water takes all day to get warm, and then never goes higher than 35⁰C. You simply can not decide spontaneously to use it. You have to know in the morning that you want to use it in the evening. The only two times I did decide to try it were just too cold for me at the maximum 35⁰. My partner didn't want to use it at all, which is our problem, not Biagio's. If the jacuzzi stayed somewhat warm so that it didn't cool off so quickly and need all day to reheat, and then get to a higher temperature, it might have been okay. But basically, I just threw way 150€ for nothing. Biagio *did* explain that it needed an hour per 1-2 degrees. I wish I had realized that it wouldn't be warmer, though. Oh well. You live, learn, and pass on your experience. :)
All in all it was a fantastic stay in a lovely property with a lovely, romantic atmosphere and fantastic host!! We hope others enjoy their stay there too and wish Biagio and his loved ones all the best for the future.
items.one_line: Bathroom walls not glass, house of thousand LEDs

items.comment_full:
Martina is a very nice host. She was very welcoming and communicative before and during our trip. The flat is in a very good location with close links to U-Bahn, S-Bahn and shops.
The flat was initially fine, but a few issues occurred during our stay. The shower is quite old and not attached to the wall (making showering difficult) and the toilet started leaking its contents onto the floor during our stay. This made the the bathroom smell quite bad. Also, we had six people staying in the flat and the beds consisted of one double bed, a sofa bed and a set of bunk beds. This wasn't really made clear on the booking, but wasn't a major problem for us.
During our first night, the residents in the flat below us were loud, causing other residents to phone the police regarding a noise complaint. Martina phoned us and asked us to go downstairs and resolve the problem. We don't feel that it was our responsibility to do this and made us feel uncomfortable.
Overall, the flat is in a great location, and Martina seemed a very nice host, but we were disappointed by the bathroom facilities. If these were changed, we would stay again.

items.comment_full:
Do NOT stay here. First, the host is very nice and helpful. She helped arrange transport from the airport, allowed us to check in EARLY, brought us a clothes steamer and changed our money into laundry coins. Very, very great host. But, still, do not stay here.
The apartment is on the ground floor and smells of paint. Every time someone in the bldg comes in or out, you can hear it from this unit as well as water in the pipes. The bathroom is clean on first glance, but upon close inspection, it was disgusting and tiny. The sink is falling off of the wall, the shower curtain is hanging by a string (it fell an hour into our stay and we were able to get it back up), and the bathtub was not clean. The most gross part was really the shower liner that had mildew and maybe mold—my husband skipped showering a few days because it was so nasty (think red mildew). Good luck with finding the perfect shower temperature—it’s possible but takes a while.
Also, the bedroom upstairs had sheets that were supposed to be white, but instead more yellow. It was really strange for the closet to be upstairs, and bathroom downstairs. That made for some fun nights where you have to carefully walk down the metal steps to the restroom. Or when getting dressed, leave the restroom, and walk back upstairs to the closet, then back to restroom for the only mirror in the flat.
The kitchen was also a joke. Tiny, no microwave, and when you attempt to use the stove, the fire alarm immediately goes off. So make sure to have someone there to fan the fire detector while you’re cooking.
Overall, it was a miserable stay and I’m not sure the host can help the layout or noise of the place. A new shower curtain liner should be installed.
The best thing was by far the location and cable TV. We were able to watch live US Football (NFL), which was very nice.
Awesome restaurants nearby, and the hosts recommendations were spot on.
items.one_line: Amazing host, haunted apartment with shower mold nightmares

items.comment:
This place is overpriced for what it is. My expectations were low, but not this low.
I do not recommend this place for people who need a quiet place to stay, and people who have OCD / care about cleanliness, those who have disabilities, and those who may worry about not getting immediate help for issues that may arise (there could be many).
Noise: This is not a quiet place. There is a preschool next door and literally right outside the big back bedroom window. Kids were screaming from about 9am until 5pm. I had all the windows shut, but I could hear them from the living room on the ot
items.comment_full:
This place is overpriced for what it is. My expectations were low, but not this low.
I do not recommend this place for people who need a quiet place to stay, and people who have OCD / care about cleanliness, those who have disabilities, and those who may worry about not getting immediate help for issues that may arise (there could be many).
Noise: This is not a quiet place. There is a preschool next door and literally right outside the big back bedroom window. Kids were screaming from about 9am until 5pm. I had all the windows shut, but I could hear them from the living room on the other side of the unit. It was torture. There are also two small dogs on the other side of the apt that bark throughout the day. They barked when I went up and down the stairs leading to the front door, and also at other times when I was inside the unit. I heard them bark many times during the day and night while I was inside (yes, the owner leaves them out at night sometimes). The door is thin and they are very very close, so the barking is very loud. The big bedroom and kitchen side is also facing El Camino Real, a major road with constant traffic including big trucks. They honk at night and you can hear the traffic go by all day and night. On the other side of the apartment in the bedroom away from the preschool, the wall is shared with another unit, and the wall is very thin. I heard them moving furniture and such, talking loudly, playing music with bass at night. The unit is also above garages and whenever someone opens it, there is a loud sound that shakes up the place. People don't go in and out of the garage too often so it is not so bad but what got me were the kids screaming all day and dogs barking incessantly. I could not stay here for the entire duration of the trip I booked because the noise was so bad and it was causing my pain from symptom flare ups to worsen.
Cleanliness: It is not clean. Everyone has different standards and I am on the cleaner side. I clean constantly and my spaces are completely spotless. I came and saw that everything was old and worn, blinds not working. There were strange stains on towels and bedding, I found big dust balls on the ground and hairs that were not mine (I have light colored hair and the hairs were black). The kitchen wares were also very old and beat up looking and some were even sticky. A lot of plastic utensils, tools, and bowls/dishes, which are unsanitary. There were drops of oil on the stove, toaster looked very beat up, the hot water boiler looked very gross inside. I did not use any of the kitchen things except for some cups I washed again because they did not look clean or safe.
The whole apartment also has a old soaked-in kind of food smell. Mystery spices, but not in a good way. You know, the kind where the walls have just absorbed really old kitchen smells.
Supplies: They provided only a half used toilet paper and almost used up paper towel. No spares. If I had a big spill or wanted to wipe down the whole apartment, I would not have been able to. If I had stomach issues and needed more toilet paper, I would have run into big trouble. I have a disability and was unable to get to a store right away to buy toilet paper and paper towels. I was in a lot of pain the day of arrival and the next day, and I had to force myself to go to the store to buy these essentials because the host left no cushion for me to buy them a little later. This is really inconsiderate and is effectively discrimination against people with disabilities.
Furniture + Hardware: Some of the hardware like blinds were also in poor condition and did not work well. I had some trouble pulling down and pulling blinds to the side, etc.
Beds have wheels and roll around very easily. They are unsafe for this reason. I am a very light person (around 100lbs) and when I sat on the bed, especially the one in the front bedroom sharing the wall with the next unit, it would roll away.
Host's Responsiveness / Availability: Emey will be very responsive before you book but not so much after you check in. Understandably, as she has 15 listings she manages on Airbnb and possibly 20 others units that she was prioritizing over my issues after checking in. It seems she is overstretched in her role as manager and she does not have adequate help. I think 35 properties is way too many for one person to manage properly on her own, and this leads to a deterioration of customer service. If I had to manage 35 properties by myself, I might prioritize the prospective money makers first too, not the ones who have already paid. If you are the kind of person who doesn't care about quality or things missing or not working, then this won't really matter, but if you're someone who will need immediate attention at those times, I advise you against booking this place. Of course, there is a chance that she won't be stretched thin at the time of your stay, but there is also a chance that she will be.
Emey was also very dismissive and rude with me when I raised some of these issues about noise, calling me a liar. I told her about the kids screaming all day starting at 9am, and she told me that's impossible because the school is only open from 3-6pm. I told her I could hear dogs barking a lot during the day and even at night
items.one_line: Preschool torture, barking dogs, thin doors, nightmare noise

items.comment:
I've stayed in many airbnbs over the years and have never written a remotely negative review or a review longer than a few sentences.
That being said, I stayed here for three nights and every single one of those nights I was awakened by an extremely loud rooster that would crow for 10-15 minute long periods. I currently live in DC across the street from a freeway and a firehouse, so trust me when I say I am no stranger to noise and can usually sleep through just about everything. The first night was the worst, the rooster cawed every hour from 2am to 6am and I basically gave up on sleepin
items.comment_full:
I've stayed in many airbnbs over the years and have never written a remotely negative review or a review longer than a few sentences.
That being said, I stayed here for three nights and every single one of those nights I was awakened by an extremely loud rooster that would crow for 10-15 minute long periods. I currently live in DC across the street from a freeway and a firehouse, so trust me when I say I am no stranger to noise and can usually sleep through just about everything. The first night was the worst, the rooster cawed every hour from 2am to 6am and I basically gave up on sleeping. The other two nights, the rooster was at it around 4 am. This is despite wearing ear plugs and keeping all windows and doors of the apartment closed. I cannot imagine how much worse the noise would have been if I hadn't brought ear plugs and strongly advise the hosts to provide them for future guests.
When I messaged the hosts, they attributed the issue to New Orleans having a feral chicken population (this is true, roaming chickens definitely became a bigger thing post-Hurricane Katrina) and said the rooster doesn't "usually crow that early." One morning I did catch a glimpse of the rooster roaming the block.
I understand not much can be done to deal with a roaming rooster, but I think it would be fair for the hosts to mention the rooster explicitly in the posting since it clearly is a fixture of the area. It didn't help that the walls of this particular shotgun apartment were extremely thin and I could hear the neighbors' conversations word for word. I stayed in another shotgun apartment in NOLA years earlier where the hosts were cognizant of their thin walls and did their best to reduce noise by hanging volume-blocking curtains and it made a big difference. Maybe that's something the hosts here could look into.
Echoing some of the other past reviews on here: the bed is EXTREMELY creaky and not comfortable and the lock on the front door isn't very secure. It takes a fair amount of jiggling to get it to properly lock. The back door leading to the yard was a bit strange; it had this uncovered glass window that could be kind of creepy at night and appeared to be secured using a horizontal wooden bar. Kitchen and living room area look exactly like the pictures and are very nicely decorated, but I did find some dead bugs in the drawers.
I had no issues with the neighborhood but I'm also familiar with Treme and have stayed in NOLA before. One small detail: there was a ton of random leftover bath products in the bathroom that I was free to use, but oddly enough, I could not find hand soap anywhere. It being Covid19 season, hand soap is kind of a must.
items.one_line: Rooster crows every hour from 2am to 6am straight

items.comment_full:
Steve was a great host and the place is very centrally located. He was quick to respond which is always good.
However there was a very scary incident with a neighbor our last day/night. We had heard a woman upset and yelling earlier in the day. She was speaking in a mix of Hawaiian and English so she seemed to be a local and other tenants were talking about her. We went to bed early so we could wake up for Haleakala, but were abruptly woken up by screaming,banging and doors slamming. She was screaming and saying horrible things and we heard a fight and people were being thrown around. Then I heard "there's blood", "I'm calling the police ". The police came and called for a medic. We were inside our rooms terrified unsure what happened or if she could break into our rooms. When we left in the morning there was blood splattered on the wall, door and mat in the unit facing ours and I felt scared unsure if she had returned. I didn't feel safe there at all after that.
items.one_line: Neighbor attacked at night, blood splattered on walls, police called

The first 5 days in the unit I was without water and barely any heat at the peak of February wintertime with daily promises that it'd get fixed. They were both out in California in different locations and over the course of my stay
items.comment_full: An overall awful nightmarish and inconvenient experience: 28 day stay at Sharon’s started with inconvenience and ended with chaos.  I arrive to a studio with frozen pipes (and flooded basement) that no one knew about or fixed before my arrival. The first 5 days in the unit I was without water and barely any heat at the peak of February wintertime with daily promises that it'd get fixed.  Sharon was on radio silence and had referred me to her husband, who warned me via text that she isn't easy to deal with .  They were both out in California in different locations and over the course of my stay the 2 referred me back and forth between themselves when it suited them. They had no one nearby to attend to the various issues and for instance it took them one week to have someone come to fold up a very heavy bed (as pictured in the ad) that I had asked for in advance of my arrival.  On top of that, the person they sent, went into the units without my permission or knowledge and my personal belongings and confidential medical papers were moved around. Worst of it all though, was that of the 28 days I was not allowed to sleep properly for 2 full weeks due to new unruly neighbours that she had installed above me and who would every single night, start their evenings at midnight and quiet down around 4-5 AM.  During that time there would be loud TV noise, music, dog barking, door slamming, playing ball with the large-sized dog, frequent loud ins and outs of the brownstone house (low sound isolation), midnight food deliveries with delivery guys desperately buzzing all units because they wouldn't open. Since I had already been in frequent daily touch with the host regarding the first troublesome 5 days, and was having health issues that I had brought to her and her husband’s attention, I did not have any energy to resume a new communication thread over these new inconvenience, hoping that the neighbours were short-term guests.  But I started complaining via email to Sharon repeatedly, and little was done to change the neighbour’s inconsiderate behavior. I suffered from insomnia for those two weeks and missed work meetings and fell ill.  The option was to stay in the low ceiling basement unit with the kitchen in full construction condition and an overheated bedroom.  Finally, upon checking out the visibly drugged neighbor who could barely form a sentence, came down, harassing me as I was trying to take my bags out the front door.  I had to scream in the end to have him leave me alone. He knocked down some of my luggage pieces and meant that they shouldn’t be there.  Furthermore, the night before my checkout the neighbours had set off the fire alarm at 3 AM, which I informed both Sharon and her husband about. I could not fall asleep until 6 AM on my checkout day and told Sharon that my checkout would as a result be delayed (in the end with 1 hour). As before she ignored that information provided.  The cleaning lady arrived while I was still packing and they next guest arrived shortly thereafter.  A completely chaotic situation. Throughout my stay, I repeatedly requested Sharon to financially compensate me for all the inconvenience caused and she systematically ignored those calls. I am very regretful that I chose to stay at Sharon's. The only upside was that the images are representative of the studio that could be comfortable without these issues.  The host could simply not remotely manage the unit from the other side of the country without having any other co-host nearby to attend to concerns properly.  I as a guest ended up paying for it with my health and sanity. Lowest rate for Sharon.
items.one_line: Frozen pipes, no water, host silent, personal belongings violated

items.comment: Our overall experience with the residence was really nice. The location is very central, right next to the Santa Maria del Fiore. So if you want to enjoy the really old and precious parts of Florence, this choice is the right one - if you can find it. Because the people in florence don't have a number for each house but more like the same number for three houses, which is kind of dumb but then again each number has a different color so you'd think that shouldn't be a problem except of course that it is  because first you have to know about the fact that these guys kind of messed up numbering t
items.comment_full:
Our overall experience with the residence was really nice. The location is very central, right next to the Santa Maria del Fiore. So if you want to enjoy the really old and precious parts of Florence, this choice is the right one - if you can find it. Because the people in florence don't have a number for each house but more like the same number for three houses, which is kind of dumb but then again each number has a different color so you'd think that shouldn't be a problem except of course that it is because first you have to know about the fact that these guys kind of messed up numbering their buildings and secondly the colors aren't so easy do distinguish.
But you know there's Google Maps so that's easy, right. Well no, because it shows you a wrong location so the weird numbering of the buildings doesn't just mess up your sense of orientation but also seems to confuse Google.
But after a bit of communication nevertheless we found it.
It's a really cozy place. Looks a bit bigger on the pictures than in real life but you still have plenty of room for you and your friends. Also you have three bathrooms. We were 5 people so there was absolutely no fighting over who gets to shower first or stuff. We really appreciated it. Another plus: It's very cleaned up and the interior decoration is lovely. It looks and feels modern, but also very homely. You are provided some of the basics for hygiene like towels and shower gel, which also is nice and surely appreciated by most people. Also you have a kitchen. It's small but you have everything you need. Keep in mind that the stove works inductively. Don't play around and wait for it to work without a pan, slowly dying of starvation. Just put a pan on the thing an it'll work.
You have no drinks or spices there so if you wanna get fancy you'll have to buy some beforehand.
You have a TV, basically, but first of all it's pretty small, secondly if you go to Florence to watch TV you probably have to reevaluate your life choices. If you still wanna use it you have to fondle around with the cable since it has a slack joint. But seriously, you shouldn't care. Enjoy the city.
So all in all our experience there was splendid because we were provided with the most important things: Bed, showers, kitchen, everything cleaned up and a working AC. We really couldn't complain about the important aspects of this place. If you want to stay in a nice, cozy and clean place with a pinch of Amsterdam in the interior, this is the place you want to be.
By the way don't drive around in a car in Florence. We parked outside of the city. There are Italians on the street of which most drive like they robbed a bank and are chased by the police. Also there are streets in Florence on which you are not allowed to drive if you don't have a permit and you probably wouldn't be able to distinguish one from the other. Just don't.
items.one_line: Florence building numbering system and Google Maps confusion odyssey

items.comment_full:
Unfortunately our stay in this apartment wasn’t pleasant.
Cleanliness was not up to standard - for reference, it would fail an “end of lease clean” inspection. The host has said they will change cleaners. One of the biggest things was that there was a viagra pill under the couch cushions - so obviously wasn’t being cleaned thoroughly (not to mention dangerous if small kids were to stay and find it)
The amenities were average. Unfortunately just missing the finishing touches like free sachets of coffee and sugar. The TV was missing it’s remote (host said a new one is already on its way) and a DVD player is available but doesn’t actually have the cords to connect it to the TV so therefore useless.
The smoke alarm was also under the kitchen sink without a battery - one very serious safety risk.
The furniture is old and damaged - either stained or broken. From googling, the apartment was sold early 2020 and the apartment has the same furniture as when it was posted in 2019.
The mattresses are quite firm so if that’s not suitable for you, I would consider a different listing.
The combination of a double bed and a single bed in both bedrooms is convenient for extra sleeping spaces but feels cluttered with the added desks, and also makes one of the wardrobes inaccessible.
The host has since updated some incorrect aspects of the ad, however the images provided aren’t of this exact apartment.
The apartment also has a constant air noise through the walls, so if white noise isn’t for you or you struggle with noise sensitivity, reconsider.
The worst part of the entire apartment is the SMELL. If you have grandparents who have smoked cigarettes inside their house for years, you’ll know what I mean. Unfortunately very pungent and hits you as soon as you walk in. The host has taken on this feedback and said they will consider changing the carpets etc.
Some other things to note is that I assumed the laundry facilities described were within the apartment but is a shared laundromat within the building. The “sofa bed” is also only a couch.
Some positives of this apartment: large windows, location, cheap - especially if splitting between 5 people, easy check in, late check out (11am), fast replies from host.
At the end of the day, if you are looking for a hotel-style airbnb, this isn’t for you. Hopefully the host can make some serious upgrades to make this listing of decent quality.
items.one_line: Viagra pill under couch cushions, no remote, smoke alarm safety risk

The amenities are fine for a short stay, with a fridge, mi
items.comment_full:
Jean’s room has its pros and cons. The room was clean, beautifully decorated, and had a spacious wardrobe. Check-in was easy, and Jean was very responsive with clear instructions. However, the listing overstates privacy-the walls are thin, and we were woken both mornings by loud snot rocketing, phlegm coughing, and spitting noises from the shared bathroom. The bathroom was clean except for the visible remains of snot and blood in the sink each morning. Additionally, the outdoor photos are misleading, as furniture is stored outside.
The amenities are fine for a short stay, with a fridge, microwave, and kettle, but no kitchen. More surface space for belongings or meal prep would be helpful. The neighborhood felt safe but was a bit noisy, with sounds from the freeway and a clay shooting range. The location is convenient, close to freeways and shops.
Overall, it’s a good option for a short stay if you want a clean, attractive room in a good location and don’t mind the noise.
items.one_line: Thin walls reveal neighbor's phlegm symphony every morning

items.comment_full:
At first glance, the room seemed clean, but after a few days, the weather turned colder and we had to keep the windows closed for warmth. With the windows closed, we found it very hard to breathe without sneezing or coughing, and smelled a mildew odor. Upon looking around, we noticed 4 substantial patches of black mold on the walls that shared the bathroom space. After discovering this health hazard, we decided to move to a new place to stay as soon as possible. The issue with the mold has been reported to Airbnb, and they made a partial refund to me and also stated that they would check back with Yankel to be sure that he would eradicate the mold issue before continuing to rent this unit.
Other than the noxious mold, the room is simple, but lacked some of the basics that would have been helpful to travelers. For instance, we would have appreciated hand soap for the bathroom sink (or any soap), or having coffee available for the morning. The location is fairly central, but despite the flat being located on a small side street, the street was fairly noisy with drunk people loudly singing or talking on the sidewalk below each night between 3 - 6 a.m. There were also a few minor inconveniences such as the wobbly toilet seat, not much toilet paper, and a few broken slats near the bottom of the futon (we were able to fix one). Though, honestly, we were there for the Airbnb Host conference and gone for 14 hours each day, so the only thing we had time to bring to Yankel's attention was the fact that the electricity circuit completely failed on our first morning when I was drying my hair. I emailed Yankel and he responded the same day by arriving on site to fix the circuit board while we were at the conference. However, Yankel also suggested that the issue was caused by a faulty plug converter that he assumed was mine. Actually, this converter was in the room when we arrived, so I had assumed that it was equipment that was provided with the rental. While I appreciated Yankel's responsiveness, after being a guest at nearly two dozen Airbnb rooms throughout the world, this is the only Airbnb location that I cannot recommend to others.
items.one_line: Four substantial black mold patches, health hazard, no soap provided

items.comment_full:
I had a terrible experience.
It was while we were cooking dinner. We steamed potatoes in a pot on the burner and then were trying to fry a steak in a pan. Of course, the burner, pot, and pan were all provided by the host. When we put the pan on the burner, the burner(electrical one) did not work. The pan was not for the electrical burner! Then we used the pot. When we put the steak in the pot for about 15 seconds, the smoke alarm went off. Very surprised, we turned off the burner and tried to open the door and windows to let the smoke out. But the alarm kept sounding very loudly. We called the host on the phone several times, but she did not answer or her line was busy. We sent her a message, but she did not respond.
After about 15 minutes, the fire department arrived. turned off the alarm, and left. The host argued that I should bear the cost of the fire department's visit of £100, saying that "When you cook, there is usually steam, not smoke. Smoke occurs when you burn the food"
items.one_line: Pan smoke, fire department arrives, host demands guest pays fee.

items.comment_full: This was an OK stay. The location is actually not as close to the city, if you take the F train, it will be 17 stops into the city, kinda rough travels when you’re out sight seeing all day. The room is small, perfect for two only.  A PLUS; there are also a few stores where you can get a quick breakfast (Deli Shop) or drinks/snacks. Requested toilet paper since we were only provided one for a 5 day stay but didn’t get a response until after check out. We do appreciate we were able to have a third guest free of charge since she slept in her own sleeping bag on the floor (not recommended by the way!). We probably won’t stay here again, but overall despite a few bumps the bed did feel “cozy”.

items.comment_full:
Wow. That’s a super bad wow, not a good wow. DO NOT STAY HERE.
I have stayed in Airbnbs many many times over the years and this place was, hands down, the WORST I’ve ever encountered. The pictures and description are inaccurate and I’m glad others have had a good experience staying at this place but boy oh boy this was not our experience!
Immediately upon walking in to the basement apartment, we noticed a strong smell of mold.
Next thing we noticed was that the beds were hastily made and there was a clump of hair attached to a wire (??) placed on the dining table. Yuck.
**Then** I walked into the bathroom. The floors were dirty, it looked like the towels were not washed and there was a poor attempt to roll them up and shove them in a basket. There was a giant spider in the bathtub, and a used (blech) soap bar in the soap dish and half-used, open shampoo and conditioner bottles around. The toilet was dirty. And, perhaps the worst part, there was clearly dirty and grimy cups on the bathroom sink that someone had used to put their toothbrushes in so there was old toothpaste in them.
Super super gross.
It doesn’t seem like this very unclean apartment was the result of the owner not having time to clean between guests because someone had tried to make the beds and someone had tried to roll up the dirty towels and put them in the basket (so there was an attempt at an appearance of cleaning, but it was obvious the place was dirty.) Also, even with a thorough cleaning, the first thing you notice when you open the door is a moldy smell so unless there was an air purifier running, the smell indicates an overall lack of appropriate attention to cleaning in the apartment.
We clearly did not feel comfortable staying in this place so we had to last minute scramble and find another accommodation in a city we aren’t familiar with with two very tired children with us.
It was a nightmare.
Then, to top things off, after we left, found accommodations, met up with our elderly parents who were meeting us in Montreal for vacation, sat down for dinner and finally took a deep breath, the Airbnb owner started calling me multiple times, leaving me a voicemail and also emailing me several times. I clearly and firmly let them know that I have informed Airbnb support about the issues with the apartment and would like them to go through Airbnb support as well as this was what I was most comfortable with and they totally ignored and disregarded that request, and kept trying to contact me! I had to finally block their number!
So, not only was the apartment a nightmare and the listing and pictures were totally misleading, the owner was harassing me after we left the apartment even though I told them to please contact Airbnb for further information about what happened.
Ugh- I have never had this happen before and it has honestly made me rethink Airbnb stays in the future.
My family and I have never gotten anything but stellar reviews from all of the airbnbs we’ve stayed in and we have always tried to give people the benefit of the doubt when it comes to little things here and there but this was completely over-the-top awful.
items.one_line: Hair on wire plus spider in tub equals nightmare apartment

items.comment_full:
The experience I had with Mallorca Villa Selection is the worst I ever had on AirBnb.
Never seen such a bad and unprofessional attitude from a host...
I'm a regular user of AirBnb (more than 40 reservations lately) cause I work remotely (I am the founder and CEO of a Tech Company).
So having a proper WiFi connection is not an option. It's a MUST.
Of course I know when reserving a villa in the middle of an Island (Mallorca), you can't always expect having a 300Mo connection... And that's fine.
In few AirBnb I went to, I had low Wifi, but I used my mobile as a router and it was ok.
When I arrived to the Villa, the Wifi was just INEXISTANT. The router was an antic. And moreover, there was absolutely no mobile signal in the area. And when I say "no signal", it means "NO/ZERO/NADA signal".
It was so inexistent I had to leave the villa and drive few kilometers away to be able to send them an email. To which they answered only the day after...
Obviously, what they advertise : "Wifi", was just inaccurate.
As I left the villa and drove back home in Palma, I asked them for a refund (as I would not be able to enjoy the villa since I was not there for vacation but for working remotely).
And the only answer they gave me was: "No, we can't, it's not our policy. Stay there and enjoy the Villa."
I was like: "Sorry but what didn't you understand? I'm here to work, I have regular meetings w/ my team, client zoom calls.... I just cannot stay here with No Wifi."
They started explaining me I should have ask them if the Wifi was working before booking, blah blah blah... lol
But....
- Do clients ask them if the dishwasher functions before they book?
- Do they ask if the pool can be used when they specify "pool" ?
- Do they ask if the electricity is ok (before bringing candles... we never know.... ^^) ?
Of course not...
The listing was just misleading. The "Wifi" info was incorrect.
And once again... that's ok. It can happen. We're all human... right?
But the way they dealt with the problem was the most unprofessional and disrespectful I ever experienced.
I am a heavy user of AirBnb and not the most annoying guest to say the least... Just check my reviews, you'll see by yourself ;)
But here, I had that very unpleasant feeling of dealing with someone who was like : "F*** you, you and your problem. You paid. That's too late. That's not our problem anymore. We'll keep your money... so just relax and move on."
As I understood they were absolutely not concerned by what was a problem to me, I contacted AirBnb for having a mediation.
And honestly... those guys did an amazing job. <3
I felt fully heard by each person I talked with and they found a resolution of the problem that was both quick, efficient, and fair.
(Thanks for that, AirBnb Team!).
But Mallorca Villa Selection.... No thank you.
Never again.
And I don't advise anybody to book a villa with them.... Of course If you don't have any problem, may be it's just gonna be fine.
But before you book with them..... just take a quick look at their clients reviews on Google.......... ^^
items.one_line: No WiFi in remote villa forced CEO to work elsewhere.
