items.comment_full:
This rental is in a great location. You are able to walk to the french quarter to all the restaurants/attractions and it has wifi. We were greeted by a guy who briefly introduced himself, the house, and then rushed out (we now believe it was to avoid all interaction or questions that might arise about the property). Upon further inspection, we began to recognize a consistent smell that we unanimously agreed can only be described as the remnants of a grandpa’s sneeze. The kitchen area was odd, with merely three dirty mini fridges that lack the common cold feature fridges are known for, without drawers for storage. The cabinets offered plates, coffee cups, and maybe 4 wine glasses for all 10 of us to share. A utensil tray sits out on the counter due to no drawers in the cabinetry...but that is the least of its charm. The kitchen faucet was broken and did not retract, and there was no hot water in the kitchen, or even water that is considered drinkable (it would be a nice gesture if management offered a Brita so guests did not have to rely on consistently purchasing water bottles-especially with the limited mini fridge capacity). Management was unable to repair the hot water issue in the kitchen, and the entire home was found extremely dirty upon arrival with gnats and flies flying around the kitchen area and a consistent supply of mosquitos that bit everyone as we tried to enjoy our stay. Speaking of cleanliness, the floors were dirty and sticky upon arrival, which we again had to ask management to clean. Management referred to all these problems as “quirks” of her “least favorite property”. The management was less of a butler/concierge and more “butt-hurt” by our requests. We were told that with this job they were kept too busy and couldn’t even spend time with their family, and that our arrival day was not a good day for them to attend to us, making the remainder of our stay somewhat awkward—I guess that is just the NOLA charm? Secondly, the breakfast that was advertised as “included” was not offered or given to us because the staff was “too overwhelmed” to fulfill their promise. To apologize for the inconvenience, we were offered a bottle of wine as a consolation prize for ten people over four days. Hmmm…should we do the math?
Moving on to more of the fun “quirks” of this charming residence: the four bathrooms are beautiful... despite only one of them being fully functional, and that bathroom not even having a closing door. In the other bathrooms, either the showers would be freezing cold, the toilet wouldn’t flush, or the light fixtures were broken making use of that particular bathroom obsolete unless you wanted to shower or use the restroom in complete darkness. We were told these problems would all be fixed by the next day. However, we left 4 days later with only the toilet being repaired and all 10 of us sharing the one shower that offered hot water and light…as we mentioned above, the door on this bathroom didn’t close completely or lock...but there was a handy dirty garbage can that we propped against the door to offer privacy. Additionally, when we first arrived, the shower drains were still clogged with hair from the previous renters, adding to the mystery New Orleans has to offer. Now, the idea of two tv’s in the same space seems like a perfect party trick for anyone looking for a good time, or to have sports on one channel and cartoons on the other, and that would be the case... if the tv’s were in sync, had a second audio output, and had more than one remote. Next, the rooms are clearly party central, since they were left obviously not cleaned beforehand. The hairs from previous parties were left in the sheets as a kind of “welcome gift” to our group, as well as the dirt and stains on the sheets/comforters to add some character. And not all of the outlets were functioning, helping the vacation be an “unplugged” experience. Additionally, the windows and doors aren’t very insulated. This makes way for plenty of bugs, outdoor conversations, and ~illicit activities~ to make their way in, bringing the NOLA outdoor experience into your very own room. If you choose this property just be sure to bring your earplugs, unless you want to hear all the juicy conversations from the other renters on the property. The furniture is... not much. Quite literally nothing to be excited about and we found it difficult for us all to lounge together after a long day. That is, except for the mysterious and large armoire, complete with doorbells and mirrors that further the suspicion toward what could possibly be hiding in there? Seriously, why is this enormous piece of furniture with no doors or drawers even in this house?
Something that is no mystery, however, is the extravagance of the outdoor areas which are advertised greatly. Don’t count on any photo-ops because these areas are consistently covered with leaves and dirt. While it does have huge potential, nothing is “insta-worthy” without bringing a handheld vacuum and a masterful filter. This outdoor space and the pool are shared with the other properties. Unfortunately, the pool looked murky constantly, and you could see mounds of sand or dirt in the bottom. Management was seen throwing in buckets of chemicals and blue dye (?) to try and make it the poolside experience promised.
Nev Schulman, above all, would love this place, as it is an ultimate catfish. Although it is not what is advertised, and if you are with friends/family that can look the other way and still have a fun experience, the location truly is great. Needless to say, it was a memorable experience. Update: our group just received a 5 star rating from the management of this establishment because we left the home in great shape..it was probably because of
items.one_line: Smells like grandpa's sneeze, three broken mini fridges, no hot water

items.comment: Don’t be deceived by the patio picture or any other staged photo. The patio is unusable due to the pigeon droppings. The droppings are down the walls and all over the floor, the smell is terrible. I would not sit outside without a hazmat suit. We did try to clean the space up with disinfectant. It made the area just a little better, so we could get some fresh air in the apartment but within a day we would have had to scrub again due to the amount of pigeon droppings. One bird was injured and trapped on the patio adding to the mess. We captured it and set it free on the street. The air conditio
items.comment_full: Don’t be deceived by the patio picture or any other staged photo. The patio is unusable due to the pigeon droppings. The droppings are down the walls and all over the floor, the smell is terrible. I would not sit outside without a hazmat suit. We did try to clean the space up with disinfectant. It made the area just a little better, so we could get some fresh air in the apartment but within a day we would have had to scrub again due to the amount of pigeon droppings. One bird was injured and trapped on the patio adding to the mess. We captured it and set it free on the street. The air conditioning unit is mounted on a small roof over the patio. The condensate water runs over the roof, dripping water all over one corner, approximately ¼, of the patio near the doors. There is a hose to clean the patio, but it is broken, and the nozzle won’t stay attached. The patio is unusable. The fully equipped kitchen is a JOKE….! The oven lights, but when it heats up you can’t close the oven door, apparently due to metal warpage. There are 3 pots and only one lid, a very small saucepan, a mid size kettle and a frying pan with a handle that is extremely loose and unsafe to use. The kitchen also lacked basic cooking implements such as a spatula, which we purchased in the grocery store. The pair of kitchen scissors with a broken handle were unusable, there is a bread knife, a handful of smaller serrated knifes, half of which are falling apart because the rivets are missing. The sink has no stopper, which makes washing dishes interesting. Sink faucet is loose and moves. There are 2 wine glasses, one juice glass, and a hodgepodge of other glassware. In the living space, two out of the four of the chairs at the kitchen table have broken backs. You can sit on them but must be careful. There is minimum natural light in the place and the loft area light bulb was burned out, we pick one up in the grocery store. The stairs have safety cable, half of them are broken. There are air freshers in the rooms and all are empty. There is peeling paint and cracked walls in a few areas. Don’t walk around with white socks on they turn black very quickly. The television is not a smart television, it is a nice but small flat screen television. See the pattern no maintenance! The bathroom is the smallest bathroom ever, the shower is literally 2x2 feet, it’s difficult for one person to be in the bathroom, much less two. The cold-water shower handle is simply pressed in place and fell off onto the floor one day. The cold-water faucet leaks under sink if not closed tightly. We left one day and on returning found that the countertop and floor were flooded due to the faucet leak. There...
items.one_line: Patio covered in pigeon droppings, injured bird trapped, oven door won't close

items.comment_full:
***This review is more about Evan than the apartment, we did not end up staying in the apartment booked due to reasons that Evan didn't disclose until less than 24 hours before our stay***
Giving us 15 hours notice, Evan advised that we were unable to reside in the apartment. We called Evan and he was cagey about the reason and when we pushed him he told us it was a plumbing issue and that he had another apartment in the same building that we could stay in and sent us a link https://abnb.me/SkScoGKJl1. This apartment was not nearly as nice, but with such little notice we had no other option. Evan was cagey about the option of us residing there whilst the plumbing issue was being fixed, suggesting that we just turn up on the day and look at the other apartment.
When we arrived Evan advised us that he had not been truthful on the phone and the reason we could not stay in the apartment was due to bedbugs and he had been trying to fix the situation but had not been able to.
So our issue with Evan is that he knew that the apartment had bedbugs and left it until the last minute to tell us this. Our view is that he did this so that at such short notice, we had no other option other than to accept the other apartment in his basement. We would also note that Evan did offer us 10% off the booking however Evan attempted to give us 10% off our booking of 6 nights at the original apartmrnt which was more expensive, not 10% off the cheaper basement room (a difference to us of about $300aud).
Communication with Evan in general was quite poor. Evan also gave us no notice on two occasions that his cousin, a real estate agent, was showing the apartment, meaning that our valuables were on display.
Based on the positive reviews for this apartment and Evan (which is why we booked!), we hope we had a uncommonly bad experience with Evan. The location was amazing and we hope you enjoy this apartment that we didnt get to stay in :-(. A word of caution though on the basement apartment we did stay in however... there are only a few reviews, but they are negative and accurate. The apartment had a strong smell of rat droppings and had not been cleaned properly and wifi was only available in the hallway and toilet. To be fair to Evan - he did give us a free bottle of wine, carried a tv down (as the room did not have one) and let us check out late :)
items.one_line: Host lied about plumbing, actually bedbugs problem

items.comment: Despite this house being well-appointed and comfortable with plenty of room for our two families (four adults and two toddlers), we had several very poor experiences with the property and host that unfortunately colored our impression in a negative light. The cleaning wasn’t finished until hours after the check-in time; parking information from the host was misleading, resulting in us receiving a parking ticket AND a dead rat on our car (issues with which the host was unhelpful in resolving); the property was not very safe/kid-friendly; and basic amenities were either difficult to find or not
items.comment_full: Despite this house being well-appointed and comfortable with plenty of room for our two families (four adults and two toddlers), we had several very poor experiences with the property and host that unfortunately colored our impression in a negative light. The cleaning wasn’t finished until hours after the check-in time; parking information from the host was misleading, resulting in us receiving a parking ticket AND a dead rat on our car (issues with which the host was unhelpful in resolving); the property was not very safe/kid-friendly; and basic amenities were either difficult to find or not present. Each of these issues is explained in further detail below, as well as suggestions on what the host could do to improve. First, I had contacted the host ahead of time to let her know that our flight would arrive in Tokyo at 10:30am and to see if we could check in earlier than the standard time listed, 3:00pm. She said that was okay, but that the house may still be undergoing cleaning. That was fine to us. By the time we arrived at the property, it was 2:35pm. A woman was still there cleaning, but told us we could come in. When she left at 2:50pm, the beds were all unmade, there were no clean towels, and wet laundry was still hanging on the balcony. We messaged the host around 4:30pm to ask if the cleaning would be finished and she assured us that it would be in several hours, later explaining that the laundry service is closed on Saturdays and that because of this it is a “late check-in.” That would have been fine had she notified us in advance, but she hadn’t...she completely indicated that it would be the standard 3:00pm check-in. Cleaning wasn’t finished until 7:00pm, right at the children’s bedtime, and even then it wasn’t a very thorough job. There were dusty/grimy surfaces, the throw pillows on the couch needed washing, the crib bedding wasn’t clean, and my husband had to pick old food out of the rug. The second issue was parking. Because we have young children and were traveling to Mt Fuji for a day, we decided to rent a car. It was an eight passenger van in order to fit all of us and luggage. I thought this would be no problem since the listing on AirBnB said “Free Parking,” but when I asked the host about it the day of our arrival, she said the garage was only big enough for a five passenger car. Well, it was indeed a TINY garage. I drive a little Nissan March normally and highly doubt that even that would fit in this garage, especially considering that empty bottles and other random junk were lining the side of it. So, after unloading, we parked in a small driveway alongside the house. As it turns out, this was the neighbor’s driveway (thought we never saw a car in it) and he had the police knock on our door around 7:30pm to ask us to move the car. My husband and my friend’s husband did so, re-parking so that the van was only in front of our door and garage, with plenty of room for other vehicles to pass (we actually saw a garbage truck pass by with no issues). A few hours later, the host messaged me asking if we’d parked in the neighbor’s driveway, and saying that he was very angry. I told her we had been, but had moved the car. I explained to her how it was now parked and asked if that was fine or if there was somewhere else we should park. She replied that it was okay as long as cars could pass. I told her there was enough room, and that was that for the night. The next morning, however, we not only had a parking ticket on our windshield, but someone had also placed a very dead, decomposing rat on the roof of our car. Though it could have been an unrelated incident (especially since the area is quite the late night scene), it was very coincidental that it happened so soon after the neighbor had been angry at us for parking in his drive. What was really disappointing, however, was the host’s response when my husband called her regarding the parking ticket and the rat. We were frustrated that we got a ticket after she told us we were parked okay and we also wanted to know who we needed to call to dispose of the rat. As my husband asked for clarification with all of this, she kept cutting him off saying that he was “blaming her” for things that weren’t her fault or problem. Eventually, he got fed up and ended the conversation, and a few minutes later she messaged me saying that she didn’t find anyone to take care of the rat. The police ended up being kind enough to deal with the rat and we parked the car in paid parking for the rest of our time there, which got quite expensive. The third issue we had with the property was that it wasn’t very child-friendly, despite being marketed as catering to families with young children. Though it did have a crib and some other amenities for infants/toddlers, we had to move a lot of items around to keep our children from breaking something or getting hurt. The biggest problem was the two steep staircases. On the middle floor, the entry to the stairs leading downwards did have a sliding screen, but none of the other staircases were blocked in any way, so we ended up having to block them with chairs and tables. And even the sliding screen wasn’t effective because it wasn’t properly installed. It was supposed to be bolted to the wall on one side, but wasn’t, so when my friend’s daughter (20 months old) accidentally fell backwards onto it, it completely caved and she tumbled all the way down the stairs, resulting...
items.one_line: Dead rat on car plus cleaning delays equals Tokyo parking disaster

items.comment_full:
This bnb is in a great location, it’s really well decorated & designed, small, but super close to the beach & lots of restaurants/cafes, & was very clean.
However, we stayed here for 10 nights, & we felt as though it lacked some very basic essentials that would have made our stay 5 stars:
-more than 1 set of sheets
-more than 1 set of towels (only 1 per person & only 1 hand towel)
-no doors anywhere, so no privacy (including no bathroom door which is directly next to the bed, so if ur not close to ur partner maybe not the place for u!!!)
-no paper towel in the kitchen and only 1 kitchen dish towel
-no beach towels / beach umbrella
-no TV
-only 1 standing fan
-VERY noisy; sandwiched between construction in front & behind the unit PLUS heavy traffic
-not enough toilet paper
-Wifi was difficult to connect to when we first arrived
-cockroach in kitchen
-no storage space to unpack suitcases
If u r planning on a 2 night stay, u will be very pleased; anything longer, not the place for u!
items.one_line: Beautiful beachside location: no privacy doors, cockroach in kitchen discovered

items.comment:
According to my own experience, I did not like my residence at Fred’s apartment.
Pros:
A lot of coffee, a fully equipped kitchen and bathroom, a clean house, and a nice host.
Cons:
Very very very bad neighbors upstairs. They made our experience so terrible… They made much noise any time… after midnight, during the day, disturbing my baby/family's sleep and giving us hard time. Fred talked to them twice but there were no remarkable changes with the fire alarm turning on at 1:00 AM, saying bad words just in front of our main door (which is 4 feet away from our bed lol) day after
items.comment_full:
According to my own experience, I did not like my residence at Fred’s apartment.
Pros:
A lot of coffee, a fully equipped kitchen and bathroom, a clean house, and a nice host.
Cons:
Very very very bad neighbors upstairs. They made our experience so terrible… They made much noise any time… after midnight, during the day, disturbing my baby/family's sleep and giving us hard time. Fred talked to them twice but there were no remarkable changes with the fire alarm turning on at 1:00 AM, saying bad words just in front of our main door (which is 4 feet away from our bed lol) day after day, their dog which spent much time next to our door barking (which is 2 feet away from the neighbor’s door) and their loud music after midnight as they sing and dance on the wooden roof up…etc. I discovered later on that the suite upstairs did not have a main door up and that explains the high noises we hear all time, a doorless suite lol. I talked to Fred and sent him videos and pictures but no remarkably big changes and they were there till the day we left (we checked out one day before our due date).
Many expired products like wipes and hot chocolate powder.
Not much space for storage, so not suitable for long stays with luggage.
Not calm with snowplows working a few feet away from our window at night, you can easily hear the chats of those walking in front of your window as well as the cars. The basement is in front of the street just 1 meter away from it. So, if you prefer calm stays, this is not the one for with semi-trucks, cars, and chatting people in front of your window.
Not a baby-friendly space at all. The baby could easily get to utensils.
We experienced water leaks for some days and hated that the host had to show up many times. He had to enter twice just to fix something for those who live upstairs. It seemed that the units’ controlling system was downstairs and the only wait to get to it is through our unit. Fred was insisting to go in one time when I was not home as he needed to fix something ‘urgent’ for our golden neighbors and that couldn’t wait and I was obliged to allow him to go in even though I hate someone going to my place when I am not there.
The dryer needs around 6 hours and sometimes more to get the clothes dry and sometimes they are not fully dry. Spending all those hours in a dryer, the clothes were never in a good shape.
On cold days, the hot water completely stops in the shower and toilet tanks will not be filled. In that case you need to turn on the sink water and keep it running which will only produce hot water to fill in the toilet tank and you may also take a shower with only hot water if you are spiderman. That water issue affected also the washer and dishwasher and you have to do the same to keep them working. When mentioning that the hot water is not working in the shower to Fred, he responded that this was because of the cold weather…..but I do not really care. I paid for full service so I had to find what I paid for.
Mentioning that there is free street parking is not correct as there is nothing to prove that as far as I looked. Double checking that with Fred one day before checking in he explained that it is free only for a limited time. Even if it was free as he said, you will rarely find an available spot at night. Check the full description of the apartment to get the details. Fred suggested parking in a school’s parking spot, but I couldn’t really afford to pay for such a parking ticket so I had to take a nice tour on a cold night looking for a hidden place to park the car I rented and was worried the whole time that it may be towed.
The TV quality was bad and its sound was awful, you need to turn on the external speaker to get a good sound, the TV system was heavy and the remote control was too slow…finding a movie or reaching YouTube took much time than expected.
Still, Fred was a nice guy, responsive but the suite did not suit us at all.
Videos and pictures are available upon request.
items.one_line: Upstairs suite has no door, neighbors sing, dance, swear constantly

Lastly, the biggest problem we had was that there were noise detectors up on the roof. The rule was
items.comment_full: This place was great considering how close it was to the beach which wasn’t crowded at all. The house did sleep our group of 10 comfortably; however, in regards to the house, there were a few flaws that we were not expecting. The house is very nice looking but seems to have to been finished cheaply. The shower door handles and drains were broken, the garage code did not work from the outside, and the upstairs shower leaked water through the ceiling all over the bed in the bedroom directly below. Lastly, the biggest problem we had was that there were noise detectors up on the roof. The rule was that there could not be excessive noise on the roof past 10:00 pm and we were continued to be bothered for making noise even when we were all inside or asleep. Our neighbors were being loud one of the nights, but we were still notified to keep the noise down by phone call at 3:00 am. The location was great but if you plan to stay here, expect house flaws and to be bothered for noise complaints.
items.one_line: Noise detector called at 3am when neighbors were loud

items.comment:
PLEASE READ BEFORE RENTING THIS PLACE!!! If you like to sleep DO NOT GO HERE!!!!
We rented this place not realizing that the appartment is literally in the FAVELA ! You can hear and see everything!!! There is no note of this in the description.
All hours of the day you will hear blaring music, yelling, screaming and of course roosters cockaling all throughout the night. SO LOUD!!!!!
We were even lucky to see and hear a GUN fight, YIKES!!!!!
Needless to say we did not stay for our full reservation, as we came for a fun relaxing holiday not a favela music festival experience.
items.comment_full:
PLEASE READ BEFORE RENTING THIS PLACE!!! If you like to sleep DO NOT GO HERE!!!!
We rented this place not realizing that the appartment is literally in the FAVELA ! You can hear and see everything!!! There is no note of this in the description.
All hours of the day you will hear blaring music, yelling, screaming and of course roosters cockaling all throughout the night. SO LOUD!!!!!
We were even lucky to see and hear a GUN fight, YIKES!!!!!
Needless to say we did not stay for our full reservation, as we came for a fun relaxing holiday not a favela music festival experience.
When we reported our concerns to the owner he stated that this is the Rio culture and to buy ear plugs! Very rude and unwilling to cooperate with us.
The apartment itself was very large and unique. Very comfortable master bed but pointless with no sleep from the noise.
The stairs up to the rooms are very steep, not recommended for families with very young children or elderly. We couldn't even bring our suitcases up.
Also the hot water runs out very quickly, take note to shower quickly.
We did like the neighborhood Ipanema has great restaurants and close to the beach, but there are so many better places to stay.
Needless to say we WOULD NOT RECOMMEND and USE CAUTION if renting this as it is in a dangerous location. Also, if you don't like it, good luck dealing with the owner.
items.one_line: Located in favela, witnessed gunfight, owner suggests buying earplugs

Toilet runs 24/7 because some genius hooked it up to a hot water line. Sprinklers, fire alarms, and the dead bolt on the entry door covered in duct tape to make them work-ish.  Roach spray thoughtfully  provided for bugs. Towels are ragged and have holes in them. Shower faucets are old, rusted, and don't work as they should. I never pan hosts, check my previous reviews, but this host comments in previous reviews that this p
items.comment_full: Great location, but place was filthy. Not secure. Duct tape was used for plumbing and electrical instead of having professionals fixing things correctly. Bedding not clean. Toilet runs 24/7 because some genius hooked it up to a hot water line. Sprinklers, fire alarms, and the dead bolt on the entry door covered in duct tape to make them work-ish.  Roach spray thoughtfully  provided for bugs. Towels are ragged and have holes in them. Shower faucets are old, rusted, and don't work as they should. I never pan hosts, check my previous reviews, but this host comments in previous reviews that this place is under construction. It is not. This place is dirty, not secure, and good luck sleeping with the busted toilet running. Also there is no thermostat in the place, it's controlled by some other neighbor. Too hot? Too bad. Too cold? Too bad. Honestly if I would have known we could have called Airbnb on day one to get out of there, we would have. You live and you learn, I suppose. Buyer beware.

items.comment_full: In all my years of using air bnb, this has been the single most disgusting place I have ever had the misfortune of being in. As soon as I stepped in, there was no fire alarm. Called Air Bnb and left immediately an hour after check in. Refund given luckily.

items.comment:
I stayed here for five days, and where to start? :)
1) Location: okay, let's just say this, because no host (no matter how good) would advertise this fact, but it's like 200 meters from a towering yellow prison. That out of the way, should that deter you? No. First off, from the cottage, it's only visible if you *try* to see it, and only barely, through one window; the rest of the scenery is lovely and rustic, a very old-world aesthetic. Secondly, the odds that while you're there, there's going to be a prison break, *and* the prisoners are going to choose to hide out in your cottage *righ
items.comment_full:
I stayed here for five days, and where to start? :)
1) Location: okay, let's just say this, because no host (no matter how good) would advertise this fact, but it's like 200 meters from a towering yellow prison. That out of the way, should that deter you? No. First off, from the cottage, it's only visible if you *try* to see it, and only barely, through one window; the rest of the scenery is lovely and rustic, a very old-world aesthetic. Secondly, the odds that while you're there, there's going to be a prison break, *and* the prisoners are going to choose to hide out in your cottage *right near the prison*? Yeah, that should be on your Czech-vacation-concern-list somewhere between "eyes gouged out by a falcon" and "shark attack" ;) Honestly, it's probably great to have the extra security around.
Apart from that: it's close to the airport, but not so close you get any noise from it. Great distance - something like 10 minutesd drive by car or 2 hours walk. It's quite far from the centre of town, but Prague has good public transit options if you want that.
2) The building: The pictures are fair. It's quaint and rustic. Soňa put a lot of care into little details travelers might have forgotten - the more you look around, the more things you see that she thought to add in case people needed them (power adapters, chargers, toiletries, on and on). The cottage was kept quite clean (though, of course, rustic). It's fully detached, so if you're COVID-concerned, no worries, you're not staying in a shared building.
All in all, quite a nice place to stay. That said, a few caveats:
A) The hot water is only enough to fill 1/4th to 1/3rd of a bath before it runs out. You basically can only sit in a puddle. With some work you can sort of extend it with multiple kettles of boiling water and may get up to 1/3rd to 1/2 bath, but that's it. Still, it's manageable. Otherwise you can shower.
B) Some people like firm beds, others soft. This bed is very firm. That said, I was so tired each night I slept generally fine - just a bit sore in the morning.
C) You can't change the temperature inside (apart from opening doors / windows). I found it fine, though maybe a tad cool. Not really a problem though.
D) Only two of the three windows have blinds, and the blinds only block half the light. Don't expect darkness when the sun is shining.
Those are really the only negatives I can think of. The kitchenette is great to have.
3) The host: If you're looking for a really "active", see-you-every-day host, Soňa (pronounced like Sonya) is not for you. That said, whenever I needed anything (such as reservation extensions) or had any questions for her, she always helped out. At one point I asked for a food delivery website, and she not only provided a great one, but later followed up to make sure I was able to get food and that I enjoyed it. :) Nice person. A couple caveats:
Pre-arrival communications were kind of subpar. I wanted to rent an electric car, as I'm used to driving one, but she said no to charging it there. She seemed concerned about the cost of charging it, and even after I offered to pay for all the electricity she seemed hesitant. So I got an ICE rental instead, and due to my lack of familiarity, ended up putting diesel in the petrol tank at one point and causing €400 in damages, left stuck freezing on the side of the road in January for three hours :( That part is in no way Soňa's fault, but... it was never clear what exactly the issue was. She did really seem concerned about money - not just talking about how expensive her electricity bills are (might explain the small water heater tank... it's electric), but also seemed really in a rush to get paid on AirB&B, sending me a note asking to pay when I paid literally as fast as AirB&B gave me the option to.
Don't overinterpret this; these things are really minor. Again, I like her, and overall would consider her a great host, and would definitely recommend her place!
Another communication issue that should be mentioned was with her mother. As mentioned, at one point, I asked Soňa where I could order food delivered from local restaurants to be dropped off, as I didn't want to dine inside due to COVID, and she helped me out. So I order food and check the box for "touchless delivery" so they just leave it at your doorstep (also saves me from an awkward conversation with a driver who probably doesn't speak English). I'm watching the tracker tick down, and it says around 5 minutes left. Suddenly Soňa's mother knocks at the cottage (I'm guessing Soňa wasn't at home), kind of aggressively.
"Did you order something? There's food here for you."
"Oh, yes, thank you. I'll get it in a minute." I start putting shoes on.
(10 second pause, then more knocking, and sounding increasingly upset)
"Are you in quarantine or something? Your food is here."
"I know, I'll get it in just a moment. Thank you for letting me know."
(10 second pause, then more increasingly-upset knocking)
"This is not room service, you know! I'm not getting it for you!"
"Please, I don't want you to do anything! Please, do nothing!"
She leaves, apparently upset; I finish getting my shoes on, go out, and the delivery person has my food. I just point at the ground, and like in the instructions, they just set it down and drive off.
But then after this event, Soňa kindly thought to ask me if I was able to order something and whether it was good. So it just feels like there was some sort of communications breakdown
items.one_line: Cottage near prison; host reassures about prison break risk

items.comment_full:
Don’t be fooled by the positive reviews which are just an act of kindness in return to the very kind employees at the bar, but it is not based on the actual stay.
I stayed five days, during which a guest had his belongings stolen, good way to spend time at the police station. There is a balcony, when it is open, two rooms of eight people each are connected via the balcony, so it means that fifteen other people can come in your room to steal your stuff. The price of the dorm bed adds up to almost being the price of a private room in a regular hotel because you have to pay for your towel and for your padlock otherwise anyone has access to your belongings. There is one bathroom and one toilet for 8 people. One for 6 if the room is for 6 people. So if you wake up around 8:00 or 9:00am, you’ll have to wait 15 minutes times 7 until all the others are done with their shower. The toilet in the mixed dorm smells like a public toilet but less so in a female dorm toilet. If you want to feel exhausted during the day, sleep in this hostel’s dorms : there is no acoustic insulation in the toilet, anyone can hear another person breathing in the toilet or bathroom. I got back in the room after midnight. Even though people walked on egg shells, one guest snapped and shouted that it was 1:30AM and it was too late to make noise. I too, the night before, could hear all the people coming in and out while I slept. People can hear each other all night in one dorm room.
If you book directly with this hostel, keep in mind that they keep your card details without saying they do, you think you are giving your card details to a third party company but their computer registers your card and they can debit your card at their will as many times as they want without your signature or pin code for each time they debit the card. If the risk of being stolen from the high traffic of people was not enough, for which there is a sign warning you about it in the elevator, the host also tries to squeeze as much money as possible from the guests by charging as much as possible. Indeed, Aïcha, the mother of the host was not pleasant at all and even shouted twice that « guests should not look at the computer screen » in spite of the screen showing the price of a booking, being oriented in the guests direction, so it is hard to not see the screen.
The night before, I had to change my train ticket and after that, because it was cheap and my stuff were already here, I put an option to extend until 3:30pm, Aïcha the mother of the host, led the employee into thinking he could tell me to sit on a chair and say he would keep this option for me, I was at the reception at 3:20pm. Twice at 3:27pm, I made sure that I would be able to confirm the option and if it was okay for me to sit and let new comers behind me, do their check-in. The employee tells me that even after 3:30pm, I would be able to as no one was going to book within few minutes after my option expires. At 3:32pm, Aïcha tells her employee to say that I can no longer use my option, that it is canceled and I have to pay the last minute price online which was double the price. I looked at the employee hoping it was a joke. The employee was caught in the middle, with the host bullying the guest into paying more. But Aïcha, the host, was not joking, she wanted to squeeze more money out of her guest. This was literally tricking the guest into paying more. She texts her son boss, the host, Saad, before he arrives, to insist in making me pay the last minute high price. He sides with her and makes up some lie that I must have come at 5:25pm and that I must have been in the back of a queue and literally tells me to shut up even though I listened to the excuse he made up. First time I hear a host say these words to his guests, I never had anyone tell me those words before in any situation. Even though I paid my last night at this hostel, they gave me the impression of ripping people off, so I did not feel like staying another night even though I paid for that last night. This ripping off, on top of all the other inconveniences, I had to book an hotel elsewhere and slept the night there. Of course, there is no way to ask for your money back for the night where you did not sleep there but at another hotel.
On top of that, check-in can be tricky, in the middle of the day, Aïcha, the mother of the host was at the counter locking the main door, she said it was “for security” and focused on her phone, several guests were left locked outside with their luggages under the heavy rain upon check-in, all the other guests panicked in trying to open the door for them, she heard nothing, until we let her know that people were waiting at the door under the rain. This event happened before she tried to rip me off. She ended up opening the door after all the guests were soaking wet and their luggages as well. Guests often get stuck locked outside because they sometimes block the door and our pass won’t open the door. When this happens, they open the door manually if they notice you are at the door and your pass is not working. This can also be
items.one_line: Guest theft, fifteen people can enter your room

items.comment_full: Loft was charming and in a fantastic location, but had very thin floor and walls. We heard everything that was happening in the neighboring unit, and unfortunately, it was occupied by a large group of guys who were LOUDLY coming and going through the wee hours of the morning. The entrance to the upstairs unit is right next to the entrance of this unit, and unfortunately, one of the guys actually entered our unit late at night one evening. What was alarming was that the other unit's door code actually worked on our unit. When we asked the host about this, he initially accused us of leaving the door open, which is completely false -- we heard the beeping of the keys and the actual lock automatically unlock. An honest mistake and was eventually resolved, but it was initially very scary. Internet was a little slow, but the smart tv is extremely slow, and the only app that worked was YouTube. Otherwise, everything worked out as we had expected and was an ok experience.
items.one_line: Drunk neighbor uses wrong door code, enters unit late at night

items.comment_full:
BEWARE!
Price of the listing was great and the location was amazing. At first upon check in, the apartment looked clean and stylish, however once we settled in, we found many issues. The place was not cleaned properly. There were several cracks in the floorboards that created space for dust and dirt to build up. There were no paper towels. The TV was not working. When we messaged the host, they told us to plug it in another outlet. We obviously tried that and it didn't work, so we just left it.
BUT the biggest issue we had, which was completely unacceptable and utterly disgusting, was the fact that the apartment was full of cockroaches! The first night we noticed a couple bugs, but didn't realize what they were, so we tried to ignore them. But the second night we saw a MASSIVE 2" cockroach on our dresser. We freaked out and took a photo and sent it to the host. Unfortunately it was in the middle of the night and didn't receive a response that late, so we tried to sleep it off and forget about it. But we ended up seeing another massive one and a few others throughout the apartment. This made us feel so uncomfortable and disgusted, we just couldn't stay there any longer. They say if you see one cockroach, there are probably much more you can't see. The owners run/ operate all the apartments in the building and must have this issue with the other units as well. We decided to pack up and find a hotel at 5am, which was a major inconvenience. We went through our luggages to ensure there weren't any cockroaches tucked and hidden in our belongings.
We left the keys in the apartment and "checked out" early. By the morning, we received a response from the host who was completely in shock. They helped us get a refund, which we expected but nonetheless, we were VERY dissatisfied and upset with our stay in the apartment, and ruined our time in Valencia.
items.one_line: Two-inch cockroach on dresser, unresponsive host

items.comment_full:
My friend and I have been staying at airbnb houses for many years but this is the first time that we have been literally kicked out of the house in the middle of the night! And we haven't yet understood the reason why this happened.
My friend and I were travelling to London and I had booked this room to stay on my own. My friend had a flight the next day early in the morning and instead of letting her roam on the streets during the night, I asked her to stay with me for a few hours at the room that I had booked. She would stay for no more that 5 hours at the room, so I thought that the host would not mind.
When we arrived at the room the lady told us that she was expecting 1 person instead of two and I explained to her that my friend would leave early in the morning to catch her flight. She did not seem to bother with that, she only mentioned that she was angry because we did not inform her about the exact time of our arrival. I informed her that I would arrive in the morning but then I had some other obligations so I told her that I would be late. She also said that she has been calling me several times to my phone but got no response. I later realised that I had failed to update my mobile number on my airbnb profile so this number she was calling was out of service. It was of course my fault that I forgot to update my mobile number on airbnb, but I did not do it on purpose. This could have happened to anyone. But I don't understand why she got so upset with that. She could have informed me about it on the airbnb messenger and I would have given her my other number. Instead, she kept on calling and then she told us that she informed airbnb about it, instead of informing me!! Phones can run out of battery, or be out of signal. I don't understand why she got so upset that she could not find me. And I dont understand why she did not inform me about it so that the issue could be resolved.
So after scolding us about this incident, she let us stay in the room. 3 hours later, she all of a sudden entered our room and told us that if we wouldn't leave within 10 minutes she would call the police. We got really upset because we could not understand why she threatened us. She said that we had told her that my friend would leave within 2 hours but she didn't. What we actually told her was that my friend would leave in A FEW hours. But still, if it bothered her so much that my friend would stay in the room for almost 5 hours, then she could have asked for extra money and we would not refuse to give it to her. But she never asked us for extra money. Instead, she treated us like we were thieves or dangerous people and threatened us by saying that she would call the police if we wouldn't leave. Even while we were getting ready to leave, she refused to leave us alone in the room and she kept on following us everywhere we were going, as if she was afraid that we would steal something. We felt like criminals and really embarrassed and humiliated. If for some reason she did not trust us, she could have just refused to host us in the first place (although I don't think we gave her any reason to think that we are not trustworthy) we also offered to show the tickets of the flight to prove that there was an actual flight after a few hours. She did not cooperate for one second. But apparently she is just racist and she did not like the colour of my skin that's why she treated us like that. She literally kicked us out of the room at 12.30 am and at this time we had to find another room to stay. This was very hard because hotels don't usually let you check in at this time. She was very rude and I really think that we did not give her any reason to be so rude. My only fault was that I failed to update my mobile number. If this made her think that we are bad people then she should stop hosting people in her house because apparently she has trust issues.
items.one_line: Kicked out midnight for friend staying five hours

items.comment_full: Let me start saying that it breaks my heart to give four stars to this beautiful place. The house is fantastic, being right on the ocean is an exquisite pleasure, communication with the host is impeccable, and really whatever depends on the owners is absolutely perfect. But there is one problem. The neighbor. The first morning I woke up to a beautiful sunset accompanied by non-stop dog barking and howling. After about an hour of nonstop barking and howling (and I mean loud and nonstop, not a second of a break) I asked the neighbor if anything can be done: maybe dogs can be brought inside or on the other side of the house, maybe they should be fed, maybe they should be given a bone, a toy, or occupied otherwise. Let me mention that I was not making any noise myself: I visited alone, I had to work, so other than actually being outside, I was unnoticeable. The neighbor said, in very rude terms, that I am not local, it is his house and he and his dog can do whatever they want, and no, he is not going to make any efforts to stop it. I went in to call Jeff, and thought the incident would be quickly over, but in half an hour I learned that... the neighbor called the police. The police officer was very nice and actually explained to the neighbor that indeed there are rules against more than 10 mn(!) nonstop dog barking in a residential neighborhood and he has to take the dog in, so the police sided with me, but it still was extremely unpleasant and creepy to even talk to a police officer on my vacation. I still do not understand what the neighbor was trying to gain by calling him: I was not creating problems, I just asked if something can be done against NONSTOP barking. Anyway, the dogs were in and out for the rest of my stay, mostly in, so not so tragic but the overall experience, extreme rudeness, some creepy touches (the neighbor’s wife was filming me at some point, on MY porch - is it even legal?) do not let me give five stars. I feel very sorry for the owner, their house is beautiful and everything else is impeccable, but you might want to think again if you want to hear the waves and not the dogs (or if you want to talk to the police). Notice that this is not the first review mentioning this problem.
items.one_line: Neighbor's dogs bark nonstop, threatens guest coldly

He thought it was funny that I would be so confused.

items.comment_full:
I did not get the apartment that is in the pictures. The pictures show a beautiful, bright, clean apartment with wood wrapped windows and wood floors., and lovely decorated bedrooms. The pictures are a dream! However, the apartment I received was not this apartment at all, and not just redecorated, it was not the same apartment. In fact, I honestly thought I had walked into a neighbors apartment by accident. It looked like someone lived there and would return any moment. I messaged the host to make sure I wasn’t trespassing. He thought it was funny that I would be so confused.
I can look past this, but it got worse the more we tried to be there. It was filthy. The coffee in the coffee maker had molded, there was grease splatter on the walls, the carpet had crumbs, the couch pillows had hair and dust, the windows were so gross that the black out blinds were a blessing. The flowers on the table had rotted and were smelling. Even the hair dryer was gross. Like, this was really a shock. We had planned this luxurious stay in a luxurious area of Oslo and this place was really bad.
To top it off, we couldn’t unpack, or even try to settle in. The closets were full, the dresser was full, the fridge was full, the freezer was full, and our suitcases were on the floor of an ALL BLACK BEDROOM (def not in the pics) with a tiny low watt light that was not even enough light to see the floor. It was just an overall mess and we ended up leaving Oslo 2 days early, which was a shame.
The host was very uncooperative and would respond with laughter when I brought this to his attention. I don’t know what is going on with the other reviews, but the apartment that is advertised is not this host’s apartment. It’s grossly misrepresented and just plain false.
I have used AirBnb for years, all over the world. I know some hosts live in their Air BnB’s and that is fine. I also know that sometimes things don’t get cleaned well. All this I can deal with, but this property is advertised as a luxury rental, in a very luxurious area of Oslo. And the pictures are not of the actual apartment! How can this be allowed? For $500/night, I could have stayed at a beautiful hotel in the same area. My biggest regret is not leaving the moment we arrived. I thought I could tough it out, but it was just all so disappointing.
Just to make sure I wasn’t overreacting about the dirt, I asked a couple of my friends in Oslo to visit and tell me what they thought of the place. They were shocked and told me this is very unusual for Oslo, especially for this area. That this is not a cultural difference, this apartment was filthy. I went to make them coffee and the coffee maker was all molded inside. It was embarrassing. They were shocked and apologized that this was my first experience of Oslo.
What a shame. The only upside is that my next Air BnB host in Paris was lovely and amazing and it restored some of my trust in Air BnB. But if you don’t see a black bedroom and gray living room in the pics, Air BnB hasn’t made this host put up pics of the actual apartment. They are fantasy pics still. Just don’t stay here.
items.one_line: Wrong apartment entirely, moldy coffee and rotten flowers

items.comment: I've never complained about lodging because usually, you know what you're getting. I rented this room hoping for a nice stay the night before having to drive all the way back to KOA airport. The check-in staff was very pleasant and the waterfall was nice, however, the room was not adequate. The room smelled like urine, but it was subtle so bearable. Then about midnight, I was woken up by water leaking on my face. There was a light rain outside, the usual Hilo spray, but the majority of the room was soaked. There were 2 LARGE puddles on the floor, one of which soaked through one of my suitcases
items.comment_full: I've never complained about lodging because usually, you know what you're getting. I rented this room hoping for a nice stay the night before having to drive all the way back to KOA airport. The check-in staff was very pleasant and the waterfall was nice, however, the room was not adequate. The room smelled like urine, but it was subtle so bearable. Then about midnight, I was woken up by water leaking on my face. There was a light rain outside, the usual Hilo spray, but the majority of the room was soaked. There were 2 LARGE puddles on the floor, one of which soaked through one of my suitcases (thank god not the one with my computer in it). The leak was running down the light fixture and the fan was spraying it around the room, which is how it hit me in the face. After seeing the leak it was noticeable that they had painted over previous leaks in the past. The amount of water was impressive, to the point I started to worry that the roof may give way. We tried contacting the emergency number which required multiple attempts to reach, eventually, staff showed up. She tried to make it sound like we weren't supposed to even be kept in that room, which was very confusing for someone to be telling you at now 1 am. They moved us to a new room, which smelled much better (though still had a faint smell of smoke) but at least it was dry. When asking for a refund they refused any refund except the tax. Needless to say, I will never stay there again, and would NOT RECOMMEND this location.
items.one_line: Midnight leak drips directly on guest's face during sleep

items.comment: All in all it was an okay stay. The location is very good, the 24 hour doorman services are very good. We had issues with the water heater and they sent a technician to fix it. The neighbors were extremely annoying, complaining about our dog barking, while being super loud very late at night. Thankfully the host was understanding and ready to accept our side of the situation rather than just take the neighbors' complaints as truth. However, the bathroom faucet was leaking water, it never got replaced. There was a small table in the living room, half broken, ended up breaking on my fiancés foot
items.comment_full: All in all it was an okay stay. The location is very good, the 24 hour doorman services are very good. We had issues with the water heater and they sent a technician to fix it. The neighbors were extremely annoying, complaining about our dog barking, while being super loud very late at night. Thankfully the host was understanding and ready to accept our side of the situation rather than just take the neighbors' complaints as truth. However, the bathroom faucet was leaking water, it never got replaced. There was a small table in the living room, half broken, ended up breaking on my fiancés foot. The bathroom light broke, luckily there was a second light. The elevator broke and my fiancé and me got stuck inside. There was an ant problem all around the house, especially the kitchen. We stayed for over a month, but never got anyone sent to clean the place in the middle of the stay or get a bedsheet change. In total, a good place to stay, but not without inconveniences worth mentioning.
items.one_line: Dog complaints, broken faucet, stuck elevator, ant infestation

The listing does say it’s a shared living space and bathroom, so we knew that going in, however, the bathroom is shared between 3 other rooms, which means the one bathroom could be shared between 6 adults, IF the rooms had 2 people each, which they are meant to, but there were times over our long stay that more than 2 adults were in a room. That being said, e
items.comment_full:
Marie was great with check-in and communication responses to resolve anything she could. However, the listing details don’t seem to read as accurate as they need to be and I wanted to leave a review for others to make sure they are aware. The listing does say it’s a shared living space and bathroom, so we knew that going in, however, the bathroom is shared between 3 other rooms, which means the one bathroom could be shared between 6 adults, IF the rooms had 2 people each, which they are meant to, but there were times over our long stay that more than 2 adults were in a room. That being said, even 6 adults sharing one bathroom makes for a lot of time waiting for your turn (my husband had to resort to relieving himself in a bottle one night, which is not pleasant), as well as the uncleanliness that can’t be managed with cleaning only weekly. There are 4 rooms total (one has a private bathroom), so the small common area living space (kitchen & main room) is shared between 8+ people, depending on how many are each in a room.
The other issue that people need to be aware of is the area, especially if you’re from elsewhere and wouldn’t know neighborhoods, which we didn’t, and may not feel safe in certain areas and situations. On two different occasions during our extended stay, gunshots were heard nearby and one afternoon the block over was barricaded off by police due to gang activity that included a drive-by shooting.
Between the lack of accuracy on the shared bathroom situation that made it sound like it was shared between one other person/room, which is not the case, as well as the neighborhood/area that we did not feel safe in, I personally would not recommend this listing, nor stay here again.
items.one_line: Husband forced to pee in bottle when bathroom locked during night

My girlfriend and I aren't overly picky or demanding but these reviews seem entirely inaccurate. Lets start with the positives.  Beautiful location in the Topanga mountains and the one owner we briefly met was very friendly. Lets move on to the rest. The "Casita" is tiny. The pictures are deceiving.  The bed nearly touches three out of the four walls and sitting on the bed I could touch the 4th. I'm 6'1" and the bed is
items.comment_full: Well. I usually won't leave a review if I don't have a great time. This however I feel needs an explanation.  First of all I don't understand how this has so many great reviews. My girlfriend and I aren't overly picky or demanding but these reviews seem entirely inaccurate. Lets start with the positives.  Beautiful location in the Topanga mountains and the one owner we briefly met was very friendly. Lets move on to the rest. The "Casita" is tiny. The pictures are deceiving.  The bed nearly touches three out of the four walls and sitting on the bed I could touch the 4th. I'm 6'1" and the bed is so short that I had to stick my feet through the bed frame in order to fully stretch out. The toilet and shower are on top of each other with no separation. When you shower, which is behind a closet door and provides no privacy, everything gets wet...the toilet paper, toilet, etc...it's terribly slapped together. The room also had no trash can, but we were provided a bag which we had to sling over a chair. The accessible deck is right outside the master bedroom which makes it unusable at night and the jacuzzi operation was not explained so it was useless to us. There is no internet either. The signal was so weak none of our devices could connect. The owner mentioned he had a booster, but produced no results. The worst was that we came up here for peace and quiet. We could have looked past all this, however at 7AM both Saturday and Sunday we woke up to someone working in their garage with a skill saw right outside our window. I looked to see where the noise was coming from and I could see a garage workshop just over the fence. An overall unpleasant experience.
items.one_line: Bed so tiny guest's feet stick through frame, shower floods toilet

items.comment_full:
“If you are not happy with the place, you can go somewhere else. We are not giving refunds or anything. The people came today to do a deep cleaning...Sorry for the trash and dirty blankets.” Not recommended!!! I have traveled many places and used Airbnb’s and this by far has been the worst experience. Place looks very similar to the pictures minus the first room being a lot smaller than shown.
The place was NOT clean. The concierge ensured the placed was deep cleaned by four people earlier that day, however, I would say otherwise.The linen had stains in both rooms, the bathroom had what appeared to be pizza grease on the toilet paper, bathroom door and bedroom door. Upon arrival, I turned on the lights and there was a roach on the wall and the floor. The chest decor was missing a wheel. The couch had stains, the dishwasher had plastic forks and knives still in there. The back porch had a full bag of trash left and the chair had dirty throw covers and sheets left. The fan had thick lines of dust on them and the filters were not clean.
The back room tv did not have a remote so I used the one from the living room to operate the tv in my room.
We had to buy wash clothes since they only provided bath towels and hand towels.
Since I have never been as dissatisfied with an Airbnb, I did not know the policy to contact Airbnb about the issues I had so I told the host and he did move quickly to get the dirty throw blankets and trash off the porch but when I told him about the other issues, he told me that “If I wasnt happy with the place, I can go somewhere else.” RUDE. The lack luster customer service was not expected and I believe he realized it was rude so he changed his response to “send us pictures of your issues and the owner and you can come to a solution.”
I was more hopeful until I messaged the host the pictures and the host brushed off the situation. He told me that was not a good first impression but that NOLA was basically a swamp and the bugs are inevitable. My sister has stayed in four different Airbnbs in NOLA and has never had bugs. He said they could bring raid (and did not). He also said they could (not would) contact the cleaning company but that I could also contact them as well. Not good customer service because the place reflects you, not the cleaning company only.
There was no welcome book like most places have to welcome guests and give suggestions on places to stay and the house rules; only a stack of wrinkled up papers telling the house rules and how to use the alarm system.
Thankfully, I bring my own linen wherever I go so I used them on top of the stained sheets and when I contacted the cleaning lady, she was very caring and expressed empathy for the situation. She explained that she was not aware of the issues and came to clean what we asked the same day.
Also, the walls are PAPER THIN. I did not believe the review until I experienced it for myself. It literally sounds like the neighbors are walking in the hallways. You can hear their television and footsteps at any moment. Quiet hours start at 10pm but you are pretty much quiet all the time so there are no complaints.
My overall first time in NOLA was amazing. Would I recommend this place, NO THANKS!
items.one_line: Go somewhere else, we won't refund, roaches on walls

"Cleanliness not great" ... "Hear music all hours" ... "Hot" ... "Three flights of stairs" ... "Informed me after that there was a $30 pet fee".
I understand that people want to be nice, but posting good reviews for dishonest Hosts with dilapidated, stinking accommodations does a disservice to other AirBnB users who end up with a terrible experience, like we had.
Here is the truth about this place (and my first nega
items.comment_full:
Don't be deceived by the 4 Star ratings. READ THE REVIEWS in detail and you will find the truth about this place. "Cockroaches" ... "No Parking - Parked in for 2 hours" ... "Cleanliness not great" ... "Hear music all hours" ... "Hot" ... "Three flights of stairs" ... "Informed me after that there was a $30 pet fee".
I understand that people want to be nice, but posting good reviews for dishonest Hosts with dilapidated, stinking accommodations does a disservice to other AirBnB users who end up with a terrible experience, like we had.
Here is the truth about this place (and my first negative review of a Host/Accommodations):
1) The smell of mold and mildew (you can see spots where rain gets into the building and unit) in the unit is only out done by the stench in the entryway staircase. But you will be on the third floor, so it is going to be too hot to open any windows to get fresh air most of the year.
2) There is no elevator! You will be walking up and down three flights of stairs every time you go out.
3) There is no free parking available! There is free parking, but in this area there are so many people (probably a lot of locals) who use the building parking lot to park free while they go and drink that you are VERY unlikely to have a spot. And if you do, it is likely that someone will park in the gravel lot entryway and park you in. There is no sign for "Resident/Guest" parking only, so people clearly know they can use it whenever, and they do.
4) The loud music from the nearby bars will play until closing time, and the drunks walking around being loud can be heard, and loud vehicles "race" up and down the street out front until late into the night trying to look cool and get attention.
5) The area surrounding this building is filled with trash and vomit from the people who walk around eating and getting drunk and throwing their trash on the ground.
6) The furniture in the sitting room has no padding. You will sit down on the couch only to crack your tailbone on the sofa's wooden structure.
7) The appliances are too old and damaged to be sold in a thrift store. The refrigerator door handle is missing.
8) There is a large building being constructed across the street. We didn't have to endure that, because we were there on a Saturday night of a holiday weekend.
The place is nasty, stinky, hot, loud, and there is no place to park. Plenty of nice hotels (some are pet friendly) nearby (with parking garages). We wish we had stayed in one, but we got there too late to change our accommodations and still make it to our show at the Ryman, or we would not have stayed here.
items.one_line: Cockroaches, mold stench, three flights of stairs.

items.comment_full:
Have you ever thought you were in paradise and then you realized it could get even better? Enter Jamie and Erin’s Contemporary Condo (or as I like the call it, Jamie and Erin’s CC by the Sea or the CCSea [patent pending]).
Honestly, I am one of the hardest people to impress ESPECIALLY when it comes to housing (for I’ve had many a bad experiences in the past) however I’m happy to say this was the best vacation find I have found so far.
Long story short, I visited Hawaii for a graduation trip and I, having this curse of wanting to live like royalty, assigned myself to select the sleeping location because my friends were going to pitch a tent and call it good... no no good people reading this review, that would not do... not for me in the slightest. And thus I began hours of research, finding a location where I would feel comfortable at.
Look, let’s get straight to the point here. A condo location on the North Shore of Hawaii? Are there bugs/cockroaches?
Ive had my fair share of horror stories involving cockroaches the size of mice running across my kitchen table.. and in my clothes dryer... and on my head boards when a wake up... So... this was a real concern for me.
I’m happy to say, Jamie and Erin’s CCSea passed with flying colors.
There are NO bugs/cockroaches at the CCSea.
As long as you listen to their rules, guidelines, and suggestions, you will have nothing to worry about. (To summarize rules/guidelines/suggestions, don’t leave the kitchen a mess and take out the garbage every night)
Now back to my first impression of the place...
When we pulled up and I open the door to take a peek inside... chills... the good kind of chills. The “Everything I could have wanted and more” kind of chills.
Beautiful kitchen that’s fully stocked with all your fun little kitchen gadgets, plates, silverware, etc..
Chic living room area with a SUPER comfy couch that still has the good “new” smell to it with a crisp TV mounted on the wall.
The master bed is MASSIVE and comfortable. (Not too hot or too cold - Good quality mattress)
The bathroom... oh my... now don’t get me wrong, I shower regularly, but I’ve never been more excited to take a shower before!
I turned back towards my friends who all had their jaws dropped, eyebrows raised, and had the corners of their mouth starting to form into a gentle grin (yup, just like the movies) saying, “Okay, I’m not leaving”
And that’s the thing, I only spent 3 nights here (should have booked my entire trip here) and I already feel like it’s a second home to me.
So for any of you that are hesitant on a non-hotel vacation. Then I’m happy to say you can rest at ease if you stay at Jamie and Erin’s CCSea (patent pending). It’s very hard to please me (mom taught me what I know) and I am overjoyed to say that this place is a winner and one I would recommend it to all my friends (as well as my mother)
And there you have it. A little piece of paradise inside of paradise. Thank you Jamie and Erin for an unforgettable adventure (the good kind). I’ll come to visit again soon.
items.one_line: Royalty expectations meet North Shore reality check

items.comment_full:
I think Jonathan is a highly responsive host who cares about his property.
First and foremost, the house is right next to (as in, only a couple of steps away from) active train tracks. I’m shocked that no one has mentioned this in prior reviews; it’s a huge deal but it isn’t mentioned anywhere. I couldn’t sleep at all. The house shakes whenever a train passes and it’s very loud. I’m a light sleeper so the noise and constant movement woke me up throughout the night and it was very hard for me to even fall asleep. In addition, there’s some type of loud buzzing noise that happens every half hour; almost like a dryer buzzer; we thought perhaps it was the ice maker since it comes from the kitchen area. For a light sleeper like me, it’s very hard to sleep through.
The neighborhood is mediocre; very residential. Some run down homes and some nice houses. If you’re vacationing and looking to walk/take in sights, it’s not the most scenic place to stay but it is indeed close to the airport and just about 15 mins from the beach. So, it’s a quick drive to most places you’re trying to get to. Very convenient.
The house is probably fine to stay in for one night but we rented it for a week, vacationing for the holiday. I don’t think it’s that kind of place for a family. We found that there was no cable TV, so it’s not necessarily a place you want to sit and lounge around if you’re vacationing; no dishwasher, so you’ll be washing dishes constantly if you don’t buy paper goods.
We couldn’t use the washer during our 6-day stay, which made it impossible to wash towels and linens. The house having a washer/dryer was one of the factors that went into choosing it because we were traveling with kids. The host offered to reimburse us up to $50 for our laundry IF we provided a receipt. That’s a little crazy. First off, what laundromat provides receipts? Second, wouldn’t you just refund your guests for any amenity that you claim exists in your home if it doesn’t really exist or work? We had to go to the laundromat.
The house was not cleaned well. On the surface, it looked clean but it definitely wasn’t cleaned well. We found uneaten food in the oven, the microwave had food specs and there were hair/bath products on the shower wall. It’s not really a big deal except that the guest pays a cleaning fee for a house that wasn’t cleaned well to begin with.
We noticed that none of the windows or doors were locked when we got in the first night after being out for dinner. If safety is a concern for you, check for that type of thing.
The beds were fine. I would say that the pillows need to be replaced but overall, the bed was fine.
We’ve traveled quite a bit in 2021 and many of the hotels we’ve stayed in don’t book the room out for 24 hours after a guest leaves because they want to clean and let the room air out. So, I think it’s a bit much to turn a house over in a couple of hours after a guest leaves.
Unfortunately, the guests before us damaged the walls and broke a glass window on an interior door - so we didn’t get the house in the best condition and the hosts had no time to make repairs because we checked in a few hours after they checked out.
Overall, the house is fine but for a certain type of guest. I am a stickler for cleanliness, practicality (washer/dryer/dishwasher/extra linens/cable TV) and aesthetics (location/decor, etc). The house was just mediocre in those ways but very quaint, otherwise. However, the host is professional and responsive. If he knows your needs in advance, I think he would try to accommodate them.
items.one_line: House next to active train tracks; shaking and roaring nightly

items.comment: This apartment is lovely, nicely appointed with great dishes and cutlery, something that you don’t always find in rental apartments. It also had nice appliances and the view is beautiful. Having said that, we wouldn’t stay here again and due to no fault of the host but the location. First, you’re in the direct flight path of the airport which is no super big deal because the planes will eventually stop at night but the bigger issue is they are located right next to a park that has free range roosters and peacocks. Dozens of these loud birds. Those birds start making noise at 2 AM, not kidding.
items.comment_full:
This apartment is lovely, nicely appointed with great dishes and cutlery, something that you don’t always find in rental apartments. It also had nice appliances and the view is beautiful. Having said that, we wouldn’t stay here again and due to no fault of the host but the location. First, you’re in the direct flight path of the airport which is no super big deal because the planes will eventually stop at night but the bigger issue is they are located right next to a park that has free range roosters and peacocks. Dozens of these loud birds. Those birds start making noise at 2 AM, not kidding. What happened to waiting until sunrise Mr. rooster?! One time it started at midnight but they quieted it down and waited until 3 AM to start the full on chorus of rooster crowing and it didn’t stop. Seriously, it did not stop. Despite us having a very comfortable bed and nice linens and pillows, when these birds started it was almost impossible to sleep, they are that loud and like I said, there is nothing you can do besides kill all those roosters and peacocks which is something we thought about every single night! All joking aside, I’d rather take a chance on the downtown locations where the drunks are usually asleep by 3 AM but to be awakened every night by these birds was crazy.
Also, the picture that shows that open viewpoint is the living room with windows that open, to us it looked like a covered terrace so I didn’t want anyone else to be confused like we were. Three of the four windows did not open because they’re being replaced, no problem there, but the terrace is 3 feet wide by probably 15 or 20 feet long alongside the bedrooms so you have a completely different view which also is nice but I did think the terrace was more the picture that they showed. Another note, the “arm chair“ is a leather chair that was incredibly uncomfortable. If you’re a kid it’s probably a blast because the minute you sit in it you slide out of it but as an adult it sucked. I had to stack up those two little beanbag foot stools they have and then drop a couple pillows into the chair so that I wouldn’t slide out while watching TV. By the way, they have a great HDTV TV set up with one mounted on the living room wall and another in the other bedroom that we did not use.
Fast Wi-Fi was a plus, they will give you the code when you check in so write it down in case you forget, it would be nice to have a house manual that listed some of that stuff. Also, when we checked in and asked the gentleman about grocery stores he seemed perplexed and told me it was a residential area to which I replied that the residents have to have a store to shop in, right? We found two grocery stores 10 to 12 minutes walk from the apartment, the first one is 10 and keep on going and the next one is just around the corner. There are also public buses available depending on which direction you go from the apartment so that’s nice to not have to rely on Uber which is wonderfully cheap but it’s nice to see the city from buses and the delightful trams and a €6.95 daily pass is great (buy it in a metro station)!
We have visited Lisbon many many times and loved the city and as much as we loved the view from this gorgeous apartment it wouldn’t be for us in the future.
items.one_line: Roosters and peacocks screech starting at midnight continuously
