items.comment_full:
If you enjoy staying in a dirty teenage boy’s dorm room, in the middle of the ghetto, without security, then this is the place for you.
First off, the listing states it’s in a gated community. Brooklyn. I get it. That’s why I picked “gated” in Brooklyn. The gate is always open, morning, evening, and middle of the night. Anyone can come in whether you live there or not. Security doesn’t even care. When we got there at 1am, there were large groups congregated by the stairwell we need to use to get to the apartment. Drunk, high, belligerent individuals making your walk to the apartment uncomfortable as well as a bit terrifying because they now see where you’re staying. Thankfully, nothing happened to us, but it was uncomfortable and we did not feel safe at all.
The apartment looked like it had not been cleaned since the photographs were taken for this listing. The white furniture was all grey if dirt and grime. The couch smelled like someone let their wet dog lay on it all day. And when we went to sleep the first night, we heard a loud clinking noise, terrified someone was trying to break in, we checked around. Only to find that it was an aluminum tray with paper towels in it that fell between the wall and the bed. Who cleans like that?! Old disgusting food tray on the bed side?! No thanks.
Additionally, the fire alarm kept beeping all night because it needed battery. We messaged the host, and no response. Only after we contacted AirBnB support did we finally get a response. Her assistant came a little while later to bring a battery for the fire alarm. I think she was under the impression we were going to change it for her, and wasn’t too happy that we said the key was in the lock box, go on in and change it. So of course… the ladder she needed to use was behind our clothes in the closet. Guess what… she changed the battery. But also in the process dropped all our clothes from the hangers in the closet and put the ladder right on our clothes! Dress shirt… dresses… t-shirts… you name it. It’s almost like she purposefully dropped it all to spite the fact she had to come over on a Sunday morning to change a battery.
This leads me to my next complaint… we’re in Brooklyn, right. It’s not the safest neighborhood, right… well the lock box, the one that holds the key to your apartment, the one that we needed to leave for the assistant to go in to change the battery on the fire alarm, well that lock box slides right off the door handle. Anyone can walk away with the lockbox, and do what they want to get it opened and freely come into the apartment where all our belongings are…. WAY TO GO! Genius! I called the host to let her know “it slides right off” and her response was “just push it against the door”… yeah… that’s not going to stop ANYONE from taking the lockbox with the key located inside.
As a side note, the hosts personal items are all over the place, including hygienic care products in the shower and bathroom, food in the fridge… it was not very welcoming. Oh yeah… and you only get two towels for your whole stay. So make sure to hang them to dry somewhere before your next shower… Good luck finding a place to put all your belongings without mixing it in with her stuff. The drawers are empty for your use, but who knows when the last time those things were cleaned. The washer and dryer are shared in the building, so if you want to wash while you’re out getting to know the town, don’t plan on it.
Needless to say, we will not be staying here again.
items.one_line: Gated community gate always open, drunk groups, filthy dirty dorm aesthetic

items.comment_full: This was an interesting one - The host was extremely friendly and communicative. I think we had an impression that it was more a private suite but this location is essentially a hotel… So still a suite but the hostess didn’t have anything to do with it other than booking communications. All good, we ended up with a nice suite (I don’t think it was specifically what we booked… The front desk gave us what they had available). The suite was really spacious but dated… For example, there was no A/C (which was not fun upon entry but after the first night of leaving  windows open it aired out). The bathroom was also a bit annoying… There was just a small toilet room with a slated door next to the shower, but no real bathroom door. So privacy was not really a thing. The real kicker was the landscaping going on literally all night. We couldn’t tell if it was a chainsaw or a leaf blower but somebody was out there from 7 PM until 7AM+ It was so loud and woke us up probably 10 times.
items.one_line: Chainsaw landscaping from 7pm to 7am, no AC, slated bathroom door

The only things that we suggest future guests keep in mind are:  it is not a full kitchen.  There is a mini-fridge, microwave, and coffee pot with coffee cups but no plates or utensils but we planned on eating out for the most part so this did not ruin it for us, but had to be creative when microwaving withou
items.comment_full:
Tim's place is great! We quite enjoyed his place! It really is great to relax in and location is close to most all you could want to do while in Nashville. Uber rides were very cheap to go down to Broadway. I would recommend his place! For the price in Nashville, you cannot beat it! The only things that we suggest future guests keep in mind are: it is not a full kitchen. There is a mini-fridge, microwave, and coffee pot with coffee cups but no plates or utensils but we planned on eating out for the most part so this did not ruin it for us, but had to be creative when microwaving without paper plates. AND if you are tall, like over 6 foot, the shower wall while in the shower slants some so made it a little awkward for my fiance to take a shower standing straight up, but he lived through it and got a shower! My fiance had cut his arm before we left for this trip and on our last night there he must have scratched his cut and got blood on the sheets. This COMPLETELY stressed me out, and rinsed the sheets and mattress cover in cold water to try and save them so they did not stain but the washer and dryer are in the main living area and I could not find stain preventer/remover upstairs. I messaged Tim at a reasonable hour in the morning before we left expecting to be scolded or deal with an unhappy renter, but Tim was nothing but nice to me and wished me safe travels. Communication with Tim is immediate, pleasant, and thorough. He even offered to let us come downstairs during Tornado Warnings!
All in all people, if you are looking to stay in Nashville at a place that is nice, safe, and cost effective, check this place out!
items.one_line: Tall boyfriend couldn't shower, blood on sheets from old wound

items.comment_full:
First of all, let me preface by saying Zsolt is the nicest guy. He provided water and snacks and he really is friendly and respectful. HOWEVER, the place he is renting is seriously in need of several renovations. The door look is pretty rickety (you have to to push in the lock with your finger because it gets stuck) and the facility is very old. The bathroom sink was the smallest I've ever seen so water splashes out when you use it and it is impossible not to flood the floor when taking a shower. The shower head could definitely be upgraded - it sprays straight into the wall (and sideways) so the water floods the small space. Like others have mentioned, hot water runs out pretty quickly. It was less than five minutes for me. There was black mold in most corners of the bathroom tile and the toilet is cracked and leaks so there's a small plastic container for the water to drip into. I had to empty out this container twice during my two night stay. I found out the hard way when it spilled over after my shower. The toilet is difficult to use...you have to turn a faucet to "flush."

The black carpet is actually some sort of felt material and the sofa bed is truly uncomfortable. I drove 8+ hours and slept like a rock because I was so exhausted but the mattress is awfully thin and the wooden slates creak. I did not feel rested and my shoulders/back still hurt.

The place needs some serious upgrading. This is the first where the facility looks better in pictures than in person. I hate to give it such a low rating because the host is amiable but the place really needs some repairs and upgrades. For the price, I would have preferred to stay somewhere else. It is okay if you're on a serious budget but you won't feel rested after using the sofa bed. I think a private room would have been a better choice, or even a B&B.

Location wise, it is in a 40 minute walking distance to the central market, about 30 min to the center (district V), about 30 min to Heroe's Square. It is doable but not very convenient.

I arrived later than I anticipated, as I drove, and there were multiple accidents and some traffic. The host set his phone to not receive calls! It was nerve wracking (2300 with two toddlers) and I had to ask a kind stranger in the building if I could use his wifi. The stranger and I both tried to call the host but it was set to Do Not Disturb mode so neither of us was able to get connected. It kind of started off our interaction on the wrong foot.

Zsolt and his family are really nice. I can't stress this enough. However, the place he is renting is a bit shabby and could use some upgrades.
items.one_line: Shower floods tiny bathroom, toilet catches dripping water.

The sheets and towels were also filled with hair from what I’m assuming was previous stays, which makes me question whether or not they were even washed. They’re didn’t smell bad, but they also didn’t smell clean. I contacted the host about all of these concerns, and they told me that someone would come and do some “touch ups” as well as leave fresh towels, but I had left for the day and I wasn’t comfortable with someone just coming in w
items.comment_full:
Would I stay here again? ABSOLUTELY NOT! The host is very responsive, however, not much happens after they do respond! This place has NO HOT WATER! And NO AC! The sheets and towels were also filled with hair from what I’m assuming was previous stays, which makes me question whether or not they were even washed. They’re didn’t smell bad, but they also didn’t smell clean. I contacted the host about all of these concerns, and they told me that someone would come and do some “touch ups” as well as leave fresh towels, but I had left for the day and I wasn’t comfortable with someone just coming in with my belongings there, so I had to wait for the next day. We set an appointment for the maintenance person to come in and change the sheets, being fresh towels and check the air and hot water. Luckily I always travel with my own towels, however, I did have to sleep ok questionable sheets. I waited for almost 3 hours past the time that we had set. No one showed. I contacted the host again, they told me that they were calling the maintenance person, who apparently does “everything”. I couldn’t wait around any longer because I had meetings, so we set a time again for the next day. Again, this person hadn’t shown at our set time of 12pm. I contacted the host AGAIN! They called the maintenance person, they arrived about 30- 40 minutes later. He changed the sheet, left “fresh” towels, that were also filled with hairs! He was NOT able to fix the hot water situation NOR the ac! So, for my entire stay, I had to take the quickest and coldest showers! Also, Alhambra on average is a lot warmer than many of cities in the LA area, each day that I was there the temperature was 90 degrees, and I had no air! I decided to use the shower in the other bathroom, hoping the water would be warmer, it wasn’t! AND that shower flooded. The water backed up, I had to turn it off and get out and let the water go down, which took a while. So, stay here at your own risk.
This place COULD be great! The location is ok, it’s a quiet neighborhood, very peaceful, but very far from things to do- so if that’s what you’re looking for, this isn’t the place for you. There is parking, however it is extremely TIGHT and hard to maneuver once back there. There is a garage, but the host doesn’t give access to that, so my vehicle had to stay in the driveway where the neighbors kids were playing. Oh yeah, and the mattresses are like hard wood floors. I awoke in agony each day because they’re SO HARD! Overall, my experience was pretty bad.
items.one_line: No hot water, no AC, hair-filled sheets, no service.

I gave this place 2 stars and I think I’m being generous because the area was nice & the space was decent for 2 people but this was basically a dogs house. The couches were full of hair & I had to constantly take dog hair out of my mouth & my belongings. The bed sheets smelled like a dogs towel not to mention the outside porch, it was a beautiful view of the tress until you actually stood outside w
items.comment_full: I don’t even know where to begin. I gave this place 2 stars and I think I’m being generous because the area was nice & the space was decent for 2 people but this was basically a dogs house. As soon as I walked in the door the unbearable smell of dog waste & hair hit me. It was really really bad, I’ve had indoor dogs & my house was nothing near to this. The couches were full of hair & I had to constantly take dog hair out of my mouth & my belongings. The bed sheets smelled like a dogs towel not to mention the outside porch, it was a beautiful view of the tress until you actually stood outside where the smell was even worse! There were dog droppings everywhere! It was impossible to hang out or enjoy the fresh air because at that point there wasn’t any, you literally have to step out into the middle of the street to get away from the stench & if that’s not enough the horse gallops that wake you up bright & early will do the trick. It sounds like she’s hosting big dog races above the space. I realize that the title says dog friendly but this is more of a house for your dog rather than yourself. There’s a fine line between pet friendly & a pet taking over your home & claiming it’s territory. What was more annoying was that at the very end of the description is where she states that she has a dog sitting business in her home which unfortunately I didn’t read because everything sounded & looked like a done deal. I wish I would’ve read it all before booking, I definitely learned my lesson. I think she needs to be more clear that it’s more for a person who is okay with having people above them who make a crazy amount of noise with dogs running & barking & having dog hair, waste & smell where you eat & sleep. Truthfully this is for people who travel with pets & agree to the rest. Apart from that, she left fruit & juice in the fridge which was nice, the area is nice as well but a little far from downtown Seattle if you’re looking to explore the city. There’s a very cute beach where people walk around & play volleyball at a close distance from the house, maybe 10 minute drive, 20 minute walk or so. Loved walking in the area & there a few small restaurants around there but it was a bit of a drive for basically everything. I told the host that my flight coming in to Seattle was going to be early & my flight leaving was going to be late, she said it was fine to come early and leave late & to leave my bags at the space until my flight approached us but I ended up coming in at the regular check in time but the day it was time to check out she came to the door, almost in an upset way & disregarded my greeting & said “check out was at 12, put the linens & towels in the laundry room” & left. It was about 12:15 PM at that point, so I grabbed all my things & went to kill time since my flight wasn’t for another 10 hours. We tried not to be at the space as much as we could because the smell & hair were extremely uncomfortable & that last part made me feel unwelcome & I do NOT recommend staying here. I wish I would’ve stayed at a hotel instead but it is what it is & I hope she works on getting the space clean & sanitized because no one should have to deal with what I dealt with even if they have a dog. As a dog owner I know it’s possible to keep hair and odors to a minimum, such as putting Febreze plug-ins, having leather couches instead of cloth, lint rolling the space using bleach maybe, actually picking up the dogs droppings. Good luck to anyone who decides to stay here.
items.one_line: Dog waste everywhere, hair in mouth, horse galloping above

I would NOT recommend renting this home!!! The location of this home is beautiful and definitely a nice setup that there are 4 masters but for this caliber of home there wasn’t even the basic seasonings and no serving dishes - the kitchen had minimal supplies. The host lives upstairs  and we were all woken up by their loud stomping at 6am!! They also did not mention before our arrival that they would have several construction workers wor
items.comment_full:
***GUESTS BEWARE*** I have been a Superhost for years and own/manage 7 homes on Airbnb but I have never encountered a host (nor a guest) so hostile and rude!! I would NOT recommend renting this home!!! The location of this home is beautiful and definitely a nice setup that there are 4 masters but for this caliber of home there wasn’t even the basic seasonings and no serving dishes - the kitchen had minimal supplies. The host lives upstairs and we were all woken up by their loud stomping at 6am!! They also did not mention before our arrival that they would have several construction workers working IN and around the home all day!! Even cutting wood outdoors for hours!! That’s all I will say about the home as my biggest warning is about the hosts themselves!
As a surprise my friend booked a taco cart for us. So, being a Superhost I decided to invite the host down for lunch not knowing I was doing anything wrong by having a taco cart outdoors. He came down, never spoke one word to us before shouting at the top of his lungs that we were all kicked out of the home because of it!! We apologized immediately but we were not able to reason with him because he was constantly yelling and I don’t believe that yelling back gets anywhere so we simply remained calm and quiet. 15 min later his girlfriend/“property manager” apologized to us for his behavior and said he has agreed to let us keep the taco cart (but we already asked the company to leave).
We go down to the beach to get away from the home and they ask us to give back their beach chairs that they specifically gave us permission to use and this was the first hour we had ever used them. Didn’t make any sense to me since we were simply sitting on them not doing anything out of the ordinary and even respectfully placed them higher up on the shore so they had no risk of getting wet from high tides.
There’s absolutely no privacy - they kept walking down looking at us and walking back up to their place. Then they interrupt us again asking us to go inside because we were being too loud even though it was 4pm on a Saturday afternoon with many people around us enjoying the beach as we were trying to do. We were not playing any music just sitting on the beach talking!! We told them we only had 45 min left before we had to start getting ready for dinner so they agreed to let us stay outside if we sat on the beach in front of the neighbor’s house!!!! Even though I told them I was uncomfortable doing this they said that’s what I had to do to stay outside so we did!!!
At 6pm we had made a celebratory toast before leaving for dinner and I acknowledge that this time we were probably being too loud for indoors but it was a total of 5-10 min and they came down yelling at us again!!! If anything, they truly were disturbing their neighbors with noise throughout the day much more than we were!! They once again tell us we are all kicked out and threaten that all of our personal belongings will be on the street when we returned!! We continually tried to speak to them in a calm manner despite being yelled at but they are hot tempered and would not stop yelling at us for a moment to have a conversation so we simply walked away and left the home to avoid any further confrontation.
While at dinner they called Airbnb and cancelled our reservation and told us if we returned to the home they would call the cops. Since all of our personal belongings were there we ended up calling the Sheriffs ourselves because we needed police escort to retrieve our items especially with their constant threats and we did not want to be berated by them again. Since they didn’t answer the door, the Sheriffs went onto the property themselves and gave us access to our unit. We packed up and left and purchased hotel rooms to stay in.
Thought the nightmare was over but then come to find out from Airbnb that they had lied to them in order to cancel my reservation and said I had 50ppl over at the house for a party when I absolutely did not have over the overnight occupancy of 8ppl for the home!!
Again, I am a reputable host and if you read my reviews many guests comment on my friendliness and accommodating personality. We did not break any stated rules and we did nothing to provoke this treatment. The hosts stated it wasn’t the first time they had kicked guests out but once a reservation is cancelled you weren’t allowed to write a review about the host which is why you don’t see bad reviews on them because they cancel every guest that they have a confrontation with so guests don’t have the opportunity to write the review. I am so happy that Airbnb recently changed this rule a few weeks ago so I am able to warn any guest from renting this home even though my reservation was cancelled. I would hate for someone to go through what I had to go through and deal with these erratic and volatile hosts! Please do not take the risk and rent this home. There are many other beautiful homes to rent in Malibu!
items.one_line: Host condemns taco cart party despite being shouted invitation

She offers them but they seem old, stale, and dusty in need of a wash! Flys/bugs throughout the house. Beds uncomfortable. Best part of her home is the pool area. Ok, the first day I was greeted by Deanie who seemed welcoming and overly helpful. She seemed a bit strange and intoxicated but I ignored it. She went over things in her home with us and assured me she would NOT enter again once we checked in. The first day she seemed to be pleasant
items.comment_full: WOW! Where do I start?! My short stay at Deanie’s was truly eventful. The house: Shower is tiny and difficult to properly wash in. Bring your own towels! She offers them but they seem old, stale, and dusty in need of a wash! Flys/bugs throughout the house. Beds uncomfortable. Best part of her home is the pool area. Ok, the first day I was greeted by Deanie who seemed welcoming and overly helpful. She seemed a bit strange and intoxicated but I ignored it. She went over things in her home with us and assured me she would NOT enter again once we checked in. The first day she seemed to be pleasant and eager to get to know us. She came down to the pool area with us with her cup full of alcohol. The second day she realized the rest of my friends arrived and it sent her into a frenzy! We were all in the pool enjoying ourselves when we noticed her staring from her balcony. The tension was thick. We all began to exit the pool to shower and go on a tour. As we are getting ready she comes BANGING on the door that’s labeled “ Private laundry for owner “. It was an additional entrance that she could walk in at her convenience. She was coming for an air bed she needed for family who she claimed were coming to stay. However, she previously said she wouldn’t come in once guest checked in. She smelled of liquor again and began mumbling and cursing about how we were ruining her home because the floor was wet AFTER WE GOT OUT OF THE POOL! I would like you to know that her refrigerator was leaking lots of water on the same type of flooring during the time we were there. We had to lay towels down because the floor was so wet from the refrigerator. (Back to the story) She began yelling, slurring her words, throwing our things, and calling us out our name. At that point, we all packed our stuff to leave. The experience was horrible. Once we packed to leave, Deanie started send me harassing and discriminatory messages via Airbnb. She said her husband who is retired police warned her about us, yet we never met or saw him, so she had to tell him we were black! She also said she knew by looking at us that we were “fraudent” I’m sure she meant fraudulent but she was intoxicated. She lives on the property, so if you want privacy and you’re black please save yourself the headache! Stay elsewhere.
items.one_line: Drunk host stares menacingly from balcony during pool party

items.comment: Negative review for the host for not willing to communicate at all and broke her promise, but positive review for the apartment for its facilities and location. The host threated us with our passport’s scans which she requested before our move in. The following is the direct quote from part of her email to us: …….. “For all of that, if you don't transfer 75€ to my account today, tomorriow I will report to Airbnb and the valuation that I will do from you in the web, if you don't transfer the 75€, you can imagine how it will be...” We replied: “Although the sink was quite dirty, we had already c
items.comment_full: Negative review for the host for not willing to communicate at all and broke her promise, but positive review for the apartment for its facilities and location. The host threated us with our passport’s scans which she requested before our move in. The following is the direct quote from part of her email to us: …….. “For all of that, if you don't transfer 75€ to my account today, tomorriow I will report to Airbnb and the valuation that I will do from you in the web, if you don't transfer the 75€, you can imagine how it will be...” We replied: “Although the sink was quite dirty, we had already cleaned the hob and tableware that I have no idea how dirty they were as shown in your photographs. Being visitors but not professional cleaners, the cleaning we have done is already better than standard. Besides, penalty of €75 is not that reasonable. The extra cleaning done by you and the extra electricity fee due to the open of the freezer's door could not cost about €75. No evidence of the scratch on the floor is shown as it can be caused by other factors. Most importantly, according to the terms and privacy of Airbnb, in the event that a Host claims otherwise and provides evidence of damage, including but not limited to photographs, visitors will agree to pay the cost of replacing the damaged items with equivalent items. Therefore, we are only required to pay the damage fee equivalent to cleaning fee for the hob and freezer. The damage fee, again, is more than we can afford that we hope you can reduce the amount of penalty (i think we are only obliged to account for the sink and the freezer). Currently, we are still in Spain. Should there be any problems, we might discuss with you directly.” And she did not reply us after that and straightly reported to Airbnb. Hence, she did not give us the time as she promised which is one day. Unable to contact and unwilling for us to find her to discuss about this issue. When we were back to UK, there was nothing we can do and we thought it was what she wants to achieve. We did not expect such an unreasonable host so we did not took any photos before we left to prove but only hers. It made our situation really bad since we do not have solid prove so we decided to take those uncertainty damage but €75 is just not the price for those. To be fair to the host, it was partly truly our fault and we are willing to take those responsibility. But we hope that the host will be reasonable for the penalty and communicate with us and do not lead to this situation. However, the apartment is truly lovely to stay for 5 people, stores and supermarkets are near. Park Guell, a famous and must go attraction is just 10 minutes walk...
items.one_line: Host threatened bad review unless 75 euros paid today

items.comment: Tangerine Dream....we wanted to love it from the pics.   It wasn’t far off in terms of Ammenties although the back patio furniture wasn’t as clean as in the photos.   Also someone’s full leftover ashtray and cigarettes were left on the small table.   This became important to us because it was hard to be inside the place.   There was only one small fan, maybe 8 in’ in diameter to cool the place.   We arrived at around 2 pm and it was muggy and hot.   We tried to air the place out via the sliding glass door and front door but air could not flow very well because the large window by the bed would
items.comment_full: Tangerine Dream....we wanted to love it from the pics.   It wasn’t far off in terms of Ammenties although the back patio furniture wasn’t as clean as in the photos.   Also someone’s full leftover ashtray and cigarettes were left on the small table.   This became important to us because it was hard to be inside the place.   There was only one small fan, maybe 8 in’ in diameter to cool the place.   We arrived at around 2 pm and it was muggy and hot.   We tried to air the place out via the sliding glass door and front door but air could not flow very well because the large window by the bed would not open.   We could not get good air to flow through the place.    The host was responding from her travels abroad but our issue was never resolved.  We left the place to enjoy Venice and figured we would just come back when it cools down.   Upon our return in the early evening I felt pricks on my feet only to realize there were ants under the bed and all around the floor.  Communicated with the host and she responded but again with no resolve.   There weeds cans of horrible smelling any spray but couple that with no free flowing air??   Luckily I found a tiny bottle of lavender spray that was supposed to repel ants (after the many I had to wipe up) it solved some of the issue but then after laying on the comfortable (based on other reviews) bed - realize- ants were on the bed!!!  I was flicking ants off of my body and having to shake off the bed linens.    I tried to sleep here but we were up at 3 am due to the heat.   We couldn’t sleep with the doors open because the screens do not lock.    We spent the night trying to stick it out then just got up and out as soon as we could at 7 am the next day.    The best part of the stay was Penny the dog and access to the bikes.  I hate that I had to give a poor review but this was our true experience.   Heat and ants...life in Cali and nature - however if I was renting my unit I would gather that guests would not want to stay in a hot sauna covered in ants.    From a consumer experience - I think a more accommodating approach by the host to ensure resolution could have helped and improved our experience.    My first Airbnb experience and I truly and honestly wanted to love it.    Instead our plans were cut short as we all felt hungover from no sleep.
items.one_line: Ants under bed, no AC, old ashtray with cigarettes left inside

On arrival she greeted us
items.comment_full:
Our overall experience with Mona was extremely disappointing. We chose her apartment because it seemed to have everything we were looking for for our short stay in Toronto (great location , basic amenities, wifi, early check in of 12 pm)
However Mona messaged us the day before we were due to stay with her and asked us to come at 4.45pm instead of 12pm like it said on her listing. This completely messed up our plans and we had to rearrange our whole day . Her reason was that Air bnb is not allowed in her apartment building and she didn't want staff to see us.
On arrival she greeted us and gave us our set of keys, we asked her for the wifi code and she told us "you won't be getting any wifi" and that it didn't work. But we could go down to the coffee shop next door and use the wifi there . ( her apartment is on the 24th floor)
She could have told us this before as we were travelling and wifi was very important for making further arrangements for our trip/planning our days out etc .
She showed us to our room and told us that window was broken and didn't shut. It was hot in the room but because of all the noise from outside we wanted to shut the window but couldn't .
We went out for the evening and so did mona . When we got back ( around midnight ) we realised there were no towels for us. Mona wasn't home yet so we wrote her a note asking her to leave us some towels out and went to bed. The next morning the note was gone but still no towels. Mona was asleep so we had to shower without towels.
When she got up she told us she forgot to wash the towels , so we only got towels on the second day.
To be fair Mona's apartment is in an absolutely great location. The room is big and spacious , plenty of room for two people . But I would not recommend staying with her to a friend. Bad, unreliable host.
items.one_line: No WiFi, host said go to coffee shop on 24th floor

Her and her friends are all world renowned choreographers who choreograph for the biggest stars
items.comment_full: I’m going to start out by saying our experience was way less than enjoyable for many reasons, but me leaving injured would be number 1. A floor to ceiling framed mirror fell off the wall onto my head and back. It was hanging on by a tiny screw and not secured at all. It should’ve been earthquake proof and anchored into the wall. The mirror wasn’t touched. Now on to the review.... I rented out this house for my boss Tricia Miranda’s 40th birthday to host a dinner party with her closest 16 colleagues. Her and her friends are all world renowned choreographers who choreograph for the biggest stars in the music business. She wanted to have something nice for them to enjoy. She hired a personal chef to cook her bday dinner and there were only 8 dinner plates, 3 cups, 0 wine glasses, 0 champagne flutes and 3 forks. She ended up having to go buy paper plates, solo cups and plastic silverware to eat off of and drink out of. She was incredibly embarrassed. The Sonos music kept shutting off the entire 2 days we spent there and during the entire dinner party. We were eating in silence while the host Daniella and 1 of our guests were on the phone for over an hour trying to figure out the sound. We finally ended up using the television for sound. The first night we were unable to use the pool and hot tub as neither one was working and we were told it would be heated prior to our arrival. We constantly had to text Daniella the entire stay because they control everything on their end. Heating the pool, turning it on/off etc... The backyard lights didn’t work at all. There were multiple remotes in each room and most didn’t work, nor did we know what they were for. 3 of the shower handles in 3 different rooms fell off due to there being no screws. The master toilet clogged a few hours after arrival and there was no plunger in the house, therefore my boss was forced to go downstairs every time to use the restroom while her guests were in their rooms. Upon check in, there were 2 maids and a child still at the property cleaning the house and parked in the driveway. We woke up the first morning to a Gardner in the backyard while all of the doors were open and we were in pajamas. (We were never communicated that a gardner would be on the property during our stay. After a lot of back and forth with the host and Daniella having to show up and manually work on getting the pool up and running for us and 1 of our guests having to help her multiple times, we were finally able to get in the hot tub. As we were enjoying the hot tub, parts of it were falling off. 5 minutes later, a pool guy shows up (unannounced) and he informed us he would be shutting off the hot tub for about 10 minutes while we were sitting in it. The host NEVER communicated to us that someone was coming to put a part in for the pool. Mind you, you can only use one or the other at a time, as we were told they can only heat up one at a time. Constantly having people on the property that weren’t our guests and not being told we would have workers showing up, it made our experience completely unenjoyable and we were unable to relax during our stay. An hour prior to checking out, is when the mirror incident occurred. I was walking 1 of our guests out as you have to lock the front door with a dead bolt in order for it to close. There is no other latching mechanism. When the door opened, this is when the framed mirror fell off the wall landing on my head, neck and back and shattering. The host showed up within 5 minutes of us contacting them about the mirror. By the time my boss came downstairs to speak to them, they had already cleaned out the mirror and the debris. She then walked them through the entire house discussing all that occurred. Now although they were extremely nice and attentive, their main concern was making sure my boss still gave them a 5 star review if they reimbursed her 50%. She was told she would be receiving paperwork to get the refund started, but has yet to receive an email or phone call. I did however end up having to seek medical attention and we are still trying to clear everything up with Airbnb to get the refund and medical compensation. I DO NOT recommend renting from this host. Yes the home is beautiful, but the stay was anything but.
items.one_line: Mirror falls on head, boss's party lacks dishes, forks mysteriously vanish

items.comment_full:
This is not meant to be a bad review but an honest one.
We found the washing machine to be a large disturbance. While in the spin cycle, it emits unsafe noise levels. Louder than the planes flying over the home! The hall light needs to be switched on and off sometimes 3 or 4 times before turning on. This caused worries about a fire hazard. The dryer also scorched 3 pieces of clothing. Again, fire hazard.
At first glance the property appears clean but everything really needs a good deep cleaning. Lots of ickys under the furniture, on cabinets, stains on couches and mattresses. One family member had rashes appear each morning after using the provided bedding. However, the washer was not large enough to be able to wash the blankets. One of the drawers in the bathroom broke the first time it was opened. The wood left for the fire pit is not for cooking, has nails and has likely been treated with chemicals to protect it from the harsh elements when this type of wood is used for homes, decks etc. we were excited to grill but the grill was also a bit sketchy. Basically we ate out a ton which was not the plan considering we rented a single family home.
We were excited to have the toiletries included but we're disappointed in the quality of them. Full of chemicals and not great for the skin.
The neighbors and children in the neighborhood were very nice. As were the owners of the home we stayed in. The homeowners are very responsive. Maybe they are new at this vacation rental thing.
The TV reception was very bad. We purchased a Roku stick and then we were able to use the TV enjoyably. It was $60 and would really make others more comfortable.
Overall, we had a nice stay in the home but it did not meet our standards of cleanness. This is disappointing because we are pretty sure we paid for cleaning and the home was not clean when we arrived. I believe we paid a cleaning fee???
I would recommend the home others but think the way the property is marketed needs some realistic adjusting.
Thanks!
items.one_line: Washing machine roars louder than planes, fire hazards abound.

items.comment_full:
The property was in a perfect location for an easy travel into Venice and a protected space. Good food options around. Barbara sent clear instructions for directions and check in.
However, unfortunately during our stay, other guests were able to access our airbnb and came into our bedroom whilst we were sleeping due to being given our flat number and code by Barbara. She did not let us know that she had made the mistake even after she realised.
It was a mistake but it was a scary experience to be awoken with a man standing in our bedroom. Luckily they were friendly but it left us uneasy to leave the room knowing people had our code- there was no way to lock the space from outside meaning he could access the space while we were away.
If you are a light sleeper I would not recommend as no fault of Barbara, you can hear every movement inside and outside. 2/3 nights we were disturbed by loud talking.
We only gave 2 stars due to the intrusion, without this I would have rated it 3.5/4
items.one_line: Man woke us in bedroom, code given to wrong guests

items.comment_full:
Unfortunately this is will not be a good review. The apartment was as pictured and the neighborhood is nice, but there were far too many problems to even begin to feel comfortable.

-We stayed for 4 nights. on the third night around 7pm I heard a voice on the terrace(which is private and requires key to enter) I jumped up to find a man outside of the sliding glass door. he asked if we were checking out that night and the manager told him we had already left. Our checkout wasn't until the following morning. I was shocked and alarmed, a strange man surprising us like this felt like nothing less than breaking and entering this event also had us questioning our privacy the rest of our stay.
-No wifi for the first 2 1/2 days. I tried contacting Luis several times with delayed response and even called Tino who didn't respond at all but apparently Tino was supposed to be my main contact for any problems.
-We stayed for 4 nights and only had 2 towels. While this may not be a big deal for all travelers, it would be nice to have an extra towel.
-Several dishes were dirty and cracked.
-Coffee pot had mold in it.
-Dirty pillows. We could see the stains through the cases.
-I know electricity is touchy in Italy, but when we arrived in the afternoon the electricity was off in the apartment and no one had mentioned that. So we searched for the box and eventually restored power.
-There were trash bags on the terrace that were full. I'm not sure with what or why they were even there.
-Toilet seat was cracked.

unfortunately for us the stress of our vacation wasn't making our flight on time but rather our frustration with this apartment.
items.one_line: Strange man surprised us on private terrace, only two towels

items.comment_full:
We’ve encountered various problems in vacation rentals but we’ve never been moved to write a negative review. We always contact the owner if it’s really a problem because we assume the owner wants to know and take care of it. We don’t want to hurt anyone’s business by putting out a bad review. But the owner’s response when we contacted him inspires us to write this. Before reserving, we suggest you ask if these things have been fixed.
This apartment suffers from neglect. The owner asks that you DON’T OPEN the (dirty) windows because he “wants to prevent people from having suicidal thoughts.” He said the windows were dirty because the apartment is on the 15th floor but these windows are designed to be easily cleaned on both sides.
During a storm, wind whistles through the living room window at a high pitch. The kitchen window lets through a lot of wind.
The fan and light over the stove work intermittently.
The freezer drawers were iced over.
The shower head is mounted in a way that makes the water strike the back wall. Holding it by hand works very well and there is plenty of hot water. But holding it flooded the bathroom floor because the shower door lets water through.
You should know that the apartment is located at the rear of the high-rise, next to six sets of train tracks. The sound overrides dialog on the tv.
We enjoyed the place, nevertheless. It is warm and if needed, there is a plug in heater. The bed is very comfortable. The big screen TV is great. The dining/living room is spacious.
When we informed the owner of these things his response, “I have consistently had 5 star ratings!” was disrespectful. He offered to refund the remaining days of our stay if we chose to leave early. We chose to stay. The problems aren’t serious but should be honored and addressed.
We’ve encountered various problems in vacation rentals but we’ve never been moved to write a negative review. We always contact the owner if it’s really a problem because we assume the owner wants to know and take care of it. We don’t want to hurt anyone’s business by putting out a bad review. But the owner’s lack of concern and kindness prompts us to write this to give people fair warning. Before reserving, we suggest you ask if these things have been attended to.
items.one_line: Host forbids opening windows to prevent suicidal thoughts of guests

items.comment_full:
One word: Cockroaches. If you do not have any problems with them, you should continue reading for a full review. If you do have a problem with them, you should stop reading and go look for another place to stay.

Sooo...

Communication with Filipe was easy - he was quick to answer my questions and provide me with the information we needed to find the apartment. I did not get to meet him myself, but he sent another person to let us into the apartment and give us the show around.

The location of the apartment is really good. It is located on at charming little street within walking distance from the lovely neighbourhoods of Bairro Alto, Alfama, and Chiado and only a 5 minutes walk from the train station Cais do Sodré, which makes it easy to get there. When we visited, though, there were a street festival going in the street just outside the apartment. And since the apartment is located on the ground floor and you step directly from the street into the living room, that made it a pretty noisy visit for the person that had to sleep on the sleeping sofa in the living room (just a few meters from the partying). So if you need a good nights sleep, you should inquire before booking that no street festival is going on.

Which takes us to the apartment itself.

The livingroom was in the back of the apartment with no windows but a fan to keep the temperature down. It was no problem for us, but if you are claustrophobic, you might consider if it will be a problem for you. The sleeping couch in the living room wasn't to comfortable, but worse was a musty smell that penetrated the apartment and THE COCKROACHES. To me an apartment full of cockroaches is neither charming nor authentic just because it is located in an otherwise charming and authentic part of a beautiful and very old city. It is just grose. Unfortunately, I had overlooked the few reviews that mentioned the cockroaches, which is why I have started mine with the warning against them. Had it not been for them, the review would probably have been more positive.

Besides from the smell and the cockroaches, the apartment was fairly clean.

But again: It is hard to beat the location - and Lisbon is a very beautiful city that you should look forward to visit and explore.
items.one_line: Cockroaches. That's it. Cockroaches everywhere.

items.comment_full: This trip was full of firsts for us. First time we have had a coffee machine with no coffee pods, first time we could make breakfast while in bed, and first time seeing a toilet in the shower. The location is fantastic, very close to downtown area and must see sights, however the accommodations themselves are smaller than expected. When we arrived I had to double check the pictures because I swear it didn’t look like that when I booked it, but they are technically accurate. The neighborhood is tranquil, however I was surprised to learn that there are materials thinner than paper and that’s what they made the walls out of. The first night was quite noisy, however the remainder of the nights were quiet and without issue. I wouldn’t necessarily stay again in this particular unit or recommend to our friends, but it definitely was worth the price for our stay. The host is very responsive, and provided lots of information.
items.one_line: Coffee maker empty, toilet in shower, paper-thin walls

items.comment_full:
I would definitely avoid this listing.
As Alex gave us the tour of where we were staying, he told us that the central heating for the house was turned off for the season and to heat the place, we were supposed to leave the natural gas stovetop on overnight. By the morning, the place would be warm. He also told us we couldn’t flush toilet paper down the toilet and instead had to put used toilet paper in a bin, and carry it out to the front of the house to dispose of in the trash bin. None of this was disclosed in his house rules on the listing and only said to us in person once we arrived at the place at 10PM. Heating was listed as one of the amenities, and according to him the insane suggestion to leave an open flame gas stove on overnight is an acceptable and safe alternative (AirBNB later confirmed this is not). There was no other heating available, and this is in a basement (anything under the windows in the pictures is below ground level) where we had forecasts showing nights dropping to 6 degrees Celsius/42 degrees Fahrenheit. The worst part is he had other guests upstairs whom we presume were also told to leave the gas on for heating, leaving us all at a serious risk.
Unfortunately, given the time, we had no choice but to stay that night as we had just finished a 9-hour roadtrip to get to New York. We, of course, did not use the stove for heating and bundled up to get through a cold night. With colder nights ahead and the safety risk present, we decided to request an early checkout in the morning as this AirBNB was clearly not up to standards.
We offered to pay Alex for the night we stayed plus the prorated cleaning+service fees. Alex declined our request and did not want to negotiate and provide a suggested price that would cover his costs. He instead came by to discuss in person and record a video for his own evidence, arguing that the gas stovetop heating was safe and we just didn’t know any better. We clearly disagreed on what was safe, what was clean, etc., but I’ll note that the interaction went fine and nobody felt threatened.
I already had AirBNB on the phone to discuss a resolution. I let Alex know I was on the phone with AirBNB, we didn’t agree with his arguments, and that we were going to go through AirBNB’s proper resolution routes to get their opinion on the matter. Alex acknowledged he understood and left, and we went back to talking with the AirBNB representative.
With AirBNB we had to wait to get escalated to another department, then talk with them, gather photos/videos to send, then wait again for the final verdict. From the initial refund request to the time we got it, it took about 7 hours and we had wasted an entire day of our vacation, leaving us stranded at 10PM once again.
AirBNB agreed with us that heating was listed as an amenity but not available once we arrived, and a stovetop was absolutely not an alternative. They gave us a full refund of the trip + extra for the inconvenience. We got lucky and found a new AirBNB to stay at despite the time, and needless to say, it didn’t have any of these issues.
Definitely record your check-in and any other encounters if you do decide to book. And be prepared to have to present evidence to AirBNB for disputes, as Alex gave no room for debate. He even tried guilting us by sending us a message saying he turned down Ukrainian refugees on other platforms to accommodate us because he really loves doing business with AirBNB:
“Also is more imported thing Nicholas.... Before your reservation.. I was have some people from Ukraine from other sites wich try to book my place in this difficult time in Ukraine..., but I., welcoming you.. because Im prefer Airbnb hosting to help people.. Anyway thanks for coming to my home... Alex”
This was just rude and unnecessary, and I definitely don’t buy this. Of course, he typed it in the AirBNB direct messages while we were working through the refund request with the company.
There were other issues pertaining to the cleanliness of the place. We found more mold in the shower/bathroom ceiling than we think anyone would be comfortable with (it wasn’t just in the grout, it was on the tiles and shower floor). The hair still stuck to the ceiling from the previous tenants indicated this was likely due to the fact that none of it was wiped down before our stay. But since the whole stovetop heating thing alone was enough to warrant a refund, AirBNB didn’t comment on the mold or toilet paper situation mentioned earlier, so I don’t know what they would say about it.
All in all, we're not happy that we had to experience the issues we did, and that an obvious violation of policy had to go through the whole AirBNB resolution process to get resolved.
items.one_line: Host suggests leaving gas stove on overnight for heat

Stuart and is wife, Lara had the audacity to make a phone call to me, not only accusing me of throwing a ludacris party, which was thrown by the individuals in the airbnb next door to us, flat 9, 23-24 Great James Street, London, WC1N 3ES which dragged out to the main road and kept not just my partner and I up, but t
items.comment_full:
‘F**k off, Don’t lie to me, you are having a party in the flat. Get the f**k out. I will be calling the police. If you don’t go on video chat right now, I will be calling the police and you will be trespassing.’ This is the phone call I received from my host this evening at 18:56. Stuart and is wife, Lara had the audacity to make a phone call to me, not only accusing me of throwing a ludacris party, which was thrown by the individuals in the airbnb next door to us, flat 9, 23-24 Great James Street, London, WC1N 3ES which dragged out to the main road and kept not just my partner and I up, but the entire street. My partner firstly this morning, around 11:30, was accosted by a neighbour who approached him getting into the elevator about the party and was furious and began to raise his voice at him. The gentleman apologized after understanding that we were in flat number 10, not number 9 and that he had been dealing with too much over the past few weeks, with a previous Airbnb guest having used the flat previously and had a party which went off into the early hours of the morning and kept us all up. My partner explained to me what happened and was quite distressed as he just came back from getting me a coffee. We mentioned to the hosts that we would be having few guests (friends and family ) to enjoy an evening for my dinner party (all vegan and plastic free). We have platters, we have a few bottles of wine and we went out to get drinks for a nightcap for our guests and on our way back into the apartment the young lady living in building 25 approached both of us and asked if we were the individuals having the party again in the evening. We explained to her that she was the second person making this complaint directed towards us, however, after we explained that it was the apartment next door, she understood and apologised. Twice we were approached for matters that had nothing to do with us and then to mention.
We have tried to stay in constant communication with the hosts throughout our stay to explain all the issues that have occurred over the period that we have been in the apartment. We were given the wrong codes for the apartment initially which left us locked outside of the apartment for over 20 minutes. After getting into the apartment block and making our way up to the flat, the code for the lock box again was incorrect. Once we were able to make our way into the apartment, all the dirty linen from the previous guest was left outside the apartment door which after the struggle of getting into the apartment was absolutely absurd. Once inside the apartment we had a look around and the host let us know a few issues that were wrong with the flat, initially suggesting the front door had an electrical issue, then saying the code was different. The guest toilet had a broken door handle and continually fell off onto the floor, not to mention that the actual toilet itself was not working and that the issue was with the water running and that if we needed it, she would tell us how to turn it back on. In the process of going around the flat we noticed that the remnants of the previous guest continued to linger with NOS canisters (psychoactive drugs) being left on the lower deck, cigarette buds and a broken champagne flute being in a plant pot . We noticed a few of the decking slats on the roof deck were broken and caused harm to one of our guests in the evening while he was walking around, as he tripped and fell. Stuart informed me on saturday at 7pm via whatsapp chat that he knew about the broken desk slats, which was not mentioned anywhere before. We made mention of these amongst the other things above and to follow. While opening up the doors to the balcony, we noticed there were quite a few ants that were wandering around the apartment and crawling on the dining table which we made sure to photograph. It was mortifying to realise that this problem ran through the majority of the communal areas in the apartment which meant that my partner and I had to thoroughly clean the apartment before our guest arrived, however, the ants continued to appear all through the evening. We sent one last image to the hosts to identify all the other issues that we did not feel the need to mention such as the broken lamp with the exposed wire and the broken bed. However, after the phone and video call with Stuart and his partner Lara, I felt the need to finalise communications with them and handle the matter through Airbnb as this matter must be dealt with in a formal manner.
.After all of this, we tried to remain in constant communication with Lara and Stuart, however, after receiving such a devastating and horrific phone call which which was on loud speaker and my guest (including my parents) heard all the cursing and swearing and threats of the police being called, and being told to get out right away, was not only embarrassing but absolutely shameful. I have never been treated in such a way and after someone threatened me and asked me to put the call on video chat, which I obliged him with and showed him my guests and the entire flat form him to then apologise for being such an aggressive and impulsive individual with the most threatening behaviour. This is unacceptable and I will not stand for this as a guest on airbnb and of this host (especially after 6 years of being a very frequent airbnb guest all around the globe). I
items.one_line: Host screams obscenities, blames guest for neighbor's party

items.comment_full:
I'm trying to think of something to complain about here, and coming up short. Hmmm ... the full-length mirror on the wall wasn't quite full-length – it topped out right around my hairline. But so what – how many airbnbs even offer a full-length mirror, especially right in your room? (I've probably stayed at all 5 of them.) And the bathroom mirror more than compensates, with generous headroom and solid, balanced vanity lighting. Hmmm, also the in-room fridge had hissy fits from time to time – nothing very loud, just sort of unusual. But again, if you simply must have cellar-temperature chardonnay at your fingertips (55 degrees, say the experts at Reininger), it would behoove you to man up, bite the bullet, and turn a deaf ear, n'est ce pas? Because, again, how many airbnbs even have an in-room fridge? (I've probably stayed at all 5 of those as well.) Or if zen quiet is a priority, you might end up pulling the plug. It's "all good" (slipping into the vernacular here). Of course, these quibbles wouldn't even be worth a mention if I weren't getting paid by the word. (Actually, I weren't – I mean, who in the Dickens gets paid by the word – I just thought it sounded good, or at least better than the alternatives.)
But seriously, folks, my nit-picky bellyaching aside, Anthony's place is basically a self-contained, ground-floor studio, clean and trim, in muted tones, with quiet, solid floors, a desk and TV (not shown), and good WiFi (once you get the l33t-sp34k password straight). Anthony runs a tight ship, leaving little to chance, and is focused and consistent in communication; I suspect also, but can't prove, that he continues to refine the experience that awaits you here.
Streetwise™ note: Silas Deane was a socialite, lawyer, merchant and assemblyman in Connecticut before attending the First and Second Continental Congresses. Deane was then dispatched under "non-official cover" to Paris, secretly negotiating diplomatic ties with the French Crown, securing materiel for the Continental Army, and recruiting French officers – notably the veteran Johann de Kalb (leveraging his own undercover assignment in the colonies several years prior) and an inexperienced but idealistic nineteen-year-old, christened Marie-Joseph Paul Yves Roch Gilbert du Motier – but recognized here by his title, Marquis de La Fayette.
items.one_line: Only complaints are mirror and fridge hisses, profuse praise

We arrived home around 11:45, we go to bath, dry our hair, just the things you do when you go home. We didn't make super loud sound, laugh loudly or talk loudly. The most important thing is: she is really impolite, she come up around 1:30am when we already lying on our bed and falling asleep. We answered '1:30 something, what happened?' 'You are noisy, keep walking around in the house and turn all the light on, my little daughter need to sleep, she can't sleep whole night because of you two!' She said it in a really rude way! After she said these she went away, we even haven't have time to open the door. Her daughter crying at midnight all the time and her dog braking too, did we walk down and knock her door or yelling to her!? You won't do that even in your own country I think.We understand she is a children. I think she try to understand that why We go home late. Even if we make noice, she can come up and use a polite way to tell us, for example: Excuse me, we hear clearly downstairs for the walking and door closing, if you can walk and close the door safely, its could help us a lot because my daughter couldn't sleep now, thank you.' If she do this I will definitely apologise. The second day we stay there, she came and sad she need to send her daughter to school tomorrow so we have to check out before 9am or after 12pm but actually the time written on airbnb, it's 11am We understand so we accept 9am. The day of check out she came up and have arguments about yesterday's night again, after that she call us to leave but I was in the toilet because I felt sick. She said whatever we happened it's none of her business, also she only give us 15mins more. I read a thousand time on airbnb website, it's written that the check out time is in 11am! Forgot to say that the first day when we arrived we already wait outside the house about half an hour. The house is totally great but I think I couldn't give a good comment. We try our best to being nice but they are really impolite and rude. Everything just want you to watch out, don't ruin your holiday mood because of this, i hope...
items.one_line: Host yelled at guests at 1:30am for basic living sounds

items.comment_full:
I wrote this review with my honesty to give some sincere advice to travelers who are considering choosing Azu’s house. All comments are based on real experience without fabrication. (Related photos can be given if needed) Also you can actually find similar comments from previous reviews regarding particular issues.
Azu’s house is a fabulous place to stay, but ONLY for several types of people who：
Do Not mind being monitored: The host lives in the unit connected to the house with only a wall separated.(You never know this until you check in) She can see everything you are doing in the house through the windows, unless every time she passes by the windows with eyes closed. She literally speculated what you were doing based on what she heard behind the wall. When I washed the pots for cooking (because most of pots and dishes in the cupboard were not clean with oil sticked on them), the rattling sound caused by pots hitting the sink made the host ask me if I was cutting a horse in her house. (Come on, cutting open a horse is gross)
Enjoy appreciating a good looking kitchen without actually using it: Because the stove knobs were all stuck by unknown stuff or broken. When I asked for help for having trouble turning on the stoves, the host accused me of damaging the stoves.(That was the first time I tried to cook, just a few hours hours after I checked in) Of course, if you do use the kitchen to cook, even if afterwards you clean up everything, she would still charge you extra fee for cooking, and of course, “ruining” her house. (not too much, just like…Ummm… a hundred bucks)
Do Not have a requirement for cleanness: The towels and the rug in the bathroom were mostly dirty with colored patches. I took some photos of them after being accused of damaging the stove knobs just in case being accused again.
Have hearing disabilities: Therefore, you do not need to bear the continual dog barks of neighbours’ and Azu’s cute dogs from early mornings to late nights.
I understand Azu treasures her house, so I tried my best to live in with care and make it look like the same as I checked in. But as she registered it as airbnb for earning money, she should be more comfortable with people’s normal use and utility consumption. Kitchen is for cooking. Sink is for washing. Table is for eating. Every time after I used the kitchen, I cleaned up all the dishes, bowls, forks, and pots. Before I left, I knelt on the floor to wipe it with paper towels because there were no mops nor rags. (I treated Azu’s house with even more care than treating my own place)
I hope Azu can be more clear about stating her house rules in the profile. Because before I booked it, under the housing rule, there was only one rule saying “No shoes in the house.” But after I booked it, after the time limit for refund, and just half an hour before I checked in, she sent me a whole bunch of rules to follow, like respect her personal belongs which scattered around the house and no extra guests or so. On Friday night, I invited my friends to the house to have dinner together for the celebration of Lunar New Year. My friends stayed in the house for only a couple hours just for cooking and eating the dumplings we made, and no extra guests stayed overnight. Also no loud music was played. Before everyone had the dinner, we first shared our dishes with our host, Azu, thanking her for hosting us and apologizing for any inconvenience we might make. Before midnight, we finished all the washing and the cleaning. My last friend left the house at 00:14. For the following days, I made no noise ( I didn’t really talk in the house) cause there was always someone hearing behind the wall.
To better clean the dishes (and also the ones in the sink were already mouldy) , I bought a new dish towel. Since there were no rags or mops to clean the floor, I bought a bunch of paper towels and left the rest of them in Azu’s house.
I did not request money for buying daily supplies that the house lacked.
I did not complain for the unclean towels in the bathroom.
I did not grumble the offence of personal privacy.
But I was fined hundred bucks for having one meal with friends in Azu’s house, even though I carefully cleaned up everything.
Scroll down the reviews, and you will find out that I am not the first one nor the only guest being squeezed money from.
items.one_line: Host monitors through wall, questions horse-washing pot sounds

items.comment_full: The hut itself is charming amongst the tropical plants. The walls are half glass, half netting, which also means there is essentially NO privacy. There should be blinds because the place is completely transparent. The listing mentions a backyard, but it’s more of a front yard, right on a drive, used by the old man living on property, not too “private.” The patio of the bnb is about 20 ft from this driveway and the main house is a stones throw up the drive. In fact, you can see his house from everywhere in the hut, including the shower…the shower is outside but the foliage is not thick enough to provide 100% coverage on the side facing his house. You can even see his house from the toilet, where a thin piece of nylon acts as a curtain on the top half, but why not use wood to cover that entire wall? I found it odd that part of the bathroom was exposed, since the toilet is technically outside too / doesn’t need a piece of wall missing for ventilation purposes. The main house is 2 floors, and it’s position up the hill almost feels like it’s purpose is to overlook the bnb that does not have blinds / curtains. The host Amalia and Kyle are not the people greeting you. Instead it is this older man, he rolled up in his truck, while we were changing, rendering us naked in this see-through hut. He said we could text him instead of messaging through the Airbnb app, but note there is almost no cell reception at the location. We tried using the Airbnb app months in advance of our stay to extend our reservation by a couple days, but never received a response from the host (not sure who manages the online account, if it’s the man or the couple in the Airbnb profile pic). One of the first things the man said to us was “you’re here for a while,” as if we were imposing (perhaps why he didn’t want to extend our stay even though the calendar showed they had availability?). He then went on to list all the house rules, that are printed out in a binder as well. He mentioned that since they are off the grid, he doesn’t allow curling irons, blow dryers, etc. and doesn’t have enough power to heat to the shower after a certain time of night (aka no hot showers after 8pm). The amenities include a “lockbox,” but I did not see such, and the hut itself does not have locks on the doors. As other reviews mentioned, the bed is NOT in the least bit comfortable and slightly smelled of mildew. The extra blankets smelled like they hadn’t been washed in months. We did vacation in off-season, so understand there is more rain this time of year. Given the location on east side of island, rain is constant so humidity is expected, but wish bedding smelled of linen. When it was really stormy, the rain came down on the roof pretty hard, so loud. The road to the bnb is a really beautiful drive. The unpaved stretch doesn’t need 4W or high clearance vehicle, flat / easy, minimal rocks, no potholes. It’s about an hour to Hilo or Volcano NP. Great bird watching from the patio! The coqui frogs take some getting used to at night. The roosters cocka doodle doo whenever they want night & morn.
items.one_line: Glass hut has zero privacy, shower visible from neighbor

items.comment:
The apt is undoubtedly beautifully and unusually decorated and furnished but it doesn’t look so good from inside the elevator with really angry Argentinians yelling at you because the alarm bell is beeping non stop and the lock into the apt is jammed
Eventually we gave up and decided to enter through the fire escape in the dark.
Sebastian will tell you we were shown how to unlock the door to the fire escape but it took the extremely helpful young woman ages to unlock and show us, and I remember saying ‘I hope there won’t be a fire in here. I’d hate to struggle like this to get out!’
items.comment_full:
The apt is undoubtedly beautifully and unusually decorated and furnished but it doesn’t look so good from inside the elevator with really angry Argentinians yelling at you because the alarm bell is beeping non stop and the lock into the apt is jammed
Eventually we gave up and decided to enter through the fire escape in the dark.
Sebastian will tell you we were shown how to unlock the door to the fire escape but it took the extremely helpful young woman ages to unlock and show us, and I remember saying ‘I hope there won’t be a fire in here. I’d hate to struggle like this to get out!’
She later found a small piece of a broken key in the lock which took till midnight to fix, which is why we had so much trouble getting it unlocked in the dark.
Sebastian offered me the cleaning money returned as I sent him the picture of the shaving hairs in the sink upon our arrival. That’s not to mention that in the other bathroom there wasn’t any toilet paper. He will now tell you this has never happened before which is why he offered us the cleaning money back. I didn’t accept this as I felt we needed more!
We arrived at 3.0 and went out immediately and returned to the apt about 8.30 after having tea with friends (no alcohol!) to change before we were due out for dinner! We never made it out as the young lady their caretaker, took till mid night to fix both the lift entrance and the fire escape door. Let me explain. There is a gate to open on arrival at the third floor. The then is a sliding thick metal door to open. Once those two are open the alarm starts to beep loudly. Then there is are two painted metal doors that has a lock and is the entry into the apt and is painted by them. This is the lock that was jammed. Obviously we had to try to unlock it and enter.
We phoned the caretaker and she came after about three quarters of an hour, by which time we had, with difficulty and in the dark entered through the fire escape door three flights up.
She was great and took till midnight to correct both locks
Day two was ruined as we were both mentally and physically exhausted from the stress of being stuck in the elevator, and having to enter by the fire escape up three flights in the dark. It was a huge trauma for us!
The lock on the front door of the apt was easily rectified with oil in the lock. That cannot be the job of building maintenance, as Sebastian maintained.
The security of the whole building is not good. The main door was rarely shut tight and as for the lock on the apt door, we were advised by the caretaker it was better not to lock it.
Therefore on top of everything the apt is not secure enough.
But it’s pretty and we gasped with delight on entering at 3.0pm on our first day. That all changed a few hours later!
I believe we should have had our money returned for the first night. Make of this what you will.
We have rented through Airbnb many many times including several in BA but also in Cambodia Thailand Vietnam Barbados Mexico and have never ever had a problem previously!
We have never requested money back in the past. This was a truly shocking situation for us. We have received no compensation from them.
items.one_line: Broken key, angry Argentinians, fire escape escape plan

items.comment_full: Let me preface this review with saying that I never leave a bad review, I'm easily satisfied, I swear I'm so low maintenance, I don't need a lot and I respect the effort people put in being hospitable. I also want to say that I never go out of my way to leave a bad review, in fact anytime I read one for a restaurant or any other place I (perhaps unfairly) judge people for being so picky, for service being slow, but I've been in the service industry and I get it so I don't complain. There are bad days. For this case, Antonio may have been having a really bad day because homeboy has not 1 bad review. So this was a fun* surprise. Maybe he got annoyed that I accidentally called him Rodrigo, a previous host's name, but anyway - his house is very clean and just as is portrayed in the photos. Except for some reason only one of the rooms was prepped with sheets for the bed, I was staying there with my mom and although we don't ever mind sharing a bed, I did rent a space with two rooms for each of us to have our own bed. I slept in the second room without bedsheets, no problem, I slept well. All communication up until our arrival was absolutely polite. However when we arrived, which was almost an hour earlier - I tried to let him know but my phone had bad signal - he made a big deal of us being there early because Gasp! -what would have happened if he wasn't there?! And I thought "um, well we would've waited, it's not that serious." And he kept going saying, "but like, really, what if I didn't live here?" And I was so surprised by how unwelcoming this was and I just thought, well you were here so why even mention it? Like, I can talk to you about, what if I could teleport so then I could just teleport to visit Màlaga and then teleport home and I wouldn't have to rent your place. But that's not happening so why mention it? We had also arrived with my brother in tow who was dropping us off before he left for the airport, which I had mentioned in my email to Antonio, but when his wife saw three of us she made a big deal of only preparing the house for two guests (which again meant she only put sheets on one bed, what?) That also annoyed me because it was very unwelcoming. At that point Antonio had already had so much to say about us being there early and I had just kept my mouth shut because it wasn't worth it for me but when his wife made it a point to let us know that, she made me feel like we were a huge inconvenience so I let her know that she was incorrect, we were only two guests, my...
items.one_line: Unprepared room and perhaps mistaken host name offended him

items.comment:
