items.comment_full:
Do not stay here. You will not sleep.
This was only my second Airbnb. I stayed at another on Soi 13, about 200 meters away. It was a fabulous experience, but I was unable to extend my stay because someone else had rented it. This property was an absolute nightmare.
I heard from both owners, Pascal and Berry. I was told someone named Fern (I will not grant her the honorific) would meet me. She didn't. Berry said most Airbnbs have self check in, but this wasn't other Airbnbs. It was theirs, and I was told someone would meet me, but none of the three could be bothered.
I was told to go to the apartment, where the cleaners (!!) would meet me. To get to the apartment you walk down a long hallway, go past the first security desk, go around the corner and take the elevator to the 14th floor. You exit the elevator, go up two short sets of stairs past the second security desk, go down another hallway, then go through glass doors into an office for... something. At the third security desk you need a badge to open the doors. I did not have a badge, because no one had met me. The security guard let me through. Here I should note that there are signs up reminding residents that renting apartments weekly or monthly is illegal. Thankfully none of the security guards care.
After the guard opened the door for me, I then took another elevator up one floor. From there, you walk around the corner to the apartment. There were two cleaners there, lovely ladies, but they speak no English and I speak no Thai. The door doesn't use a key, like all the other apartments in the building. It uses a key card, which requires a code. That took a couple of tries. And so, no one was there to guide me through a process that was like something out of a John le Carre novel. Berry told me it was all in the book-lengthy email she had sent me. But I didn't have the email handy because, again, someone was supposed to have been there to greet me.
Once inside I got a sick feeling in my stomach. The reviews said the place was old, but it's dingy and shabby and depressing, like the rest of the building. It reminded me of a hostel I stayed in while in Moscow, which was in a repurposed brutalist apartment building from the Soviet era. I don't know what the cleaners actually did. Clean the floors? Who knows. There were grease stains on the kitchen table and all shelves were dusty. The sofa is covered in cloth in a garish giraffe-skin print, and it was covered in dust. I wouldn't even sit on it unless I used a towel, and usually I sat in one of the kitchen chairs. Has the sofa ever been cleaned? I doubt it. So immediately upon moving in I had to clean the place before unpacking.
One thing I filtered for when searching for an apartment was a washing machine. I prefer to do my own laundry. The apartment does not have a washing machine, despite what it says in the advertisement. I messaged Berry about this, and she was quite rude. You see, there are laundry shops in the building where you can PAY them to wash your clothes. You know, just like everywhere else in the world. This is not a "lost in translation" misunderstanding. It's a deliberate lie. Pascal quickly responded to offer me a voucher to do laundry, which would indicate that he's had this problem before and chose to let it continue. It's the equivalent of saying the place has a pool, but you have to go to another building and pay to use it.
The duvet is too small for the bed, so I had to sleep with it at a diagonal to keep my feet from poking out. and isn't really a duvet. The inside is sort of a synthetic blanket. Not comfortable at all.
There is a construction site next door, and I mean literally 10 meters away across the alley. They start at 7 am and would sometimes put out lights so they could continue until after 9 pm. At that point the music on Soi 11 is roaring. I'm pretty sure the main offender is a club called Hemingway's, but now that covid restrictions are slipping away it would be loud enough to rattle the windows at 1 am. (You'd think the local businesses, apartment complexes, hotels, etc. would join forces to do something about this.) And then there's the bird. I read reviews about the bird. But I grew up in a rural community, and I fondly remember waking up to birdsong. I'm an avid hiker and camper. The Ambassador Hotel has a "Bird Oasis," basically a jail cell the size of a classroom where they keep a bird that is so loud it is quite literally unbelievable. It sounds like something out of Jurassic Park. I could hear it over the vacuum cleaner. I could hear it in the shower. And I could hear it when it started squawking at 7 am. If you stay here you will not get a minute's peace. It is nonstop cacophony.
I used the noise as an excuse to get away for a few days. I went to Pattaya. I came back to the pterodactyl waking me up. I went to Phuket. I came back to more sleepless nights. I realized I was paying for an apartment but was using it for luggage storage, which was silly. I was able to move back into the previous Airbnb, where it is as quiet as a tomb. Let me be clear about this: It was worth losing about $500 in rental fees to NOT stay in this apartment.
items.one_line: No check-in, three security desks, cleaning staff confusion, absolute nightmare

items.comment_full:
Hey guys, I really hope you read this. For your vacations sake please avoid this place. For your money‘s sake for your health I beg you don’t book here. I wish I could insert all the photos I took of the rooms. I got my money back from airbnb but just don’t do this to yourself. We complained to our host about the mold in the bathroom and they tried to convince us that it isn’t mold, it’s cement, yeah sure. We were only using 2 of the 3 beds, the ones that didn’t have that many broken wood pieces under the mattress. The information in the page is not correct: it says it has a fire alarm, which it didn’t. We didn’t have more than one roll of toilet paper, we didn’t have working electricity next to the beds, we weren’t listened to we really felt unsafe. I cannot describe how it felt to be talked to in that way, after we described our issues to the host. They first said we won’t get a refund, but they can give us a vip airport shuttle drive.?!?Then they told us that if we aren’t fine with the room and the offer they’ll kick us out, so we chose to stay, because it was only 2 nights left to our trip and we obviously didn’t want to be homeless for a night. (Even the offer of dropping us off with a vip shuttle was a scam, as the guy on the hosts photo came to drop us of, so yeah that would have been the vip shuttle lol 🤣 )I never had any stranger talk to me like that even though we tried to be cooperative. For instance the morning after the complaint we wanted to head out, and one of the staff came to our room, and said he’ll let people in to work on the issues, we didn’t want that as everything was in our room and we couldn’t trust people there Anymore. And what did you want to do about the mold? Put chemicals on them DURING our stay to make it more dangerous ? Why not before ? Why did the guests before us not need everything fixed but now that we complained you want to do something about it ? That’s not fair. It should have all been fixed before. Also the fact that nobody was surprised when we talked about the issues. We talked about the bed consisting of broken pieces of wood and nobody was like: what??? Let me see! I guess they were just well aware..
Anyhow, their answer to this message will be interesting to read :/ i‘m Really sorry for everyone who stayed here I hope this is not the only experience you had with Turkish people I swear in all the years I come here never has someone been so rude to me, Turkish people are not at all like the people of this airbnb! Please I beg you, do not book this place. If you need any photos, info’s or anything about my trip there or have more questions, find my name on any social media account I’ll provide you with good Airbnb’s with „normal“ hosts..
items.one_line: Mold denial, broken beds, VIP shuttle instead of refund

items.comment_full:
*WARNING! Please read / After having exceptional experiences staying at countless Airbnb properties as well as renting out our own property when we vacation, we finally experienced total upset at Sara’s place. It should be noted, this is our first poor review of a stay and it is definitely not easy to write this. I will start with the major and very concerning issue about our stay. After arriving in the apartment my husband sat down in a dining room chair. He immediately fell through the seat after it collapsed beneath him. He is not heavy (75kg). We soon discovered (after removing the slip cover), that the chairs are old and in poor shape. We notified Sara by text message of the incident / broken chair, but did not have a tone of complaint. Shortly after the chair incident, my ten year old daughter and I opened the armoire doors while looking for a pillow. The door I went to open, opened easily. But when my daughter opened her door, the handle immediately pulled out of the door. It clearly was already broken as it pulled out instantly and easily. But we didn’t think it was such a big deal. The armoire is old and not in perfect shape and we figured it’s part of the charm of an old apartment. It really didn’t bother us. I did not want to bring it up with Sara as I didn’t want to come off as a complainer. I assumed she already knew about it. We left the apartment and followed all instructions for check out and left the apartment tidy. We were also sure to turn off the air conditioner (we both checked this was done!) WARNING....What happened next is every Airbnb guest’s worst nightmare. After our departure it was reported by the owner Sara that we had caused damage to her apartment and would owe her $450! She also messaged us to say we left the air conditioner on (definitely not true) as well as a light on. All of this was totally shocking. We later realized the entire event felt like a set up....broken chair covered in a slip cover, metal door pull loosely inserted in armoire door. ...we were totally being taken advantage of and it felt awful. I did mention in my reply to Sara that a light may have been left on and for that I apologized. We truly couldn’t believe the accusations as they were incomprehensible. This event has really been a huge disappointment but we won’t let it ruin our memories of our time in Rome, a city we love. Please play it safe and choose one of the countless other properties in the neighbourhood. This one is sadly not worth the risk.
During our stay there were other issues that came up, however because we had such a short stay, we just tried to move on and enjoy our time in Rome. Normally I wouldn’t share them all, but because of our sad experience, we feel it’s best all potential guests are well informed. Here is a further summary of more minor issues with the apartment:
-The apartment is dark. It is not nearly as bright as shown in the photos.
-The air conditioner is old and not very strong. It is difficult to work. There is only one and it was located in the bedroom and does not cool the entire apartment. Therefore if anyone is sleeping on the sofa bed in the summer (it was over 35C during our stay), they will be hot at night. As well, the guests in the bedroom won’t have privacy as the bedroom door always needs to be kept open to cool the apartment.
- A lot of furniture is old and in rough shape. The newer furniture feels cheap. A lot of the furniture does not feel very clean.
-Bedding is not what you would expect from a plus property (polyester covers).
-Bathroom is old and dated.
-The building hallway (staircase) is almost pitch black at night and can only be lit once you reach the apartment door upstairs.
-Sara’s fill in host (person who initially opens the apartment) when she is away does not speak any English and therefore could not relay any pertinent information. She was not waiting for us outside the building when we arrived (on time), but rather was inside the suite and we had no idea which suite she was in. We waited for half an hour and then entered the building when another random person unlocked it and proceeded to locate the unit without any information. It was a difficult check in process.
-Laundry soap is not provided (we searched everywhere).
-The apartment smells musty.
And now for a few positives:
-The mattress in the bedroom is comfortable.
-The neighbourhood is authentic and lively in the evenings.
-Sara replied to texts well during the booking process
items.one_line: Dining chair collapses, armoire handle yanks out, multiple furniture failures

items.comment_full:
THIS IS A TRAP HOUSE
When we checked in he talked our heads off for about 45 minutes or more about how clean and pretty our dog was whom was allowed to sleep in his garage even tho his place isn’t “pet friendly” (literally the best thing about my experience)
The property was listed as a private room and bathroom but SURPRISE the bathroom is actually shared and the door doesn’t lock. So make sure the light is on or the water is running so someone knows you’re there.
In the photos you can’t tell that the bed is an air mattress but ROOM B has an AIR MATTRESS so don’t expect good sleep if you’re over 30!
Also, host is really inconsiderate! Like I get it, I searched this app asking to sleep in your home! However, I do expect people to act as if they have house guest!
During the first night my wife and I were forced to listen to loud base filled rap music all night until she got so fed up she decided to turn the music off around 2 or 3am just so we could get a few hours of rest. I know what you’re thinking. Where was the host during all of this? On the couch in a drunken slumber after playing dominos and loud talking all night with outside house guest! (Again it’s his house so he’s free to do what he wants but the music and bass made it very uncomfortable).
During the second night of our awful stay I heard my dog barking like crazy (whom by this point was put into our truck due to her anxiety) so as I opened the front door to figure out why my dog was going crazy there was another couple looking to check into their room. Turns out they had been ringing the door bell and calling him for more than 15 minutes. My room was in the front of the home and even I couldn’t hear the door bell over the music.
Again you might be asking , where was the host? Yeah, he was again on the couch in a drunken sound asleep with rap music blaring! Host wouldn’t even wake up after several aggressive attempts to wake him. So I let the couple in, turned off the music, and forced myself back to sleep. (Wouldn’t be surprised if he’s got a few more bad reviews on the way for the other room he’s listing).
Needless to say check out was at 12pm but we left at day break.
So if you likes to be’s in the trap this spot is definitely for you!
NEVER STAYING HERE AGAIN!
items.one_line: Trap house features air mattress and unlocking bathroom door

The bathroom also had clumps of hair on the floor! So disgusting!  The microwave had crusted food in it. The dishes were dirty. The air conditioner was filthy and the clean filter light was on. There were yellow stains on the pillow. The whole room and all the bedding reeked of air freshener, it was nauseating! There were two dead cockroaches in the room and a used condom wrapper under the bed. And the sleeper sofa's mattress was so thin and uncomf
items.comment_full: We would never stay here again! When we checked in there was standing water in the bathroom(from a leaking toilet) which caused a very musty smell. The bathroom also had clumps of hair on the floor! So disgusting!  The microwave had crusted food in it. The dishes were dirty. The air conditioner was filthy and the clean filter light was on. There were yellow stains on the pillow. The whole room and all the bedding reeked of air freshener, it was nauseating! There were two dead cockroaches in the room and a used condom wrapper under the bed. And the sleeper sofa's mattress was so thin and uncomfortable that my girls could not sleep on it. The first night my 12 year slept on the couch not the pull out bed and my 6 year had to sleep in the bed with my husband and I which made for a very crowded bed! The next day we complained to management and requested that we check out and be refunded our remaining 4 nights but they told us they would only refund us if the room was rented for those nights by someone else. We were not willing to pay for two hotels so we decided to just tough it out. Management brought up a fold up futon mat so one child slept on that and one slept on the couch for the rest of our stay. Management also sent up someone to fix the toilet which they did so at least we didn't have to deal with a leaky bath. Management also said they sent up housecleaning to clean the room again but they did not clean the microwave or all the hair from the bathroom floor or the bugs or the used condo from under the bed( which we found at the end of our stay as I was checking under the bed for any belongings). I understand that it's an old building but that's no excuse for a dirty room. I also do not think this room is acceptable for 4 people unless they replace the pullout couch with a much better mattress. The hotel pool had poop in it when we went the one and only time to swim. When maintance cleaned it out they only told us to stay out of the pool for 3 hours and did not inform the rest of the guest lounging around the pool at the time. We went around and told them about the poop in the pool. The hotel also did not post a sign telling people not to swim. On the plus side the hotel is right in downtown Waikiki so walkable to everything. However we will never stay here again. We definitely would have paid more for a nicer place and enjoyed our stay more.
items.one_line: Dead cockroaches, condom wrappers, and refund denied catastrophe

It should be illegal to charge a cleaning fee when the cleaner obviously just st
items.comment_full: The communication from Laura was non existent (genuinely). The cleanliness of the flat was a let down, from the disgusting bedsheets, pillows and mattress to the floors and shower. The listing was inaccurate as we didn’t get a ‘choice’ of pillows, instead all we got was 2 sweat-stained, sacks of long grain rice, atop a mattress from an episode of criminal minds. The duvet cover had obviously been the nearest thing to hand when a previous traveller had a dodgy stomach, as it had big brown stains all over the place. It should be illegal to charge a cleaning fee when the cleaner obviously just steps in for 5 minutes, shakes up the bed sheets and leaves again. In this flat I actually found myself unironically asking  my girlfriend if she had shat her pants because the smell in the flat was so foul, but it wasn’t her, it was emanating from the bathroom before either of us had even had a chance to sit on the toilet. The street parking was abysmal and we soon received a note telling us not to park outside. Be prepared to bring bicycles or really comfy walking shoes for everyone as we had to park on another street, 15 minutes away. The listing says it’s a residential area, and asks us to be respectful and keep the noise down for the neighbours, so I obviously expected the same courtesy in return, but late at night and early in the morning we were woken by the iron giant stomping on the floor above us. I thought all the anvils in the world had already been melted down for other purposes, but I must be wrong! Because apparently they’re all being stored - and regularly dropped all over the place - in the flat above this Airbnb! If you’re wanting to watch the TV and relax think again! No blinds or curtains means you can be watched 24/7 as you watch some crappy Netflix movie to try and distract yourself from the rancid smell coming from the bathroom. The TV stand looks like it was built as a 4th year woodwork student project and made it the night before it was due. As well as the nightstands next to the bed being held on by the spiderwebs that have formed in the crevices. We did end up spending our own money (£26) to replace the dodgy yellow sheets. If you’re thinking about having a coffee, make sure and bring a pickaxe for the sugar and coffee as you won’t get any from the tubs otherwise. Please bring shower gel and shampoo as the listing states that these are provided, when in fact all that was provided is hand soap! If you’re too hot then tough. You’re not allowed to touch the thermostat, and it doesn’t make a difference even if you do, so make sure and crank the windows wide open and prepare to have the worst sleep ever, waking up every 10 minutes to strange noises outside and constantly wondering if the curtain is just blowing or if someone is climbing in the window. If you’re a barber then you’ll be used to seeing hair everywhere, however I am not, so I’m not too sure why it was on the sheets, pillows, shower and sink! The WIFI is that fast that your phone can’t even find it, so if you want to connect to any internet then bring your own router! The only positive thing was getting away from the flat.
items.one_line: Sweat-stained rice pillows, brown duvet stains, unbearable bathroom

items.comment_full:
This is not a room, but an extremely small space(The space was far too small to comfortably open a suitcase ) made only with a curtain.
The curtain is not thick and even allows feet to be seen.
When I heard about this in advance, I assumed the space would be located in a corner of the house, somewhere with minimal daily noise and a reasonable level of privacy.
However, this space is actually located right next to the living room and kitchen.
Many people live in this house, and my space is in a high-traffic area.
I can hear even the smallest noises, and cooking smells come in immediately.
On the first night, I went to bed early at 9 p.m., but without exaggeration, I woke up around 20 times due to noise.
The next day was relatively quieter and I managed to fall asleep, but at 6 a.m. I was woken up by someone cooking, talking, and eating.
I am not a sensitive person. When I first saw this space, I honestly just found it ridiculous.
But now, I am angry.
items.one_line: Curtain room woke guest twenty times on first night

items.comment_full: Adorable on the inside - we can see why they named it The Cottage. Amanda and Austin were nice and let us check in early the first day so our daughter could have lunch and a nap. The neighborhood, however, is not somewhere we felt comfortable staying with a 4 year old. My husband even contemplated asking if we could get a refund and stay somewhere else. It is in the industrial section of Nashville. There are factories and the train depot within a quarter mile. A lot of run-down houses mixed in with a few that have been renovated. The neighbors, directly across the street, got into a legit fist fight as we were pulling in one afternoon about 2:00. They were straight up punching each other, yelling cuss words, dogs were barking,  police came. Quite a scene! Our daughter kept questioning what was going on, but we just went straight into the house as quickly as possible. Each time we came and went, we went quickly from the car to the front door and right inside. Did the same thing when  we were leaving. The house is beautifully decorated inside, but the neighborhood is definitely not somewhere that we would feel comfortable staying at again. We just need to do better research on Google maps or such before making a reservation in the future.
items.one_line: Neighbors punch each other; police arrive during check-in

items.comment_full:
BUYER BEWARE! As you can see from my track record, I’ve stayed in Airbnbs all over the world... and I’ve never had an experience like this before. First of all, let it be noted that this is all in regard to Sinida, who is listed as Heather’s co-host. I never spoke to or met Heather, so I don’t know what her deal is. To start, Sinida gave me faulty directions to enter the apartment, so that was a debacle in its own. Luckily another guest was there to let me in (and said she’d had the same difficulty). FYI, didn’t realize there were other guests staying in the place but that’s just one of many things the listing failed to mention. I entered to discover that Sinida (and/or Heather?) rescue PIGEONS that live in cages IN THE KITCHEN. There’s also 3 (very cute) cats... but they definitely neglect their litter boxes bc the whole place just smells like dirty litter and birds!! The other guest warned me that “sometimes they set the birds free in the bathroom”... oh hellllll no. She also told me that our “eccentric” host also seemed to sleep on the living room floor... another detail missing from the listing!
So after I went out that night, I returned to the house and had a friend with me. I warned him that the host may be sleeping on the ground and said I would check things out first to make sure we wouldn’t disturb anyone. I peeked my head in and didn’t see anyone sleeping anywhere- but the TV was on and blaring! We quickly and quietly slipped into my room and moments later, Sinida started screaming my name at the top of her lungs, telling me to check my phone. She’d messaged me on Airbnb saying I wasn’t allowed to bring guests back with me (also not listed anywhere on the listing) and that I needed to leave in the morning. She continued to scream my name at the top of her lungs (with no regard to the other guest staying there) and then started banging on the door. I opened the door and she repeated the same sentiment to me. I didn’t argue w her at all- it was late and her behavior was terrifying me tbh- and I said yes, I’d leave the next day. The next day, she continued to scream my name and give me a countdown every 15-30 minutes of when I needed to leave. Honestly it was harassment at that point. And then as all my stuff was packed up and I was about to open the door to leave, she banged again and screamed that she was going to call 911. I opened the door and said “are you kidding me right now??” On my way out, I pointed out to her it said nothing about not bringing any guests over and I grabbed my pizza box from the fridge (it was Roberta’s- come in, can’t waste that!) but realized shortly after she’d EATEN some of it, to add insult to injury!!! She also messaged me- after I left- “at least he was cute! hahaha” (in regard to my friend, after very aggressively throwing us out!!) All in all, a VERY bizarre experience with a potentially mentally unstable host... 10/10 would not recommend!!!
Oh yeah, she also changed the price of the first night to DOUBLE and then canceled my second night (I’m guessing so it showed as “available” for the next sucker). I contacted Airbnb and they apologized for this awful experience and refunded me for the second night she was trying to charge me for.
items.one_line: Pigeons in kitchen cages, birds released in bathroom

items.comment_full:
We’ve stayed at dozens of Airbnbs, but this was by far the most stingy. Key issues:
• Only 1 chair for 2 guests, and just 2 mugs, plates, and bowls.
• Keurig but no coffee, tea, or kettle.
• 1.5 rolls of toilet paper, no hand towels, dish towels, or hand soap. When requested, we were given more face cloths instead.
• Dishwasher but no pods; no dish cloths or drying towels.
• Floors left our feet black; glasses had a cloudy film; visible dust on most surfaces.
• Bedroom ceiling fan dropped dust like a “dusty New Year’s Eve.”
• Couch was broken and extremely uncomfortable.
• Thermostat kept resetting to 73° whenever we slept or left.
• Very poor communication: management company only available 9am–8pm; twice we waited 48+ hours with no reply and had to contact Airbnb directly.
• Our car was towed after we missed the plate-registration step ($220 + Uber).
Great location, but due to cleanliness, stocking, and communication issues, we wouldn’t stay again and recommend others

items.comment_full:
Feedback to Airbnb
My daughter and I stayed for 5 nights, except for the 1st and last night, there was power tripped every night! The first time it happened, it was late at night. Thankfully both my daughter and I had showered as when my daughter used the hair dryer to dry her hair, the whole apartment blackout!! Besides darkness, there was a beeping sound, we used our mobile phones torchlight to find the switch box and locate the beeping sound. We found the switch box at the entrance of the apartment and turned off and on all switches but the whole apartment remained in darkness!! We also could not use our mobile phones to text or call for assistance as the wifi was running on a modem and electricity was cutoff. We figured we could sleep without the air conditioning heater and with our wet hair but we could not sleep with the continuous beeping sound in the apartment!
Finally, we managed to locate the beeping sound from a box in the wall. We pressed some buttons and eventually the beeping sound stopped.
Next morning, I informed the owner and he showed me where to turn power supply back on should we experience a power tripped again. The power switched box was located some steps outside of the apartment. We thought it was once of but later in the night, again we experienced a blackout when we used the hairdryer. This time, I used my mobile phone torchlight and went out of the apartment to reset the power supply switch to the apartment. We no longer dare use the hairdryer and went to bed with wet hair.
Next morning, the kitchen sink and toilet sink could not drained off used water. I informed the owner of both the blackout and drainage choke. He came into the apartment and figured out that the choke was due to the drainage system being turned off when the beeping sound came on after the power tripped. When he turned it back on, used water spilled out from a closed closet and wet the floor next to the closet and next to the kitchen sink. He wiped away the used water on the floor but the apartment smelled of waste water!! He then gave us another hairdryer to use. I tested the “new” hairdryer but shortly a few minutes, we experienced yet another power tripped. This time it happened in the day. I immediately returned the “new “ hairdryer to the owner and informed him that again power supply was cut off. Each time it happened, we were cut off from wifi , television and heater.
In addition to the above, after a toilet flush or use of the toilet tap, there is a rumbling sound. The noise would cease after some seconds.
It was indicated the apartment was for 3 people and we were charged for 3 persons but only my mom and I stayed in the apartment for the whole duration. My mom informed the owner the same and requested for an adjustment to 2 persons but he said Airbnb charged by per apartment/night use and not by number of person. To our knowledge, Airbnb would look at the number of person renting the place.
It was overall an unpleasant stay. Other than the 1st night, we were inconvenient every night. We slept with wet hair as we could not and dare not use the hairdryer, television, wifi and heater were interrupted and endured noise from the toilet.
Can we be compensated for the inconvenience? We were so distressed each time we encountered the blackout 😫😫😫
items.one_line: Hair dryer causes blackout, beeping alarm, mobile dead, trapped in darkness

So if you like taking baths in a freezing cold room where everyone can see you getting hypothermia - this is the place for you!

This place was so bad, I ended up losing hundreds of dollars just to not stay here for my trip.
The short version - the two tiny space heaters left the place unbearably cold (curled in front of both together I was still shivering) - and your bathing options consist of a showerless tub, not in the bathroom, but in the middle of the big cement living room (where two windows lack curtains). So if you like taking baths in a freezing cold room where everyone can see you getting hypothermia - this is the place for you!
The long version - I had asked if it was OK to check in early since I had 4 giant 50lb suitcases that I couldn't easily uber around the city till 3 with. The owner was nice and said he just needed time to clean and then yes he'd message when I could come over. Around 1:30 he did and I arrived - very grateful and ready to love the place since he had already been accommodating. When I arrive I find a giant flight of stairs to the loft that there was no mention of anywhere (not even mentioned when I told him I had 4 very heavy suitcases). There is no elevator, nothing, so it is clear my only option now is to drag each suitcase up the stairs step by step when I'm not even strong enough to l lift one off the ground. (I was traveling for work and the suitcases were full of art display stuff that was the whole point of the trip - so there was no way for me to travel light.) I spent the next hour painfully dragging them up for flights of stairs, stopping occasionally to try and catch my breath and not cry in frustration. Finally though I got all my bags into the loft.
Looking around the space I quickly realized that the pivoted of the tub is so zoomed in - it leaves out a very important detail - it is not in a bathroom but rather in the living room. You know the one big room you are eating and sleeping in? So if you are staying with anybody - I hope you are comfortable bathing together. Also there is no shower at all - which is in the listing but I failed to notice when I booked this place. I was frustrated now by the bathing situation I found - after all that work dragging the bags up four flights of stairs - but I just accepted my predicament and looked around the rest of the place.
I was quickly noticing how cold I still was. like shivering cold, nose red cold. I thought I'll curl up in bed for a bit till I warm up. That's when I realize the bed only has one thin blanket on it that barely stretches across the bed. For that matter, there's only one towel draped over the tub. Talk about slim amenities. I knew that the blanket was not going to do anything to help with how cold I was. Mind you I'm wearing three shirts and a sweater over top since I dressed for warmth. It was 12 degrees the night before when I landed in Chicago - so I'm very bundled and still shivering. I look around for a way to turn the heat up and that's when I realize the place only has two very tiny space heaters in opposite corners that give of nowhere near enough heat for the large cement room.
I messaged the owner explaining that I am shivering cold and is there any way to turn the heat up. At this point I had pulled the two space heaters into the same corner and was sitting on one of the two folding chairs (provided for dining) in front of them - still shivering. The owner answers that is plenty warm in there and that it is up to people who live below if they want to turn the heat up or not. He mentioned that it was 70 degrees in the space when he was in there - but there is no thermometer to actually see the temperature - so it's just a he said she said with what the temperature is. But at 70 degrees - my nose isn't red and runny, I'm not physically shivering in several layers of sweaters and a coat. Maybe it was close to 70 in one of the apartments below but that was not what it was a floor above in a giant cement room with the windows not even adequately covered. (several of them had no curtains at all)
At this point I'm still shivering curled in front of the heaters, not willing to even walk across the room away from them to get something out of my bag because I'm so cold. So I called the actual airb&b company - which I have never done before - to see if they could help me. They say they will look into it and need to call the owner and so they will call me back. After that I receive a text from the owner saying the people below turned the heat up and in a few hours it should get warmer. That text is followed shortly by a second that asks if I'm getting out so he can change the locks.
The company calls me back and says due to his cancelation policy he's only willing to refund half my money. I ask what part of his cancelation policy covers not having basic amenities like heat and why I am the one to be out the money when he is the one who didn't provide a livable space. But it seems no matter how bad the place is when you arrive - the company still lets the owner off the hook and keep the money.
It's clear I can not spend the week like
items.one_line: Bathtub in frozen living room with windows, no curtains

items.comment:
DO NOT BOOK!!
I’m sure clients who actually get to stay here enjoyed their experience. My family was fully expecting to enjoy our week in Paris with this apartment as our home base.
Instead, upon our arrival in Paris, we got a ride to the Airbnb’s address and, after messaging the host that we had arrived, promptly received a notification that our stay had been CANCELLED. No other messages were sent, no explanation provided and no responses to my messages asking what had happened. We were effectively homeless in Paris with all of our luggage.
In our previous communication with the
items.comment_full:
DO NOT BOOK!!
I’m sure clients who actually get to stay here enjoyed their experience. My family was fully expecting to enjoy our week in Paris with this apartment as our home base.
Instead, upon our arrival in Paris, we got a ride to the Airbnb’s address and, after messaging the host that we had arrived, promptly received a notification that our stay had been CANCELLED. No other messages were sent, no explanation provided and no responses to my messages asking what had happened. We were effectively homeless in Paris with all of our luggage.
In our previous communication with the host, we had received an address for the Sweet Inn Office so we decided to walk with all of our luggage for the 30 min walk through the cobblestone streets of Paris to get to this office. It took closer to 45 minutes with our luggage and due to the fact that I am also recovering from hip surgery. When we arrived at the office, there were three people there. The first gentleman asked how he could help us, the other two did not acknowledge that we were even there.
When we explained our situation and asked for an explanation as to why our Airbnb was cancelled the gentleman essentially said “oh well, not really our problem.” Upon our pushing further, he eventually shared that the woman who was supposed to clean the apartment prior to our arrival never showed up. I said that we wouldn’t mind doing a later check in if they needed more time to get someone there to clean it. He said no, that they didn’t have ANYONE else who could clean. I don’t know about you, but I find it hard to believe that this business couldn’t find a single person in the entire city of Paris capable of cleaning this apartment for a day.
Incredibly frustrated at this point, we asked what he suggest we do - reminder that this whole ordeal meant we had no place to stay for an entire week. He gruffly suggested we go find another apartment on Airbnb. And that he couldn’t help us because it was “not their problem anymore.” After continuing to express our frustrations with him, the man eventually called a hotel he used to work at and told us they said they MIGHT have a room available for us. But he couldn’t confirm so we had to go there on our own. He didn’t offer to help us confirm a room over the phone, he didn’t offer to help us get an Uber there with all our luggage, he just sent us back outside with all our stuff.
Beyond aggravated, we stepped outside and called Airbnb. They offered a refund for the stay and sent a list of other apartments, the majority of which were FAR outside our price range and all of which were outside of the city center, where we had planned to stay. We decided our best shot was to try and find a hotel and, considering that the man at the office did not sound confident we would be able to get a room, we called every hotel we could research within a walking distance and could not find a room anywhere. Mind you, at this point we had been traveling for nearly 16 hours. We were starving, exhausted and so incredibly disappointed.
After not being able to find a hotel, we put our bags and myself (still recovering from surgery) in a tiny Uber and drove the 25 minutes to the hotel that the man from the office used to work at. My family walked for twice that amount of time to get there. We were lucky enough to get one of their last rooms for the one night (at a higher nightly price than the Airbnb was purchased at) but would have to switch hotels in the morning.
We switched to a different hotel the next morning and are able to stay here for the rest of our trip. We are grateful to finally have a place to stay for our trip but we missed a FULL day and a half due to the ordeal. Not only did we have to skip our plans for our first day in Paris because we had to find a place to stay, but we also had to miss a full day tour we had booked for the second day because of having to switch hotels. This tour was non-refundable and cost us over $300. On top of the $100+ in car rides/ubers around the city to get to the initial Airbnb and then to the hotel, AND the additional expense of the hotels that were more expensive than the Airbnb we booked, we are out over $500 due to this whole situation. Beyond the money, these are two whole days of our first family trip since the beginning of the pandemic that were essentially written off due to the lack of care, customer service, and common sense from the host and Airbnb itself.
Amongst sharing all this info with AirBnB, they offered us $200 on top of the refund - a far cry from making up for all this, if you ask me. $200 does not compensate for two days of time and more than $500 of actual money. I have been a customer of Airbnb for years, as have many of friends, and I have never been treated like this before. However, it makes me question where the organization’s values truly are and I doubt the sincerity of their claims to help “anyone belong anywhere.” We certainly did not feel any sense of belonging for the first two days of our trip and have not felt the “humanity” that the company is supposedly known for.
I write this not only to express my frustration, but to warn other users to be aware of the potential risk you incur by booking through a platform like this or with this “super host.”Evidently,
items.one_line: Cancelled upon arrival with luggage; homeless in Paris

items.comment:

items.comment_full:
Overall our stay was OK. The best thing was the view and the quick access to the beach. There were some cleanliness issues that were a bit concerning but not a huge deal but still made me wonder why they charge a cleaning fee in the first place.
Word of caution, backyard was full of goose poop. At some point my 2 year old was clenching something in her fist. I thought it was a rock, but turns out it was goose poop.
They really had the most basic of the basics for amenities. Motel shampoo for example. Dried out makeup remover towelettes. Old coffee in the cabinet, no sugar, no napkins. Practically empty soap dispensers and laundry detergent. A broom, but with no dustpan. Maybe a few more amenities would have gone a long way, and a quick refill of products for new guests.
I also found it a bit odd that the property manager/host asked for us to throw in a load of laundry for them before we checked out. What’s the point of charging a cleaning fee? I’ve done a lot of traveling in my life but never had a host request that of me. Maybe it’s common practice amongst some Airbnb’s, but if I was a host, I would be too embarrassed to ask. That’s why there are cleaning fees! I still washed and dried their sheets before I left and their towels were in the wash by the time I left.
There was a portable air conditioner for us which we greatly appreciated, being that it was a hot summer weekend, but it was so loud that it gave my husband a headache at night. The place offers a nice breeze though so you can probably do without if loud noises bother you.
Communication seemed to be an issue here as well, especially since I messaged Evolve the next day after our departure about a make up bag that I left in the bathroom and I still have not received a response. I have a friend who lives nearby and she would have gladly picked it up for me if whoever cleaned the house reported it missing and saved it for me.
Ultimately I would stay in Portsmouth again but not at this specific Airbnb, nor any Airbnb’s that Evolve manages. The owner of the house came by the first day by paddle boat and met my husband and daughter in the back yard by the water. She was nice and asked if we are all settled in. I was inside unpacking so I didn’t meet her. I hope she and Evolve take my review and the recommendations I wrote to them privately into consideration. This place has loads of potential and it would be a shame if they didn’t change a few of these things to make their future guests a bit more comfortable, and ultimately very happy!
items.one_line: Toddler finds goose poop is not rock in backyard

Apartment is great and same as in pictures. The area is called Crown Heights. items.comment_full: Me and my girlfriend stayed in the apartment which is on 4th floor and Subway (Utica Ave) is just 5 minutest walk away. We booked this place based on all the positive reviews but our personal experience was totally different. On our arrival we were greeted by the couple and they are such a lovely people. Apartment is great and same as in pictures. The area is called Crown Heights. However on our first day out we decided to explore the area but it ended soon when we were followed by a group of men who tried to talk to us and when we didn't respond they stated following us and shouting. We turned on busy road  and then they stopped. Anyway we didn't let them ruin our moment. We came back to apartment in the night around 9ish and there was a house party going on with loud music and some guy on microphone. We were tired and fell asleep even with rumbling of sound in the whole apartment but only to wake up in the morning to find out some one was shot dead right outside our apartment. Area was secured by police barriers. We were really scared and wanted to move out but could not due to limited funds. Around 500 people attended the house party and it was all over the news. We decided to leave early in the morning and return before dark and it really impacted our very first holidays in NYC. Then every evening some guy parks a car outside apartment with all doors wide open and plays really load music with massive speakers and of course a microphone. I thought I might ask them politely to turn down the music but how do you speak to 40-50 people who are drunk and abusive to each other. Few days later another guy was shot dead on the same street. Anyway there is a fire station opposite apartment which is very busy and goes on all night with very loud sirens and also police sirens because of police station being on the same street. We did not feel safe at all in this area and if I return to NYC I will not stay in Crown heights or even in Brooklyn. We found queens to better area for stay and shopping. It feels safer and shops are cheaper with large variety. For travel I would recommend getting subway weekly metro card which covers whole New York including ferry and trams. Airport entry costs $5 which is easily accessible by subway to both airports in the city. I would suggest to get subway map and also download offline maps (I use HERE Maps) to make life easier.
items.one_line: Murder outside apartment after loud house party

I messaged the Host ("Tommy") about this, and he replied to throw it out, which I did. This also necessitated cleaning up sticky messes in
items.comment_full:
W A R N I N G
A surveillance detector picked up what might be a hidden listening device (a "bug") in an artificial plant right next to the bed in this unit.
We called the County Police; an officer came out and we filed a police report.
• Proceeding this unsettling issue, we arrived to the unit, and found stale-dated food and condiments in the refrigerator and freezer, much of it several months old (including fish and one item from 2015),
I messaged the Host ("Tommy") about this, and he replied to throw it out, which I did. This also necessitated cleaning up sticky messes in the fridge, then hauling this load of garbage out to faraway dumpsters. (Host Tommy claimed the refrigerator had been meticulously cleaned before our arrival, which was NOT true).
At the time of our booking, there were misleading statements & omissions in the Host's listing. This included not alerting guests that the TV won't work unless guests bring their own equipment to hook it up to (which we don't travel with) and that the "dedicated work space" had a door. Although there is a small student desk in a dark, unlit area of the loft, there is no "door" to close off the space, so any noise from the living area below was a distraction. Host Tommy then claimed that the lanai was a "dedicated work space" "with a door". The lanai has a high bar table with a small diameter tabletop & two very uncomfortable, tall bar-stools, & can be quite windy. That is NOT a "dedicated work space" "with a door."
There was also a problem with the ceiling fan: it wouldn't turn on with the wall switch-&-slider. Someone prior to our stay must have pulled the fan cord; it was in an "off" position & so the wall switch wouldn't turn it on. Unfortunately, the fan is 20 feet up from the floor, and the pull cord is too high to reach. Host Tommy seemed upset that we "bothered" him about how to get it to work. He thought we were just "idiots" & didn't understand how to turn it on. He claimed it was "on" - but it wasn't. To get it turned on necessitated my husband (a climber) stepping onto a high rail & tugging on the cord.
When we messaged our concerns to the Host about amenities not being as described in the listing (no usable TV) & about the old food in the refrigerator, Host Tommy became defensive & belligerent.
Host Tommy accused me of "complaining". He also said I "complained" about the beach when I asked where a boogie-board beach might be found (versus a hard core surfing beach).
The Host then fabricated a story about us & reported that a "neighbor" said we had a third person - a child - in the unit with us. This wasn't true, as it was just the two of us - my husband and myself, and no visitors.
His claim of a child being with us was especially baffling, because aside from a few maintenance workers, we didn't encounter any other guests, saw no neighbors, nor did we see any children in the vicinity.
HOWEVER - I had a video chat and some calls on speaker-phone with my grandson a couple days earlier.
This made me think that the Host probably has a LISTENING DEVICE in the unit, and had overhead my grandson and me talking!
We figured Tommy possibly listened in on my video calls with my grandson on my cell phone, probably thought we had a child with us, & then possibly fabricated the story about a "neighbor" allegedly seeing a child with us, so as not to expose himself as having bugged the place to listen in on us and other guests.
Host Tommy's false claim & accusation (that we had a child with us) was probably made in retaliation - but this false reporting that we had a 3rd person with us is what clued us into a probable "bug" or listening device being in the unit.
It also led us to take the next step:
I then downloaded an app onto my smartphone, which detects hidden spyware, like bugging devices, surveillance equipment & hidden cameras & microphones.
I took it around the unit and did a "search" and scanned the place.
And it went off! It lit up, & began to buzz (a buzz buzz buzzing sound) when we put it next to a fake palm plant in a pot right beside the bed (note: Host Tommy keeps several fake plants in the unit.
The detector sounded a positive alarm for the presence of a possible surveillance device.
My husband then downloaded a different app that detects spyware, and his app did the same thing with this same artificial plant.
We called the County Police Department right away.
An Officer from the Maunaloa Police Department arrived and we filed a police report. We then packed up and left the unit immediately. We vacated the unit two and a half days early from when we were due to checkout, as we no longer felt safe.
It is so creepy, Host is creepy, and we felt extremely violated.
This was my first and only negative experience with any Host.
All prior encounters have been wonderful, friendly and respectful, and my privacy has never been compromised. I have also had lovely, positive reviews written of me by every Host I've ever encountered.
I consider this Host and this awful experience an anomaly; it was extremely unsettling, very disturbing, and quite frightening.
Because of the extreme possibility of a listening device being in the unit - and the lack of cleanliness & Host Tommy's untrue allegation & false claims - I would advise not booking this unit - or any unit that Host Tommy might be associated with, not under any condition.
items.one_line: Hidden listening device found in plant, moldy fridge from 2015

items.comment_full:
Our stay at Casa Sevilla started out well, we were a large group (11 women) who were staying for 3 nights from Friday to Monday. The property does have an underlying smell of mould, even on the towels. It is very basic.
"Basic" would have been fine, but on night two, we left the world of "basic" and entered the realm of "problematic".
On Saturday, there was a huge cockroach in the apartment. After the requisite panic, screaming, and crying had ensued, we managed to kill it.
On night two, as eleven women were sharing the two bathrooms with timed military precision - all afraid of the possibility that the cockroach had friends or family in the area that would be making an appearance - we saw a mouse .
We didn't manage to catch the mouse and it remains at large. A number of the women had no other choice but to book a hotel room for the last night - incurring additional expenses and naturally dividing the group.
Needless to say, we would not stay here again.
items.one_line: Cockroach panic, then mouse appears, eleven women flee to hotel

items.comment_full:
TERRIFYING EXPERIENCE. EMBARRASSING HOST.
The apartment is tiny, located on the ground floor. Upon our arrival we unfortunately discovered that where the only available window of the studio apartment faces, there is a lot of garbage from the neighbors that is not picked up, but increasing every day. We basically spent 2 days with the window closed because an unbreathable smell was coming in.
In addition, we had dog feces under the window, and as if the disgusting smell coming from outside was not enough, we discovered that there was also a problem with the plumbing, as at the times when the neighbors eat lunch and dinner practically all the smell of the food they were cooking enters the house.
In short, there was no breath in the house either with the window closed or with the window open. It stinks both inside and outside.
Fortunately, I documented everything and Airbnb refunded us the remaining part of the vacation and we escaped from this house.
I never leave negative reviews, but this time I decided to write it all down, because as if all that was not enough, the host even got pissed off because we left, and she never apologized to us in fact she even accused us.
Never seen such an incompetent host in my life.
If you don't want to find the stench in your house look elsewhere....
items.one_line: Dog feces, plumbing stench, garbage heap, airbnb refund

items.comment_full: The check in and communication with Tomas and Catarina were perfect. They were very quick in their replies and trying to be helpful.  The location of the place is also nice.  Cute park in front, lots of restaurants. But that is all the positive things I can say... the first night we were cooking dinner and then we noticed a huge cockroach running in the kitchen floor, we thought this might be just a one.. but then as we started looking closer the place was infested!!!! We could see the antennas sticking out from a hole as if if they were waiting for us to turn off the lights to have a fest with our dinner.  Sadly i thought the cockroaches were the end of the story.. but no one of the cushions in the main bed smelled like public toilet,  there was a piece of glass in the sofa bef, the towels and sheets had stains on them (they looked not properly washed), the cahinets and pillows smelled terribly like mold.. and with all of this we had a 7 month old baby and a toddler.. we did not even want to stay that first night but it was too late and we had no option. I contacted the hosts and although they were very responsive apologetic they did not really offer me a satisfying solution. We still had to sleep in that apartment.. the next day we left to a new and clean and cockroach free place.

There are people that reside in the guest house, and their vehicle overwhelms a considerable amount of the space. The gate is difficult to get open, so don't be afraid to give it a good push! When you enter the pin, be sure to press the lock symbol afterwards, and wait for the door to unlock itself. Be patient! To use the stove, hold your finger down on the power button for a couple of se
items.comment_full: I am either the pickiest guest or the bravest, but here's the ultimate rundown of this Hollywood Hideout! The alley is tight, and it can be rather difficult to maneuver a larger vehicle into the parking area. There are people that reside in the guest house, and their vehicle overwhelms a considerable amount of the space. The gate is difficult to get open, so don't be afraid to give it a good push! When you enter the pin, be sure to press the lock symbol afterwards, and wait for the door to unlock itself. Be patient! To use the stove, hold your finger down on the power button for a couple of seconds. This Matthew dude has a crazy-cool speaker thing-y in his bathroom that is able to be connected via Bluetooth. Simply flip the switch to the on position, press the music button, and link your phone to the device. It has various light settings as well. I hate to say it but you're going to see bugs. If you don't want to see bugs, go to Alaska. But seriously, Matt tries his absolute best to see that those creatures are gone for good but Florida wins every time. I'm not from Florida and have never stayed in an Airbnb before, so I made sure to give Matthew a phone call to bring up my concerns (and even stressed them a tad to include my group's concerns). Matt responded immediately. There was a fan in the bedroom located on the right-hand side that needed to be operated with a remote. We weren't able to find it but if you ever have questions you should shoot him a text. The lighting is incredible. However, some of the lights wouldn't work—specifically in the dining room. The stools are adjustable. And no, it isn't you. His front blinds suck. Hopefully when you stay there, they'll be gone. If you're staying with roughly 4 or 5 people, bring additional towels and washclothes. Pull upwards on the washing machine door before you close it shut. The neighborhood is really neat. The people are laid-back, helpful, and they love their cool cars. It's like the cheap version of Hollywood, CA.  The location is the perfect distance from fun activities, necessities, and jobs. I love that the area isn't tourist-y at all. You really feel ingrained into the community which makes it extremely hard to leave. You'll start to call the house home. I am absolutely moving to Florida. The beach is nearby, and there are plenty of great spots for taking photos. My modeling agent was able to photoshoot me within minutes away. You can't just take a quick stroll to the beach. The preferred methods of travel are via the free bus, the free golf cart (use the "Circuit" app), or an Uber. Safe neighborhood? Ehhhh- it's a suburb. A car slowed down and watched my friend and I walk by—who knows why. Safety in numbers. For whatever reason you're staying in this Airbnb, Florida is so affordable. All of the activities that I did were either free or dirt cheap. If you're feeling the city-vibe  and hate the drive, take advantage of the free bus and swing on over to Miami where you might spot a celebrity. High-end brands that I recall from one of the malls were Louis Vuitton, Prada, Hermés, Fendi, Dior, etc (brands that you wouldnt find at a regular outlet). If you're like me and love the indoors and outdoors, also visit the Florida Everglades (1 hour drive). My group found an alligator, Florida hard, soft-shelled turtles, etc. One week isn't enough. Pay for two weeks. Overall, this house was 4 stars for me. Definitely worth visiting again but wasn't perfect. I have high standards. If you have any questions and want a woman's point-of-view you can message me too if that's possible. I pride myself on finding fun stuff to do. Hit that button and button and book, book, book! God bless : )
items.one_line: Bugs guaranteed; use stove power button technique or else

I have to be brutally honest with this review.
DO NOT STAY AT THIS PLACE!! KONA ISLANDER IS A DRUG TRAFFICKING COMPLEX!!!
After a year in lockdown due to Covid, my daughter and I took a trip to Hawaii (where I’m from). We wanted to really have the best experience so we booked this place because of the location. Upon entering, it looked humble, simple, but totally workable. I took off my shoes and walked to open the sliding door. Instantly my bare feet were covered in grime. So even though we booked a week, we stayed less than 12 hours. What follows is why, my horror story.
After sunset and dinner, my teenage daughter and I settled in to get ready for bed, excited about the following day, since we had just got there. I pulled out the futon couch as I like a firm bed and the “bed” there was so soft. It seemed old maybe? The futon wasn’t any better. Definitely a really old throw away. Covered in stains and the metal bars were so torturous. That really didn’t matter, because we wouldn’t sleep anyway. As soon as it got late, around 11 pm, the two other tenants in that part of the building started and DID NOT STOP ALL NIGHT!! In fact, when I left at 10 am the next morning, the one guy was still screaming. One neighbor had people coming and going ALL night long, playing music loudly and generally partying. I’m not a prude, but the walls are thin and it was loud. At one point he got in a fight with his girlfriend. There was a lot of screaming. That was disturbing, but not even the worst of it all.
The neighbor upstairs.. I am still traumatized by this. It was our first day here, after a year of lockdown!!! This neighbor was angry, screaming every 10 to 15 minutes “Get the f**k out of my house”. I have video from the entire night. Although the video is dark and you can kind of see inside my room, you can perfectly hear both neighbors. It was horrible!!!
I contacted this host, Jacey, and we messaged throughout the night. She told me she was filing a police report. I told her I was afraid of them retaliating since there was only the three units occupied.. they would know it was me. She made me feel like everything was being taken care of. I have her on text saying she was filing a report. Etc etc. She did. I such thing! She did nothing to help me and in the end tried to blame me, as if it was my fault. So in the morning, my daughter and I left, with no sleep, on our first day on vacation. Yay. Of course it happens to be spring break so accommodations are limited and last minute, expensive. I contacted air B and B, they told me to request a refund from the host, which I did. Well this host, miss Jacey denied my refund because “I left early”. For real!! Yeah I sure did leave early. She is refusing to work with me. I booked a week, and stayed 12 hours!
I know a lot of people in Hawaii, and was later told that that place is a drug trafficking place. Meth or whatever.
So I warn you, please don’t stay here. Not only will it traumatize you, ruin your trip, and waste your money, but you could actually be in danger! Being from Hawaii myself, it also doesn’t correctly represent the local Hawaii that I know! I am still disgusted by this entire ordeal!
You’re on vacation!!! Go to a hotel where if there is a problem, they fix it and maybe even upgrade you!! Or you can rent from this thief of a lady and feel really annoyed and deceived, like me!
We went to the Hilton Waikoloa. Three times the price, but at least we know there aren’t junkies hitting their wives all night!!!!!
items.one_line: Filthy complex with stained futon, drug trafficking allegations

items.comment_full:
This is the most disgusting place I've ever stayed at. An Airbnb can't be any worse than Lolita. Please, everyone , read that before you book.
I arrived late at night. Self Check in was available any time after 6 pm. When I came to my room at 3 am, I saw a strange man who was sleeping in my bed. I was shocked, so was that guy. As it turned out later, that host rented out this room for the same dates. I didn't know what to do and where to spend a night. I tried calling and messaging Lolita. She didn't answer and I started knocking on the doors of the other rooms, looking for her. Other guests told me that they have never seen her. Perhaps I woke the whole house up. But I just had no idea what to do in this horrible situation. She replied to me in 15-20 min, apologizing for her mistake and asking me to go to another free room. Obviously it wasn't a room I booked. It was incredibly cold in that room. I got a text from Lolita in the morning , she offered to move to a room that I booked in the first place if I wanted. I decided to stay where I was but I asked her to do something regarding the heating. Lolita promised to bring a heater and added that I can turn a radiator on in my room, by turning the knob around. I didn't figure out how to do that so I messaged for instructions. Right after that some woman showed up who turned the radiator on and started yelling at me that it's supposed to be cold in nyc , as this is not Florida, etc. Itd be harder to find a more disrespectful woman than her. However, she promised that radiator will be efficient enough to warm room up. Then, at 9:30 pm, Lolita texted me that I can move to a room I originally booked. I answered that I'm fine with staying where I was but she insisted on me, moving. I found it a bit strange but agreed. The next day some woman broke into my room at 11 am or so when I was sleeping. She didn't knock, she simply opened the door. Sorry, but who does that! It was a cleaner who said that she'll be cleaning my room and she asked me what I was still doing there. In short, I packed my stuff and moved it along with the heater to another room. I wasn't at home a whole day. When I came back at midnight, I realized that someone was there. Heater was gone and replaced with a chair, plus there was one more blanket on my bed. A radiator wasn't working at all again. I had to sleep in my clothes as it was totally impossible to change clothes in a cold room like that.
And btw, bathroom lock on the first floor was broken so it was impossible to close the door. I was getting nervous every time that someone might walk in. Also I saw a cockroach there. There are some positive things about my stay, as the room itself was pretty clean, cozy, with TV and Netflix. And kitchen has all the necessities. But host's behavior is not acceptable! And the way she's keeping her rooms cold to save money on the bills. I wouldn't recommend to stay at Lolita's house at all.
items.one_line: Stranger sleeping in booked bed at 3am, unresponsive host

items.comment_full:
All it took was 15 mins to ruin this otherwise wonderful stay which we even extended by 2 more days.

On the day of our checkout, we left a bag at Jason's place because we had an evening train to Peterborough from Kings' cross which has a direct line from North Ealing.

In our absence, Jason's Mom went through our personal stuff in bag, took out few belongings and kept them as collateral against 1 towel which she thought we stole and costed her 20GBP.

It was raining heavily outside and since we were getting late, I asked my wife to meet me directly at station with our 1.5 yr old son while I bring the bag from Jason's house. I was drenched from head to toe and soon as I entered their house I was accused of theft! She said she gave us 2 big towels and 1 hand towel and now one of the big towel is missing. So either I pay 20GBP or let her search through all my remaining luggage (3 full sized bags)!

Jason just pretty much stood there and did not say much (I can understand his position). Although at end he said that he won't put the house on Airbnb but I am sure that's not going to happen. Arguing with her would have been pointless and that time and I was already getting late to catch my train. I ended up feeling humiliated, angry and 10 GBP short.

How I wished I had more time on my hand that evening. I would have loved to call up Police at their place.

Jason was very helpful throughout the stay. He even answered our late night call at his work when we were clueless about which bus to take. But Jason's mom is a whole lot different person altogether. In the beginning she was a bit strict with her rules but we didn't think much about it because it was her house and she is allowed to keep it the way she wants. But the way she conducted herself at the end is indecent and unforgivable.

We got the smaller room on the left side. Bed made a squeaking noise. We were told that we are using electricity by filling water bottle from her big refrigerator. We used up all the dishwashing liquid! But we ignored all these small things because this place was so close to North Ealing station and bus stop plus the locality was amazing and the house was really neat and clean.

I can see this place worked well for lot of other people so maybe my experience was just an exception. But even in this exception what stands out is the behaviour of Jason's Mom and how un-trustworthy and unpredictable she can be.
items.one_line: Host's mom held belongings hostage over missing towel

Here's what I wish I knew before booking: 1. PRIVACY I booked this Airbnb because it said it could accommodate four people, while there was sleeping spots for four people (including a futon that just rocked back and forth on a piece of plywood) there were NO DOORS inside the cave! That means no doors between sleeping areas, but more importantly, NO BATHROOM DOOR! The sunken bathroom was one thi
items.comment_full: ***BEWARE*** While my husband and I enjoyed the adventure of staying in the cave, the other two members of our party spent their day in Santorini going from hotel to hotel to find another accommodation. Here's what I wish I knew before booking: 1. PRIVACY I booked this Airbnb because it said it could accommodate four people, while there was sleeping spots for four people (including a futon that just rocked back and forth on a piece of plywood) there were NO DOORS inside the cave! That means no doors between sleeping areas, but more importantly, NO BATHROOM DOOR! The sunken bathroom was one thing that set it apart in the photos, but I didn't realize from the photos that the door and window in the bathroom had no way of closing and therefore anything you do in the bathroom can be heard or seen from other spots in the cave. I had booked this Airbnb for myself, my husband, his father, and his new stepmother. While the listing never said open sleeping quarters I could guess that from the pictures and that would have been fine...open bathroom is not fine. They told us they wanted to relax and my husband and I should go off on our own and then secretly spent the day finding another hotel with a room and lugging their bags up the million stairs. This al could have been avoided had the listing included this information. 2. LOCATION Hope you're good with directions and don't drink too much at night because this cave can be tricky to find. I was uneasy when despite my asking Rena never gave me directions to the cave, but upon arriving I understood why...you will walk down little uneven winding stair pathways -- down, down, down -- with no signs and a sea of similar looking stucco while dwellings. I tried making landmarks on my mind to help find the place (look for the top of a tower with bells, look for a turquoise blue gate, look for the second path that says stop, look for the GARBAGE CAVES). Yes, several landmarks will help you get there, but none say you're home like the two smelly drippy garbage caves next to the Airbnb. While you couldn't smell them while in the Airbnb, it was not the kind of landmark we were looking for. 3. BATHROOM So, I've already explained our biggest gripe here, NO BATHROOM DOOR (feel like I can't stress that enough in a listing for four people), but I thought I would touch on a few more things. Because the cave is a CAVE there can be condensation that accumulates and as a result there is a dehumidifier in the bathroom. This dehumidifier however, does not get rid of the strong putrid white truffle smell that happens at night. This made it hard to feel clean after showering there. The shower is a handheld only, which can be tricky for those of us who are not used to...
items.one_line: Cave apartment has no doors, bathroom completely exposed

items.comment:
We stayed 6 nights in June, was greet by host's assistant. Florence was hot, in the 90-100 degree F. Perfect location. Second floor, spacious. Walkable to the Market Central, duomo, and academia. However this is the worst place I stayed out of 4 airbnb locations in Italy.
* kitchen sink - faucet fell apart, put it back on - workable but not ideal. * Lamps in both bedroom broken, still light up, but broken from the neck of the light bulb, electric hazard, *First floor shower was unusable due to clogged drain. *Second floor toilet flusher - non functional - had to use a big pot of water
items.comment_full:
We stayed 6 nights in June, was greet by host's assistant. Florence was hot, in the 90-100 degree F. Perfect location. Second floor, spacious. Walkable to the Market Central, duomo, and academia. However this is the worst place I stayed out of 4 airbnb locations in Italy.
* kitchen sink - faucet fell apart, put it back on - workable but not ideal. * Lamps in both bedroom broken, still light up, but broken from the neck of the light bulb, electric hazard, *First floor shower was unusable due to clogged drain. *Second floor toilet flusher - non functional - had to use a big pot of water to flush - inconvenient, but workable. *Second floor shower was clogged, we had to clean the drain ourselves. *Second floor shower tower hanger broke, * Master bedroom (actually an attic) AC cannot overcome the heat from the ceiling skylight on hot days, even with a fan running - we had to sleep on the pull out couch the whole time. * Electric circuit is not adequate for normal use, the circuit breaker kept break off every time we start washing machine, once when we started electric stove. Had to go down to the first floor of the building to reset the circuit breaker. We had to turn everything off (except refrigerator) to do laundry. * minimum supplies provided, two rolls of toilet paper for each bathroom, 4 packs 10ml shampoo (size like 2x2 inch hand sanitizer wipe) and 2 bars of 2x2x.025 inch bar soap, not the welcome package to get us through a weeklong stay. No paper towel. No laundry soap. 2 pods of dishwasher soap. First day afternoon we strangled to find a convenient store for supplies in a new city with minimum help from the host. On the fourth day, the host asked if we wanted to call a service, we only have one more night to deal with, schedule to check out on day 6 morning. We were not comfortable with service personnel working in the apartment while we were out touring the beautiful city of Florence.
All the issues are fixable, however, if the owner is not going to fix the issues and continue renting out the place like what we found, we won't recommend it.
items.one_line: Broken faucet, clogged drains, circuit breaker failure

The next day we went to Tusca
items.comment_full:
We stayed at Marco & Brunos apartment for 5 nights. There couldn't be a better location for an Airbnb than this. We took the train in from Rome to SMV and got a little lost walking to the apartment but that was our own fault. We saw Bruno waiting outside for us even though we were several minutes late. He kindly helped us with our bags, he was so sweet. He showed us around the apartment and gave us some great tips and restaurants to try out. We were absolutely exhausted so we actually went to the grocery store around the corner and made sandwiches for dinner.
The next day we went to Tuscany to look at wedding venues with our wedding planner and had a fantastic time. Like I said, the location is fantastic. That night we noticed that our neighbors were EXTREMELY loud. Screaming, stomping, yelling, slamming, from both children and adults, men and women. It was unbelievable. We come from NYC so we don't mind a little noise but this was on another level. It felt like they were in our apartment! This went on from at least 7pm-midnight. Needless to say we got no sleep.
The next day was my birthday and we went to the outlet malls (The Mall) via SMN. It took 40 minutes to get there and we had so much fun and bought a lot of amazing stuff at a great discount and had lunch at the Gucci Caffe. When we got back, we decided to have a beer in the little courtyard area of the apartment. I called my mom to let her know we were safe and sound. At this time it's probably 10 o'clock and a note from above falls on my head telling us to be quiet and stop making loud phone calls. I was perplexed. I immediately said "hello? hello??" and they shut their window. I am NOT a loud person and neither is my fiancé whatsoever. It would've taken this person 1 minutes to shout down to us, instead they look the time to write us a letter!!!! Needless to say, this made us feel very awkward and uncomfortable so we continued the rest of our evening inside. It was a shame because it was a beautiful night and we wanted to enjoy my birthday.
The following few days were great and we really enjoyed our stay. Bruno and Marco made dinner reservations for us and always answered us promptly if we needed anything. They truly take pride in their business. Unfortunately, the yelling and screaming persisted through out our entire stay, early morning and night. I know they have a few other air bnb locations which we would love to check out next time we're in Firenze, but sadly the noise level was very frustrating for us.
items.one_line: Great location destroyed by neighbors screaming at all hours
