items.comment_full:
On the whole very nice, but one quite major issue.

The good stuff: Flat was in a great location, we walked pretty much everywhere. Ana was brilliant, sweetly prepared an extra bed at the last minute for a surprise motherly visit. Nespresso machine; well fancy. Very comfy bed.

But,

The ceilings are paper thin! When we arrived Ana did mention that it was an issue she was aware of and would be rectifying in the future, however if we had known the extent of it, I don't think we would have stayed. Perhaps we were just unlucky with our timings, or the current neighbours, but you could hear everything! If I spoke Portuguese we could have made out the conversation. The music and tv probably wasn't that loud, but it came directly into the bedroom, clear as day.
And then, the first night the neighbours adult fun and games started at around 22:30 and came to a mutually beneficial conclusion within the hour... manageable :-) The second night, they began preparing what I assume was some form of exciting playtime apparatus at around 02:00. They continued to work said apparatus to its fullest extent until around 04:30 or 05:00, when both parties, I imagine, fell into a state of exhausted, blissed out unconsciousness! A highly irritating, though quite amusing tale for us, but if there had been children around, we would have had A LOT of explaining to do :-p

As I said, Ana explained that it was something they were going to try and resolve and I hope she does, 'cause the place was great except for that.

Oh, and the shower temperature jumped all over the place too, but that's not as funny to talk about.
items.one_line: Paper-thin ceilings broadcast neighbor's very active love life

The host knew I was unhappy, and said if I still gave him a five star review, he would give me one. I don't think it's okay to bribe a guest for five stars when you know they did not enjoy the stay.

items.comment_full:
I will say that the apartment is cute, very clean, with a very modern bathroom and a comfortable bed. But if a nice view, good neighborhood, and helpful building staff are important to you--keep looking. This isn't the place.
I spent one miserable day and two miserable nights being afraid to go out because of the guy running up and down the street yelling some kind of drunken rant, not to mention the heavy police presence afterward and helicopters circling that made me very nervous. Were those gunshots that I heard or firecrackers? Hard to say for sure, as I stared out the window at crumbling concrete rooftops laden with laundry as far as the eye could see. I didn't take any chances. I ordered in. Then I watched order after order (no fewer than seven) get cancelled by the drivers there to pick them up. Later, I learned that Uber Eats had at one point suspended service to that entire community because too many drivers complained of getting mugged. Did I lose money on those orders? Yes, I certainly did. Will Uber Eats make it right? Who knows. That isn't this host's responsibility. What IS this host's responsibility, however, is the abysmal conduct of the desk staff downstairs when I asked for assistance tracking down some local phone numbers so I could find out where my money (and food orders) were going. This staffer didn't just refuse to help me. Oooh, no. He even made the remark (allegedly, since it was in Spanish) to another guest that it wasn't his job to help me with that kind of problem and that I should just find the phone numbers on that online search engine we all love to hate.
Well... I had indeed tried to find the phone numbers online, but those numbers went to what sounded like FAX machines and I couldn't get through to a soul. Only after I tried that did I ask for assistance. My partner back home messaged Davide and told him I was having problems with the staff being unhelpful, and Davide's only response was, "Hello Rhonda, I'm sorry to hear you're going through this situation. Do you need numbers to order food? Hello Rhonda, I hope you had an excellent stay with us; I remind you that tomorrow the check out is no later than 12 noon; please leave the keys on the table of the apartment when you leave and close the door when you leave; the elevator card can be given to the guard at the entrance of the building. Excellent trip back home."
Sounds pretty mechanical to me. Maybe a stock reply with a couple sentences tacked on the front to make it seem like he might have read my partner's message. This fairly well sums up my entire impression of this stay: it's all very mechanical. The basics are there. Never mind the details. No special touches. No one cares. It's a money making adventure for someone who has no real desire to be a host, and it shows.
What's unfortunate is that they also mention "tour services" and other "services" as add-ons. Well, let me tell you about the "service" I got with the airport pickup. Okay... I'm not a spoiled rich American who expects to have my nose wiped when I sneeze. But I KNEW that I would be out of my element during this short stay in Mexico City, so I paid more for this apartment based on the extras advertised. I could have (and in hindsight, should have) taken a much cheaper airbnb closer to the airport with no extra services. But the idea of having someone meet me at the airport with my name on a sign, ready to help me find the best, simplest, cheapest money exchange or (gasp) an actual working ATM sounded like a good plan. I told the person I'd been referred to for shuttle service in no uncertain terms that I do not speak Spanish and that since he stated his driver could only accept cash, I would need to find a way to get said cash. Well... when I landed, I had in my inbox a wall of text, all in Spanish, and a photo of a vehicle parked somewhere that might or might not be close by.
Anyone in the tourism or hospitality business show KNOW that foreigners landing in any airport outside their own country may not have access to cell service, as their sim cards may not work. Mine did not. Neither did the airport wifi. I was able to connect to it, but it was too weak for me to use any online translate tool or download the images this driver kept trying to send, and voice messages presumably all in Spanish. Only when I said, "forget it. I'll call an Uber," did someone suddenly materialize in front of me holding a sign with my name on it. Wow. Magic. Turns out, this driver had a credit card reader in his vehicle. So all that fuss over me needing cash to pay him was pretty much all for show. And probably some good old tax evasion, too.
To make a long story short, my stay was awful. It could have been much better with only a tiny bit of attention paid to details. Again, let me say that I paid more for this stay based on the extra services and benefits advertised. The apartment was indeed cute. It was very, very clean. But that's the limit of what I can say was delivered as advertised. I do not recommend this stay for anyone who is traveling to Mexico for the first time or who may need some extra flourishes to make their stay feel comfortable and safe. Because believe me, with this one, you're on your own.
items.one_line: Cute apartment, drunken rants, gunshots, no food delivery

The husband invited me out to some outings. The kitchen is very modern - there's lots of condiments. The room is decent (it can get a bit cold but there were extra covers no drama there). They also picked me up from the bus stop and showed me a shortcut on how to get to bus stop (it's about a 15 min walk). I was happy with the place however things started going downhill quick - and it really comes down to the communication of
items.comment_full:
Well I'll start with the positives - it's a clean house in a nice leafy suburb. At first the hosts were friendly - we chatted - they even gave me a Fitness First voucher. The husband invited me out to some outings. The kitchen is very modern - there's lots of condiments. The room is decent (it can get a bit cold but there were extra covers no drama there). They also picked me up from the bus stop and showed me a shortcut on how to get to bus stop (it's about a 15 min walk). I was happy with the place however things started going downhill quick - and it really comes down to the communication of the wife Jacqui I believe her name is and her OCD about the kitchen and everything else. The husband was pretty chill but simply put the rules were just very extreme and gave me a lot of anxiety and was something I'm not used to.
While I understand you should wash up after yourself and things of that nature I was reprimanded by SMS for things like walking over a bathroom matt and leaving an edge furled up, or not putting the toaster back in the exact same spot I found it in - it just gave me a whole lot of anxiety that I was going to do one thing wrong and get kicked out.
In fairness I did burn one of the pots slightly and so but I offered to pay to get it replaced (accidents happen - I'm not perfect) - and I put a $70 tip down to the couple to handle any minor issues that may come up so they'd stop complaining.
The whole situation was very tense as I felt like as soon as I came home I was going to move one thing or walk over one matt wrong and there would be issues.
In the end on my 20th day I forgot to wash the rice cooker/slow cooker - (first time I forgot to wash up after myself) - I called the husband to apologize and he said it was cool - then the wife basically told him I have to not use it again, and then out of nowhere she said I have to leave the house... then I find out she wants me to leave that same day - over an unwashed pot. It's madness - and I'm really not happy that hosts have that power to just kick you out without even a 24hrs notice over a pot - I had no issues leaving early if necessary. It got really bad where she said she would call the police if I came back like it was just insane - honestly very stressful experience - I think as a short term this place might work for you - or if you're a clean freak - but this couple (the wife mainly) - is just very OCD about everything and it really made me uncomfortable in the house.
Couple of things to note also:
- There is no washing machine on the premises and even though the wife said that she would wash my clothes she didn't... and then told me to go to a laundramat like 1.6km away - this is stated on the ad but I didn't see it just keep that in mind.
- The wife does walk into your room when you're not there - she entered my room on at least 3 separate ocassions - one time she took a jug of water out that I drank from - another time she would open the windows). If you have an issue with this best to bring it up and just be aware she won't tell you she's going in or that she has gone in for any reason. So be prepared for that if privacy is important to you (not too big a drama but a bit weird that she entered without telling me).
items.one_line: Friendly hosts turned into OCD kitchen police overnight

items.comment_full: The location of the property is great. The communication with management was prompt. The guy provided info of how to get to place and bus schedule. Upon arrival he gave useful tips about the island and places we should visit. He also let us borrow the charger for the length of our stay. However, his attitude was really unpleasant. He threw a few rude phrases at us that I preferred to blame on his second language. One of which was that I was stupid. He also wanted to know who we were to each other which is quite frankly non of his business but whatever. The room was much darker and smaller  than the pictures. It was really tight for two people. The bed linens weren't clean. It had a stain at the bottom that awfully reminded period spot!!! He cautioned us about possible construction noice. There was no construction noice. However, the neighbors were extremely loud and ware having constant fights. Also, when new guests arrived at 8am, the property manager was giving them a greeting speech on the porch right behind our doors. We could hear every single word since there's no isolation. On the date of our departure the guy knocked on the door at 12:05 with words you MUST leave now! We have other guests! Leave now!  On the bright side he let us leave our luggage till 5:30pm. We asked if we could leave it till 6:00pm but he had to go to work. The only good thing about this place is it's location. The manager needs a good attitude adjustment!
items.one_line: Host called guest stupid and questioned their relationship

items.comment_full:
I stayed one week (solo traveling). The apartment looked kind of like in the photos but there are some aspects I should mention, this is an old and not beautiful building, at all, the aisle can be really scary depending of the person.
- The apartment was clean enough to accept it, but I can tell they didn't clean it well or they did it X days ago. I could see dusk in several surfaces.
- It is quite and calm but in the weekends can be noisy (you can hear your neighbors cooking, watching tv, etc).
- The shower was uncomfortable to use, the pressure was not enough 50% most the time (like I had to lay on the wall while taking a shower because the water flow was so weak). The host told me that it was normal.
- I didn't have hot water when I got into the apartment.
- I didn't have internet for 1.5 days.
- I didn't have ELECTRICITY for one night and part of the next day.
- The locker in the building door was kind of broken too.
- The bathroom smelled really bad sometimes (I had to pour water in one of the floor drains (but they should do that before every host or when they clean the apartment).
- I had to wait some minutes to get the keys, I mean they were punctual but not perfectly punctual.
Pros:
- Martha was super nice and I think she did all she could to solve the inconveniences I had.
----------
Me quede una semana en el apartamento. Honestamente, no me parecio que lo limpiaron bien, vi polvo en varias partes de la casa y por ejemplo, el bano no olia bien, por lo que supuse que tenian tiempo que no lo limpiaban o por lo menos no tuvieron la precaucion de echarle agua al desague para que no salieran los gases de la red de cloacas. Cuando llegue no tenia agua caliente o por lo menos no me dijeron como usar el calentador.
Una noche y parte del dia siguiente no tuve luz, y tampoco tuve internet por practicamente dos dias. Las sabanas y almohadas aunque estaban limpias, me parecia que olian un poco a polvo. El apartamento es casi siempre silencioso pero los fines de semana en la noche no mucho.
El edificio es bastante viejo y los pasillos estan sucios y bastante descuidados (no forma parte del Airbnb pero me parece pertinente mencionarlo).
A pesar que creo que en realidad no limpiaron el apartamento como se debe o mas aun, que tenian tiempo que no lo rentaban y simplemente no lo repasaron desde la ultima limpieza, considero que el trato de los host fue muy bueno, me refiero al trato personal. Martha por ejemplo, estuvo muy atenta cuando yo estuve ahi.
items.one_line: No electricity, no internet, no hot water, yes problems

items.comment:
Before we arrived, David messaged us explaining that his HOA is trying to eliminate AirBNB stays, and if we were questioned by anyone we were to lie and say we were his personal guests. The gate number provided did not work and we had to wait for someone to drive in so we could follow. Upon entering the apartment, there was a strong cleaning solution smell - which we later found out was a chemical used by an exterminator to kill a COCKROACH INFESTATION.
Some of the main points:
- Dozens of dead cockroaches in every room.
- Mouldy food left down the side of the refrigerator.
items.comment_full:
Before we arrived, David messaged us explaining that his HOA is trying to eliminate AirBNB stays, and if we were questioned by anyone we were to lie and say we were his personal guests. The gate number provided did not work and we had to wait for someone to drive in so we could follow. Upon entering the apartment, there was a strong cleaning solution smell - which we later found out was a chemical used by an exterminator to kill a COCKROACH INFESTATION.
Some of the main points:
- Dozens of dead cockroaches in every room.
- Mouldy food left down the side of the refrigerator.
- A pair of dirty underpants and empty water bottle under the bed which we assume were from the previous guests.
- The spare pillows provided were disgusting and brown stained, and stored in a closet which had mould on the walls.
- A strong smell of vomit & faeces from the living room storage cupboard with fan, blankets and pillows (we assume the smell came from the unwashed blankets).
- Out of date/used food left in the refrigerator from previous guests
- David knew there was a cockroach infestation but did not tell us and let us book the apartment anyway
- Outside stairs were dirty and not very well kept
- Bathrooms were dirty and had no toilet paper
David was also initially very rude, after sending multiple pictures of dead cockroaches he responded with "What are you trying to show me?" and proceeded to tell us he in fact knew about the COCKROACHES and then suggested someone came around to vacuum up some of the dead cockroaches. Before we entered the property David assured us that the property would be clean, but it was dirty and unhospitable.
There is nothing more disgusting than hearing the sound of dead crunchy cockroaches underneath your feet. We initially decided not to heed the warning of a previous guest who mentioned a cockroach infestation, but we should have.
We have picture proof of everything mentioned including our message chat with David, however we cannot upload them to our review.
items.one_line: Host tells guests to lie to HOA about stay

items.comment_full:
I would not recommended this place, we left our stay early for multiple reasons

As we checked in, we were told to make sure we bring 30 Euro in cash, s
items.comment_full:
If all you care about is a great location, this is the room for you.
If you care about a hot shower, a friendly host, cleanliness, or not being pandered for extra money in cash when you arrive… look elsewhere. And don’t worry, I’m sure she will make up lies in her response to this, as she has done with many others. Just wait.
Like you, I saw good reviews, fell in love with the view from the window (not a balcony as she says in her description, and then says “is not a balcony” in her response to a bad review)
As we checked in, we were told to make sure we bring 30 Euro in cash, so that we can pay her mother.. sorry.. her “cleaning service”. This is not outlined in her charges as Air BNB policy dictates, but rather is carefully hidden in the last line of a VERY long description. By design I’m sure. She makes sure to tell you in cash, because Air BNB “charges a fee”. When we declined, her customer service skills went out that beautiful window. She responded that we could in fact pay through Air BNB, she would just be charging us more.
We rarely heard back from her for anything we needed the rest of the trip. What’s worse, is the room was anything but clean. Piles of dust in the corners, disgusting stains in the bed, and blankets that smelled anything but clean. Definitely not the extra “Covid ready” checks she describes. She threw a sheet over the couch to hide the stains on that also.
The building is antiquated, with a beautiful view, but that’s all it has going for it. She has pictures of herself, and her personal belongings EVERYWHERE. As if she still lives here in between guests. The shower has 5 mins of hot water tops. Once you use that, you won’t get another warm shower for 6 hours minimum. Great if you’re alone, but if you’re a couple, you’d better shower at different times of the day.
We never saw Vita (the mother) again after check in. Which was ok because she doesn’t speak a word of English anyway, and we speak very little Italian. Definitely not her fault. Francesca manages the listing virtually from who knows where, and is by far the rudest host we’ve ever had the displeasure of working with.
Rome is beautiful, and this room is in a great central location, but most of these rooms are smitten by that fact and overlook the disgusting truths. We spent way too much for 3 nights here, and absolutely should have gone elsewhere. Save yourself the trouble and headache.
items.one_line: Host demands cash for hidden cleaning fee, no hot water

items.comment_full: Regrettably this was our first unpleasant and unfortunate abnb experience. The apartment is three flights of steep stairs up and although pleasant looking, with a grocery store close by, it is very sparingly equipped in terms of basic kitchen equipment, crockery, cutlery, cleaning materials and has only one chair with desk. One bed, one coach and two bar stools. Amenities, such as the toilet seat, desk, one of the two barstools and plumbing were broken. We had booked for a 29 day stay, but stayed only three nights due to a really bad cockroach infestation which was such that it would require professional fumigation. The host argued that we didn't allow him time to address the issues before we left but we had been communicating with his manager by cellphone. A mistake we won't make again. We tried solving the problems ourselves but it became intolerable.  We could not be expected to stay in an apartment during a professional insect fumigation, which was later offered. We left after conversation with the host and his manager. Two hours after we left, we were offered another apartment. Why this was not done at the outset if it was available, we don't know and we had already paid a hotel, as we  were stranded and needed to go somewhere. The host was not pleasant and refused  any kind of mediated agreement on a refund . He argued cockroaches could happen to any host and believed we were unreasonable as it was high season and he would be prejudiced.  We suffered financially and had to find alternative accommodation in high season too. We were eventually repaid only 40% of the money paid, by Abnb. The manner of interaction by the host himself was most unpleasant and reproachful and contradictory to what his manager communicated. We hope the issues are fixed for any other future guests, information provided in full and remember to communicate only on the abnb platform!
items.one_line: Booked 29 days, stayed 3, cockroach infestation too extreme

However, when we reached the immigration at Suvarnabhumi Airport, the immigration staff asked for a photo of the host’s government ID during the process of applying visa-on-arrival. I called and sent mes
items.comment_full:
*WARNING* YOU COULD BE FUSED TO ENTRY INTO THAILAND
This was the worst Airbnb experience for me.
WHAT HAPPENED:
Upon reservation, the host told me Airbnb is legal in Thailand, but a TM30 form is needed. So we provide our information for filling the form. Also, the host told me only his name, contact number and address of the house should be provided for passing the immigration.
However, when we reached the immigration at Suvarnabhumi Airport, the immigration staff asked for a photo of the host’s government ID during the process of applying visa-on-arrival. I called and sent messages to the host but he just won’t pick up my calls.
After we stuck in the immigration about 1 hour, the host replied to me in Airbnb (he said his phone ran out of battery first but later he said he is without a mobile phone, which is a self-contradiction).
The host said he has hosted more than 7000 groups of guests in four years but all other guests can enter Thailand without a photo of his government ID and asking why we would need it? So I tried to ask different staffs in the immigration, but all gave me the same answer, it is a must for the host’s government ID.
I tried to reason with the host but he kept disentangling himself from our situation. He thinks we are criminals and said it is not his responsibility to help us. Upon my strong request, he sent me a photo of his government ID, which has a date stamp “30 DEC 2018 for my guests applying for visa”. From this watermark, it is easy to find he lied to me about “all other guests can enter Thailand without a photo of his government ID”. A self-contradiction again.
The funniest thing is the host refused to change the date stamp to the day we were stuck in the immigration. We tried to block the date stamp and passed the immigration eventually after a 3 or 4 hours delay in immigration.
MY THOUGHTS:
1. The host’s attitude is much below a “Superhost”. He wasn’t for helping us solve the situation but only like “don’t bother me”. He thinks the immigration refused us to entry because we are criminals, LOL.

2. The host also lied about the “hosted more than 7000 groups of guests in four years but all other guests can enter Thailand without a photo of his government ID” thing, at least some ppl in DEC 2018 asked for his government ID and he gave it.
I totally understand his concerns about sending a photo of his government ID to others. But I don’t get it as he has sent to others before. And he sent me one photo with a wrong date stamp but refused to change it to the correct date.
3. It could be just bad luck for us be asked to provide the host’s government ID. But at least the staff in immigration told me it is a must. So consider it before booking. If you are going for the visa-on-arrival, I strongly don’t recommend you book this. Imagine you are at immigration but the host refuses to provide it to you, then you can do nothing but fly back and ruin the vacation.
ONE MORE THING: there were birds waste on bedsheets, we found it on the first night, didn’t tell the host since he’s got irresponsible attitude. Not sure if it was there at check-in or some birds snapped in.
items.one_line: Host unreachable at immigration, contradicts self about dead phone battery

Open the door, I’m in Room 3!
items.comment_full: First off, the location of this place was great, and five minutes away from LAX. The room itself was clean and tidy and the bathroom was ok. The reason I’m giving it 3/5 stars is because of the other guests that stayed during our time there. The main problem came our first night, when we heard loud banging at the front door at around 2 AM. It woke us up. My boyfriend and I ignored it initially. Then, the person came to our window and began slamming his fist on the glass. It was scary, we almost called the police. He yelled, “I know you’re in there, I can hear you! Open the door, I’m in Room 3! Open the door right now!” My boyfriend went around and confirmed he was another guest before letting him in. I know this wasn’t the host’s fault, but this experience really made us feel unsafe and I did not appreciate the lack of privacy and courtesy. The man could easily see through the blinds of our room and was yelling at us when we were sleeping. It was not something I was prepared for and we had difficulty falling asleep after this encounter and felt extremely uncomfortable. The man seemed aggressive and hostile. Additionally, no other guest respected the quiet hours and were banging dishes around and slamming doors at 1 AM both nights of our stay. We got little to no good sleep both nights. The neighborhood, while close to many things, is quite sketchy and I was not aware of this before our stay. We also knew the bathroom would be shared, but not by two other sets of guests. Anyway, the stay was overall unpleasant, but I can’t blame the host for the guests’ behavior. If the guests’ were not so inconsiderate this place would be 5/5.
items.one_line: Guest pounded door at 2am, slammed window screaming

items.comment:

items.comment_full:
Ella and Tomas were very nice people, the place is very affordable (considering it is in Gothic Quarter) and the location is fairly close to the beaches.
However, an event happened which makes our stay far from ideal: the hosts decided to THROW A PARTY on the second night without any notice in advance (that is, before we checked in).
There was no mentioning of it from the moment I booked the place until the very moment we arrived, then we were informed that they would host a party the second night from 7pm-11pm. (we would cancel the stay if we knew this in advance.)
We were expecting a small, private party, but turned out close to 15 people showed up and (of course) the whole thing lasted until 2:30am WITH LOUD MUSIC. We planned to visit a fairly faraway attraction the next day so we decided to sleep early, but we ended up having no choice to sleep until basically 3am in the morning.
Some further problems:
(1) The door is essentially two very thin slides of wood and cannot be locked from the inside. Now imagine there are 15+ tipsy people just outside of your door who dance and shout;
(2) There is only one bathroom. Under normal circumstance, it would be totally fine. But the night when the party took place, we could not take a shower or even brush our teeth, because the party guests kept knocking on the door;
(3) Ella and Tomas remained very well behaved during the party, but I cannot say the same about some of the party guests, who got very rude when we tried to walk past them and get some water from the kitchen (for god's sake);
(4) While some basic tidy-up was done immediately after the party, the wine glasses were not washed until 24 hours later which stank.
I consider myself a reasonable person, and every time I use Airbnb I would respect the culture of the hosts and their customs. However, we did pay an amount of money to stay there (don't see us as customers, see us as clients), and we expected a lot better than this.
Again, I don't wish this review to be personal: Ella and Tomas were indeed very nice and offered to give us some tips regarding Barcelona. However, I regrettably have to give a negative feedback for our stay because of the event.
items.one_line: Host threw secret party; fifteen guests until 2:30am

The guests in the other unit were very loud, keeping us up past 1am multiple nigh
items.comment_full: Overall, this place felt clean for the most part, the mattresses were great, and the neighborhood was quiet. However, during our stay, the air conditioning did not work. We reached out to the host and after a bit of back and forth, she said she would send out maintenance but never did and never checked back in about it, and there were no ceiling fans to accommodate for the broken AC. We noticed holes in our sheets that had been taped over, and we found a dead cockroach under one bed. The walls are also very thin. The guests in the other unit were very loud, keeping us up past 1am multiple nights. The private office pictured in the listing actually isn’t in the unit at all. It’s in a separate building off the backyard but it was locked for us and we couldn’t access it. The wifi quality is very poor and we actually ran out of toilet paper, having to buy our own roll to finish the trip. This place has promise, but neglected by the host. We hope your experience is better than ours was!
items.one_line: No AC, dead cockroach, holes taped in sheets, no office

items.comment_full:
First of all, the views really are SO stunning!! They continued to blow me away throughout my stay. I will be honest and say that I was surprised when I first arrived in the apartment because the photos on the listing must have been taken quite a while ago: the furniture, linens, and layout of the place have been changed, so it didn't look as I'd pictured, and the apt is kind of worse for the wear. For example, the curtains are quite torn and stained, but they still work. The sliding screen door to the patio is slightly broken and torn, but not a big problem. The place had been cleaned but not deep-cleaned, so deep-set grime in the tub and bathroom sink, etc. (I cleaned some of it after I arrived). But I quickly stopped caring about these things because of the views.
The peafowl really are very loud, though beautiful. Not very walkable, but I got groceries delivered. Multiple days of maintenance and fire alarm testing during my stay, but rarely heard neighbors. Beautiful setting.
items.one_line: Peafowl very loud and beautiful, stunning views though

items.comment:
Really cool hostel with lovely staff, but I had a lunatic in my room. First of all, this place is in a great location, has lovely staff members and genuinely has great facilities, I would highly recommend it.
However, I was unlucky enough to be blessed with a guest who played music on her phone, sang to herself and drunk baileys from the bottle by her bed. This is not the hostels fault, but a bad experience for me.
I complained to reception, politely as I know it isn’t their fault, and mgmt came to the room, however this was after 30 minutes and 3 of us had left the room.
Managem

items.comment_full:
We would not recommend this listing to anyone. We chose Frank’s listing as it was advertised to have a kitchen. This was important as we couldn’t afford to eat out every day. When we arrived the kitchen was filthy and at the time Frank acknowledged this and joked about how he should clean it. As well as this the bathroom was quite dirty. The kitchen however was never cleaned once, the sink was constantly filled to the brim with dirty dishes to the point that it was unusable. There were rotting vegetables on the counter which attracted fruit flys. When we opened the oven it had used tinfoil that had clearly been there for a long time, the knobs were sticky and the hob had no extractor fan which meant the kitchen filled up with steam very quickly. The fridge also had rotting vegetables and a terrible smell.
On one morning of our visit, my friend with whom I was staying was in our room in the apartment while I was out. Frank called from the hall so she went out and he mentioned to her that the toilet was blocked. At his request she went and looked at it. The toilet was flushing slowly. Frank then accused her of using feminine products to block the toilet. He pointed and shouted and got her to use the brush to try unblock the toilet despite her having said no such incident had occurred. Frank was very aggressive in the conversation. At this point she mentioned the obvious uncleanliness of the bathroom in general particularly the toilet bowel.
We reported these incidents to Airbnb with photographic proof and they confirmed it was not up to standard and offered us a refund for the rest of the nights if we wished to leave. However we decided to stay as we could not afford to get anywhere else in the city.
After reporting it to Airbnb, Frank knocked on my door while my friend was out and asked me to speak to him. He asked why we had mentioned these issues to Airbnb and I pointed out the visible uncleanliness of the kitchen and why this was frustrating as we had picked this place because a kitchen was listed. Frank told me that Airbnb had requested some of the money for the cleaning fee back and that he wasn’t going to pay it. Frank then said he would prefer us to leave and I told him that we could not afford to go elsewhere. He then mentioned the bathroom incident and I said that the toilet was visibly unclean before we got there, it had clearly not been our fault as there were no “feminine products” in toilet and that the way he had treated my friend was aggressive and had created an uncomfortable atmosphere in the apartment. After this he told me aggressively that we had to pack up our things and leave, which obviously upset me as it left myself and my friend, two young women on their own, without somewhere to stay.
I went back into the room and Frank followed me shouting about how we were a disgrace and how dare we make a report to Airbnb. He continued to shout at me through the door after I had closed it while being very visibly upset. I instantly contacted Airbnb to explain the situation and we were put in contact with the extremely helpful Marcos who had already been taking our case.
After a long period of calls from Airbnb to us and to Frank, Airbnb told us that had Frank had said to them that we were allowed to continue to stay, while he was saying to us that he didn’t want us there. At this point we also felt extremely unsafe in the apartment after the aggression we had experienced. After another confrontation with Frank where he reiterated that he didn’t want us there and angrily shouted at us, we knew it was unsafe for us to remain here. Airbnb were helpful with trying to find us another place but because Frank told them he would let us stay, as he clearly did not want to refund all the money, we could not be refunded for the whole trip if we left, only for the remaining days. This meant we could not afford to stay anywhere else.
Luckily a contact has managed to put us up for a few days but it was quite a frightening and unsettling experience especially we are two young women in foreign city.
items.one_line: Filthy kitchen, fruit flies, host calls from hallway, porter kicks out guests

When trying to get some fresh air in the room, it was filled with the smell of dough which is baked throughout the night - lovely for the first 3 minutes, unbearable thereafter.
At around 5am someone was picking up the garbage, around 7am they got delivery of the new raw materials.
From then on, they constantly transported goods and produce between the baking facility and the sales room, smashing heavy steel do
items.comment_full:
I was very surprised when i noticed that the flat is linked in the patio with the production facility of a bakery. Their washing room/toilet is just right-below the bedroom.
When trying to get some fresh air in the room, it was filled with the smell of dough which is baked throughout the night - lovely for the first 3 minutes, unbearable thereafter.
At around 5am someone was picking up the garbage, around 7am they got delivery of the new raw materials.
From then on, they constantly transported goods and produce between the baking facility and the sales room, smashing heavy steel doors and dumped waste into the garbage which is pretty much in front of the flat.
I have talked to one resident at the entrance and I feel extremely sorry for the ones living there and worse the ones who have invested into property.
Probably I would also try to rent it out on short term but at least openly mention it in the advertising.
I was keen to leave the apartment but the host talked me into staying (and resolve the issue with the bakery).
Of cause nothing changed so I left on my own the next day after not having slept for 2 nights and claimed my money back.
I was still forced to pay for x nights, so he has coverage until he finds new "guests".
So, some money lost but at least I found afterwards a wonderful flat and finally found some sleep.
And a lesson learned: NEVER, EVER, EVER AGAIN!!!
Apart of that the lightbulbs were much darker than on the fotos and the curtains were only covering 4/5th of the window.
I sat in underwear at the table but quickly decided to change, once noticing that I am facing the other flats on the floors above.
Needless to say that certain other small advertised items were missing (shampoo and soap) or in ridiculous amounts (2 hangers for your 1 month garderobe).
Oh, and mentioning hangers - when I arrived I was trying to find something like a wardrobe.
The first one I opened was just covering the naked drainpipe of the flats above (unfortuantely in the bedroom - and you know what that means).
The second was the storage room for the vacuum cleaner.
(In case you are so unlucky and book the flat - the "wardrobe" is a bar hanging behind the kitchen. But bring your own hangars!)
The overall cleaningness was ok but the shower curtain was very yellow-brownish at the bottom. Usually not a good sign.
I was renting temporary places almost troughhout the whole last 2 years of Covid and never experienced anything close to that.
items.one_line: Bakery neighbors dump waste, smash steel doors, pick garbage at 5am

items.comment: Where do I even begin? This place is not easy to find, especially by car! Furthermore, although it is a great location close to many sights, it is in a dark, creepy alley.  The place was not very clean or sanitized. There were labels over the toilets saying they met the criteria, but when we lifted one of them up, there was poop in the toilet. The place smelled like cigarettes upon entering, and I found hairs on the bidet, sink, and bathtub of which I have photos. I have photos of the things in the house to prove it was not cleaned. They clean it themselves and not up to a very good standard.
items.comment_full: Where do I even begin? This place is not easy to find, especially by car! Furthermore, although it is a great location close to many sights, it is in a dark, creepy alley.  The place was not very clean or sanitized. There were labels over the toilets saying they met the criteria, but when we lifted one of them up, there was poop in the toilet. The place smelled like cigarettes upon entering, and I found hairs on the bidet, sink, and bathtub of which I have photos. I have photos of the things in the house to prove it was not cleaned. They clean it themselves and not up to a very good standard. They also use the same sponge in the kitchen to clean the dishes and leave for each guest instead of leaving a new one, and you can tell it's been used.  Once I started to mention some of these things, they were nice at first and offered for their son to clean it the next day. We did not feel comfortable because all of our stuff was already laid out and I let her know this. Then they became very aggressive and didn't understand why they couldn't come to check it out. I again explained that we didn't feel comfortable (also, her son is the one who smelled like cigarettes) and we also cleaned it because we had to use the facilities. After that, if I had a question about something, they did not answer. I was really excited to use the laundry but my clothes did not come out dry and there is no other option on the machine to dry them. I tried contacting Maria but received no response on this matter. I thought they would give us a partial refund, but they have not so far.  I know there are many great reviews on here, but most are old. This place needs a deep clean and proper sanitization between guests.  Honestly, I know people have had different experiences, but we stayed at 7 different places in 12 days and this was the worst. You can do better in my opinion.
items.one_line: Unflushed toilet, same sponge for all guests

items.comment_full:
Dear Inai,
With all due respect I must say absolutely not to your request for monies.
If we must speak earnestly; let us begin with you should fire your housekeeper for horrendous job she does and the fact that she charges you is robbery.
Let’s start there.
Now, I would like you to close your eyes and picture what I experienced upon checking into your Airbnb:
I was exhausted from an 8 hour flight that was delayed deplaning one more hour in Paris.
Then another hour and a half in traffic to arrive in Paris center. All I wanted was to shower and sleep.
Upon entering your place, the terrible smell was immediate but I figured that opening the living room window would alleviate it. It didn’t.
Then I undressed and showered- there was no bath mat so I used the darkest towel to step on- so that I would not slip and fall. I realized the towels although new were unwashed with the paper tags still on them. Not very comfortable but I knew your intention was in the right place.
Now imagine that I just want to take my medicine and sleep, so I open a bottle of water and get into bed.
Where the sheets, pillows, and duvet STINK of other peoples’s body odors and scalp.
I was trying to overlook that bad smell in the apartment but now I had dirty smelling bed linens to deal with too. I was so exhausted that I actually considered sleeping for a few hours to get the energy needed before moving out. However, it was so bad smelling and uncomfortable that I had to get up and leave right there and then. The bad smells were stuck in my sinuses.
Of course, this would have been even easier to do if your apartment had had wifi but even without knowing where I was going, even without a taxi, even with exhaustion/jetlag, even without knowing if I would be refunded my money;
I GOT THE HELL OUT OF YOUR APARTMENT.
I am sorry to have to tell you this about how you live or about how your maid takes advantage of you-
But I will not have demands made on me for your lack of cleanliness or responsibility.
I have been a Supehost on Airbnb for years. I know what it takes to make guests comfortable and happy.
items.one_line: Unwashed towels with tags, moldy shower, terrible smell, housekeeper robbery

items.comment:
I'm having a hard time writing this. I don't want to be harsh because I felt there were good intentions, and Yumi and Gen were very responsive and did their best to make us comfortable. However I was also very frustrated by the following things:

- The bed near the bathroom smelled of pee.

- The hot-tub was empty and dirty when we arrived. Took 3 communications to get it cleaned and set up for use. Apparently it's been leaking for 2 months. I ended up not wanting to use it because I felt bad about the whole interaction, but at least the kids enjoyed it.

- Ceilin
items.comment_full:
I'm having a hard time writing this. I don't want to be harsh because I felt there were good intentions, and Yumi and Gen were very responsive and did their best to make us comfortable. However I was also very frustrated by the following things:

- The bed near the bathroom smelled of pee.

- The hot-tub was empty and dirty when we arrived. Took 3 communications to get it cleaned and set up for use. Apparently it's been leaking for 2 months. I ended up not wanting to use it because I felt bad about the whole interaction, but at least the kids enjoyed it.

- Ceiling fans were dirty. We found this out when we turned them on and discovered that they also needed maintenance. I think someone came and fixed them since they stopped making noise when we tried them again later in the week.

- The oven never worked. I guess that's why it was being used to store pots and pans. That's probably also why there was a turkey in the freezer when we arrived. I never even mentioned it to Yumi since I figured she must already know. We used the toaster-oven and the grill instead.

- The grill had a lot of fat in it and was flaring badly. I decided to burn it clean but stupidly left it running when we went out for a couple of hours on Tuesday morning. Fortunately nothing bad happened, but I felt terrible.

- The tap water tasted a little funky. We tried the filter in the kitchen but it tasted worse. My guess is it hasn't been maintained either. We bought a case of bottled water.

- Having the handy-man arrive early on Wed morning and stare at my fiancé through the bedroom window (which is right beside the front door and has a gauzy kind of curtain) was not cool. I get it, she's beautiful. She's also trying to sleep in on her vacation. So was I when the guy arrived. Then Yumi & her SO arrived, the kids woke up and the sleeping in wasn't in the cards that morning. I did my best to be polite about the situation, and eventually they decided to come back later, but... wow.

- Cloths dryer failed on Wed or maybe Thurs night and then there was a whole bunch of communication about getting it replaced. That didn't happen while we were there, but fortunately there were plenty of towels and a mostly sheltered cloths-line out back.

I feel that the majority of the things listed above are the kind of maintenance that could and should have been handled before we ever arrived, rather than disrupting our vacation. Previous airbnb experiences have not have issues like this. On the positive side, I liked the layout of the kitchen, and while it's not mentioned in the listing, there's an awesome VitaMix blender. So... smoothies!
items.one_line: Bed smells like pee, hot tub empty two months, oven never worked

We arrived back at the apartment at 3:00( check in time) of which the cleaning lady was no where near completion.  Around 3:20 I texted Nelson saying this is unacc

items.comment_full:
Two stars. Not only are these people sketchy and tried to charge me $140 for 1 hand towel, 1 wash cloth and 2 pillowcases they say I took (??), their place has some serious issues you should be aware of before you book:
AC/Heat – while it does have AC and heat, you can’t really control it. There is literally a HOLE in the wall that goes all the way through to the neighbor’s apartment for both of your separate apartments to access the one AC/heat control module. So when the neighbor decides they wanna be warmer or colder, then you’re along for the ride. Predictably this system isn’t just annoying and uncomfortable, it means the AC/heat doesn’t even work right because it’s trying to control the climate of two completely different areas separated by a wall with a hole in it. For example, the neighbor loved to turn the heat on every morning regardless of the temperature, but when they turned it on to just 61, our place would get well over 70 degrees and we’d just have to open the door to not sweat because there’s just no way to control the actual temperature based on the temperature set on the module. And of course, you can imagine how private it feels knowing there’s a hole in the wall that goes into your neighbors’ place with a flimsy little door on it…
Kitchen/Refrigerator – this place claims to have a full kitchen, but it actually only has a tiny mini-fridge (which is really hard to control the temperature on anyway and not freeze things in the fridge section, etc). Everything else is mostly good but the stove only has 2 working burners – we didn’t need more, but be aware in case you do. We made do with the mini-fridge, but it became a much bigger problem when we got the Shelter in Place order for COVID-19 and couldn’t really stock up on food like we needed to.
NOISE – you will be woken up by leaf-blowing gardeners at 7:30am at least once a week. One time they were even on our roof blowing leaves off at 7am walking past the skylights. Besides that, you should know that this is smack dab next to one of the busiest intersections in LA (Barham is a constantly busy cut through between Burbank and Hollywood, and the 134 and 101 freeways). This wouldn’t probably be a problem if the owners opened their wallets to install proper double-paned windows like it needs, but it still has very old single-paned windows you can hear everything through. And the garbage trucks will come at 6:30am and drop the dumpster for the restaurant next door right outside your window (the restaurant is very good btw). Even with earplugs, this place is loud night and day.
Dust/Cleanliness - this place is very dusty, so if you have allergies like me, beware. The vents just shower the place in dust when the AC/heat is on. Just pretty dusty and dirty all around.
Generally this place smacks of a place where the owners wanna make a lot of money but not pay anything to actually do necessary improvements. This might not matter to you if you’re staying short-term and just looking for a place close to Universal (which it is), but longer term stays should be aware of the issues. Really nice neighborhoods to walk around, though.
items.one_line: Neighbors control your AC through literal hole in wall

items.comment_full:
Whilst I agree the views from Level 80 are spectacular, this room did not have the "executive feel" that we believed it showed in the photos.
I'll start with the kitchen and basic amenities.
There was only one chux cloth, no dishwashing liquid, no basic kitchen items like aluminium foul or cling wrap, and not one sharp knife to be found.
We had to try to cut out salami with a butter knife. It was horrible.
There was coffee, tea, salt and sugar provided. But no milk, no oil or butter, making it hard to say its an executive style.
The bedroom door did not lock. The latch was broken and closed but would not clasp. The bedroom window did not open either.
The bathroom only had three toilet rolls, which was not sufficient for three adults for three nights. The body wash provided was watered down, and was literally water. I had to open the new bottle provided and refill this.
The bathroom sink had a large mould and mildew ring around the sink plug area.
We moved the lounge backwards to make more space in the loungeroom and found multiple stains all over the floor.
The bedroom carpet was also heavily stained on the right hand side.
The tv showed a connection for Foxtel, but there was no Foxtel box. Only free to access TV.
We also had issues with checking in, with a line of miscommunication to collect the keys going backwards and forwards.
This was definitely frustrating when we were already in the city, but couldn't utilise the car parking as we couldn't collect the keys.
I would happily stay here again, however this is definitely not an executive style or feel room with the inclusions.
items.one_line: Salami cut with butter knife, moldy sink, watered shampoo

items.comment:
We have been to Maui several times and this was our first time at Donna’s. Her rental is nice and yes she obviously did some nice things there. She is also classified as a super-host but for us she was not. It was 1 day before our arrival and we had not received our passcode to enter the unit. She blamed Airbnb!!

After the first couple of days she sent us a message that she was on her way over to deliver 4 new bar stools to replace the 2 existing ones. We messaged back that we are not there but are on our way. We travel with a Wyze Cam (IP camera) so I logged in to discover
items.comment_full:
We have been to Maui several times and this was our first time at Donna’s. Her rental is nice and yes she obviously did some nice things there. She is also classified as a super-host but for us she was not. It was 1 day before our arrival and we had not received our passcode to enter the unit. She blamed Airbnb!!

After the first couple of days she sent us a message that she was on her way over to deliver 4 new bar stools to replace the 2 existing ones. We messaged back that we are not there but are on our way. We travel with a Wyze Cam (IP camera) so I logged in to discover she not only entered but she snooped around. This gave us a knot in our gut. If you're new to Airbnb or VRBO - HOSTS DO NOT ENTER UNDER ANY CIRCUMSTANCES UNLESS IT'S AN EMERGENCY OR THEY HAVE PERMISSION. She then sends a message that she missed us and was hoping to meet us. Really? Meet us?? … we just said we are on our way there!! We think she wanted to snoop around. She left after finding our camera – kinda hard to meet us after getting caught snooping n camera! A true super host would not have done what she did. We have paid money for privacy and she violated that privacy. We had things in our unit that are private! Then to add insult to injury she also said we had the AC too low at 61 degrees – which was not true. That really pissed us off. I have never in my life turned an AC down to 61 and I worked in refrigeration for a few years so I understand cooling. If you read other reviews, they mention how it does not cool the unit. Future renter's should note that her AC is inadequate as it only cools the living room and kitchen area - it does not cool the bedroom.

During our 9 day stay we had 1 cockroach each day for the first 3 days. Yes we have pictures and videos. They were small and dying (on their backs) so at least we know that she sprays. We did not mention this to her as we do understand they are common in tropical areas. We also ran out of TP (1 roll provided) shampoo, and conditioner. So I messaged her because her amenities stated they are provided. She responded that her cleaner puts out only enough to get everyone started, but generally guests bring their own. I double checked and she lists those items as being provided. Not sure why she is not providing them. Not wanting to meet or deal with her (because of the entry) we just purchased them. She did offer to get them and claimed to have stopped by as she left a weird voicemail (awkward stuttering ) that she just stopped by but the screen door was locked. We were there and nobody knocked. Not sure if she's being honest. It's obvious that she's knows we had a camera or she would have just walked in.

In the meantime, we were doing laundry and the loads were taking about 5+ hours to complete which is unreal. She has an all in 1 unit (washer that dries) and we just thought that's how they work. This was frustrating to deal with every day. Unbeknownst to us something was wrong. On day 7 we grilled a large Mahi-mahi dinner and had a load of laundry going. While eating dinner the washer flooded the bathroom, hallway, and into the kitchen. We stopped eating and cleaned up the mess. Yes we have video and pictures. We immediately contacted her and she basically asked us to clean it up. She told us to use the complex washers and dryers. We think her washer was not draining - which is why it was taking so long and eventually flooded.

We came to Maui for our daughter’s wedding and regret getting this condo. We were not happy with it and not happy with Donna. This experience has left us with a bad taste in our mouth. We spent over a week on the other side of Maui and our host gave us a partial refund for stuff out of their control. We did not request the refund or say anything to them … they just did the right thing.

We have had several good and great experiences with other hosts on Airbnb and VRBO. All those host had great communication and understood privacy.
items.one_line: Host snoops with camera footage while guests are away

I would have picked a different place, but it would be a great fit for young adults who are unbothered by smoke smell and want to stay out late. The location was very convenient. items.comment_full:
Do NOT stay here. Laura was great and the location is fantastic, being next to cafes, a supermarket, the metro, and a cab stand. However that’s where the nice things I have to say end.
A/C didn’t work, had terrible air flow because the bedroom windows had to remain shut or the smell of the alley would taint the whole place, and had visible mold. I was ok, but my 3 friends all left with a bad cough we think from this.
The plumbing system doesn’t have water in the pipes to prevent backed up… “waste”… air from coming into apartment whenever anyone uses the plumbing. For example, if any of us took a shower or even washed our hands, the entire place reeked of… “waste.”
My friends and I had to buy candles and fans to offset stagnant, moldy air. Most comfortable sleeping arrangement wasn’t any of the 3 bedrooms, but was instead the couch by the open balcony.
I generally don’t like leaving negative reviews of places, but this apartment felt like a biohazard charging 5 star pricing.
items.one_line: Mold, broken AC, sewage stench, biohazard apartment

items.comment_full: Dominika let me rent a room which was adjacent to hers. The room was big and the bed, which was a switchblade sofa-bed type, was alright, though not very comfortable. At first, Dominika was very sociable, perhaps too sociable. She began to tell me personal details about her life, to which I patiently listened. She knew a bit about my personal reasons for coming, and there seemed to be some resonance and empathy between us. She was very friendly, even intimate with me, during the first 3 days. However, I soon began to withdraw, as I was very preoccupied with my own reasons for visiting. This was when some trouble began: she began to complain that I wasn't clean enough, that I left the toilet dirty, that I left the bath dirty, that I threw garbage in the wrong places, that I fell asleep with the lights on etc. she even complained I had the alarm set in the morning because she could hear it as the walls are paper thin. I tried to explain to her that I keep things generally clean and tidy but that it might not be to her standards, which is not my fault. I am, after all, a guest. I also did not deny that I misplaced an item in the wrong garbage bin. However, this did not repeat itself. Even if it did, I should not have been treated the way i have: she argued, bickered, moaned and complained constantly, accusing me and even one time very hysterically shouted at me for waking her up because of my alarm clock - something I should have supposedly coordinated with her. This is strange, as I shouldn't give a detailed plan for my stay to my host. I stayed for a long time - 9 nights - and she was not used for such a duration apparently. She began to act as if i am somehow disturbing her constantly, invading her private space etc., when all I did was sit in one of her couches which are near the kitchen, which I am allowed to use (it is even posted in the pics as a space one can use). She also had 5 more guests coming and going in the interval of my stay: 1 lady, 2 school girls and one married couple. She obviously overbooked, sharing her apt with up to 3 new people at a time. I generally disliked my stay during the latter half of my visit. I found her difficult to handle, overly sensitive, slightly hysteric. She did not make me feel at home, although i paid a fee which was very close to a three or even four star hotel (without breakfast or special amenities). The apt is generally clean: the bath especially. The toilet is also neat. The kitchen well equipped. However, the floor is only vacuumed, not cleaned with a mop. If you look for privacy, given the paper thin walls, you should look elsewhere. However, you can...
items.one_line: Host complains about alarm sound through paper-thin walls

I also lugged my bags up four flights of stairs because it seemed the elevator didn't work. It works, everyone. It's just slow to warm. I got a workout. No big deal. I do, however, find a house manual might've been helpful for staying here (hope I didn't mis
items.comment_full:
Rhea was wonderful at accommodating my not yet solidified business travel. Key retrieval was easy enough (bonus points that the person handing off the keys was gorgeous).

I imagine this is my lack of New Yorkness (though I'm no stranger to living in big cities), but it took me a while to figure out how to get in the front door. I also lugged my bags up four flights of stairs because it seemed the elevator didn't work. It works, everyone. It's just slow to warm. I got a workout. No big deal. I do, however, find a house manual might've been helpful for staying here (hope I didn't miss it!) - how do I get in the door, where are the lights, what should I do with the garbage, where can I find more toilet paper?

Since Rhea lives here, I found all the kitchen items I needed for making tea and cooking. There was plenty of space to spread out (I don't like to put things away like some folks do when traveling) and I found the place adorable, comfortable and homey, just like I like it. The until it blew, the bed side lamp was a nice touch for reading in bed!

The 24 hour market around the corner had everything for quick items or fresh juice, which was lovely. The entire area was walkable (I only ate once in the immediate area at Emily's, which was delicious. Try the burger but split it with a group as an appetizer. It's good, but not THAT good!), it was perfect having the subway so close (I didn't take one taxi to get around), and it was diverse and neighborhoody. I had even planned to buy myself flowers and found some already on the table (probably weren't for me, but they were nice).

Now the PSAs: I found no microwave, which for some... And this place gets warm, even with the fan and window unit. I like my AC at 62 every night even in winter so take that for what it's worth. I was able to sleep well enough, but I was definitely warm.

Second, if you are a light sleeper, bring earplugs and/or an eye mask. It's not a raucous place by far. But it is a city apartment where it's normal to be intimate with your neighbors at some level. I slept with soundscapes or earplugs and was not too disturbed during sleep or in the morning. I found a cover tent helped with the light.

There's a noshoesinside policy. As a fellow inside non-shoer, I urge this to be respected. For the primpers, though, that means no checking out your outfit with your shoes. Just so you know.

Overall, just what I needed for my first trip to NY. I would stay here again for sure!
items.one_line: Confused about front door, slow elevator, gorgeous key person

I hosted a weekend of photoshoots here, and the space was stylish enough with gorgeous lighting, but as far as accommodating a stay, I'd say the amenities were subpar. 
If you are like me and cook most of your food, you'll be struggling in the kitchen, which is about as big as a dorm kitchen. The sink is the size of a single textbook, making washing pans a li
items.comment_full:
The place is great if all you need it for is a place to crash, but don't have many requirements. Douglas is a great host who is very communicative and willing to help in all situations. The apartment itself could use some work though. I hosted a weekend of photoshoots here, and the space was stylish enough with gorgeous lighting, but as far as accommodating a stay, I'd say the amenities were subpar. 
If you are like me and cook most of your food, you'll be struggling in the kitchen, which is about as big as a dorm kitchen. The sink is the size of a single textbook, making washing pans a little messy. Additionally, the stove was covered in grease and stains, which is preventable if it was cleaned in between guests. The sink also smells, which I'm sure could be fixed with a drain deodorizer, again, something the cleaners could easily take care of.
I moved the rug around a bit, and unfortunately had to vacuum (another cheap dorm size item) under as it was like the dirt had been literally swept under the rug. The mirror in the guest bathroom (not shown in listing) needs to be replaced or cleaned, and the handle on the tub is broken. One of the windows in the living room is cracked, but I did appreciate the ability to open the windows (many places, especially hotels don't allow high rise windows to be opened), and let some air in while I was trying to figure out the thermostat.
The lights are in weird places for the different rooms, which took me about 2 days to figure out which switch went where, and the thermostat is oddly located in the hallway. The TV also didn't work - not a big deal, but would have been nice for background noise.
The coffee table shows visible signs of wear, and should probably be resurfaced or replaced.
The washer took 3 hours for a small load, (a huge waste of water) and is combined with the drier, which I didn't realize on my first attempt at using it.
The beds are great, and there are lots of extra pillows that are not terribly comfortable, but no extra pillowcases.
Another big thing was the lack of a first aid kit, because when you need it, you NEED it.
Some minor things included needing to buy a gallon of water as the tap water is terrible, and the foot pedal of the trashcan did not work. The listing also states that this is on the 8th floor, but it's on the 7th, if you care. I don't, but upon rereading the listing, I noticed that was inaccurate.
I think this place has a lot of potential if some work is put into it. I have stayed in a lot of bnb's in DC, and this one doesn't quite meet the standards of most DC stays.
items.one_line: Tiny sink, greasy stove, smelly drain, dorm-sized vacuum

I tried to shut it off and brown water with chunks continued to pour out of the faucet onto the floor and onto the counter. I woke my partner, (who is a builder by trade) He turned the water valve off under the sink. He explained that the rubber gasket in the handle must have disintegrated, it would have to be replaced. I had a rag/towel of my own that I brought for such things as cleaning shoes
items.comment_full: Very early in the morning I lifted the single handled faucet on the tap of the bathroom faucet to wash my hands, and brown water started running out around the connection of the faucet handle and base. I tried to shut it off and brown water with chunks continued to pour out of the faucet onto the floor and onto the counter. I woke my partner, (who is a builder by trade) He turned the water valve off under the sink. He explained that the rubber gasket in the handle must have disintegrated, it would have to be replaced. I had a rag/towel of my own that I brought for such things as cleaning shoes, so I used this to clean up the mess including the water that had ran into the cabinet drawers to avoid any damage to the wood. The next morning I waited until a little after 9 to go tell Maria (living downstairs) what happened, because I didn’t want to wake her too early. However I did want to give her time to fix it before we left so her new guests, the next day would not be inconvenienced. Maria came to the apartment first, and while we were waiting for her husband I explained to her very nicely that something smelled bad in the kid’s room. I could not pinpoint the location of the smell, but wanted to make her aware in case possibly a child had stayed prior to us and wet the bed or something. I told her it was not a problem at all, I just wanted her to be aware of it. I did not mean to offend her, but apparently I did. When Maria showed her husband the sink and translated the problem, he remained calm, but he was giving me very angry looks and Maria translated that I had broke the faucet and would need to pay to replace it. This conversation continued for a bit and then my partner stepped in and said, no, we were not going to pay for it. The faucet broke through normal wear and tear and I did nothing to cause this. My partner tried to use an analogy and explain that after years of use an item would break and that was normal and it was nobody’s fault. This did not translate well and Maria’s husband took it to mean the faucet was very old. He was pretty angry. My partner was angry as well and told them, fine, we will pay for it and write about this in our review. It was not a pleasant interaction at all. I had intended to tell Maria we were having trouble with her coffee maker as well, but thought better of it by this point. I was actually thinking I might need to pay for that, as it seemed to work for the first few minutes and then no longer would. As coffee makers are a more intricate machine, I could understand we may...
items.one_line: Brown water explosion from bathroom tap, flooded cabinet

items.comment_full:
We were booked to stay a month, but cut it short due to:
- a leak above the bed which wasn't dealt with over two days (we moved out after this so not sure if it's been fixed)
- mouse in flat
- overhead shower head had not been cleaned so the individual holes were blocked. Rather than unblocking the limescale, 6 holes had been drilled into the shower head which meant water went everywhere as it came out of the top as well as the bottom of the shower head
- Washing machine was not in the flat but in the basement of the entire building. This is not mentioned in the listing. Not a very pleasant space, lots of mice droppings and a leaking pipe, next to electric wires. Very unsafe.
- The flat is small but very stylish but it needs maintenance - wobbly shelf in kitchen etc
I would not recommend this flat.
It is expensive, and not worth the money.
The owner did refund the weeks we cancelled, and paid £155.99 in compensation.
items.one_line: Leak, mice, drilled shower, basement washing in hazard

items.comment_full: The location was convenient for our purposes. However, it is a bit of a trek to the subway. The main issues were the inaccuracy of the listing, cleanliness, and overall feel of the apartment. The listing stated there were four beds but this was not the case. There are multiple photos of the main bedroom from many different angles making it appear as if there are more beds than there really are. One of the “beds” turned out to be the 30+year old red sofa pictured in the living room. This may have once been a functional hide-a-bed but instead it was non-functional, the tiny mattress was filthy and urine-stained, and there were no bed linens or pillows available for it until we asked for them late at night. It was communicated to the host that there were four of us who would be using the space and that all four beds were needed. This issue was never addressed prior to booking. The host was somewhat accommodating and was able to provide a cheap cot-sized roll-away bed the next day. However, this was placed in such a way in front of the sofa that essentially made the living room unusable except for sleeping. The apartment is very old and looks it. The windows throughout the apartment were in various states of disrepair and many did not have adequate drapes or shutters to block out the sunlight when needed. The one small bathroom does not look to be up to code with regard to ventilation and the shower curtain barely reached down to the tub, making it easy for water to leak onto the floor. The drain in the sink was inoperable for filling it to shave and the newly installed toilet paper holder was upside down and unable to hold the roll effectively. The Keurig coffee maker did not work properly and the kitchen is outfitted at a very basic level. Overall, the place was a bit of a dump and in need of a major remodel to be considered suitable in our opinion for Airbnb. The price for the space was outrageous given the quality and inaccuracy of the listing. Nowhere else in the country except in NYC could anyone get away with charging so much for so little. Honestly we are not sure how their rating on here is so high. There are very few options in this area of Brooklyn… in this case we wish we would have just stayed in a hotel. It would have been cheaper!
items.one_line: Sofa bed urine-stained, broken, requires emergency cot

items.comment: At first I thought the hosts were nice. I booked the flat for 77 days. However, after that I got one disappointment after the other. I noticed the “new” furniture when I arrived, it was missing a desk and other furniture which I needed, and they also added a new bed with an extremely thin and uncomfortable mattress. Also, the flat was not properly cleaned when I got it, dirty windows, b
items.comment_full: At first I thought the hosts were nice. I booked the flat for 77 days. However, after that I got one disappointment after the other. To the point of the hosts insulting me calling me a pig in a private message on Airbnb. For starters, they changed the furniture without updating the photos or notifying me, they updated them only after I left. I noticed the “new” furniture when I arrived, it was missing a desk and other furniture which I needed, and they also added a new bed with an extremely thin and uncomfortable mattress. Also, the flat was not properly cleaned when I got it, dirty windows, balcony full of cigarettes, dust everywhere, dirty dry dishes stored, etc. Basically only the bed and the toilet were somewhat clean, they did the minimum. When I arrived the “co-host” was the one getting the flat ready along with a dog, that made the flat smell like pets, and the dog was overly “friendly” and tried to play with me. Even though I asked before renting the place if any pets are going to be in the flat and the hosts said no. As I have phobia from animals, thankfully I did not freak-out too much as the dog was small, but I was still left with a semi cleaned flat with different looking furniture than advertised which smelled like dogs. They gave me only one set of bed sheets even though I booked over 2 months and it took them weeks until they got me another set of extremely poor quality so that I can comfortably wash and change my bed sheets. The flat door is broken from the inside so you can be locked inside if you forgot the key outside. The building location is hard to find, while it’s easy to get to public transport I was told by the locals that it’s not a safe area by Budapest standards and near it is a famous violent street in BP.  While it’s not likely something will happen to you, better be careful! The flat is on the 10th floor. Only one elevator available, and it broke on average once a week. More than once it was not working for more than 24 hours. Also, once the key to the building entrance was removed and I was unable to enter the building until the hosts found a solution which took hours. This along with many other issues with the flat are one part of my bad experience. The other part was the host insulting me! For the long period of my stay the host charged me like 40-50 euros as cleaning fee. This money is supposed to be used to hire someone to clean the flat after I left. However, because I did not care much about cleaning the last couple of weeks due to an injury the flat was dusty and a bit messy. The host sent me the following message “Why did you live here like a pig? Sorry the pigs are cleaner Than you!”. She also requested an extra 100 euros for cleaning because she claimed that cleaning took 6 hours. Greed is truly one of the original sins. Cleaning per hour is Budapest usually does not exceed 4 euros per hour. She already charged 40-50 at the start, and then requested an extra 100 euros! I reported her to Airbnb and got this reply “regarding your case, your host has already been told that there was not a valid claim since she already charged a cleaning fee”. Yet, it still bothers me to get such treatment. Your luck with the hosts might be different than mine. But it seems that the hosts does not understand the purpose of the cleaning fee charged (it’s not extra income) or how short-term accommodation platform operatives. Let alone basic manners. With such a long review am still keeping it short as much more questionable behavior took place most annoying was them not following what was agreed about and insulting their guest!
items.one_line: Host calls guest pig, changes furniture, leaves dirty apartment

items.comment_full:
The house is nice, modern, well-designed, has a comfy bed, spotless, but the area is full of big cockroaches. They don't live in the house, the house is super clean, but there are a lot outside and they come out after dark.
I live in Mauritius and I know they crawl and fly into houses but even there I haven't seen so many. Apparently, the door or windows were open before our arrival, so instead of relaxing after a long drive from Paraberdoo we had to catch roaches all over the house till 1 am. After that night we kept all the windows and doors shut after sunset and that helped, although we did find one in the toilet even with that measure.
Being someone who'd rather sleep in a car than in a house with a roach, I would have been thankful to see a review like this before booking.
Note that WiFi is not just weak, it is more non-existent, only works on the deck outside, but not in the house.
items.one_line: Cockroach hunting until 1am, toilet finds included, wifi only on deck
