She checked us in & but decided to tell us last minute that we weren’t going to be in the same room as the descrip
items.comment_full:
PLEASE BEWARE OF THIS LOCATION! PLEASE READ!
My fiancé and I originally booked this reservation thinking we’d get the room with the pictures displayed. When we showed up, the host was slow to respond and gave us absolutely NO instructions regarding where to go, where to park, how to check in, etc. Once we got a hold of the host after messaging and calling, A Cuban lady answered & met us at the back gate while we were looking around trying to find where we were supposed to go. She checked us in & but decided to tell us last minute that we weren’t going to be in the same room as the description, proceeding to put us in a different room that we didn’t reserve. She gave us the codes and the password, the parking pass that is limited to one street that you can never find parking at, and the password to the Wi-Fi, which might I also mention, doesn’t exist. Once we brought our bags in & settled inside we both noticed that there was human hair in the shower, on the sink, & on the floors. There was residue that covered the floors that was sticky and greasy. We also checked the fire alarm and it had roach eggs in it. The location itself is conveniently located by the beach, HOWEVER, the area is extremely loud, there’s people partying every night, arguing every night, and playing music until the crack of dawn. We got one towel, one hand towel, and one roll of toilet paper for the entire trip. This is truly the most BARE MINIMUM of a stay you can get. Book a hotel and don’t waste your time.
items.one_line: Wrong room, hair everywhere in shower, nonexistent Wi-Fi

items.comment:
PROSPECTIVE CUSTOMERS BEWARE!!!
The first thing to take note of is that the person I shall be referring to as the "Host" is not Kevin, but rather the co-Host. This Host was the management of the apartment building. I have never even talked to Kevin so all the complaints and comments here are directed to the building management.
Booking this place was one of the biggest mistakes that I've done while traveling. My family and I booked this area with the expectations that it would have been an excellent place to stay due to its numerous amenities and quite expensive price. Unfortunately, o
items.comment_full:
PROSPECTIVE CUSTOMERS BEWARE!!!
The first thing to take note of is that the person I shall be referring to as the "Host" is not Kevin, but rather the co-Host. This Host was the management of the apartment building. I have never even talked to Kevin so all the complaints and comments here are directed to the building management.
Booking this place was one of the biggest mistakes that I've done while traveling. My family and I booked this area with the expectations that it would have been an excellent place to stay due to its numerous amenities and quite expensive price. Unfortunately, our expectations were not met.
Firstly, the check-in process involved a total of walking 0.3 miles to a KeyNest store and then another 0.3 miles going back. Though this may only be a small distance to travel, walking with luggage makes it a much more burdensome experience. But that on its own was tolerable.
What was unacceptable, however, was the fact that the apartment had not been cleaned when we checked in. This was made rather obvious by the fact that there were bins full of rubbish, dirty bathrooms (e.g. urine on the toilet bowls, the footprints of the previous tenants? still fresh on the floor, etc.), dirty towels thrown haphazardly in the closet, a dirty kitchen sink, and a kettle with used teabags found inside. Not only that, the clothes iron described in the amenities was leaking water (although that was the least of our concerns).
We contacted the Host regarding this and they offered to have the apartment cleaned the following day. Although we tried to find a different place to stay when told this (as we didn't want to sleep in a dirty apartment), there was no available place that we could reasonably go to at that time of the night (e.g. they were on the other side of the city and we were carrying heavy luggage with us) so we had to stay the night in what was essentially a dirty and unsanitary environment. When we woke up, we felt itchy and noticed some red spots on our body (not entirely sure if that was from the apartment but it certainly wasn't there beforehand) and decided that enough was enough and that we could not stay in this place any longer. As such, we cancelled our booking and left the area as soon as we were dressed.
But that was not the end of our ordeal. Although the Host agreed to refund the cleaning fee, they charged us a total of approximately £ 721 even though the nightly fee was only £221. This was in spite of the fact that we only stayed in that dirty apartment for one night. After reviewing the cancellation policy which stated that we did still have to pay for the price of 2 nights, our total fee was still way more than what should have been charged to us and so we requested a refund. But here's the kicker. Although we clearly cited the receipts from Airbnb that stated that the price of stay per night was static, the Host had the gall to say that the charge for our second day was actually £500 (something which was more than double the £221 that was initially agreed upon). So not only did we pay the price for an expensive "luxury" apartment, we also had to deal with a filthy environment and are now currently in contention with a greedy company that is trying to claim more money from us than is due.
So, prospective tenants, beware...
items.one_line: Expensive apartment filthy, key nest blocks away, numerous issues

items.comment:
Where to begin? Let's start with a few experiences during my stay.
My Stay:
The second night that I was in Robert's place, the neighbor's house caught on fire. It was about 3/4am and Robert even paid me an early morning visit to make sure everything was okay. Prior to me finding out the neighbor's house was in flames, I heard what I thought was rustling and tapping at the window of the bedroom. I thought there was a prowler and I was going to end up on a True Crime TV show on some basic cable channel. During Robert's visit, he (and a firefighter) helped ease my worry, there was nothing
items.comment_full:
Where to begin? Let's start with a few experiences during my stay.
My Stay:
The second night that I was in Robert's place, the neighbor's house caught on fire. It was about 3/4am and Robert even paid me an early morning visit to make sure everything was okay. Prior to me finding out the neighbor's house was in flames, I heard what I thought was rustling and tapping at the window of the bedroom. I thought there was a prowler and I was going to end up on a True Crime TV show on some basic cable channel. During Robert's visit, he (and a firefighter) helped ease my worry, there was nothing back there. That's mainly just an FYI for anyone else who hears that rustling again, because I heard it the next day in the morning sunlight, so it wasn't anyone trying to break in. The only thing I can suggest Robert adding is at least a few sharp knives. The only sharp thing I could find was a fork, so I slept with that under the bed and a can opener under my pillow (in case I had to bludgeon an intruder hahaha). Okay, okay, maybe that's just me and my outrageous imagination, but it couldn't hurt...
The Space:
Let me tell you, I have stayed in some AirBnB's that look one way in photos and another in person (aka worse) and I was honestly expecting the same with this place. There was no way I was going to pay the low amount I did without it being disgusting, but to my happy surprise, THIS PLACE WAS GREAT! There was fresh bedding, a ton of towels, a tea/coffee cabinet, a separate water/snack cabinet (that came in handy when I got in at 4am and hadn't eaten in nearly 24 hours), and so many little things you might forget at home. Items of note are: 2 ply toilet paper (and SO MUCH of it... getting to an AirBnB on a vacation and seeing that they only have one half-used roll of TP and having to go out to buy your own sucks), feminine products to get you through any surprises, a high speed blowdryer (not those lame low power dryers like you find in a hotel), several body washes to choose from, and razors. These sound silly to mention specifically, but if you're a woman I know you appreciate this information. ALSO, NO BUGS!!!! Specifically, NO SIGNS OF BED BUGS! I know that this is me being particularly extra, but I am petrified of those things so I always rip apart bedding to look for signs of them. None here! And even though there are spaces under the doors and around the A/C for critters to crawl in, the only bug I encountered on my trip was a spider on the front door on my last day. Now that I've mentioned the spaces, I have to say it can get pretty cold. He has heat and A/C, but if you'd rather not heat the whole place up, I suggest nice, warm clothes to sleep in depending on the time you visit.
The Neighborhood:
It's a little run down, but I wouldn't call it a scary place. It's an up and coming neighborhood with plenty of hipsters. I read the reviews before booking and people had mentioned that their Uber drivers had advised them against walking after dark so naturally I was on alert, but I was staying at Robert's alone and routinely walked back between 11pm-1am by myself. I can't exactly recommend that, but I can say that I personally did not feel unsafe (although I still walked with the key between my fingers because momma didn't raise no dummy). Just like any city in America, keep your wits about you and be smart. For instance, don't drunkenly stumble home by yourself in the wee hours.
The Location:
I LOVE the location. It's just outside the French Quarter in the Marigny Triangle. Google Maps/Waze will tell you that it's a 30 minute walk from Elysian Fields to Canal St., but that walk does NOT feel like 30 minutes. I can safely say that I walked anywhere between 2-4 miles every day that I was there. It's so easy to wander, everything is pretty close to each other, and there's always something to see on your walk. You WANT to walk! I went to Kermit Ruffin's place in Treme when I was there, but was told that the walk back during the day might be possible, but was told to definitely get an Uber back at night. Unless you have physical ailments that prevent you from walking long distances, I do not recommend staying in the Quarter. It's overpriced, loud, and by looking just a few streets outside of it, you can find places like Robert's that give you access to the REAL NOLA, and not just the tourist Quarter.
Will I Stay Here Again?:
ABSOLUTELY. Robert was so nice, hospitable, and really thought about what one may need if their luggage were lost. I recommend this place wholeheartedly!
items.one_line: Neighbor's house burns at 3am, guest sleeps with fork and can opener

items.comment: We were looking forward to relaxing in this beautiful space on our vacation to San Diego, however on our first morning we were awoken by loud construction sounds around 8:15am. The hosts did not mention that there would be construction taking place during our stay, let alone the early start time. When we contacted the hosts about this, they responded with justifications about how their project was delayed and didn’t expect it to still be going. However, they of course knew in the days before our arrival that the project was not done. They did *not apologize for the disturbance*. We asked polit
items.comment_full:
We were looking forward to relaxing in this beautiful space on our vacation to San Diego, however on our first morning we were awoken by loud construction sounds around 8:15am. The hosts did not mention that there would be construction taking place during our stay, let alone the early start time. When we contacted the hosts about this, they responded with justifications about how their project was delayed and didn’t expect it to still be going. However, they of course knew in the days before our arrival that the project was not done. They did *not apologize for the disturbance*. We asked politely if they could have the crew start at 9am and they told us they were on a tight schedule and not to worry, that we can use the pool and the crew wouldn’t bother us. Our primary concern was not the crew, but the loud noises in the morning that woke us up, though we also did not feel that we could relax in the space while construction noise continued all day.
On day two, the loud noises began at 8:45am - better, but not as late as we had requested. They worked until after 7pm that day.
On day three, we were abruptly awakened to extremely loud pounding at 8:20am. When we messaged the hosts we were told they normally start at 8am. It was at this point they finally apologized, which we appreciated, but then they immediately minimized our concern by telling us they didn’t think it would be an issue because no other guests had complained.
Around 10:20am I opened the curtain to find the host and apparent cable guy standing at our door ready to enter. He said he wasn’t sure if we were here, despite our car being parked in front of his house, and then asked if they could come in to look at a cable. No message or warning ahead of time even though we had been messaging about the noise prior to this.
At this point they offered to give us a $50 credit or let us leave and refund our last night. We happily accepted the offer for the refund and packed up and headed home early.
While this construction project will eventually end, the hosts cavalier attitude and lack of consideration for their guests likely will remain. Overall, we felt that they wanted our money, but not the inconvenience of honoring our reasonable request to start work at 9am. Also, at bare minimum they should have disclosed that there would be construction during our stay ahead of time.
items.one_line: Host knew about construction, didn't apologize, offered pool

items.comment:

I opened the door and I went to my room. The kitchen it’s always dark because everything it’s closed
items.comment_full:
I don’t know where to start. I’m going to say the best of Jermaine’s place it’s that I had to stay there for just one night.
The area is not that bad, but those two blocks from the avenue to the apt, are kind of empty. Dirty. Looks abandoned.
I took the keys and when I opened the black door there was a hallway that looked in construcción or without finish. Ok. When I went to the door I saw a rat on the background but I thought Ok, it’s hot, it’s Brooklyn, it’s New York, it’s ok.
I opened the door and I went to my room. The kitchen it’s always dark because everything it’s closed and there was a little bit of smell. It’s ok I thought, it’s New York. But when I went to my room…ok. This is not what I was expecting. I’ve been in really bad rooms but this one is one of the worse.
I’m not a really demanding person and I can adapt to everything but honestly I was expecting something way to different.
The room was really really small.
The bed looks like someone slept in there last night. It didn’t look clean.
Man, you can’t show this room as an “Amazing room with workspace”. Be honest. I had my carry on and mu suitcase open and I barely could walk in there. Also the “workspace” it was just a simple chair with a small desk table. Come on.
There’s a fan on the ceiling and there was another fan on the “workspace”. I thought why two fans? And I realized in the night that the one on the ceiling just don’t work when you turn the lights off. There’s a window but it’s closed, and I thought that with the rats outside it’s convenient. Especially because the window it’s closed with a wall. There some bars and after that, a wall. So there’s no air at all. That’s why you need the fans. Anyways I woke up this morning and I was al sweat. There’s no air circulation there.
Ok. I went to the bathroom. It’s weird. The shower it’s ok. But not the sink.
First, man you are charging me 25 dollars for a clean fee. The bathroom it’s not clean at all. The showers was full of hair and dust or something like that. And it really needs a little bit of bleach.
The sink was so weird. First, again full of hair. But thats not even the worst, I couldn’t use the towel because smelled really disgusting. And today in the morning when I was brushing my teeth, my shoulders where touching both walls. Yes. As you read. I’m 1.82 cms. Not that tall or big. But I barely can fit in the place to use the sink.
I also forgot to tell that the room looks like it’s falling in a hole. If you leave your suitcase stand, it’s going to roll to one of the walls. The same happens with the door for the same reason.
I went for a walk and I returned around 10:30. The hallway was completely dark and With some rats. I had to turn on the flashlight from my phone.
The whole night there was over 70 dollars. 25 are from cleaning fee. I can’t believe it.
Honestly man I’m not trying to bother you, I know it’s nyc and I’ve been in other rooms before but you can’t charge that for this room.
I think if I’m going to offer a service, I would do everything I could to make everyone feel comfortable.
I’m annoyed about Airbnb having no standards for the rooms the show on the app.
And I’m also disappointed about the reviews. Man I’m not staying in hotels and beautiful rooms but at least decent rooms. I’m not expecting a luxury room but at least a clean and with air flow . And bathroom.
I went there because of the reviews but I don’t understand why people don’t say anything but good things.
I’m not trying to tell you to not go there but at least know what are you going to get there. I know someone is going to say that I’m wrong but you’ll see everything when you are there. I wish someone wrote a review like this before.
In the other hand, thanks for allowing me to stay one hour and a half longer Jermaine. I didn’t see you but I appreciate that. Thanks.
items.one_line: Brooklyn hallway rat sighting, tiny unfinished room, awful first night ever

We were not able to enjoy it or ourselves and spent twice as much money finding things to do that did NOT include relaxation at home with the family.
The host gave us the run-around about AC,
items.comment_full:
The home was cute and very coordinated. It had a welcome vibe and looked exactly like the pictures! We fell in love. The community was quiet and safe. The neighbors were friendly and the island vibe was present.
However, the weather was unbearable. The original post/description said there was air conditioning, fans in every room, a nice breeze... it was over 90 degrees EVERY DAY inside the home. We were not able to enjoy it or ourselves and spent twice as much money finding things to do that did NOT include relaxation at home with the family.
The host gave us the run-around about AC, and grossly exaggerated the cost. Several other neighbors in the community purchased FULL SIZE units for their home during our visit because of the heat. We ended up purchasing multiple fans and window units, leaving them behind for the next visitors.
The photos were not all taken from the property. The ones that were had been ‘Zoomed-In,’ giving a closer appearance to ocean and amenities.
The “five min walk” was more like 15. It included a downhill slope on a main road with no sidewalk (no lighting in the evening), across a BUSY six lane intersection (the final part having a yield for drivers, but a blind corner that hid pedestrians, and then through a busy hotel parking/drop off entrance.
The address was not provided until the property was actually booked, however on the listing, the ‘circle’ on the map implied the property was NOT across a 6-lane highway.
Assuming there are no children or senior citizens (60+) in your party, it is unlikely to cause an issue.
The outer appearance was great! However, once you opened anything up it wasn’t clean.
Lots of bugs... ants, cockroaches (and many feces). Dead and alive. There was a giant RAT that made his home over in the washer/dryer area. Dishwasher was rusted and falling apart. Drawers had miscellaneous trash. Refrigerator had lots of old food. Trash can had gum and miscellaneous remnants preventing it from opening... forcing the user to leave it open or to use their fingernails for throwing things away.
The host said the pest control company and the cleaner would be called, but neither visited.
After going back and forth with the host, and being referred to as a HEADACHE for my concerns, contact was ceased.
items.one_line: AC lie escalated into buying window units left behind

items.comment_full: The apartment was very cute and located in a nice neighborhood. However, I would not recommend this location if you plan on cooking your meals. The kitchen was very under stocked and every time we tried to turn on the stove or oven the fire alarm went off instantly. There was no hood over the stove. We were unable to cook anything and ended up having to make make microwave meals. There was a coffee pot but nowhere in the kitchen to plug it in. We had to move it to the dining room to use it. The apartment was fairly clean,  but I wouldn’t consider it worth the $50 I paid for a cleaning fee. We had to wash most of the pots and pans before using them. Which is not surprising because there is no dishwasher or even a dish drying rack. We were told to leave cleaned dishes in the drying rack upon departure, but there was not one available. Overall our stay was okay, but not sure it was worth the price we paid. Probably would have been better off just getting a hotel.
items.one_line: Stove triggers fire alarm instantly, cooking becomes impossible

I am not a spoiled brat but even before realising about the noise problem I felt gutted by the mess, however at 10PM I had nowhere to go so I stayed the first night anyway. In the morning I waited a bit for host response but as there was none by 9ish I simply found the hotel and left. Airbnb had to step in for refund of unused days. My advice is: book this only if you're not a light sleeper and ask for more pics and apt to be cleaned well.

items.comment_full: This place appears beautiful via the pictures on Airbnb, however the house could use some deep cleaning and a whole new security system. First off the home needs a new shower curtain in the main bathroom. The current was has mold on it. The bedrooms were all clean and nicely sized. The house could use some modern appliances like a toaster and some new utensils. The biggest issue with this house was the location and security provided. The neighborhood if this Airbnb is one of the worst in Nashville. We experienced and incident where a man knocked on our door trying to lure us with a makeup bag claiming it was ours. We did not answer the door and the man became angry and knocked harder. He eventually left and threw a pair of sunglasses on the ground at the end of the sidewalk in front of the home. I contacted the host of this Airbnb and they were very kind but could not offer us any other locations to relocate nor a refund to get a hotel. My guest staying with me were very alarmed by the situation and the cops were called. The cops stated it was one of the worst neighborhoods and we needed to keep our doors and windows locked. After contacted the Airbnb host regarding the situation they sent me a security code for the security system I had no idea they offered at the house. It did not work properly and none of the windows were even included on the security system. The front door lock situations was incredibly hard to use and did not feel at all safe. We struggled to figure out how to lock and unlock it. Overall, we remained safe by locking everything we could and pinning sheets over doors and windows so the surrounding neighbors could not see in, but this company should be ashamed to not alert guest that it is an up and coming neighborhood and that they should be prepared that neighbors to both sides were not Airbnb's. They also need to update the lock systems and security of this home. Pretty place, could use some deep cleaning and some paint and also a new security system for guest to feel more safe.
items.one_line: Stranger tried luring with makeup bag then threw sunglasses

items.comment_full:
The bathroom was leaking nonstop with smelly murky water from a hole in the celing. We were informed only when we check in but didnt know it was that the situation was that bad. We sent a picture to Laszlo the next day with the brown smelly water splashed over the toilet walls. It stinks so badly thoughout our stay, we had to bear with the smell. Dirty and brown smelly water rain on us whenever we takes our shower.
There was nothing Laszlo can do as he said it is under the Hotel management's care. We stayed for 9days and waited until the 8th day before the repair man came, we thought it was pointless for them to repair because we will be checking out the next morning but Laszlo said the arrangement has already been made. We were not in the apartment when a few of the repair man and building management entered the house, we were uncomfortable as we have our luggages and valuables in there. Laszlo assured us that the management are professional and our stuff will be safe, i still feel that our privacy is invaded.
Laszlo is friendly and nice, he helped us with our luggages late in the night when we checked in. He is responsive to messages.
Location is good. Walkable to most attraction places, if u dislike walking then Museum station will be the nearest, followed by Townhall station. IGA supermarket is just right at the corner of this apartment and closes late in the midnight.
There is no nice view when we were there because part of Hyde Park (right outside our apartment window) was undergoing some recontruction work.
The fire brigade is right around the corner and the siren from the fire engine goes on and on through the night. We hardly get a good sleep, the loud siren goes on every hourly, my kids kept waking up due to the noise.
There is no heater in the house. We first communicated with Laszlo over messages and confirm that there is a heater in the house. However when we reached, we were adviced to on the aircon to the highest temperature, the aircon simply gives out cool air and not heating up the room. We have young kids and it was really too cold in what feels like 6degrees (according to forcast app). Eventually my kids caught a cold during the stay.
Everything looks like the photo but due to the leakage and smell, it really brings down our holiday mood. I think the apartment badly needed some repair and upkeep.
items.one_line: Ceiling bathroom leak rained brown smelly water on showers

items.comment_full:
I had a really unpleasant experience staying in this AirBnB and would recommend other people interested to stay away. I will just list some of the issues we had.
1. Gas Leak in the Unit
Around day 60 of our 90 day stay we noticed a strong gas smell in the apartment two days in a row. On the second day my fiance called the fire department and then gas company came out. To the hosts credit they quickly put us into a nicer unit for the night. During the required inspection by AirBnB the following day their was a small gas leak discovered in the heating unit inside the apartment. My fiance worked from home during the entire 90 day stay so it's upsetting that she was exposed to this for 2 months during our stay.
2. Unit was extremely dirty at check in
The unit was very dirty at check in. Their was a full rotting pizza left in the oven, the toilet in the bathroom was covered in urine, counters were dirty just in general it seemed that the unit had not been cleaned at all before we moved in. We immediately contacted the host who was not able to send out cleaners until Sunday when we checked in on Friday. To the hosts credit they did resend out the cleaners. Still I'm really disappointed in checking in to a dirty and gross apartment and having to sleep their for 2 nights without other options besides cleaning it ourselves which we do some of.
3. Timer on the AC unit
One of my requirements when looking for an AirBnB this summer was a location with AC. At this unit the AC itself is on a timer that can be set for a max duration of 1 hour and needs to be physically set again after the hour to keep the air conditioning running. This means that if you set the AC and go to bed it will shut off after 1 hour and the house can either get or hot or you can wake up and set the timer again. This was extremely frustrating and I feel that it needs to be stated explicitly in the listing for this unit. I'm okay with restricting the temperature to something reasonable which they did but having a 1 hour timer on the AC while advertising that the unit provides AC is deceptive and hostile to tenants.
4. Dumpster Parking
This is minor but still worth mentioning that the parking space provided for this unit is squeezed right against the dumpster on the drivers side. Parking spaces are pretty tight so it's almost impossible to get out of the vehicle without bumping the car door or yourself against the dumpster. This is minor in that someone in the complex has to get that spot but still impacted my enjoyment of the unit. Also to be objective our spot was always open and we didn't have any issues with or spot being taken or blocked in.
5. Homelessness Issues
I'm not from LA and I know the situation is not great everywhere in this regard so take this with some context. That being said their was a row of 10-20 homeless tents about 100 yards from the unit on the corner of Inglewood. Their would frequently be homeless people walking down our street and by the unit screaming and just doing other homeless people things. Their was only one issue I personally saw after the fact where a man was mugged while walking and had his phone stolen. We had a dog and did not ever have any issues but it just generally felt pretty unsafe to be walking around the area especially at night. Again this is an issue everywhere in LA but being one of the first apartment complexes to the major street corner on Inglewood I think makes it worse. If you stay somewhere deeper inside a neighborhood and away from major intersections I think the interaction and safety would be better.
Overall I really did not enjoy our stay at this unit and would recommend those looking to go else where especially considering the price.
items.one_line: Gas leak, rotting pizza oven, urine toilet, extensive cleanliness failures

items.comment_full:
I’m very surprised to see so many good reviews and NO ONE mention the amount of drug dealers and questionable looking people sitting right outside the apartment on the neighboring steps... as soon as I got out of my Uber I was approached by gentlemen asking if I wanted to “buy smoke and poppies”... if that wasn’t bad enough, it took an hour for Simon to respond as I was having a little trouble finding the spot due to my late flight... The loud music played all night along with their loud talking by the neighbors, and the smell of marijuana was coming so strong through our windows that we had to close them... we arrived to a hot apartment so we wanted fresh air but had to result to the ACs to avoid noise and the smell. and if that wasn’t bad enough, I woke up to Pop Smoke blasting at 8am...
Due to the price point I wasn’t completely shocked at the location being in the hood or even the obnoxious loud noises outside my steps but I was still willing to get through this very quick weekend work trip and still give it the benefit of the doubt...
To end it off, Simon then accused me and my husband of smoking in his apartment as if there wasn’t a group of people right next door outside all day smoking... he then kicked us out of the apartment in less than 24 hours, at 9pm at night. Frustrated, I was able to find accommodations by the grace of God, to find the next morning that Simon conveniently had our booking still listed as current and hadn’t notified Air bnb of the “smoking cancellation”. Ironic right? He kept it up until Sunday, when we were supposed to check out...
All in all, it is walking distance to shops and the subway, it is NOT in Manhattan or anywhere relatively close, the water gets super hot which is great, he had more than enough utensils, towels, toiletries and the rest.
the WiFi sucks.
Please DO NOT get sucked into this man’s finesse unless you absolutely have to...
items.one_line: Drug dealers greet guests; Pop Smoke alarm clock

items.comment_full:
This apartment is nicely designed and very well appointed in every way except in one major detail. We were two couples traveling through Spain and Bordeaux and spent two nights. At the time of booking the listing described “2 full baths” and the translation to English stated that the “toilets are separated” which to any normal person would mean more than one toilet. To our disappointment there is actually only one toilet with a sink in a small closet next to the entrance. The master bathroom has a shower, sink and vanity of comfortable size but no toilet. The second bedroom has more of a “wet” tiled closet that you step into which includes a shower head and a small sink but there is barely room for a single person to turn around and bend over. If we had known this description from the beginning we would have booked elsewhere, especially considering the astronomical price we paid we would have much rather found a hotel were we could all be more comfortable.
We contacted the host for a resolution but they were not very amicable. We eventually received a partial refund for the inaccuracy of the description but only until Airbnb stepped in.
All in all this apartment is very nice, (minus the baths) is in a good location and the access in and out of the building is easy. However if you are expecting two full baths this would not be the place to book; hopefully the host updates the description to the amenities accurately so that no one else runs into this issue.
items.one_line: One toilet shared between couples, shower is phone booth

items.comment:
So the location is great except for the grocery stores nearby are very small and don't offer a lot. The place is great for a solo traveler or even a couple except the hot water lasts for 10 minutes so you have to turn the water off every time you're not using it which is great for the environment but terrible when you're there in February. The heater works great. Too great. You'll get really hot in the upstairs bed at night.
The wifi is spotty. It works fine when you're surfing the internet but if you are making a video call it's going to be terrible so beware people traveling and working
items.comment_full:
So the location is great except for the grocery stores nearby are very small and don't offer a lot. The place is great for a solo traveler or even a couple except the hot water lasts for 10 minutes so you have to turn the water off every time you're not using it which is great for the environment but terrible when you're there in February. The heater works great. Too great. You'll get really hot in the upstairs bed at night.
The wifi is spotty. It works fine when you're surfing the internet but if you are making a video call it's going to be terrible so beware people traveling and working. I work teaching English online with VIPKid and had a hard time so don't stay here fellow teachers. The shower door wouldn't close properly so whenever you step out you're stepping into a puddle. I had to shower in the corner to try and prevent this from happening. Also they had a shampoo and soap dispensary, the shampoo was filled but the soap was not. I don't care if an Airbnb doesn't have shampoo or soap but I care if you're offering it and not providing it lol. It's like "would you like some water?" yes "sorry we don't have any" In all fairness they did bring soap to my request. I stayed there for a month and got one towel, when I asked for more I got another one towel. I guess in Europe people air dry?
You never see the host so the service isn't personalized. Sure check in is easy, sure you can communicate with them through whatsapp (during working hours), but you don't REALLY feel like they care probably because they have many other properties they're managing. That's the difference between staying in a shared airbnb and an entire apartment. People who share tend to care more however I have stayed in entire apartments before with airbnb where someone comes in and tells me how to use everything from the washer to shower and finish off by saying "if you need anything you can contact me" it's personalized. No one did that.
Speaking of the washer, where you put the detergent was soooo filthy. I have no idea why? I did spill hot chocolate on the carpet on my second to last day so they're probably going to reply to this review painting me terrible with that but after everything I experienced I did not want to get on my hands and knees and try to get hot chocolate out of the carpet in the living area. The place is nice and looks as pictured. I felt safe in that area and if it weren't for all of these things I mentioned before I'd stay there again. I love Budapest and I plan on going back but not to this place.
items.one_line: Hot water dies in ten minutes, shower floods corner

6. I stayed a whole month, and still had to pay for intermediate cleaning, and the rates they charged were extreme for Greece. €40 to clean, excluding change of sheets at €10 a bed. Im an airbnb owner in The Hague, where income and living standard is way higher, and my cleaning lady does not charge that much!
Overall, I had a nice stay, but Im happier Im gone. And would definitely avoid Upstreet managed apartments in the future.

items.comment_full:
Let me start by saying the communication from Loft is amazing, we appreciate the fact that they attended to every issue that we had, BUT some of the issues shouldn't have been issues in the 1st place.
First, and most importantly, the stay had BEDBUGS. This is the reason we decided to leave the stay. At 1st, we thought it was mosquitoes, as I'm prone to getting bit by them, but after a few nights, I got bit so much on 1 night I woke up around 4:30 am. We turned on the lights and found two bed bugs in the bed. I reached out to Loft and they stated that they would send a fumigator the same day, which is great, but after dealing with the other things listed below, we decided to leave the stay anyway. Loft told us they didn't know why this was an issue as they had a fumigator in the place within the last month. This made us feel as if this is an ongoing issue for this particular property. To their credit, Loft's owner reached out to me directly and offered to comp the rest of our stay for free, but we declined as we wouldn't have been comfortable even after a fumigation.
2nd: The sheets weren't clean upon our arrival (human hair in them as if they hadn't been washed). They had the sheets changed quickly, but when they were changed, they were full of some type of animal hair. I didn't complain, I just took it upon myself to use a lint roller and get as much of it up as I could. It was an inconvenience.
3rd: There was an issue with the internet which was resolved very quickly, and we thank Loft for that, but again, it shouldn't have been an issue in the 1st place. If you promote a certain internet speed, you should make sure it is available upon your guest's arrival. After they sent a tech to switch out the router, the internet was phenomenal.
4th: The stay is listed as self check in, but it's not. There is no way to get into the apartment without someone letting you in at the main entrance gate. After which there is a short walk way to the stay. So technically, it's not a "self check in".
Lastly, it's a not a huge deal, but the place looks bigger in the pictures than it is in person. It's not bad for two or less people. But it is kind of tight.
That is pretty much everything. Again, we thank Loft for their rapid responses to our issues. We hope that they resolve the issues the stay has, and continue to prosper in their business.
items.one_line: Bedbugs discovered at 4:30am after mysterious bites

In the pictures, the apt looks clean, but in reality the appliances in the kitchen are outdated, with a rusted toaster and coffee machine. When the electric kettle is switched on the fuse blows out. The sink was stained, in the cupboards and draws were dirty.
We stayed there 5 days at the end of November and the temperature in LA was low, hence we needed heating. On arrival day the apartment was really cold. We asked the host, Travelartist (who introduced himself as Bill, but we don’t know his real name, which is weird) switched on
items.comment_full:
Positive aspects are the location and parking permit.
In the pictures, the apt looks clean, but in reality the appliances in the kitchen are outdated, with a rusted toaster and coffee machine. When the electric kettle is switched on the fuse blows out. The sink was stained, in the cupboards and draws were dirty.
We stayed there 5 days at the end of November and the temperature in LA was low, hence we needed heating. On arrival day the apartment was really cold. We asked the host, Travelartist (who introduced himself as Bill, but we don’t know his real name, which is weird) switched on the thermostat using a wire. Apparently is was needed to reset the device.
Note that the kitchen and bathroom have no heating at all (which is not mentioned on Airbnb, otherwise we would not have booked).
The only source of heating is an ancient furnace in the living-room, mounted in a hole in the floor next to the bed. The furnaced stopped working when it reached the temperature set on thermostat, but it didn’t reactivate once the temperature dropped. We tried during the night to reactivate the furnace from the thermostat using the wire Bill gave us. We alerted Bill several times by email and calling him. The second night we had no heating at all and we asked the host to bring us an electric radiator. We could smell gas in the room and we were afraid of the carbon monoxide level. First, Bill mounted another thermostat but it didn’t help. Then he brought in the last night an old radiator that ran only at low speed and didn’t have enough heating power.
Thus, we slept 5 nights in a cold room and checking every hour the flame in the furnace as we were afraid of gas leak poisoning. We would have expected the host to refunded a part of the money for the loss of heating and not offering the services described on Airbnb. However, we didn’t ask for a refund.
Instead, the host offered to reimburse $100 to us provided we refrain the writing a review. He would transfer the money after 2 weeks, when the period for reviews on Airbnb expired.
We believe it’s important that other guests are aware of this experience.
We have pictures that document everything about cleanliness and heating furnace.
Most of the time heating is not needed in LA, so probably this issue was so far not noticed. That said, I would not recommend this apartment.
items.one_line: Ancient furnace next to bed, electric kettle blows fuse, no kitchen heat

items.comment:

This is a list of some of the issues that require urgent attention.
1) fawcets consistently leak in the attached bathroom
2) toilet seat is broken
3) dishwasher is broken
4) cleanliness is poor with dust balls in corners on the day of check-in (after a cleaning service has attended!)
5) carpets are dirty and full of stains
6) attach
items.comment_full:
The apartment is well located and if you are staying for 1-2 nights, many of the issues can be ignored. We stayed for nine nights and this meant we noticed the significant state of disrepair and lack of attention to detail in the apartment. This is a list of some of the issues that require urgent attention.
1) fawcets consistently leak in the attached bathroom
2) toilet seat is broken
3) dishwasher is broken
4) cleanliness is poor with dust balls in corners on the day of check-in (after a cleaning service has attended!)
5) carpets are dirty and full of stains
6) attached bathroom has a blockage and does not drain water without plunging and takes over 8-9 hours to drain which is not only smelly but also quite disgusting
7) ant infestation in the apartment which has previously been mentioned by previous reviewers and not addressed
8) design of apartment with a glass ceiling means it can become a greenhouse/hot box during warm and sunny days making it impossible to use the living space at any time of the day especially not to sleep. This made the stay very very uncomfortable.
We have photographic evidence of all the above issues.
The hosts are welcoming at first but when we had issues, they were rude and unhelpful. They charged for us a fifth guest but as the apartment was so hot and stuffy due to the design of the living space it was impossible to use the sofa bed to sleep. They charged us for extra linen for the sofa bed which we did not use and have refused to refund this sum which is poor.
They are hard to contact as they go to the "mountains" and considering a previous guest had difficulties checking in and had to book an emergency hotel for one night, this is disappointing that they have not addressed this.
Lara and Stuart bank on location for their apartment - there is no doubt the location is convenient and very good. But the amenities, service and lack of attention to detail including cleanliness would urge me to recommend to consider staying elsewhere.
We are very disappointed with the stay in the apartment and would never recommend it to anyone. We appreciate that this is not a serviced hotel, but we had expected a certain standard of cleanliness and amenities in the apartment as Lara and Stuart have advertised and claim that they "take pride" . However, they fell very short.
items.one_line: Bathroom blockage takes nine hours, ant infestation, glass ceiling greenhouse

When we arrived, had to wait for host to purchase electricity, so we could shower at least and charge our phones. Place wasn't ready for guests. Totally destroyed my new socks because stood accidentally ok the nail which was sticking out in the lounge, having a big hole in my socks, imagine it could be my foot.
Very overpriced - we paid R1600 because except a good view we expected some comfort as well. But place is falling apart, needs good renovation and deep cleaning. Cockroach, dog's hair..Feels like we paid R1300 for the view and R300 for the place. We wanted to check in to a place which already ready for guests and stay peacefully for 4 nights, we didn't want someone to come every day and fix new problem. That's why we Checked out only the day earlier as we were tired of not sleeping for 3 nights because of the toilet noise and Squicky bed. This is unacceptable. Never again. We asked host to return R1600 for the last night which we didn't stay at the location,but she only returned R1000. These days for R5300 which we paid for 3 nights having unpleasant surprises every day, we could have rented 2 bed with nice views of mountain and ocean in a safe area in Cape Town for 2 weeks. Pity.
items.one_line: Broken everything, toilet screams nightly, bed squeaks, unsafe fireplace

We also couldn't find a hairdryer, which was listed but I guess the hosts took it with themselves for their vacation. The biggest problem wit
items.comment_full: We stayed in Ula and Dean's place for 3 days. They were away in a vacation during that time, yet they were responsive to our messages and their neighbour let us in. The house is nicely decorated and the room itself is OK, it is exactly as in the photos. However, the wifi connection was terrible, and the room was quite cold (although we didn't turn on the heating).  The shower was either very hot or very cold, couldn't really set the temperature properly. We also couldn't find a hairdryer, which was listed but I guess the hosts took it with themselves for their vacation. The biggest problem with the listing is the building itself. It's a large block of council houses. When we entered the building, there was vomit on the ground floor, and someone has clearly urinated in the elevator. The smell of the ground floor and the elevator was really terrible during the entire trip. I know it's not the hosts' fault but those who wish to stay here should be aware that they are not exactly staying in a nice neighbourhood. We also heard people having a pretty big fight in the street after midnight on our first night there. So, overall, for the reasonable price you can choose to stay there, but I'd suggest to look for other places (possibly in a different neighbourhood) if you can afford it.
items.one_line: Vomit on ground floor, urine in elevator, terrible shower temperature control

- We got into the apartment . We were 4 adults and five kids. So we needed all the rooms.
- We turned on the ACs in the rooms.
- We then showered our 5 kids, put them to bed.
- Then, we (the adults) showered. While showering the 4 of us fe
items.comment_full:
We had several problems. Basically nothing was working out with this apartment.
We tried to discuss with the host Rosalia. But she refused to listen to any of our arguments.
So here’s in detail the situation and a description of what happened.
- We took the keys from the box that the host left us at the entrance around midnight.
- We got into the apartment . We were 4 adults and five kids. So we needed all the rooms.
- We turned on the ACs in the rooms.
- We then showered our 5 kids, put them to bed.
- Then, we (the adults) showered. While showering the 4 of us felt light electric shocks when touching the faucets of the shower in both bathrooms. This is a real hazard! The problem could evolve and lead to electrocution. That is not safe for us, nor our children.
- While one of the adults was showering, we turned on the washing machine because we had laundry to do for the baby.
- That’s when we lost electricity. We checked the board and all the breakers of the electric panel inside the house were ON.
- We started using the light of our cellphones to be able to see. It was 2am in the morning, even more. We called the emergency number that the host gave us. She didn’t reply. Although we tried several times.
- While using the light of our cellphones to find our way, in the home, we started to see cockroaches coming from everywhere. First we saw 2 cockroaches, followed by another 2, that we killed. There were other ones that slid under the couch and other places. This is not a case of one or two coming in, there were many and we have pictures to prove it as well. We sent some of the pictures to the host. But we have more.
- Our only option was to leave the house at 5.00 am to go to a hotel. Imagine! At 5.00 am, 2 families with 5 kids looking for a hotel!
We tried to solve the issue with the host. She didn’t want to know anything about it!!
- She started blaming all this on our late check in!! It doesn’t make any sense!! Electric shocks in the shower don’t have a schedule, cockroaches don’t have a schedule.
- She said cockroaches are normal this time of the year. We understand if saw one! But seven or even more!!! That was not part of the deal.
- She said that the washer was full of water. Well of course!! The electricity went out and the washer stopped working!!
- She said that we created an overload and that’s why we lost electricity. But there was no warning that we can’t run all AC’s with the washer. If we rented a house like that, it is because we wanted to use all the rooms and all AC’s.
- She said that she hired a locksmith to open the door because we forgot the keys in the door from the inside! Well again, there was no warning about not to leave the keys in the door. We don’t have these locks in Canada. It is the first time I see a door without a handle from the inside to open it. Also you have to know and understand that we left in a rush at 5.00am in the morning.
- She said that we used the stove and it was dirty. Well, it was like that when we arrived. We definitely didn’t cook at 2am!!
We are asking for minimal understanding. But our host didn’t show an ounce of understanding or compassion! On the opposite, she’s blaming us for everything!!! EVERYTHING!! We felt electric shocks in the shower, we lost electricity, had no more AC, and saw at least 7 cockroaches. What would you have done in our situation with kids? Trust me, it was not an ideal situation for us to have to find a hotel with kids in the middle of the night... but we also couldn’t stay for all of the reasons mentioned above. And the host didn’t answer on the emergency number she gave us.
We’ve been in Airbnbs multiple times and have never been reprimanded for anything. We are very aware of our rights as consumers. We tried to solve this in a decent way but the host didn’t want to. She didn’t show any leniency.
And because of all the troubles, and because of our host attitude and lack of understanding and her disrespectful attitude, we want our host to reimburse us also the nights that we spent at the hotel to replace our stay in her apartment.
items.one_line: Electric shocks in showers, lost power mid-laundry, breaker trip

The house cat uses the toilet as a human does. Just sayin.

items.comment_full:
It’s not a terrible place; if AirBnB asked me to rate the room it could have been higher but they asked me to rate my stay. Here’s my assessment:
Check in is lockbox, quick and easy.
Neighbor has 10-12 outdoor dogs and several cats. The cats can enter the room if not careful and the dogs bark. So much barking.
The hosts sleep in the room directly next to you.
The hostess kept the house super clean and the common area furniture and TV was nice.
The house cat uses the toilet as a human does. Just sayin.
Neighborhood, we were told, was a rough area. We found it to be decent and the people friendly. It’s not a quaint cottage, it’s a working-class area.
Bedroom was as pictured, small and basic. There weren’t enough hangers or any drawers so we lived out of our suitcases. Their were chocolates on our bed the first night, a thoughtful touch. They need new, matching towels and the bed could be replaced. That would help class it up and make it more comfortable.
We had limited room in the fridge and none in the freezer but the hosts had a “use anything you need” policy which was nice.
The hostess was helpful and super nice. The host we barely met. We did get locked out and as the hosts live there with you while you rent the room, so we figured they’d use on of their keys to let us in. They said they had to call a locksmith to let them in (saying both the front and back door already had a key on the inside) as they couldn’t open either door with their own house keys. They demanded about $110 for the locksmith without a receipt and told us it would be $150 if we waited (80 and 123 euro). We would have paid when we got home, after seeing a receipt even though we were so confused as to why leaving your key inside of one door would require a locksmith but the host was angry and yelling and we were shocked that a host would treat a guest like that and it was quite alarming while it happened. The hostility and menacing tone were enough to send us to a hotel after a few nights and rent a different AirBnB entirely, one where we wouldn’t be yelled at while on vacation. It was bizarre.
If you need someplace to crash within 15 minutes of Dublin and you don’t need a pretty view, you can’t beat the price. The value IS good. Just don’t get locked out!
items.one_line: Neighbor's ten dogs bark constantly; toilet-trained house cat

I tried to communicate to him that he needed to clean the freezer and that the tv did not work the very next morning through Airbnb. I did not receive a
items.comment_full:
The place was ok not the best but if you have to you could make it work. Be prepared to bring an iron, toilet paper, sponge to wash dishes or disposable dishes. If you need ice you will have to go and purchase because the host thinks built up ice in the freezer shows the refrigerator is working.
I was a bit surprised by the unkempt appearance of the host when he arrived and that should have been my cue but I was taught not to judge.
I tried to communicate to him that he needed to clean the freezer and that the tv did not work the very next morning through Airbnb. I did not receive a response until late afternoon. He did not even call or message to say he was coming he just came which I found to be very intrusive.
When I attempted to address the issues he asked me to communicate by WhatsApp. I refused and he immediately became unfriendly.
He arrived at the apartment inappropriately dressed in a pair of extremely short shorts, hair was unkempt as if he dragged himself out of bed and was annoyed. That was a bit much for us but I continued to be professional.
While I was addressing the concerns he kept playing with the remote and staring at the tv until I had to tell him I need him to pay attention. He did not give us a clean sponge ( brown and dirty with food particles attached) for the dishes nor did he clean the freezer even after we spoke to him about it.
The surrounding area had a lot of garbage piled up, garbage bins overflowing. For the two days it just stayed there and was there before the weekend we found out.
This place was far from the center, I can admit that but if the host was a bit more courteous we could have made it work. I asked about his cancellation policy and he just said we can leave whenever we like.
After he left he started an endless argument by text messages, accusing us of breaking his tv and that an iron was not listed in the amenities. That was fine we could have purchased an iron but due to his aggressive behavior and continuous messages we started to feel unsafe. It was strange how this guy was jus happy going back and forth with two women about something so silly. I asked him to stop texting but he continued.
I understand this guy does not own the place he is a caretaker but his ill mannerism, intrusive behavior and his very disheveled appearance is enough to drive customers away.
items.one_line: Unkempt host arrives in very short shorts inappropriately

But the hosts just kept telling us their appartment is not insured and they can‘t do anything about it. They kept contacting me but there was
items.comment_full: Avoid this appartment at all costs. It is unsafe to stay here, we had a break in and our things got stolen worth 1500€. Of course it‘s not anyones fault of the break in but the hosts reaction was not acceptable. First we were told not to go to the local police, which I did anyway. Someone came to fix the window with some screws but that was it. We felt unsave during out whole stay. The Airbnb team did a great job and gave us all the support we could get. But the hosts just kept telling us their appartment is not insured and they can‘t do anything about it. They kept contacting me but there was never a sincere apology or understanding towards us. Because their biggest problem was the window where they broke in. But the nightmare continues. At our departure then we left the appartment clean as we found it and left the keys inside the door. After we left that day we got lots of calls, but as we were sightseeing I couldn‘t always answer. We were accused to have locked the appartment, i was even shouted at on the phone .it is not mentioned anywhere were to exactly leave the key in the appartment and we left it on the door inside. As we usually do in all our previous stays. A holiday should be enjoyed but for us it was a terrifying nightmare. And the hosts behaviour is rediculous. I travelled the whole world but i never had such a bad experience which I wish no one to have.
items.one_line: Break-in theft; hosts told not to call police

When did it turn into a business, designed to get as much money out of you, but offering all fancy and new surroundings deep cleaned by professionals to perfection? I’m torn because the latter is what I’ve become accustomed to, so at first I was completely turned off by the state of this rental. Maybe I missed the secret code for “party house” by not noticing the abundance of twin beds stuffed into each room. I booked a last minute stay for my visiting nephew and my elderly mother. I did
items.comment_full:
Remember when AirBnB first started it was just people letting you come stay at their house for super cheap? When did it turn into a business, designed to get as much money out of you, but offering all fancy and new surroundings deep cleaned by professionals to perfection? I’m torn because the latter is what I’ve become accustomed to, so at first I was completely turned off by the state of this rental. Maybe I missed the secret code for “party house” by not noticing the abundance of twin beds stuffed into each room. I booked a last minute stay for my visiting nephew and my elderly mother. I didn’t realize how much I paid (IN FEES especially) until later, and it was about 5 times more than this stay was worth. I relied too much on the fact that Farhad is promoted as superhost, but looking back none of the reviews gave honest reports about the state of the house. It’s more of a tear-down than a rehab, but again, I have to check my privilege, because…did I die? Become infested with bedbugs or attacked by cockroaches? No. Was I able to take a hot shower and cook a meal? Was I right in the middle of a cool neighborhood with free parking? Yes!
I did not get a good nights sleep ( the beds are hard, there’s a bar across the street with music raging til 2am, a houseless person camped in the front yard at 11pm and had to be asked to leave). I have no idea what a $150 cleaning fee is used for at this place because it is in serious need of deep cleaning. With that said, if I had only paid a couple hundred bucks for this stay (which I would equate to crashing at the dude’s house I used to smoke weed at in high school) then I probably would have not complained as much. Good on Farhad though, for getting a piece of that capitalist pie that AirBnb has become. And shame on me, for how uppity my arse has become.
items.one_line: Teardown house disguised as superhosts with fancy fees

We stayed for 5 nights with our first host and then moved to Alex's place.
When we arrived, Alex greeted us at t
items.comment_full:
Honestly, this was a bit of a shock for me, as I have been using Airbnb at least for 6 years and have done many travels, recommended many friends but I had never experienced such an unprofessional and rude treatment by a host.
Please allow me to walk you through the situation. We were in Paris since the 3rd of May, but stayed with another amazing host, who unfortunately did not had availability to accommodate our entire stay, so we decided to split it between two bookings. We stayed for 5 nights with our first host and then moved to Alex's place.
When we arrived, Alex greeted us at the door and welcomed us into the building. Then he proceeded to escort us to a completely different accommodation, which was a single tiny room and he quickly left us. I realised (within 10 seconds) that this place is not the one that we had booked and chased Alex down the hallway.
After I told him about this, the "fun" part began for us. At first he tried to convince me that the place is the same, only the wallpaper is different. Putting aside the fact that his ad states apartment, but all his bookings apparently are single rooms on one floor in a residential building, he tried to convince me that "2,4 or 6 meters more" (the comparison between our original room and the new one) is not such a big deal. "Sorry, room not available" were the words that he continued to repeat. He then gave me the option to chose between two rooms which were very very small, as opposed to the one that we had booked.
Upon asking him why were we charged for the booking and why was it available when we had made the reservation 4 weeks ago, but now isn't, he told me that there is an issue with the pluming in the room. "DO YOU WANT A ROOM WITHOUT A TOILET, EH!?!?"
When I answered "no" in a calm manner, he proceeded to speak to me with a raised voice: OF COURSE YOU DON'T WANT A ROOM WITHOUT A TOILET, SO YOU HAVE TO WAIT, TODAY IS 8TH OF MAY AND THE PLUMBER CANNOT COME. TOMORROW WHEN HE COMES YOU WILL MOVE TO THE OTHER ROOM!"
By the way, he never offered to move us the next day, so I presume this was also a lie.
He then called me to his office downstairs "to work things out" and asked if I wanted a refund or a discount. I told him that I do not want a refund, as we do not have the time and energy to move and search for a new hotel from noon, because those were our last two days in Paris.
Afterwards, I got really frustrated and went up to the rooms, my wife calmed me down and we chose one of the rooms, because we simply did not want to ruin our last two days of hard-earned honeymoon vacation.
Upon unpacking, we began to see that even this room has not been properly cleaned!!! I want to attach pictures, but I'm not sure that there is such an option, when leaving a review.
I rarely complain about these types of situations and just let them slide, but his rude attitude was what drove me over the cliff.
If you take all this under consideration + the fact that there was constant construction work from the apartment below us, from 9 AM until 7PM, you can begin to imagine how we felt.
I love AirBnb, but stays like this need to be properly addressed, so nobody else will experience what we had to go through - lies, rude treatment and lack of taking responsibility.
items.one_line: Escorted to wrong apartment; host tried denial

items.comment_full:
Looking at the past reviews, it seems that my visit only was a bad timing, but I do have to let know that my stay was really bad.
Multiple issues happened during my stay at Javier's place, which seriously deteriorated my experience :
- Insalubrity : cockroaches were found in the room, including one crawling on the bed in the morning. Javier said fumigation services occur 2 times a year, but there they were.
- Missing utilites : electricity was missing in the bedroom. As the result, the TV did not work, A/C heating did not work, and same goes for power outlets. As a result, we spent our nights in the cold, and without TV.
- Defective equipments : flush did not work properly and we had to manually fix it everytime. The bedroom's window could not close. It is worth noticing that this is the window that faces the street, one can easily imagine how unsecure we've felt during the entire stay.
- Miscellaneous : the water heater is only of a 50L capacity which is hardly sufficient for a 2-people flat. We had to wait 35min between each of us' shower. It is a detail, but that adds up to the total.
Plus, all of this could have ended another way, but Javier only offered help during my last night in the flat, and declined my request for a partial refund. Needless to say that AirBnb granted it immediately looking at the pictures and videos.
Apart from all this, location and check-in process are worth highlighting.
items.one_line: Cockroaches crawling on bed, no electricity, unfixable plumbing

items.comment_full:
After staying for an entire week, I came up with the following assessment. What seems to be the selling point turned out to be the worst part. First, the grass, do not walk barefoot. I walked barefoot and ended up bleeding with the razor-sharp weeds that are growing. At first, I thought I stepped on glass but realize there is are pesky weeds that are growing that feels like your stepping on needles. On to the bar area, It looks pretty much shut down; the 2 TVs are covered, the two beer taps have nothing attached, perhaps the host can offer it as an add on, the general counter needs to be cleaned.
The pool and Jacuzzi were ice cold, we used it for 1 hour during the whole week. Across from the pool, is a neighbor that has a stable of horses. The horse droppings can be smelled from a distance and the number of flies that come from there is insane, you cannot open the back yard door without letting in several flies at a time. The host should consider pest control since I found myself chasing flies out of the home.
The bathroom shower head is leaking into the wall and creating a mold situation that can be smelled, and the hot water tank seems to be of low capacity since the hot water runs out rather quickly.
Lastly on a Saturday Morning around 9:30 AM, a team of landscapers showed up while I was having breakfast with my daughter in the backyard. They used very loud tools (Lawnmowers, Weedwhackers etc.) I approached them and asked if they can come back in an hour, I told one guy in Spanish and another guy in English, and they didn't seem to care and continued cutting grass until they were done.
items.one_line: Razor-sharp weeds and horse flies plague entire backyard

items.comment_full: e have been members of airbnb since 2009, have both rented our place and traveled all over the world for work and personal travel. We have had nothing but 5 stars and have only given 5 stars. However, we have never had an experience like this before. We rented this place for 57 days with our small children during covid. When we arrived, the place was different from the photos in the description, the furniture is all different and not new. Even the outside photos of the house are old, the house is gray with a teal door. The place had cobwebs and spiders everywhere (my kids were terrified of spiders and wouldn't go to bed the first night), including the oven (I've literally never seen cobwebs in an oven before), which the owner told me when it wouldn't work initially that the pilot light must have turned off since it was just 'cleaned'. The children's room had bunk beds (not like the photo in the listing) and the fan is positioned right over the top bunk that my 4 year old son hit his head on the fan blades twice.  The location is about 4 blocks from the beach, which would be great except the entire block is surrounded by homeless encampments and we had to dodge human feces and drug paraphernalia in order to walk to the beach. Then we noticed the fleas. Each night, we did not sleep well because there are no screens on the windows and it was very very hot. And we woke up with bites all over, which we mistakenly thought were mosquito bites. We have never had a bug issue before, so we initially thought it was mosquito bites. Then after 10 days or so, we noticed our dog was scratching like crazy and we turned her over to see she was infested with fleas! Then we started looking and found them on our bed (our dog slept in her own dog bed on the floor) and found some jumping off the couch. We contacted the owner, who was great at communicating whenever we had an issue. She seemed very nice and responsive. Her response was 'there is bug spray in the garage' and to use 'essential oils' to kill the fleas because it was nontoxic, which I appreciated. So I personally went to the store to buy flea medication for our dog, flea shampoo, the eucalyptus spray the owner said to use, the whole lot and I came back and spent hours cleaning the entire house from top to bottom and doing hours of  laundry washing everything, spraying everywhere and vacuuming the beds, having to take off work to do so. Then we went to bed and woke up again with more bites. We informed airbnb, who was extremely unhelpful, saying that if you rent a place for a monthly rental it is up to the owner to decide to give you a refund. The owner wanted to come over to spray with more essential oils that day, which we waited for her for 4 hours and when I reached out to see where she was she informed me she did not leave yet and it would be yet another 2 hours to get there. We were so grossed out at the fleas and bites, we could not stay there with our small children being stuck inside, we did not feel comfortable there, especially surrounded by the homeless encampments and the very unsecure house. We also barely slept with the windows open because we would hear people going through the recycling in the middle of the night and we could hear the homeless people talking/fighting. We were scared someone might break in. We also had 2 packages stolen. After countless hours on the phone with airbnb, the owner agreed to refund us 37 nights out of the 57. She agreed to charge us for 20 nights, even though we only stayed there for 12 nights. We were charged $3180 for 12 nights.  We also spent over $200 in medication and cleaning supplies and went to a hotel to figure out where to go next, since we had expected this place to be our home for 57 nights. Airbnb never did ask to help relocate us, they would not call us back, we had to call and re-explain our situation every single time. It was a nightmare to work with airbnb. I would not recommend anyone staying in a monthly airbnb, you basically have no rights as the renter. I am sure the owner will clean it now, but I would not recommend staying here if you are a family. This place is best for one or two young people looking for a cheap place that isn't bothered by the surroundings and just looking to be close by the beach.
items.one_line: Cobwebs everywhere including oven terrify kids on first night
