The former tenants took the hair dryer, so I was without one for about 2.5 weeks during my 4 week stay, but Karen ordered me a new one via Amazon.  My main issues were an enormous cockroach I saw 3 weeks into my stay that was looking over my shoulder reading my book with me as I sat in
items.comment_full: Karen's place is in a great convenient neighborhood near some really good restaurants, a Whole Foods, Russ & Daughters and SoHo.  There's a great laundromat down the street also that does laundry within a day. I wouldn't call this neighborhood SoHo, but the Lower East Side (LES). The apt was in good condition.  The former tenants took the hair dryer, so I was without one for about 2.5 weeks during my 4 week stay, but Karen ordered me a new one via Amazon.  My main issues were an enormous cockroach I saw 3 weeks into my stay that was looking over my shoulder reading my book with me as I sat in bed, but it's NYC and these things happen, and some construction going on right next door. Construction is also not new to NYC and out of Karen's hands. If you wake up and are out of the apt from 7am, to 5pm, are a tourist that is never home during the weekday, and/or not working from home, then you could easily avoid the construction that was like a drill hammer the size of the entire wall of the apt that shook the entire apt non-stop, including the beds, from 7am to 5pm.  Weekends and nights were silent.  It died down a bit to some sporadic bangs throughout the day that also shook the whole apt, by the end of my stay.  Karen tried her best to work with me and Airbnb to give me a refund, but Airbnb offered me no other affordable options in NYC and no discounted rates at other available Airbnbs, so I was stuck largely due to Airbnb's policies.  I was in NYC working from this apt and the noise was so loud that I couldn't hear my meetings and people could not hear me, even with headphones.  I'm giving Karen 3 stars for the giant roach and having no means of spraying it to death and because, if I'd known about the construction before booking and having to deal with a month of absolute hell, then I would've booked somewhere else and I really would've liked that option.  Otherwise, even with the giant roach, I would've given her 5 stars.  I took out the garbage upon leaving as instructed, even though there is a cleaning fee, and ensured my food was out of the fridge (no freezer), but left some dishes.  I just have to point these things out, as I rarely leave a not-so-stellar review.
items.one_line: Enormous cockroach reads book over shoulder in bed

items.comment:
First off I just want to say Airbnb has been so empathetic to my situation and has handled it so fast and well.
Made it to California all the way from Canada. I get to my air bnb two hours early, no big deal I just wait outside. I had messaged the host (NOT Victoria, she made someone named Alina N her cohost but no one was responding). asking if I could check in earlier, but no response at all. I was getting a bit nervous as to why she didn’t respond but just kept waiting. As I’m waiting outside a girl walks onto the porch and asks who I am. I say I’m the air bnb guest staying at the loft
items.comment_full:
First off I just want to say Airbnb has been so empathetic to my situation and has handled it so fast and well.
Made it to California all the way from Canada. I get to my air bnb two hours early, no big deal I just wait outside. I had messaged the host (NOT Victoria, she made someone named Alina N her cohost but no one was responding). asking if I could check in earlier, but no response at all. I was getting a bit nervous as to why she didn’t respond but just kept waiting. As I’m waiting outside a girl walks onto the porch and asks who I am. I say I’m the air bnb guest staying at the loft and she gets really weird all of a sudden, confused you could say. She invited me to wait inside and I accepted. 5 minutes later, a guy named Brandon walks in claiming to own the house and has no idea I’m staying there.
Turns out, the girl who walked in earlier was his cousin who paid, I quote, “so much fucking money” to stay there for the month... He had the audacity to offer a “room at the back”. I refused. It also turns out, that the host wasn’t even responding to me on the app, but texting him non stop as he was freaking out. He started becoming hostile with me saying he can’t do anything but give me a dingy room at the back of the house, and when I still refused he says “well, you’re not even supposed to check in until 3. You’re on my property, I could have reacted very differently since you’re technically not supposed to be here.”
Obviously this was an indirect threat and I politely said I’ll wait outside, but he got “nice” again and said “no no it’s okay”. My gut was screaming to get out of there. He then goes “wow the host is asking me to speak in private”, super sketchy!! I asked him for the hosts number and called her from my own cell. She said there was nothing she could do, that I shouldn’t have an “attitude” and that it’s best if I just leave and cancel.
I refused and said if she couldn’t offer me the place I booked, SHE has to cancel. Her response was “the app is glitching for me” and “I’ve been on the phone for 50 min and no response from Airbnb”. Her app worked fine when she was taking my payment and confirming all dates, but sure. She then proceeded to send me countless rude messages saying how I’m not humble, how I should have understood that mistakes happen, I’m selfish blah blah. I mean, of COURSE I have an attitude.... you literally scammed me of a loft I PREPAID for. No logic. Her English was terrible too, and my reason for pointing that out is that if you can’t communicate effectively with the people you’re hosting, don’t host.
Anyways, her excuse was BS because I was able to get in touch with Airbnb in literally 5 min. FIVE MINUTES. They were supportive and refunded me within the hour. I’ve only ever had good experiences so this was definitely an abnormal situation. Glad I got out and it’s probably for the better how it ended up. I have a beautiful place in Santa Monica now for a few days and so happy.
Nevertheless, kind of a scary situation to be in and I can’t believe the audacity of some people. Brandon, how dare you threaten a paying guest by saying “I could have reacted very differently to you being in my house when I didn’t know”. These low life, flakey LA folk give the rest a bad name.
items.one_line: Surprise occupant claims he owns the house mid-visit

The location was ok but it did not inspire a sense of security. The apartment its
items.comment_full:
This booking was the worst experience I ever had with AirBnB. From the time of the booking, I informed my host I will check-in at midday. He confirmed the time with me on the day of the arrival. I preferred to check in at 5 pm but he really asked me to be there between 12:00--12:30. However, he did not show up and because I had to be somewhere else by 1 pm, I was forced to drop my luggage at a nearby café out of the kindness of their heart and leave. The host said he will be back by 3 pm. He arrived at 3:40!
The location was ok but it did not inspire a sense of security. The apartment itself when I entered felt like a bomb has exploded inside. Even the trash bin in my room (dry trash) was not empty. He explained to me what was the cause of this mess, which made sense, but I don't understand why he couldn't ask someone else to manage his AirBnB if he is busy with other life obligations, especially because it was not something that could not be planned. He had plenty of planning time.
The first night the host tried to enter the apartment but because he did not have part of the key he couldn't and apparently decided to ring the bell at 3 am. I woke up but since I did not expect anyone at that hour I did not investigate how to open the door. The next night he arrived at 01:40 am talking on his phone and woke me up again.
This experience made me re-evaluate if AirBnB is even worth it and if I wouldn't have been more comfortable in a hostel. Overall, I wish Craig all the best but I can't recommend booking with him at least in the mid-future.
items.one_line: 40-minute late host, apartment bombed, trash in room

items.comment_full:
This is certainly not “muy cerca” to Polanco. It’s quite far, and in a pretty bad area. I am pretty adventurous, and I didn’t feel comfortable going out at night right away. During the daytime, there is a market and a strong police presence. They are trying to make it a good area, but it’s not there yet. I learned the most from speaking with an uber drive from the north part of the city, who knew the area better than your average uber driver who immediately tells you that it’s a bad area and to be safe. The more knowledgeable driver told me that this apartment is in a pocket of an area that is ok, but the areas surrounding it area very bad. So as long as you don’t venture too far, you’re fine, even at night. That made me feel better, but still…not very fun for an Airbnb claiming to be close to Polanco.
One positive, I learned the word “robo” in Spanish, which I had never heard before. I was hoping they meant there were a lot of robots in the area, but alas “robo” means robbery.
And there is only one bathroom, not 1.5 bathrooms as listed. I really only need one bathroom, so I didn’t bother reporting this to Airbnb for a reimbursement for the mis-listing.
Other than those things, the apartment itself is fine. It’s a decently nice apartment. You can hear the neighbors and their baby, but the area is quite quiet.
But I think my biggest issue was with that I found the host very difficult.
The microwave didn’t work when I arrived on Monday. This is a big deal because there is no oven, so I had no way to reheat leftovers. Very important in a bad area with no restaurants to walk to at night! But the host ordered a new microwave online, presumably to save a few dollars, and I didn’t have a microwave until Friday. That’s more than half of my one week stay that I was without microwave. The host should have replaced it on Tuesday, as she promised. (I would have reported this to Airbnb if not for the lie saying it would be replaced on Tuesday. In retrospect, I should have reported this to Airbnb. But I think it was too late).
Also, I found the host very difficult to get alone with. Before moving in, she asked me for a lot of information about me, but then refused to give me the information I needed until the day of my flight, which means if I didn't have cell access (this is common when traveling internationally), I wouldn't have been able to get where I needed. Fortunately, I have a “works everywhere” mobile plan for one more month, but it was scary. During my flight, I didn’t know if she was going to send me the information I needed or if my phone would work in the new country.
The host also crossed some lines in requests/demands. After entering the apartment to install the new microwave, she told me to not to leave the TV in standby (I’ve seen estimates that the energy costs of a TV in standby are something like $5 to $10 per year) but to turn the TV off. And to unplug all appliances every time I leave the house. (This seems to be common in Mexico, I later found, although completely unnecessary). I understand that she pays the energy costs, but that’s part of the deal of an Airbnb. I’m going to treat the Airbnb like my home, and I’m willing to pay those pennies per week to leave the TV in standby and have it turn on quickly. These were unreasonable demands and I found them very obnoxious.
The host also at one point messaged me "Hellloooooo" when I didn't respond fast enough to the unreasonable demand, which actually did not ask for a response. This is what I would expect from a child or a maybe girlfriend, but not an airbnb host. I chose to block the host on WhatsApp.
Most obnoxiously, there is a tip jar in the apartment. And there is a message next to the towels saying something like "I'm going to charge you if you get these dirty." Sometimes towels get dirty, this is the cost of doing business.
I chose a different Airbnb for my remaining two weeks in Mexico City, and I’m glad I did. I wouldn’t recommend this Airbnb. It’s not a good value compared to others in Mexico City.
items.one_line: Learned Spanish word robo meaning robbery near Polanco

items.comment_full: Very nice apartment - convenient location. But a few incidentals-takes about 5 minutes to get hot water in the MBR shower, bed in MBR is thin and rickety-groaned loaded with every movement, sheets didn’t stay on bed as they were too big for the thin (maybe 8-9 inches) mattress, batteries went dead for tv remote last night there-batteries for bedroom remote weren’t compatible so asked Debo if there were any on the premises. items.comment_full:
Check-in was easy, instructions provided was easy to follow & detailed.
The room was clean, however, the bathroom had traces of hair & dried up nasal mucus in the sink. The toilet was not clean either.

The bathroom itself smelled distinctly of dog and there was mould on the kitchen ceiling. Ev
items.comment_full:
We were a little disappointed by the place when we got there as it didn’t reflect the photos, clearly taken a long time ago. We were happy enough not to raise this with the AirB&B host as it's a great area and one we were keen to explore. However we soon soon found that the apartment was somewhat lacking, we were woken up by renovations in the flat below and when we asked about a bath-plug we were initially ignored and on following it up were made to feel as though the request was somehow unreasonable. The bathroom itself smelled distinctly of dog and there was mould on the kitchen ceiling. Even though the view was masked by windows too dirty to look through we decided to stick with it out of convenience and a sense of ‘fuck it, we’re here now.'
Then in an embarrassing and unfortunate twist of fate we realised we had arrived for our booking a day late. With dinner on the hob we received a message from the landlord to inform us of this, that we were not meant to be there and that if we did not pay we would be turfed out. We were reluctant to pay another 80 quid given the state of the place and asked, all things considered, if we might leave early in the morning. Thirty minutes later a man called Charlie (a propa east-end wide-boy) arrived at the door to kick us out.
We of course obliged but pointed out that given that we had been rented a poorly maintained and dirty room in a building site and were on the point of eating dinner it was a rather excessive and brusque way to be. Especially seeing as we had paid for three nights, only stayed two, and they had made it clear that the flat was was free.
Anyway, not an ideal stay but if you’re looking for a room in an interesting location, you don’t like baths and are better with dates than us, this could be for you.
items.one_line: Dinner on hob, arrived wrong day, muddy windows

items.comment:
Airbnb is being overrun by massive professionally managed companies. The result is that you get the worst parts of effectively staying at a hotel but none of the benefits neither of hotel services nor the original Airbnb intent.
Right after booking, I get an automated email a few screens long, with a bunch of irrelevant information. Part of it -- a request to fill in a form. I open the form -- it's like 3 pages long. First question -- my name. Hey, you already know my name, why are you asking?! Also, I'm going on vacation, not applying for a mortgage, I don't want to fill in forms for you
items.comment_full:
Airbnb is being overrun by massive professionally managed companies. The result is that you get the worst parts of effectively staying at a hotel but none of the benefits neither of hotel services nor the original Airbnb intent.
Right after booking, I get an automated email a few screens long, with a bunch of irrelevant information. Part of it -- a request to fill in a form. I open the form -- it's like 3 pages long. First question -- my name. Hey, you already know my name, why are you asking?! Also, I'm going on vacation, not applying for a mortgage, I don't want to fill in forms for your condo HOA. You figure out how to deal with them, it's none of my business.
Ok, so we arrive at the destination. Now, I have to dig through pages and pages of automated emails to find check-in instructions. Part of the directions are in the last email, but the code to the door is at the first one. Thank you for giving me a puzzle to solve at 11 PM after 4 hours in transit, I really needed it.
The unit itself is fine, but in the best traditions of professionally managed units: looks great on pictures, but somewhat dysfunctional in practice:
1) There's literally a dozen pillows spread around the unit, which makes for a good staging, but where are we supposed to put them when we actually use the place?
2) There's no washer and dryer in the unit, so we have to use the condo one. The host doesn't provide detergent (really, it's $10 for a lifetime supply at Costco). Ok, so I go downstairs to an ABC and buy my own. Go back -- surprise! I need to install an app to use the communal washer. Another surprise -- the hours are such that I can't use it today and I have to take time from my vacation tomorrow to accommodate for the condo laundry hours.
3) The AC is very loud. I sleep in earplugs, but that doesn't help.
All in -- it's not exactly a waste of money. But just beware of what you're getting into.
items.one_line: Automated emails puzzle guest with fragmented check-in instructions

For 2 nights we had a dribbling shower head
items.comment_full:
The apartment was located pretty near 2 tubes stations, so that was really convenient. However, the entire apartment was quite messy, but our rooms were not toooo bad. Slightly stained carpets and random used towels hung in the room, but tolerable. The part we found the most off putting was the toilet - it's pretty clean but when we first arrived, there was no water pressure anywhere. We had difficulties flushing and even washing our hands at the sink. But it got better. One thing however did not- the shower head. We could not get it to work at all. For 2 nights we had a dribbling shower head (with barely ANY pressure), so both my friend and I had to squat/kneel in the bathtub, cup our hands and splash it on ourselves. The bathtub did not look clean enough to be used for its purpose. When we contacted the host, they only replied after we left (since they were not around), but thankfully we were only there for 2 nights. We were quite ready to find another place after our first shower but we had already booked our transport from the tube station closest when we were leaving so we did not out of convenience. If you're not planning to shower, and were just staying for a night, it'll be fine. But if you were like us, who need a good shower from the polluted London air and summer heat, it's gonna be an extremely difficult and unpleasant shower.
There also seemed to be some kind of party going on every night below the apartment as well, with the smell of you-know-what that comes into our room and toilet, and the loud club music into the early mornings.
items.one_line: No water pressure, had to squat cup water on self

items.comment:

Initially I was going to leave at least 2 or 3 stars, but he has been threatening to leave me 1 star if I leave an honest review about him. I am willing to risk him leaving a dishonest review about me, just to warn future travelers about him. I am a lawyer with no reason to lie on this young man. //
The room itself is a good place for the price as listed, which was less than the price he actually charged me. Picture a guy’s college apartme
items.comment_full:
Beware: this person gave me a false address that he said is for “safety”, then he charged me a price for a room someone else slept in while I was there. Initially I was going to leave at least 2 or 3 stars, but he has been threatening to leave me 1 star if I leave an honest review about him. I am willing to risk him leaving a dishonest review about me, just to warn future travelers about him. I am a lawyer with no reason to lie on this young man. //
The room itself is a good place for the price as listed, which was less than the price he actually charged me. Picture a guy’s college apartment feel—not pristine but comfortable enough, a clearly lived in apartment. //
There were two other bedrooms, and two other guests. We got a long great and they were nice. //
My room was nice and spacious with great views. Living room offers beautiful view of the city. Bedroom was clean and very comfortable with big nice mirrored doors. Bathrooms and kitchen were messy tho, with fruit flies in kitchen and an overflowing gross frig that I didn’t want to even leave my leftover food in. //
Chinatown was a nice central spot for visiting Honolulu, not walkable to beach but that was fine for me. //
My only issue, which really bothers me, is that I reserved the master bedroom with a private bathroom, but he made me pay that price for a smaller room with no bath. This was a last minute trip and so I was glad he was willing to accommodate me the day before I was traveling. After I had requested the reservation, he contacted me to say the master wasn’t available after all, but that he had another room I could have. Only after I arrived and talked to the other guests did I learn that this was part of his scam. I said that if things changed I’d like the master but that it was no problem at all, I was glad to have a guest room at obviously the guest room (not master) rate. It wasn’t actually Joey who met me, it was another guy named Imran who met the building next door to give me keys. They said they couldn’t tell me the real address bc of safety and illegality. I also had to wait outside in the pouring rain with leather luggage bc the guy was so late. Luckily it was Hawaii rain, so warm, but still a very uncomfortable start to a stay.
The night I arrived I asked that guy about price adjustment when I checked in, and he said Joey would handle it. Imran said it would be no problem, and honestly I wasn’t worried. Never for one second did I presume this person would charge me for a room I did not stay in! So on my second to last day, I contacted Joey twice to remind him to make sure to adjust for the room difference. He sent me long messages saying no, he did not think he should change the price. I should’ve paid around $54/$56 per night based on the available guest room listing that was available when I requested a reservation, but instead I paid around $80. I honestly was dumbfounded. I wasn’t even worried about this before the end of my stay. I was scammed. There was a man who stayed in the master bedroom, but I paid for that room I didn’t have. //
I asked for a price adjustment on night 1. However, 8 days after leaving Hawaii this person “Joey” is accusing me of “stressing” him out, “harming” him and “taking advantage” of him. He also admitted that he had to pay his last guest $60 to change her review. I am not interested in being bribed. His behavior is not just unprofessional, but frightening. Although I was a perfect guest, he is threatening to leave me a 1 star review unless I delete this and I’m sorry but it’s not worth it. //
Stay at your own risk. It’s a decent place for $50-some per night, but not worth being scammed at $80+. I can’t believe hosts are allowed to bully and threaten people this way.
items.one_line: Host threatens bad review if guest leaves honest review

items.comment_full:
This is a pretty apartment with a good amount of space and lots within walking distance, but there were a few drawbacks for us with the most significant being the significant noise from the surrounding clubs at night from about 10 pm to 3:30 am every night we were there from Wednesday - Monday.
There are two full bathrooms (one is separated into two parts) not 2 full and a half bath.  
The apartment is more cute than functional.  There were no bedside tables or dressers in two of the bedrooms.  There were a few things broken including toilet seats, toilet paper holders, blinds on the screen separating the single bed from the living room, missing rubber tip at at least one of the hard metal doorstops on which my husband sliced his foot open getting out of bed in the middle of the night necessitating a 2 am run for medical supplies.  
The kitchen items are cheap quality but mostly sufficient (except for not enough mugs or glasses for 6 people, let alone 16, and not enough bowls, etc for 16) but were scattered between the two kitchens so we had to hunt around for things.  There were enough towels but only a couple of washcloths.  I didn't realize from the listing this was actually two separate apartments side by side and you can see the hall between them from the street.  
The apartment was otherwise relatively nice for 6 people with nice TVs, a relatively nice space to spend time in (though the futon couch was quite hard), and bedrooms decently spacious. However, beds were quite firm (all of them, including spare mattresses)
It is directly surrounded by at least 2-3 very loud clubs making it difficult to sleep due to the loud music and the constant yelling and honking (lots of people on the streets and outside the clubs) and occasional sirens every night.  I actually got up to see if there was a drag race outside the window at 320 am on Sunday morning (there wasnt one, just tons of people pouring out of the clubs) due to loud group chanting and revving engines.  One bedroom is farther back from the street and wasn't very loud but all 3 bedrooms at the front of the apartment were extremely noisy.
items.one_line: Door stopper slices foot, surround sound clubs, missing bathroom fixtures

items.comment:
My husband and I visited in the end of July (July 28-31), which is why our experience may differ from yours! To start: We loved the location and view from the apartment - HOWEVER, due to the record high temp in Florence during our stay we were only able to stay in the apartment for 1 night. items.comment_full:
My husband and I visited in the end of July (July 28-31), which is why our experience may differ from yours! To start: We loved the location and view from the apartment - HOWEVER, due to the record high temp in Florence during our stay we were only able to stay in the apartment for 1 night. We were aware there was no AC in the apartment, but relied on another guests post that 2 fans made it more than sufficient. Maybe that’s true for June, but definitely not in July. So take this review as more of a PSA:
Con: Lack of AC - We spent the first night there but woke up drenched in sweat and had to shower before going out even though we both showered before bed. After a morning of exploring Florence we came back to rest following lunch and it was miserable in the apartment even with windows open and fans going. After 45min of sitting inside miserable we made the decision to bite the bullet, cut our losses (financially speaking) and check into a hotel to salvage our trip. We still used the apartment as a basecamp during the day if we needed a bathroom and to use for the views at night. The balcony gives a spectacular view, but it’s not really usable to sit out and eat a meal. There are a TON of birds that perch on the roof across and get way too friendly/ try to grab any food you have and pooped all over the table/chairs/ floor, which made it unappealing to use during the day. Camilla warned us about the birds during check-in & said this was due to the downstairs guy constantly feeding them, but they were sparse at night which was great!
PROS: location is spectacular and right in the middle of the action but not loud at night! WiFi worked great and they gave awesome suggestions from food/drinks/ ships around - really appreciated!! We had a wonderful experience regardless of the AC issue and enjoyed using the balcony in the evening to enjoy a glass of wine and the view of the Duomo! Shower pressure was great. Bed was decent and front futon was comfy!
We don’t want this to be a bad review but more of a PSA as to July in the heat. There wasn’t anything wrong with the apartment necessarily. Not as updated as many others we’ve stayed in, but was as pictured.
items.one_line: Record heat forces guests to abandon apartment after one night
